The Red Sea crossing is given in Isaiah 49:10 as a prelude to the Second Advent in the very next verse (vs. 11).

[The Red Sea crossing is given in Isaiah 63:12 in the context of]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4937}[the Second Advent (vss. 1--6), and the Jewish remnant in that passage (vss. 15--19) are Tribulation Jews awaiting deliverance by the Messiah (vss. 7--9).]{.class_4938}

[There is nothing, I say, "nothing" (absolutely NOTHING) on this earth that will ]{.class_4938}[prevent ]{.class_4937}[you from learning the Bible like sitting under a ]{.class_4938}[Bible corrector ]{.class_4937}[whose faith has been deposited ]{.class_4938}[in a lost pile of papers that disappeared from this earth more than 1,500 years ago. ]{.class_4937}[The "original" of Psalm 77 disappeared long before it could be put into any book of "original autographs" containing Isaiah, Malachi, Matthew, and Paul.]{.class_4938}

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ534.xhtml}

PSALM 78

78:1 "Give ear, O my people, to my law: incline your ears to the words of my mouth.

2 1 will open my mouth in a parable: I will utter dark sayings of old:

3 Which we have heard and known, and our fathers have told us.

4 We will not hide them from their children, shewing to the generation to come the praises of the LORD, and his strength, and his wonderful works that he hath done.

5 For he established a testimony in Jacob, and appointed a law in Israel, which he commanded our fathers, that they should make them known to their children:

6 That the generation to come might know them, even the children which should be born; who should arise and declare them to their children:

7 That they might set their hope in God, and not forget the works of God, but keep his commandments:

8 And might not be as their fathers, a stubborn and rebellious generation; a generation that set not their heart aright, and whose spirit was not stedfast with God."

[As anyone can see at a glance, the Psalm is an extremely abbreviated history of the nation of Israel from Exodus 7 to 2 Samuel 10. All of the commentators seize on 1 Corinthians 10:3--12 to ]{.class_4938}[spiritualize ]{.class_4937}[the passage, utterly disregarding the fact that it was written explicitly for ]{.class_4938}[Israelites ]{.class_4937}[and their children (vs. 6)---exactly as Simon Peter quotes matters regarding the Holy Spirit in Acts 2:39. No ]{.class_4938}[Gentiles ]{.class_4937}[are considered in either quotation. The ]{.class_4938}["generation" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 6 is a reference to the generation of Hebrews 8:9, and the ]{.class_4938}["generation" ]{.class_4939}[of Hebrews 3:10 is the ]{.class_4938}["stubborn and rebellious generation" ]{.class_4939}[of this Psalm (vs. 8). This will give advanced revelation (found in 1611) which is purposely obscured by Kroll, who erases the word ]{.class_4938}["parable" ]{.class_4939}[in verse 2, and converts it to an "allegory." Briggs alters it to "poem"; Baethgen alters it to "proverb"; and Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, swept along by the riptide of stupidity, declare that it is a "similitude." (The way of the transgressors may be "hard," but it is not LONELY. There are always plenty of Biblical scholars on the "broad way" to cheer you up as you mess with the Book.) Spurgeon says "analogies" as soon as he sees the word, although he has to cite Matthew 13:13 as a cross reference, and the word there was NOT "analogy." Who ever heard of the ]{.class_4938}["Analogies of the Kingdom of Heaven"]{.class_4937}[?]{.class_4938}

"[O my people." ]{.class_4939}[The Psalm has nothing to do with Paul's Gentile converts in 1 Corinthians 10 according to the first laws of exegesis: Always ask (1) Who is speaking? and (2) To whom is he speaking?]{.class_4938}

[Far from being just a similitude or an "analogy" or a poem, the English text (which was evidently obscure to the commentators) says that what you are about to read is to be classified as ]{.class_4938}["dark sayings." ]{.class_4939}[There is nothing "dark" about Paul in 1 Corinthians 10:9--12, where he says that griping and lusting as the Israelites did will bring God's judgment on a disobedient Christian. That is as transparent as air.]{.class_4938}

[Much can be said ]{.class_4938}[devotionally ]{.class_4937}[about the first eight verses; little insight is needed to pick up the "spiritual" applications. Bullinger wisely notes that since the same Hebrew word was used for "parables" and ]{.class_4938}["dark sayings"]{.class_4939}[ that there is going to be more to the history "than appears on the surface." ]{.class_4938}[Yes, there certainly will be. ]{.class_4937}[When we get to verses 24, 26, 44, 51, 54, and 65, there will be all kinds of trouble.]{.class_4938}

[Verses 3 and 4. The truth should be passed down from generation to generation (see Paul's desire in 2 Tim. 2:2). As the truth about God goes from mouth to mouth, it will tend (perfectly naturally) to gradually eliminate GOD (see the degeneration demonstrated in the "relaying" that went on from God to Balak---Num. 22:12--15---through Balaam and Balak's "attachés"). What is to be passed down is God's ]{.class_4938}["wonderful works" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 4), the proof of His ]{.class_4938}["strength," ]{.class_4939}[and the things for which He should be praised (vs. 4). The writer of Proverbs is on this theme constantly so that his children will be wise (Prov. 1:8, 10, 15; 2:1; 3:1, 4, 10, 20, etc.). The ]{.class_4938}["fathers" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 3) go back to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob (see Acts 3:25) and pass the truth on to the heads of the twelve tribes (Deut. 1:15) who give it to the families in the tribes (Josh. 7:14).]{.class_4938}

[The commandment to pass on the ]{.class_4938}["testimony" ]{.class_4939}[and the ]{.class_4938}["law" ]{.class_4939}[to the children is clearly given in Deuteronomy 11:19--20 and Deuteronomy 4:9. As a matter of fact, if a man was a king over Israel he had to hand letter a ]{.class_4938}[personal copy ]{.class_4937}[of the law for ]{.class_4938}[his own reading ]{.class_4937}[(Deut. 17:18); a thing which no Gentile president, king, pope, priest, dictator, ambassador, prince, duke, earl, emperor, or "chairman" has done since the foundation of the world. King Alfred of England is the only one who made a token "stab" at it. You always remember something better if you have to copy it by hand. (This explains my own habit of hand-lettering more than 40,000 lines of notes in ten different notebooks, plus 1,600 pages in the Bible. I do not trust my memory.)]{.class_4938}

[The purpose for passing down the truth from generation to generation is negative: ]{.class_4938}["might not be as their fathers, a stubborn and rebellious generation\.... and whose spirit was not stedfast with God" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 8). There are three ]{.class_4938}[positive ]{.class_4937}[things in view:]{.class_4938}

[1. That they ]{.class_4938}["might set their hope in God."]{.class_4939}

[2. That they might ]{.class_4938}["not forget the works of God."]{.class_4939}

[3. That they might ]{.class_4938}["keep his conunandments" ]{.class_4939}[(see Deut. 30:10).]{.class_4938}

In the New Testament, Paul delivers this charge to faithful stewards (1 Cor. 4:1--2). This charge comes in seven mysteries (eight if you count 1 Cor. 2:7 and 1 Tim. 3:9 as another one) which they are to keep, preserve, and pass on to the next generation (2 Tim. 2:2).

78:9 "The children of Ephraim, being armed, and carrying bows, turned back in the day of battle.

10 They kept not the covenant of God, and refused to walk in his law;

11 And forgat his works, and his wonders that he had shewed them.

12 Marvellous things did he in the sight of their fathers, in the land of Egypt, in the field of Zoan.

13 He divided the sea, and caused them to pass through; and he made the waters to stand as an heap.

14 In the daytime also he led them with a cloud, and all the night with a light of fire."

[There is a problem with the ]{.class_4938}["children of Ephraim" ]{.class_4939}[turning back in the day of battle. They didn't ever turn back in the ]{.class_4938}["day of battle" ]{.class_4939}[(see Judg. 3:27, 5:14, 7:24, etc.). Several judges come from Ephraim (Judg. 10:1, 12:15; 1 Sam. 1:1). There are only three weak places, and in view of the fact that Joshua represented Ephraim in the searching out of the land (Num. 13:8), it would be pretty risky to accuse Ephraim of "turning back" in the ]{.class_4938}["day of battle." ]{.class_4939}[In fact, they cut off more than they can chew (Josh. 17:14--18) and failed to drive out all of their enemies (Judg. 1:29), but as far as that goes, so did Judah (1:19), Benjamin (1:21), Manasseh (1:27), and Zebulun (1:30).]{.class_4938}

[The only other defection was Jephthah's case (Judg. 12), but here, the Ephraimites did not "turn back"; they just didn't offer assistance. In 1 Samuel 14:22 they are hiding, but they come out of hiding to ]{.class_4938}[attack ]{.class_4937}[(1 Sam. 14:22). It is Ephraim who fights for Gideon (Judg. 3:27).]{.class_4938}

[To circumnavigate this problem, the commentators decide that the word "Ephraim" here stands for "Israel" (as it does much later: Hosea, Amos, et al.). This would make Psalm 78 the only place in the entire Bible where Ephraim's ]{.class_4938}["children" ]{.class_4939}[are mentioned as Israel ]{.class_4938}[before ]{.class_4937}[the division of the kingdom after Solomon. (First Chronicles 9:3 and 12:30 are the literal descendants of Ephraim, not some other tribe or Israel collectively.) Second Chronicles 25:7 is the ten northern tribes, ]{.class_4938}[not the nation of Israel, ]{.class_4937}[when Israel becomes the ten northern tribes, in contrast to Judah and Benjamin (Ezek. 37:16; Hos. 4:17, 5:3). Ephraim is a TRIBE, and the ]{.class_4938}["children of Ephraim" ]{.class_4939}[are ]{.class_4938}[not ]{.class_4937}[the children of Israel---not even where the word is substituted AFTER the division of the kingdom. ]{.class_4938}["Ephraim" ]{.class_4939}[is desolate ]{.class_4938}["among the tribes of Israel" ]{.class_4939}[(Hos. 5:9), and that is why it is omitted in the book of Revelation (Rev. 7:4--8).]{.class_4938}

The "easy way out" for the commentators was the wrong way. They can get into a stew faster than an oyster.

[Note: ]{.class_4938}["Zebulun and Naphtali were a people that jeoparded their lives unto the death\...For the divisions of Reuben there were great thoughts of heart\.... Gilead abode beyond Jordan: and why did Dan remain in ships? Asher continued on the sea shore, and abode in his breaches\...Ephraim, being armed\...turned back in the day of battle" ]{.class_4939}[(Judg. 5 with Ps. 78). The context was the Second Advent AGAIN. ]{.class_4938}[Asaph was prophesying. ]{.class_4937}[This "dark saying" is something that is yet future. Verses 10 and 11 (]{.class_4938}["They kept not the covenant of God\...forgat his works"]{.class_4939}[) may have application to Ephraim as "Israel" in Hosea, but this would be AFTER 1 Kings 13. ]{.class_4938}[Asaph is prophesying.]{.class_4937}

[Verse 12 mentions the ]{.class_4938}["Zoan" ]{.class_4939}[of Numbers 13:22, which the scholars call "Tanis"---see Isaiah 19:11. It was the capital of Egypt; the word meaning "the place of departure."]{.class_4938}

[Verses 13 and 14 are self explanatory; historically, this is Exodus 12--15 (see ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Ex]{.class_4937}[odus: chaps. 12--15).]{.class_4938}

78:15 "He clave the rocks in the wilderness, and gave them drink as out of the great depths.

16 He brought streams also out of the rock, and caused waters to run down like rivers.

17 And they sinned yet more against him by provoking the most High in the wilderness.

18 And they tempted God in their heart by asking meat for their lust.

19 Yea, they spake against God; they said, Can God furnish a table in the wilderness?

20 Behold, he smote the rock, that the waters gushed out, and the streams overflowed; can he give bread also? can he provide flesh for his people?

21 Therefore the LORD heard this, and was wroth: so a fire was kindled against Jacob, and anger also came up against Israel;

22 Because they believed not in God, and trusted not in his salvation:

23 Though he had commanded the clouds from above, and opened the doors of heaven,

24 And had rained down manna upon them to eat, and had given them of the corn of heaven.

25 Man did eat angels' food: he sent them meat to the full."

[We have discussed all of these verses in great detail in ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus, ]{.class_4937}[which see. We will be brief here. Note how verse 15 misled the commentators when they tried to handle Psalm 74:15. The constant interchanging of the "depths" of the Red Sea with the ]{.class_4938}["great deep" ]{.class_4939}[of Genesis 7:11 (see Isa. 51:10, for example) completely stupefies the commentators. They cannot see the future reality, so they stop with the TYPE as the final fulfillment---]{.class_4938}[which it is not. ]{.class_4937}[Verse 16 shows that there was no trickling of water out of one boulder when Moses went to work. Whatever he did with his rod, it had to slake the thirst of over 5,000 plus cattle. What was needed were ]{.class_4938}["rivers"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 16). That they came is beyond question, for look at 2 Kings 3:17, 20. Verses 17--19 are fully covered in ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus.]{.class_4937}

From a Christian standpoint, the "born again" child of God can:

1\. Tempt God.

2\. Ask according to his lusts.

3\. Speak against God.

4\. "Believe not" God.

5\. And even refuse to trust His salvation (vs. 22).

There are easily over 300,000,000 Christians who profess to believe every "Fundamental of the Faith" professed at Bob Jones who, at the same time, are trying to "hold out to the end" and die "in a state of grace" because they have no assurance of salvation.

Jimmy Swaggart, of motel fame, criticized Baptists for years for "preaching that damnable doctrine of eternal security," but neither he nor his fornicating buddy Jimmy Bakker lost their salvation through homosexuality, adultery, pornography, or embezzlement. (Evidently only "Baptists" can lose salvation; Charismatics are "exempt"!)

[The ]{.class_4938}["fire" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 21 is literal (see Num. 16:35 and Lev. 10:2.)]{.class_4938}

"[And had rained down manna\...the corn of heaven\...man did eat angels' food" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 24--25). Observe that it can "rain" water, hail, fire, or BREAD. The word "rain" is a verb, not a noun. We are told that the]{.class_4938}[ "angels' food"]{.class_4939}[ is "the bread of the mighty ones," but nothing further develops. A great silence descends over the faculties and staffs of Liberty University, Bob Jones, Arlington, Springfield, Dallas, Lousiville, Chicago, Denver, Los Angeles, New Orleans, Wheaton, Moody, Fuller, PCC, Santa Rosa Christian, and the revision committees of the ]{.class_4938}[ASV, NASV, RSV, NRSV, NIV, NKJV]{.class_4937}[, and the next piece of trash coming up. Everyone forgot old ]{.class_4938}[Leviathan ]{.class_4937}[(Psa. 74:14), whose wounded heads will feed someone in the wilderness. This feeding in the wilderness is mentioned FOUR times in the gospels, which is more times than the Virgin Birth, the Ascension, the prodigal son, the good Samaritan, the rich man and Lazarus, the Sermon on the Mount, the Lord's prayer, or the raising of Lazarus.]{.class_4938}

The food, here, can be food FOR angels, or food FROM angels. It probably is "from," instead of "for," but an angel certainly can eat food: they do in Genesis 18:8, and they do in Genesis 19:3. When Christ comes up from the dead He eats food (Luke 24:43). The commentators are in too big a hurry. They leave a text before they see what is in it. (It reminds one of a billboard standing near the Texas state line that says "You are now leaving Texas---WHY?")

[1. The events of verses 20--25 will all take place again (Rev. 12:14; Micah 7:14), and that is why Satan tempted Christ to produce bread in the wilderness (Luke 4:3--4). All three of the temptations were connected with Bible commands ]{.class_4938}[that ]{.class_4937}[will fulfill ]{.class_4938}[Biblical prophecies. ]{.class_4937}[All the commentators missed all of the references; ]{.class_4938}[all, without one exception.]{.class_4937}

[2. Satan is, in some way, connected with this ]{.class_4938}["manna." ]{.class_4939}[It can breed worms, and Christ denies that it is the ]{.class_4938}["true bread"]{.class_4939}[ (John 6:32). Since it is round and white, the world picked up the truth without knowing it and called their round white cake "angels' food cake."]{.class_4938}

[3. It is the ]{.class_4938}["corn of heaven" ]{.class_4939}[in distinction from the ]{.class_4938}["corn of the land," ]{.class_4939}[found in Joshua 5:11. It is connected with the NORTH because it comes from snow and hail (see Job 38:22 and Isa. 55:10). It is connected in some dark way with ]{.class_4938}[snowflakes; ]{.class_4937}[the word itself (]{.class_4938}["flakes"]{.class_4939}[)]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4939}[appears one time in the Bible; it is connected with Leviathan (Job 41:23).]{.class_4938}

[If any scientist had enough brains to fill out an income tax form, he would have spent his time (1700--1900) in the genetic laboratory experimenting with ]{.class_4938}[snow, hail, ice, ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[frost]{.class_4937}[, with the invention of bread in mind; not fooling with gold, as the old alchemists.]{.class_4938}

78:26 "He caused an east wind to blow in the heaven: and by his power he brought in the south wind.

27 He rained flesh also upon them as dust, and feathered fowls like as the sand of the sea:

28 And he let it fall in the midst of their camp, round about their habitations.

29 So they did eat, and were well filled: for he gave them their own desire;

30 They were not estranged from their lust. But while their meat was yet in their mouths,

31 The wrath of God came upon them, and slew the fattest of them, and smote down the chosen men of Israel.

32 For all this they sinned still, and believed not for his wondrous works."

[We have commented on the passage at length in ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus. ]{.class_4937}["For he gave them their own desire" ]{.class_4939}[is the old sad story of Jeremiah 6:19. The worst thing God can do to any man is to let him have his own way. That is fatal every time, for "man" is not what he thinks he is---"the measure of all things"---and his natural course is downward, usually at breakneck speed. All dispensations end in apostasy, ]{.class_4938}[including the millennial reign of Christ.]{.class_4937}[ "The New Age" movement is a joke---a genuine farce. There is nothing "new" about it; it is just atheism disguised as "progress." It is simply old, unregenerate, Adamic flesh parading as deity.]{.class_4938}

[Your ]{.class_4938}["own desire"]{.class_4939}[ (if you are a sex obsessional neurotic) is to take the reins off the flesh and let it glut itself on pornography. From this you ]{.class_4938}["reap corruption"]{.class_4939}[ (Gal. 6:8), and if God doesn't stop your ]{.class_4938}["desire"]{.class_4939}[ you wind up as a sadist, child molester, or sex murderer.]{.class_4938}

[Your ]{.class_4938}["own desire" ]{.class_4939}[(if you like to escape reality) is beer first, cocktails next, straight whiskey (or brandy, wine, or gin), and then marijuana, followed by cocaine, heroine, or crack. You wind up in a mental ward or state hospital with a destroyed mind, a bankrupt income, and an early grave.]{.class_4938}

[Your ]{.class_4938}["own desire" ]{.class_4939}[(if you are fond of running things) can lead you to a grave at thirty three years old (Alexander the Great), being beaten to death by a mob (Mussolini), killing yourself (Hitler), or even pretending to be God (any pope).]{.class_4938}

[If you ]{.class_4938}["desire"]{.class_4939}[ a modern Bible that you can understand easily, Satan will give you one: there are more than ninety on the market today.]{.class_4938}

[No man ]{.class_4938}[naturally ]{.class_4937}[desires carrying a cross; no man ]{.class_4938}[naturally ]{.class_4937}[desires giving up the world; no man ]{.class_4938}[naturally ]{.class_4937}[desires going against the current of his day and time; no Adamic man would think of putting Jesus Christ ahead of his own flesh and blood (Luke 14:26); no man enjoys ]{.class_4938}[self denial ]{.class_4937}[(Rom. 8:13); and no man likes to live entirely by faith, doing right all the time, with no visible proof that he will be rewarded. When God ]{.class_4938}["gave them their own desire," ]{.class_4939}[He gave them sickness and death with it (compare Ps. 78:30--31 with Num. 11:33).]{.class_4938}

"[For all this ]{.class_4939}[\[sickness and death\] ]{.class_4938}[they sinned still," ]{.class_4939}[just like Pharaoh did even after Israel had left (Exod. 14:4--6). Man in incurably wicked (Jer. 17:9). Christ calls His own disciples ]{.class_4938}["evil"]{.class_4939}[ (Matt. 7:11) without even investigating their lives or their moral condition.]{.class_4938}

78:33 "Therefore their days did he consume in vanity, and their years in trouble.

34 When he slew them, then they sought him: and they returned and inquired early after God.

35 And they remembered that God was their rock, and the high God their redeemer.

36 Nevertheless they did flatter him with their mouth, and they lied unto him with their tongues.

37 For their heart was not right with him, neither were they stedfast in his covenant.

38 But he, being full of compassion, forgave their iniquity, and destroyed them not: yea, many a time turned he his anger away, and did not stir up all his wrath.

39 For he remembered that they were but flesh; a wind that passeth away, and cometh not again."

[Again, we have covered the passage in ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus, ]{.class_4937}[which see. ]{.class_4938}["When he slew them, then they sought him," ]{.class_4939}[as in Numbers 21:7 and Judges 10:9--10. They remembered (vs. 35) at times, ]{.class_4938}["nevertheless they did flatter him with their mouth." ]{.class_4939}[See it in Joshua 24:24 and Ezekiel 33:30--33; lip service, as in Mark 7:6 in New Testament times. The trouble is "heart trouble" for ]{.class_4938}["their heart was not right with him." ]{.class_4939}[The issues of life come from the ]{.class_4938}[heart. ]{.class_4937}[Bob Jones Sr. said, "Give God your heart, and He will straighten out the kinks in your head." America always got pious when the casualty lists (1918, 1945) began to mount, although since "Nam" piety of any kind has disappeared. ]{.class_4938}["They lied unto him with their tongues" ]{.class_4939}[like Ananias and Sapphira (Acts 5:4). The flattery is illlustrated in Jeremiah 42:5 in no uncertain terms; Zedekiah does it even before that (Jer. 38).]{.class_4938}

"[But he, being full of compassion, forgave their iniquity." ]{.class_4939}[See remarks under Exodus 34:7 in that commentary, and note the tremendous difference in the way sinners are saved under the Law and the way they are saved under Grace. ]{.class_4938}["Did not stir up all his wrath." ]{.class_4939}[He just stirred up a little bit to the tune of several hundred thousand deaths (Heb. 3:11, 17; 1 Cor. 10:5, 8; Num. 16:49). If God had vented his full wrath on Israel (Exod. 32:9--11), Moses would have had to start all over again and beget twelve tribes to fulfill the promises given to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Fortunately, Moses and God never got in the "killin' mood" at the same time. Verse 39 is self-explanatory. Spurgeon gives us from the passage: the hypocrite's feet (vs. 34), the hypocrite's memory (vs. 35), the hypocrite's tongue (vs. 36), the hypocrite's heart (vs. 37), and the hypocrite's allegiance (vs. 37).]{.class_4938}

78:40 "How oft did they provoke him in the wilderness, and grieve him in the desert!

41 Yea, they turned back and tempted God, and limited the Holy One of Israel.

42 They remembered not his hand, nor the day when he delivered them from the enemy.

43 How he had wrought his signs in Egypt, and his wonders in the field of Zoan:

44 And had turned their rivers into blood; and their floods, that they could not drink.

45 He sent divers sorts of flies among them, which devoured them; and frogs, which destroyed them.

46 He gave also their increase unto the caterpillar, and their labour unto the locust.

47 He destroyed their vines with hail, and their sycamore trees with frost.

48 He gave up their cattle also to the hail, and their flocks to hot thunderbolts.

49 He cast upon them the fierceness of his anger, wrath, and indignation, and trouble, by sending evil angels among them."

[Again, ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus ]{.class_4937}[handles the details. The number of "grievances" (vs. 40) ]{.class_4938}[is ten. ]{.class_4937}[Five charges are laid to Israel which can often be applied to members of the "one body."]{.class_4938}

1\. They provoked God.

2\. They grieved God (Eph. 4:30).

3\. They turned back (see 2 Tim. 4:10).

4\. They tempted God (see 1 Cor. 10:9).

5\. They limited God by their lack of faith (see Num. 13:27--29 and Phil. 4:13.).

[These sins came from a root sin mentioned in verse 42, ]{.class_4938}["they remembered not his hand, nor the day when he delivered them from the enemy." ]{.class_4939}[For the Christian's part, it is this failure to fully appreciate salvation (years after the transaction has taken place) that causes the sins listed above. In the New Testament it is called (1) not giving God the glory and (2) not being ]{.class_4938}[thankful ]{.class_4937}[(Rom. 1: 21). For ]{.class_4938}[Israel, ]{.class_4937}[this meant forgetting what took place in Egypt under Moses (vss. 43--51).]{.class_4938}

[The events of verses 44--49 will take place in the Tribulation. The historical past is described in ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus.]{.class_4937}

"[By sending evil angels among them," ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 49). Every plague that came was a "message" from God to Pharaoh, and one of the meanings of the word ]{.class_4938}["angel" ]{.class_4939}[is "messenger" (see Mal. 3:1). Angels are ]{.class_4938}[spirits ]{.class_4937}[(Heb. 1:14), and a ]{.class_4938}[spirit ]{.class_4937}[(1 Kings 22:21) comes to Ahab and deceives him (1 Kings 22:22). These "spirits" are angels, according to Job 4:15--17. In the Old Testament they appear to people (Judg. 6:11--15, Num. 22:31--35). You will note that angels are connected with the drying up of the Euphrates in Revelation 9:14 and with the plagues that hit nature (see Rev. 7:1). The plagues in Egypt are carried out by destroying angels who respond to Moses' uplifted rod. Abaddon, the Death Angel (called ]{.class_4938}["the destroyer" ]{.class_4939}[in Exodus 12:23), completes the ]{.class_4938}["anger, wrath, and indignation and trouble" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 49. Undoubtedly, he was assisted by ]{.class_4938}["evil angels."]{.class_4939}

78:50 "He made a way to his anger; he spared not their soul from death, but gave their life over to the pestilence;

51 And smote all the firstborn in Egypt; the chief of their strength in the tabernacles of Ham:

52 But made his own people to go forth like sheep, and guided them in the wilderness like a flock.

53 And he led them on safely, so that they feared not: but the sea overwhelmed their enemies."

PSALM 79

79:1 "O GOD, the heathen are come into thine inheritance; thy holy temple have they defiled; they have laid Jerusalem on heaps.

2 The dead bodies of thy servants have they given to be meat unto the fowls of the heaven, the flesh of thy saints unto the beasts of the earth.

3 Their blood have they shed like water round about Jerusalem; and there was none to bury them.

4 We are become a reproach to our neighbours, a scorn and derision to them that are round about us.

5 How long, LORD? wilt thou be angry for ever? shall thy jealousy burn like fire?

6 Pour out thy wrath upon the heathen that have not known thee, and upon the kingdoms that have not called upon thy name.

7 For they have devoured Jacob, and laid waste his dwelling place."

It would appear that the theme of this third Book of Psalms is deliverance from the United Nations. [Israel does not ]{.class_4938}["shew forth thy praise to all generations" ]{.class_4939}[after Christ's blood atonement; not in any sense of the word, by the wildest flight of the most vivid imagination. The reading of the ]{.class_4938}[New King Jimmy Version ]{.class_4937}[is a BLASPHEMY to the work and words of the Author of Scripture. No wonder Kroll can make no application. But as far as that goes, Baethgen bombs out with Briggs and Hupfeld; Davidson bombs out with Ewald and Hengstenberg; and they all wind up in the "landfill" with Dummelow, Motyer, Yates, Duhm, Hitzig, and DeWette. Nothing is more tragic and ridiculous than correctors of the ]{.class_4938}[King James Bible ]{.class_4937}[trying to "exposit" the "scriptures."]{.class_4938}

[Verse 13 is fulfilled in Ezekiel 34:11--28 to the letter and is mentioned so many times in the Old Testament (Deut. 32:43; Psa. 126:2; Isa. 14:7, 35:2, 44:23, 49:13, 54:1, 55:12; Zeph. 3:17, etc.) that only the faculties and staffs at Liberty University, Bob Jones University, Tennessee Temple, BBC, Wheaton, Fuller, Moody, Spurgeon's Theological College, Grace Theological Seminary, Dallas, New Orleans, and Louisville could miss it. They missed it. ]{.class_4938}["Blessed are those who have nothing to say, and cannot be persuaded to say it."]{.class_4937}

[There wasn't ]{.class_4938}[one ]{.class_4937}[difficult verse in the entire Psalm, but since the entire Psalm had a doctrinal application to the future, the Scholar's Union just busted their fool necks on it. We should not be surprised, for after all, none of them ever professed to have read "the Scriptures." The Scriptures are ]{.class_4938}["given by inspiration of God," ]{.class_4939}[and since the first and main purpose for these "inspired Scriptures" was to teach DOCTRINE (2 Tim. 3:16), it is no wonder that the Alexandrian Cult misses the doctrinal references almost every time they show up.]{.class_4938}

PSALM 80

80:1 "Give ear, O Shepherd of Israel, thou that leadest Joseph like a flock; thou that dwellest between the cherubims, shine forth.

2 Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh stir up thy strength, and come and save us.

3 Turn us again, O God, and cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.

4 O LORD God of hosts, how long wilt thou be angry against the prayer of thy people?

5 Thou feedest them with the bread of tears; and givest them tears to drink in great measure.

6 Thou makest us a strife unto our neighbours: and our enemies laugh among themselves."

[Here we go again! The theme is identical to the previous Psalm. This one is ]{.class_4938}["upon Shoshannim Eduth," ]{.class_4937}[which roughly means "the hexachord of testimony, "according to Schleusner. (According to most expositors, it means "the testimony of the lilies.") Psalms 45, 60, and 69 contain this title. Bullinger puts it on the END of the previous Psalm (Ps. 79).]{.class_4938}

This time we will dispense completely with the irreverent bungling of Motyer, Kroll, Yates, Briggs, Davidson, Hengstenberg, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Ewald, Hupfeld, Lange, Ellicott, Clarke, and Dummelow. Paper is expensive these days.

"[Shine forth" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 1) is a prayer for the Tribulation appearance that we have found before (see Psa. 31:16, 67:1; Isa. 60:1; Dan. 9:17, etc.). ]{.class_4938}["O Shepherd of Israel" ]{.class_4939}[is a call for the Shepherd of Isaiah 40:11 and 63:11 who led the flock out of Egypt. He is being asked to do it again, ]{.class_4938}[and He will ]{.class_4937}[(Heb. 4:1--10). ]{.class_4938}["Joseph\...Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh" ]{.class_4939}[points out the sons of Jacob who went down into Egypt first and did not leave until his bones were carried out, around 1500 B.C. His two sons and his younger brother are mentioned in the passage. ]{.class_4938}["Come and save us. Turn us again, O God, and cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved" ]{.class_4939}[will be fulfilled at the Second Advent (Rom. 11:26), and not one time between 500 B.C. and A.D. 2002. Note how Psalm 80:4 matches Psalm 79:5. Again, note how Psalm 80:6 matches Psalm 79:4. If the Scholar's Union missed Psalm 79, you can give up hope that they can read Psalm 80. It is the same theme.]{.class_4938}

[Joseph's sons and his brother are mentioned in connection with the Ark (vs. 1) because of Numbers 2:17--24, where they are pictured as "setting forward" as soon as the Levites take the tabernacle forward; this would include the Ark. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown unwittingly say that this prayer (vs. 4) has to be for "Joseph" because the ten northern tribes have gone into captivity! In ]{.class_4938}[Asaph's ]{.class_4937}[day? Asaph's sons are listed in 1 Chronicles 25:2, and the ]{.class_4938}["son of Asaph" ]{.class_4939}[in Hezekiah's time (2 Kings 18:18) is NOT a first or second generation ]{.class_4938}["son."]{.class_4939}[ Asaph is a ]{.class_4938}["seer"]{.class_4939}[ (2 Chron. 29:30), and he prophesies the dispersion of the northern tribes ]{.class_4938}[before ]{.class_4937}[they go into captivity. However, the ]{.class_4938}["us"]{.class_4939}[ that shows up in verse 3, and continues throughout verses 6, 7, and 19 (and ]{.class_4938}["we"]{.class_4939}[ in vss. 3, 14, and 18), is Israel: all twelve tribes. Joseph is sorted out first because of his association with Egypt, and the fact that his sons are the first to go into captivity (see Amos 5:6, 5:15, 6:6, and Obad. 18).]{.class_4938}

[Doctrinally ]{.class_4937}[speaking, God can be angry at a prayer request (vs. 4). See Isaiah 1:15 and Proverbs 15:8 and 28:9. A prayer for "world peace" is about as rotten and as stinking a request as any fool could make. There are three world wars on deck, which God Himself has ordained to take place; so every pope since 1800 who prayed for "peace" was an enemy of God and an enemy of the Scripture. His "prayer life" wasn't worth fifteen cents.]{.class_4938}

"[The bread of tears" ]{.class_4939}[is ]{.class_4938}[unleavened bread, ]{.class_4937}[according to Deuteronomy 16:3. It was given to "jail birds" in the "slammer" (1 Kings 22:27). Isaiah 30:20 adds ]{.class_4938}["the water of affliction," ]{.class_4939}[which would be, again, for a prisoner. When the Psalmist says ]{.class_4938}["the bread of tears," ]{.class_4939}[he is referring to prisoners (see Ps. 79:11). The Bible speaks of these two "breads," plus ]{.class_4938}["the bread of heaven," "the bread of life," ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["bread of deceit."]{.class_4939}[ Job 6:7 is a good illustration of the text, for he is the greatest type of a Jew in the Tribulation.]{.class_4938}

80:7 "Turn us again, O God of hosts, and cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.

8 Thou hast brought a vine out of Egypt: thou hast cast out the heathen, and planted it.

9 Thou preparedst room before it, and didst cause it to take deep root, and it filled the land.

10 The hills were covered with the shadow of it, and the boughs thereof were like the goodly cedars.

11 She sent out her boughs unto the sea, and her branches unto the river.

12 Why hast thou then broken down her hedges, so that all they which pass by the way do pluck her?

13 The boar out of the wood doth waste it, and the wild beast of the field doth devour it."

"[Turn us again" ]{.class_4939}[matches verse 3. It is repeated a third time in verse 19. No one could miss the import except "you know who." Verses 8--13 liken Israel to a vine that God planted and took care of. The cross references are numerous in both Testaments. In the New Testament, notice in particular Matthew 20 and 21:33, and in the Old Testament, notice in particular Isaiah 5; Jeremiah 2; and Ezekiel 15 and 17. Joseph's destiny was to spread out as a ]{.class_4938}["fruitful bough" ]{.class_4939}[(Gen. 49:22), so ]{.class_4938}["the hills were covered with the shadow of it, and the boughs thereof were like the goodly cedars. She sent out her boughs unto the sea, and her branches unto the river" ]{.class_4939}[(i.e., to the Mediterranean and the Euphrates). The idea is clear. Israel received special privileges from God to insure they would prosper. First He stumped, blasted, and rooted the ground to be planted (vs. 8); then He cleared the land and planted the roots deep (vs. 9). It was so well fertilized and well pruned (vs. 10) that it probably took a staff to carry ONE CLUSTER of its grapes (see Num. 13:23).]{.class_4938}

"[Broken down her hedges\...pluck her\...waste it\...devour it." ]{.class_4939}[The question of verse 12 was answered in Deuteronomy 28:15 and Leviticus 26:14--43 many long years before Asaph asked the question in David's day or Israel asks the question in 2002 plus. Note the ]{.class_4938}["wild beast" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 13, which ALL the commentators miss. This ]{.class_4938}["wild beast" ]{.class_4939}[is defined in Hosea 13:8 as the Son of Perdition---the ]{.class_4938}["beast"]{.class_4939}[ of Revelation 13.]{.class_4938}

[1. ]{.class_4938}["Like a lion" ]{.class_4939}[(Hosea 13:8).]{.class_4938}

[2. ]{.class_4938}["As a bear" ]{.class_4939}[(Hosea 13:8).]{.class_4938}

[3. ]{.class_4938}["As a leopard" ]{.class_4939}[(Hosea 13:7).]{.class_4938}

[4. ]{.class_4938}["The wild beast" ]{.class_4939}[(Hosea 13:8).]{.class_4938}

[Note that the beasts match Daniel 7 to the "T." All three appear as one composite ]{.class_4938}["behemoth" ]{.class_4939}[in Revelation 13:1--2.]{.class_4938}

[You don't have to be told what the Alexandrian apostates did with this---the ones who believed in "the plenary, verbal inspiration of the original autographs." Their spiritual depth and grasp of the Scriptures is so wretched and defunct that only living on an expensive campus could con a young man into thinking they were even qualified to ]{.class_4938}[breathe. ]{.class_4937}[(A man said his wife's cooking was so bad that pygmies came from Africa to dip their arrows in it. That pretty well describes the scholarship of the revisors, translators, schools, faculties, and scholars connected with the last thirty English revisions and the last ten "World Congresses of Fundamentalists.") An exposition of the text, ]{.class_4938}[historically, ]{.class_4937}[will be found in Isaiah 56:9, 13:9; Hosea 2:12; and Deuteronomy 28:14--26.]{.class_4938}

80:14 "Return, we beseech thee, O God of hosts: look down from heaven, and behold, and visit this vine;

15 And the vineyard which thy right hand hath planted, and the branch that thou madest strong for thyself.

16 It is burned with fire, it is cut down: they perish at the rebuke of thy countenance.

17 Let thy hand be upon the man of thy right hand, upon the son of man whom thou madest strong for thyself.

18 So will not we go back from thee: quicken us, and we will call upon thy name.

19 Turn us again, O LORD God of hosts, cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved."

"[Return\...look down\...visit this vine\...quicken us\...turn us again" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 14--19). All will come to pass as prayed. Kroll, being Premillennial, can pick it up. Spurgeon's ]{.class_4938}[Treasury ]{.class_4937}[can't do anything with it. This time Kroll is able to locate God's ]{.class_4938}["right hand" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 17), but only because God in His mercy stooped for Kroll's benefit to write ]{.class_4938}["THE SON OF MAN"]{.class_4939}[ in the text. Not even a "rambling wreck from Georgia Tech," or a "total loss from Holy Cross" (or a "Champion for Christ" at LU), could misread the passage. But notice that Kroll missed the identification on eight previous occassions (EIGHT of them! Psa. 17:7, 18:35, 20:6, 21:8, 45:4, 48:10, and 60:5).]{.class_4938}

[Inspirationally, ]{.class_4937}[a man is saved (vs. 19) when he calls upon the name of the Lord (vs. 18). ]{.class_4938}[Inspirationally, ]{.class_4937}[when Christ shows up (vs. 17), a man can be confirmed in salvation, provided God "quickens" him (vs. 18). But the Psalm is aimed at a Tribulation Jew, exactly where Hebrews 3, 6, and 10 are aimed.]{.class_4938}

PSALM 81

81:1 "Sing aloud unto God our strength: make a joyful noise unto the God of Jacob.

2 Take a psalm, and bring hither the timbrel, the pleasant harp with the psaltery.

3 Blow up the trumpet in the new moon, in the time appointed, on our solemn feast day.

4 For this was a statute for Israel, and a law of the God of Jacob.

5 This he ordained in Joseph for a testimony, when he went out through the land of Egypt: where I heard a language that I understood not.

6 I removed his shoulder from the burden: his hands were delivered from the pots.

7 Thou calledst in trouble, and I delivered thee; I answered thee in the secret place of thunder: I proved thee at the waters of Meribah. Selah."

[The first seven verses deal with Israel praising God in its feasts for deliverance from Egypt. The speaker switches from the third person to the first person in the most distracting fashion (vs. 5), where God Himself becomes the speaker in verses 6 and 7. The Psalmist speaks for Israel as ]{.class_4938}["I"]{.class_4939}[ in verse 5 (]{.class_4938}["I heard a language that I understood not"]{.class_4939}[), but immediately places Israel into the second person (]{.class_4938}["Thou calledst in trouble"]{.class_4939}[), and then speaks for God as ]{.class_4938}["I"]{.class_4939}[ (]{.class_4938}["I delivered thee; I answered thee"]{.class_4939}[). The only other way out is to claim that the ]{.class_4938}["I" ]{.class_4939}[of ]{.class_4938}["I heard a language that I understood not" ]{.class_4939}[matches Hosea 8:4 and Amos 3:2. It is possible for God to speak of not knowing something that happens right in front of His face. Observe: ]{.class_4938}["I never knew you: depart from me" ]{.class_4939}[(Matt. 7:23). Obviously God knows every man's birth, life, death, thoughts, background, feelings, words, ideas, imaginations, motives, works, and beliefs; but still: ]{.class_4938}["I never knew you." ]{.class_4939}[Thus ]{.class_4938}["I heard a language that I understood not" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 5). However, the former meaning is probably correct; very often a prophet will switch persons.]{.class_4938}

Verses 1 and 2 are self explanatory.

[The ]{.class_4938}["solemn feast day" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 3) is the date of the Second Advent. This date is the Feast of Tabernacles (see 2 Chron. 7:9; Neh. 8:18; Hosea 9:5, 12:9; Lev. 23:34; Deut. 16:13; 31:10; 2 Chron. 8:13; and Ezra 3:4). This is THE outstanding date on the calendar of history from 4000 B.C. to A.D. 2000, for it dates BOTH ADVENTS and is commemorated once a year by the sun itself, ]{.class_4938}[which is four days off center from the earth's orbit; ]{.class_4937}[these days are September 20, 21, 22, and 23. The ]{.class_4938}["statute" ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["law" ]{.class_4939}[that was ]{.class_4938}["ordained" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 4--5) was to confirm the appearance of Jesus Christ after the Marriage of the Lamb (see comments under Ps. 19:4--5). All the commentators\....etc., etc.]{.class_4938}

[Here, Joseph and Jacob are both identified as ]{.class_4938}["Israel" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 4--5).]{.class_4938}

Verse 6 is self explanatory (see Ps. 68:13).

[Verse 7 was an answer to Psalm 50:15. ]{.class_4938}["The secret place of thunder" ]{.class_4939}[would be Mt. Sinai (see Exod. 19:16 and 20:18), although the thing will be repeated in the Tribulation (1 Sam. 2:10). You say, "Where do you get THAT from?" That's easy---]{.class_4938}["Selah"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 7), right in front of the noses of the Scholar's Union. You know what they did with it, and you don't have to be told. Revelation 10:3 and 16:18 are not in the Book to be ignored.]{.class_4938}

81:8 "Hear, O my people, and I will testify unto thee: O Israel, if thou wilt hearken unto me;

9 There shall no strange god be in thee; neither shalt thou worship any strange god.

10 I am the LORD thy God, which brought thee out of the land of Egypt: open thy mouth wide, and I will fill it.

11 But my people would not hearken to my voice; and Israel would none of me.

12 So I gave them up unto their own hearts' lust: and they walked in their own counsels.

13 Oh that my people had hearkened unto me, and Israel had walked in my ways!

14 I should soon have subdued their enemies, and turned my hand against their adversaries.

15 The haters of the LORD should have submitted themselves unto him: but their time should have endured for ever.

16 He should have fed them also with the finest of the wheat: and with honey out of the rock should I have satisfied thee."

[The rebuke that God gives Israel, here, was prefaced by verse 7, which said, ]{.class_4938}["I proved thee at the waters of Meribah." ]{.class_4939}[This was Israel's sin (see Exod. 17:3--7), and it is described in much detail in our ]{.class_4938}[Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus. ]{.class_4937}["Hear, O my people"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 8), as in 78:1. Nothing that follows is difficult. It was discussed in ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus, ]{.class_4937}[which see. God will fill their mouths (vs. 10) as a mother eagle will fill the mouth of an offspring, for He brought them out ]{.class_4938}["on eagles' wings"]{.class_4939}[ (Exod. 19:4). Verse 12 is clear from Numbers 11:18--21. Observe the apparent contradiction between verse 16 and Deuteronomy 32:13. One verse said that God did feed them with those items, and the other said He would have if they had obeyed Him. Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, and Kroll find the reference in Deuteronomy, but not knowing what on earth either passage is about, they pretend that there isn't any problem.]{.class_4938}

[Now God testifies ]{.class_4938}[against ]{.class_4937}[His people. His complaint is the same complaint voiced by Joshua when Israel entered the Promised Land (Josh. 24:14, 19). It is the same complaint and warning that God gave them before they left the wilderness. Turning a deaf ear to this warning is the thing that caused both Israel (2 Kings 18) and Judah (Jer. 39--40) to go into captivity: ]{.class_4938}["there shall no strange god be in thee" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 9). God ]{.class_4938}["gave them up" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 12) like He gave up the Gentiles in Romans 1:24--25. Stephen describes this very thing in Acts 7:42.]{.class_4938}

[The ]{.class_4938}["they" ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["their" ]{.class_4939}[in the passage (vss. 12, 14) is Israel, but the ]{.class_4938}["them" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 16 is in contrast with the ]{.class_4938}["thee"]{.class_4939}[ of verse 16. This produces a strange thing among the commentators. ]{.class_4938}["Their time" ]{.class_4939}[is given to God's enemies in the ]{.class_4938}[NIV ]{.class_4937}[and the ]{.class_4938}[RSV, ]{.class_4937}[but the same expression is attributed to Israel by Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, and Spurgeon. (Kroll wisely keeps his mouth shut so no one will spot his ignorance: Prov. 17:28). The ]{.class_4938}[ASV ]{.class_4937}[leaves the text as it stands in the ]{.class_4938}[AV]{.class_4937}[, but the ]{.class_4938}[NKJV ]{.class_4937}[(Curtis Hutson, Harold Okenga, James Price, F. F. Bruce, et al.) goes along with the ]{.class_4938}[RSV ]{.class_4937}[of the National Council of Churches and writes "their fate," without identifying WHOSE fate. ]{.class_4938}[The Living Bible ]{.class_4937}[says it is the enemy's fate, and so do the ]{.class_4938}[RSV, NRSV, ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[NIV]{.class_4937}[. But they could have changed it. They would have ]{.class_4938}["submitted themselves unto" ]{.class_4939}[the Lord. If they had done this, then they would have fed on what Israel fed on in Deuteronomy 32:13. The enemies would not have just been ]{.class_4938}["subdued" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 14), they would have ]{.class_4938}["submitted" ]{.class_4939}[themselves to God and Israel, so they would have shared in Israel's physical and material blessings. Note the Holy Spirit's comments on this in Romans 11:12, which all the\...you know by now!]{.class_4938}

"[He should have fed them"]{.class_4939}[---the]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4939}[enemies that God subdued and came into submission---with the things that He fed Israel (see vs. 10). ]{.class_4938}["He should have fed them also" ]{.class_4939}[clinches the case.]{.class_4938}

Of course, we can find all kinds of spiritual truths in the Psalm.

[1. We got saved when we were]{.class_4938}[ "in trouble," ]{.class_4939}[and at that time we called upon the name of the Lord (vs. 7).]{.class_4938}

2\. Testings followed our salvation (vs. 7).

[3. From that point on, we were to listen to God, not to man (vs. 8), and since ]{.class_4938}["covetousness\...is idolatry" ]{.class_4939}[(Col. 3:5), we were to have no "strange gods" (vs. 9).]{.class_4938}

4\. God will provide FOOD (vs. 10 with 1 Tim. 6:8).

5\. God will fight for us if we yield to Him (vss. 13--14).

6\. Conversions will follow our obedience (vs. 15).

[7. And the converted enemies of God (see Rom. 5:10) would enjoy ]{.class_4938}["honey out of the rock" ]{.class_4939}[(see 1 Pet. 2:3).]{.class_4938}

Spiritual truths are a "dime a dozen" in the Psaltery.

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ823.xhtml}

PSALM 82

82:1 "GOD standeth in the congregation of the mighty; he judgeth among the gods.

2 How long will ye judge unjustly, and accept the persons of the wicked? Selah.

3 Defend the poor and fatherless: do justice to the afflicted and needy.

4 Deliver the poor and needy: rid them out of the hand of the wicked.

5 They know not, neither will they understand; they walk on in darkness: all the foundations of the earth are out of course.

6 1 have said, Ye are gods; and all of you are children of the most High.

7 But ye shall die like men, and fall like one of the princes.

8 Arise, O God, judge the earth: for thou shall inherit all nations."

[But here, the spiritual truths give way to Biblical doctrines that are hard to hear, so those ]{.class_4938}["dull of hearing" ]{.class_4939}[(Heb. 5:11) are now about to "bomb out" right and left. One will abort, one will miscarry, one will throw in the sponge, and another will move through the Psalm like a drugged sleep walker. All it takes to throw the Scholar's Union is a passage that deals with the SUPERNATURAL (Leviathan, water in the universe, Behemoth, dragons, unicorns, manna, "gods," etc.), and suddenly all of them---including the "born again, militant Fundamentalists"---go weak in the knees, dim in the eyes, deaf in the ears, and blind in the heart. We are now at such a passage.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 1. ]{.class_4938}["The congregation of the mighty" ]{.class_4939}[is not any congregation of earthly judges, and when ]{.class_4938}["he judgeth among the gods," ]{.class_4939}[there is no reference to Israelite judges, any of Aaron's seed, or anything else. Look at 1 Kings 22:19 for the Holy Spirit's description of the ]{.class_4938}["congregation of the mighty."]{.class_4939}[ Verse 6 is what destroyed the spiritual sensibilities of Kroll, Duhm, Motyer, Clarke, Lange, Dummelow, Yates, Baethgen, Hengstenberg, and Charles Haddon Spurgeon. You will note that God often makes Charlie pay for his sin of leaning on the corrupt ]{.class_4938}[RV]{.class_4937}[, at times, and occasionally aping Westcott and Hort by talking about "better renderings" and "better translations." There is a price to pay for strutting like a peacock before the Holy Spirit. No man states the truth concerning the power and authority of the ]{.class_4938}[King James Bible ]{.class_4937}[any better than Spurgeon ]{.class_4938}[when he is in his right mind ]{.class_4937}[(see the ]{.class_4938}[Bible Believers' Bulletin, ]{.class_4937}[September 1990), but alas, ]{.class_4938}["all flesh is grass" ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["all have sinned," ]{.class_4939}[etc., so when Charlie tries to impress the stupid apostates of his day---who taught the faculties and staffs of every major Christian college and seminary everything they know---God puts him down as a candidate for blindness at a later date. This is one of those dates.]{.class_4938}

"[Selah" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 2). ]{.class_4938}[That ]{.class_4937}[is the date line. It quickly eliminates every Nicolaitan who was trying to "bring out the intent of the original author" by "translating the Hebrew text in up to date language, so it might communicate to the receptor," etc. (Interpretation: "He's got a job that takes a lot of guts: he strings tennis rackets.")]{.class_4938}

"[Arise, O God"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 8). There it goes again, in case you missed the ]{.class_4938}["Selah."]{.class_4939}[ But at Liberty University they can't find EITHER expression in ANY version. Kroll (absolutely spaced out) says that ]{.class_4938}["Arise, O God"]{.class_4939}[ is just a "metaphor taken from the common gesture of judges who sit as they hear a case and rise up to pronounce a sentence."]{.class_4938}

[No, that is exactly what the expression didn't mean one time ]{.class_4938}[anywhere ]{.class_4937}[in the entire Bible. See Psalm 3:7, 7:6, 9:19, 10:12, 12:5, 17:13, 44:23, 26, 68:1, and 74:22. It wasn't a "metaphor" ]{.class_4938}[one time out of ten. ]{.class_4937}[Trust Lynchburg to steal your money as well as your Bible.]{.class_4938}

"[For thou shalt inherit all nations" ]{.class_4939}[gave you the third clue as to the time, and Kroll still missed it. He had to apply the ]{.class_4938}[second half ]{.class_4937}[of verse 8 to the Second Advent because he was Premillennial, but being carnal (as well as stupid), he had to limit the first half of the verse to human judges before the Church Age. He, as everyone of his peers, ignored the fact that the "gods" will be here in the Tribulation, and they will be reigning not only as kings but as absolute authorities over all judicial matters on earth (Rev. 17:12). They will be in the same position as in Genesis 6:1--6. (Note the word ]{.class_4938}["mighty," ]{.class_4939}[as in Ps. 82:1.)]{.class_4938}

[When Christ applied the verse to human judges to prove His own deity (John 10:34--36), He knocked every translator, every reviser, every commentator, and every "exegete" slap out of the rink. At no time did God ever say "all of you ]{.class_4938}[Israelite judges ]{.class_4937}[are children of the most High." To tell the truth, none of them were; ]{.class_4938}[not even the saved ones. ]{.class_4937}[The judges in Israel were Levites (Mal. 2:4, 7--8; Deut. 1:16, 21:5), and not one of them was a "child of God." When John uses the expression (John 11:52), he uses it in a Jewish sense that was not doctrinal. Jesus Christ Himself allows that the Jews of that time were not only NOT the "children of God" (John 8:42--44), ]{.class_4938}[they were not even the children of Abraham ]{.class_4937}[(John 8:39).]{.class_4938}

[That isn't all: ]{.class_4938}["all the foundations of the earth" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 5) were NOT ]{.class_4938}["out of course" ]{.class_4939}[at anytime between Asaph and Paul, and they are not now, nor were they when Moses appointed human judges over Israel (Exod. 18:22, 25; Num. 11:16--17).]{.class_4938}

Someone is "off their trolley" again; they have "one oar in the water."

[The problem came from Exodus 22:28. With this and John 10:34, the Bible-correcting apostates took the liberty to throw out the entire context of Psalm 82, since they couldn't understand it. The context (vss. 1, 5--6, 8) was the Second Advent, and the type was ]{.class_4938}["the days of Noah," ]{.class_4939}[for there the "gods" drowned like]{.class_4938}[ "men"]{.class_4939}[ (see vs. 7), at least according to 2 Peter 3:6; Jude 6; Job 22:16; and Revelation 20:13 in the English text.]{.class_4938}

There is nothing like Elizabethan English to make a debacle out of a seminary; especially if it is a bunch of book mad stuffed shirts who think the sun rises and sets on "plenary, verbally inspired, original autographs." (A duck is a bird that walks like he has been riding a horse all day.)

[Dual application:]{.class_4937}[ The judges in Israel could be ]{.class_4938}["gods"]{.class_4939}[ in the sense that Moses was a ]{.class_4938}["god"]{.class_4939}[ to Pharaoh and Aaron (Exod. 7:1). You can be a "son" of God because you represent THE Son of God. (The Pope is not shy in the least; he simply applies God's titles to himself, John 17:11.) The human judges could judge ]{.class_4938}["the poor and fatherless\...the afflicted and needy\...the poor and needy,"]{.class_4939}[ so they acted as gods for these people. Final Authority. Get it? You got it yet? Christ merely quotes the passage ]{.class_4938}[to show that they have no right to accuse Him of claiming to be something that He is not. ]{.class_4937}[He doesn't tell you ]{.class_4938}[one thing ]{.class_4937}[about the Psalm or what the Psalm was dealing with. So the Nicolaitans---who have been correcting the Psalms now for ten to three hundred years---can't find out what is going on. When Christ says that it is written in the Law ]{.class_4938}["I said, ye are gods," ]{.class_4939}[the]{.class_4938}[ "ye"]{.class_4939}[ doesn't have anything to do with ]{.class_4938}[anyone ]{.class_4937}[to whom He is talking who got ]{.class_4938}["the word of God."]{.class_4939}[ The ]{.class_4938}["ye"]{.class_4939}[ was a reference to angels from Genesis 6 who will show up in the Tribulation.]{.class_4938}

[Observe: ]{.class_4938}["ye are gods\...but ye shall die like men" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 6--7). You do not say: "I have said ye are ]{.class_4938}[cats, ]{.class_4937}[but ye shall die like cats." You could say: "I have said ye are dogs, but ye will drown like ]{.class_4938}[fishes." Two ]{.class_4937}[things that aren't the same cannot be equated, so the scholars will have to "fix up" the ]{.class_4938}["men"]{.class_4939}[ so they don't stand in opposition to the ]{.class_4938}["gods."]{.class_4939}[ (That is what Scofield did with Genesis 6:2, so the ]{.class_4938}["daughters of men" ]{.class_4939}[could simply be messing around with the "sons of men." The text said ]{.class_4938}["sons of God."]{.class_4939}[)]{.class_4938}

[This is fourth-grade English. The disjunctive conjunction (]{.class_4938}["but"]{.class_4939}[ vs. 7) shows us that we are not dealing with ]{.class_4938}[men; ]{.class_4937}[there are no human judges in verses 6--7. The contrast is between someone who might truly be a "god" but he will die ]{.class_4938}[like ]{.class_4937}[a man---not a "god." Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown go to bat for every Bible-correcting Nicolaitan at Liberty University and say that what the Holy Spirit meant to say in verse 7 was "not like any ordinary man." This solves the problem for the Bible-rejecting Fundamentalist who has no grasp of the Scriptures. "I have said you are above ordinary men, but you will die like ]{.class_4938}[ordinary men "; ]{.class_4937}[i.e., ]{.class_4938}["I will tell you what the Scriptures should have said ]{.class_4937}[because as they are written it is impossible for ]{.class_4938}[me ]{.class_4937}[to understand them." You see where this type of reasoning winds up? It winds up with "YOU need a different translation because the Holy Bible is impossible for YOU to understand. After all, if I, with my twenty five years of formal education, can't understand it, how could YOU possibly understand it? Here! Buy this Nutty Imbecile's Version (]{.class_4938}[NIV]{.class_4937}[). Cash, check, or money order!" See how it's done?]{.class_4938}

[The judges in Genesis 6 corrupted the entire earth with their decisions. They made decisions worse than the ones made by the "nine old men" since 1933. These supernatural ]{.class_4938}["gods" ]{.class_4939}[are identified as ]{.class_4938}["gods"]{.class_4939}[ in the following places: Genesis 3:5; Exodus 15:11; Psalm 86:8, 95:3, 136:2 (Note THAT one! lmgine thinking those ]{.class_4938}["gods"]{.class_4939}[ are Israelite judges!), and Jeremiah 10:11. They are called ]{.class_4938}["the sons of God"]{.class_4939}[ in Job 38:7 and Genesis 6:2, so the only way Scofield could remove ]{.class_4938}["children of the most High" ]{.class_4939}[from Psalm 82:6 was to pretend that the]{.class_4938}[ "gods"]{.class_4939}[ were NOT literally ]{.class_4938}["the sons of God." ]{.class_4939}[They were the "sons of Seth"!]{.class_4938}

Had enough of "higher scholarship"? Is this enough from the "recognized scholars" whose "loyalty" to lost pieces of paper is "unquestioned"? Gotta belly full yet, or are you like the little boy who was told "If you eat one more bite, you are going to bust wide open"? He replied, "Please pass the cake and everybody stand back. "

[The ]{.class_4938}["gods"]{.class_4939}[ have been here before and ]{.class_4938}[will be here again. ]{.class_4937}[Idols are the mementos that man has used since the flood to commemorate their presence (see Isa. 44:10; Ps. 96:5, 97:7). They came down ]{.class_4938}["in the likeness of men" ]{.class_4939}[according to the New Testament (Acts 14:11), so every angel in the Bible appears as a ]{.class_4938}[young man ]{.class_4937}[(Judg. 13:6; Gen. 19:5; Acts 1:10; Luke 24:4, etc.). Scofield got rid of them again by saying "angels are ]{.class_4938}[sexless."]{.class_4937}[ But when he says ]{.class_4938}["ye shall die like men," ]{.class_4939}[he meant they would drown in the flood (2 Pet. 2:4--5; Job 22:15--17), and so they did. At the time they were judging, ]{.class_4938}[Enoch was prophesying the Second Coming of Jesus Christ ]{.class_4937}[(see Jude 14).]{.class_4938}

There is a chance that this Psalm may be a pre Deluge Psalm written by Enoch (see remarks under Ps. 90).

[All the commentators "blew it." It is evident that once a man begins to mess with the ]{.class_4938}[King James ]{.class_4937}[text that the Author of Scripture begins to mess with his mind, and pretty soon he cannot get the simplest doctrinal and prophetic truths in order. This statement is proved the moment some blockhead on the faculty at Bob Jones, PCC, Santa Rosa, BBC, or Tennessee Temple tries to cover up his infidelity. We will cite an A 1 example, honed to perfection at Liberty University. Having been told that ]{.class_4938}["all the foundations of the earth are out of course" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 5)---which matches Isaiah 24:19--20---Kroll, of Liberty University, alters them to "the fabric of the nation." (So help me, Delitzsch and Keil, that is what Lynchburg did with "the Hebrew text.")]{.class_4938}

"[Yet in the highly poetic language of the Psalm\...when the fundamental basis of society, the very principles of morality, are not followed by the judges, the very fabric of the nation is shaken."]{.class_4938}

Go sit on a tack.

That is modern, "militant Fundamentalist" scholarship in 1992. It is a combination of stupidity, unbelief, arrogance, blindness, and equivocation wrapped up in one neat ball of piety and propagated by men who "don't like Brother Ruckman's language." (I hope to God they don't, and I hope to God they never will.)

PSALM 83

83:1 "Keep not thou silence, O God: hold not thy peace, and be not still, O God.

2 For, lo, thine enemies make a tumult: and they that hate thee have lifted up the head.

3 They have taken crafty counsel against thy people, and consulted against thy hidden ones.

4 They have said, Come, and let us cut them off from being a nation; that the name of Israel may be no more in remembrance.

5 For they have consulted together with one consent: they are confederate against thee:

6 The tabernacles of Edom, and the Ishmaelites; of Moab, and the Hagarenes;

7 Gebal, and Ammon, and Amalek; the Philistines with the inhabitants of Tyre;

8 Assur also is joined with them: they have holpen the children of Lot. Selah."

[The entire Psalm is Daniel's Seventieth Week. The ]{.class_4938}["hidden ones" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 3 are hidden in Sela, Petra, and their enemies are listed (vss. 6--8). The details of the destruction of enemies is given in verses 9--17, and the words ]{.class_4938}["as" ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["like" ]{.class_4939}[in verses 9 and 11 tell us that Gideon's battle is a type of Armageddon which we could have gathered from Isaiah 9:4--6. You know what "all the commentators" are going to do. They are absolutely predictable. Their messed up minds cannot understand the ]{.class_4938}["things of the Spirit" ]{.class_4939}[(1 Cor. 2:14) any more than an unsaved man, once that Book corrects or insults their scholarship.]{.class_4938}

Verse 1. This is the prayer of the remnant during the last three and one half years of Daniel's Seventieth Week.

Verse 2. These are the enemies of Psalm 2.

Verse 3 shows us that they are ecumenical (UN). They have decided to finish the job that the Roman Catholics started in 1933 (Hitler, Heydrich, Goebbels, Hoess, Stangl, et al.).

[Verse 4, ]{.class_4938}["Let us cut them off from being a nation." ]{.class_4939}[This is the official policy of ]{.class_4938}[Pope John Paul II ]{.class_4937}[and the Roman Catholic hierarchy, with the agreement of Saddam Hussein (]{.class_4938}[Thirty Days, ]{.class_4937}[Ignatius Press, Dec. 1990, pp. 22--25). The plan is genocide with the approval of the UN, which professes that such a thing was "criminal."]{.class_4938}

[Verse 5, note "perfect global unity" by "citizens of the world." This is "Earth Day," as in Genesis 11:1--3. ]{.class_4938}["They have taken crafty counsel\...they have said\...they have consulted together with one consent; they are confederate" ]{.class_4939}[(Ps. 83:3--5).]{.class_4938}

[One world, one people ("EIN VOLK! EIN FUHRER! EIN REICH!"), one shepherd, one flock. Perfect unity, peace on earth, everyone together, all in agreement, one accord, one body, one faith, perfect integration, no difference, class leveling, "we the people"! ]{.class_4938}[The kingdom has come!]{.class_4937}

[There is just one thing wrong with it. It is anti God, so God is going to destroy it (Dan. 2:24; Zech. 14:2--3; Joel 3:16, 2:11; Rev. 14:16; Zeph. 3:8). ]{.class_4938}["They are confederate against thee." ]{.class_4939}[The "New Age" is simply "man against his Maker," "humans versus God," or "I had a dream!"]{.class_4938}

[Israel's enemies, in verses 6--8, represent more than the ancient tribes of David's time or the time of the judges, for the context is ]{.class_4938}["Selah" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 8). No one has yet located the antitypes to these nations (]{.class_4938}["Edom\...Ishmaelites\...Moab\...Hagarenes\...Gebal\...Ammon\...Amalek"]{.class_4939}[), but they certainly are here NOW, with different names. Judges 5 (Sisera) is the way these enemies are to be destroyed, and we know from comparing Judges 5 with Psalm 68 that all of this is FUTURE---past 2002.]{.class_4938}

[Notice that ]{.class_4938}[ten ]{.class_4937}[kingdoms are listed, matching the Antichrist's cohorts in Revelation 13, 17:12.]{.class_4938}

[I will be as brief as possible with the destructive critics of the Holy Bible: Fundamentalists foremost, Conservatives and Evangelicals next, Liberals and Modernists last; all the same crew, ]{.class_4938}["one consent\...confederate\...."]{.class_4939}

From verse 3 to verse 15, Kroll can't find anything but the dead historical past. He generally describes, in a general way, the general plan of Israel's enemies and the general destruction of them sometime before the First Advent. He gives one reference in Maccabees. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown have the Antichrist present in 1000 B.C., and Jehoshaphat fulfilling verse 12. Yates says you cannot identify the period of time referred to with any certainty (i.e., HE can't), and Motyer has the "basic spiritual situation displayed" as "secular opposition to the Kingdom of God."

There isn't any "Kingdom of God" from Adam to Jesus Christ.

83:9 "Do unto them as unto the Midianites; as to Sisera, as to Jabin, at the brook of Kison:

10 Which perished at Endor: they became as dung for the earth.

11 Make their nobles like Oreb, and like Zeeb: yea, all their princes as Zebah, and as Zalmunna:

12 Who said, Let us take to ourselves the houses of God in possession.

13 O my God, make them like a wheel; as the stubble before the wind.

14 As the fire burneth a wood, and as the flame setteth the mountains on fire;

15 So persecute them with thy tempest, and make them afraid with thy storm.

16 Fill their faces with shame; that they may seek thy name, O LORD.

17 Let them be confounded and troubled for ever; yea, let them be put to shame, and perish:

18 That men may know that thou, whose name alone is JEHOVAH, art the most high over all the earth."

[There are TEN enemies listed (vss. 6--8), so there are TEN kings in Revelation who are ]{.class_4938}["confederate" ]{.class_4939}[against God (vs. 5). They will have to match. What follows is the Holy Spirit's famous use of ]{.class_4938}["as"]{.class_4939}[ and ]{.class_4938}["like"]{.class_4939}[ (see Hosea 12:10) to teach Biblical truths that the Greek and Hebrew scholars are unable to find. Not one commentator whose name appears in this commentary could tell anyone ]{.class_4938}[where ]{.class_4937}[the prayer of Asaph in verses 9--16 was answered. Some of them suggest Jehoshaphat, but this, of course, is nonsense. The ]{.class_4938}["wheel" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 13) is set in motion by a wheel (Ezek. 1:15--17) called]{.class_4938}[ "O wheel"]{.class_4939}[ (Ezek. 10:13), and the ]{.class_4938}["stubble before the wind" ]{.class_4939}[was defined by the Holy Spirit in Daniel 2:35 as being a reference to the Gentile powers at the Second Coming of Christ. The ]{.class_4938}["perpetual end" ]{.class_4939}[of Psalm 9:6 is given in verse 17 as perpetual trouble and shame. No one could miss the references but\...you know who. "Where is stupidity? Where is unbelief? Where can blindness and arrogance be found?" In any Christian institution of "higher learning" where Bible correctors are paid to LIE about final authority in the life of the believer.]{.class_4938}

With the Midianites given right in front of your face (vs. 9), and Isaiah telling you that this battle is the Second Advent of Jesus Christ (Isa. 9:1--7), Kroll, Duhm, Davies, Ewald, Hupfeld, Hengstenberg, Spurgeon, Thrupp, Whitney, Wall, Cradock, Scofield, Baethgen, Perowne, and Hitzig can no more locate the battle than they could locate Oak Bowery, Alabama. Spurgeon tells us that the ten kingdom confederation of the Son of Perdition, out to destroy Israel, is really "the enemies of the church" (p. 427). Not one commentator in a thousand years has any idea of what is going on. So for a moment, let us pretend that we have the Scriptures to read: the Holy Bible, the words of God. Let us pretend (at the risk of being accused of "heresy") that the faculties and staffs of Bob Jones, Wheaton, Moody, Fuller, Oxford, Harvard, Yale, Cambridge, Stuttgart, Berlin, Tennessee Temple, Dallas Theological Seminary, Grace Theological Seminary, Baptist Bible College, and Pacific Coast Bible College, with all of their libraries and all of their doctoral theses and all of their degrees, are simply unfit for service: 4 F.

[1. There are ]{.class_4938}[seven ]{.class_4937}[descriptions of Armageddon in the Old Testament. These are David's attack on the Philistines (2 Sam. 5:24), Barak's attack on Sisera (Judg. 5), the Red Sea crossing (Exod. 15), Joshua's "long day" (see Josh. 10:13; Hab. 3:11), and Gideon's "300" (see Judg. 7, Isa. 9:4--5). There are some more details furnished by David's encounter with Goliath and Hezekiah's confrontation with Sennacherib, but here before us are Barak (]{.class_4938}["as to Sisera, as to Jabin"]{.class_4939}[) and Gideon (]{.class_4938}["nobles]{.class_4939}[ ]{.class_4937}[like Oreb\...Zeeb\...Zebah, and as Zalmunna"]{.class_4939}[).]{.class_4938}

[2. The ]{.class_4938}["sword of the Lord" ]{.class_4939}[must be present (Rev. 19:15; Judg. 7:18). A minority must destroy a majority (Rev. 9:16, 19:19; Judg. 7:6--7). The enemies must come from the EAST (Judg. 6:3; Rev. 16:12). Baal worship must be present at this time (Rev. 2:14, 20; Judg. 2:13, 6:25).]{.class_4938}

See how easy it is when you go by the "original English" instead of the "original Hebrew"?

[3. The Antichrist will get it in the HEAD (see Judg. 4:21 and compare with Ps. 7:16 and 110:6). His opposition will come from ]{.class_4938}[heaven ]{.class_4937}[(Rev. 19:11; Judg. 5:20). A ]{.class_4938}["woman" ]{.class_4939}[(see Song of Sol. 6:4, 10) will finish him off (see Jer. 4:31, 31:22). [4. All the earth will be at ]{.class_4938}["rest"]{.class_4939}[ after this (Isa. 14:7 and Judg. 5:31). There will be a Millennial "Sabbath."]{.class_4938}

[Nothing to it. ]{.class_4938}[No knowledge of Hebrew or Greek is necessary. ]{.class_4937}[No college course in Hebrew history, Biblical archaeology, or systematic theology has to be taken; ]{.class_4938}[not ONE. What the greatest Scriptural exegetes and Hebrew etymologists could not find in ANY set of manuscripts copied from ANY copies of other copies was given in 1611 in the universal language of the twenty-first century. ]{.class_4937}[You are to believe (if you are stupid enough to think that Bob Jones III or Bob Jones IV are Bible believers) that this was ACCIDENTAL and had nothing to do with the Author of Scripture, the Holy Spirit. Wanna buy the Brooklyn Bridge?]{.class_4938}

"[The houses of God in possession" ]{.class_4939}[(vs 12). Notice the ]{.class_4938}[plural; ]{.class_4937}[the reference is to the ]{.class_4938}["synagogues" ]{.class_4939}[of Psalm 74:8, which, you will remember, were carefully removed from the Bible by the misinformed, unenlightened "Fundamentalists."]{.class_4938}

[Kroll (Liberty University) knows a crisis when he sees it. The words ]{.class_4938}["houses of God"]{.class_4939}[ stare at him. He stares back at them. They will not retreat. He continues to stare; they stare back at him. "Out, out, damned spot! " (Macbeth), but the spot will not get out. What are ]{.class_4938}["houses of God"]{.class_4939}[ in the plural? Kroll's eyes get watery; now they are fogged. He gets up, goes to the kitchen, and gets him some Maxwell House (singular) and then skips the verse and pretends it wasn't in Psalm 83.]{.class_4938}

Spurgeon adopts this view and says that the ]{.class_4938}["houses"]{.class_4939}[ are a reference to "all the dwellings of the tribes of Israel." But isn't the wording remarkable in the "King's English"? The word used was ]{.class_4938}["houses,"]{.class_4939}[ not "tents" or "dwelling places" or "habitations" (Masoretic publication). The ]{.class_4938}[RSV ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[NIV ]{.class_4937}[decide that it must mean "pastures" or "pastureland." Duhm says it should be "homesteads."]{.class_4938}

[How about ]{.class_4938}["houses of God," ]{.class_4939}[just for novelty, okay? The Pope's UN troops will want to take possession of every synagogue in Israel, and they will burn them to the ground when they invade the land (Ps. 74:8). Your "leading commentators" were really only dedicated to one task: how to erase advanced revelation as quickly as God revealed it.]{.class_4938}

"[As the fire\...as the flame\...make them afraid with thy storm" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 14--15). Thus it will come to pass ]{.class_4938}[literally ]{.class_4937}[in the Tribulation (Rev. 8:7, 16:8; 2 Thess. 1:8; Mal. 4:1; Joel 2:3--5, etc.).
