PSALM 92

92:1 "It is a good thing to give thanks unto the LORD, and to sing praises unto thy name, O most High:

2 To shew forth thy lovingkindness in the morning, and thy faithfulness every night,

3 Upon an instrument of ten strings, and upon the psaltery; upon the harp with a solemn sound.

4 For thou, LORD, hast made me glad through thy work: I will triumph in the works of thy hands.

5 O LORD, how great are thy works! and thy thoughts are very deep.

6 A brutish man knoweth not; neither doth a fool understand this.

7 When the wicked spring as the grass, and when all the workers of iniquity do flourish; it is that they shall be destroyed for ever:

8 But thou, LORD, art most high for evermore.

9 For, lo, thine enemies, O LORD, for, lo, thine enemies shall perish; all the workers of iniquity shall be scattered."

[Much of the Psalm is ]{.class_4938}[devotional ]{.class_4937}[and describes the temporary prosperity of the wicked. However, the Psalmist speaks for himself, as well as for Christ, in verses 10 and 11. It is tempting to think that David wrote this Psalm because of Psalm 89:24 and the reference to the ]{.class_4938}["horn" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 10).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 1 is self explanatory. It is ]{.class_4938}["good" ]{.class_4939}[to give thanks and to sing praises because the ]{.class_4938}["most High" ]{.class_4939}[is worthy of praise, and He is the one from whom all blessings flow. In the New Testament, His ]{.class_4938}["name"]{.class_4939}[ is "Jesus," so songs like "Jesus What a Friend of Sinners," "Jesus Lover of My Soul," "Fairest Lord Jesus," "Jesus the Very Thought of Thee," and "All Hail the Power of Jesus' Name" make a good hymnal. Not one of these songs will fit into the ecumenical movement. Any pope engaged in that godless business must put the ]{.class_4938}["name" ]{.class_4939}[in second or third place. This explains the popularity of the Catholic "Mary" who, in the Bible, is identified as the Queen of Hell (see Jer. 44:17--25). No Mohammedan is going to tolerate a United Nations congregation standing and singing "All Hail the Power of Jesus' Name." This modern, worldwide adultery ("integration," etc.) being committed by the religious, educational, and political leaders can only end in a world war (Rev. 6:4). For the last time: There can be no peace on earth till there is ]{.class_4938}["glory to God in the highest," ]{.class_4939}[(Luke 2:14), and there can be no ]{.class_4938}["glory to God in the highest" ]{.class_4939}[until mankind honors ]{.class_4938}[the name ]{.class_4937}["which is above every name" ]{.class_4939}[(Phil. 2:9). That name is NOT "man," "mankind," "Buddha," "goodwill," "Mary," "Mohammed," or "Martin Luther King Jr." The ]{.class_4938}[name is ]{.class_4937}["Jesus."]{.class_4938}

[If what the Bible says is true, "world peace" is just one big, tragic joke. In the Book, man's trouble is ]{.class_4938}[sin, so ]{.class_4937}[there can be no relief from wars, which are the ]{.class_4938}[result ]{.class_4937}[of sin. ]{.class_4938}["Thou shalt call his name JESUS, for he shall save his people from their sins" ]{.class_4939}[(Matt. 1:21). No salvation from sins; no remedy for war. It is that simple. It is a simple plan that God Almighty rigged up to destroy every nation on this earth that refused to believe what He said. It'll "do the job" (Isa. 28:29).]{.class_4938}

[The ]{.class_4938}["praises,"]{.class_4939}[ here, are a form of singing accompanied by ]{.class_4938}[musical instruments]{.class_4937}[ (vs. 3). John Wells says that "singing is the music of nature, the music of ordinances, the music of saints, the music of angels (Job 38:7), and the music of Heaven" (Rev. 5:8--9, 14:2--3). ]{.class_4938}["Lovingkindness" ]{.class_4939}[is to be praised at the start of the day, for that is what you will need to get through the day. When the day is over (]{.class_4938}["every night" ]{.class_4939}[vs. 2), you can praise God for his faithfulness for that day (see Lam. 3:23).]{.class_4938}

Again, the Calvinists try to throw the orchestra out the window. Andrew Fuller (in Spurgeon) says, "Instrumental music, the more I think of it, appears with increasing evidence to be utterly UNSUITED to the genius of the Gospel dispensation."

Fuller would have made a good Campbellite.

[Adam Clarke, another dead orthodox Calvinist, says, "Whatever may be thought of this gloss, one thing is pretty evident from it; that instrumental music was not in use in the church of Christ in the time of Eusebius, which was near the middle of the fourth century." (Aren't we "spiritual"? Aren't we "scriptural"?) Where were the ]{.class_4938}[church buildings ]{.class_4937}[before the fourth century? Where could you find a "pulpit" anywhere in the New Testament? No ]{.class_4938}[choir? ]{.class_4937}[No Sunday School? Isn't it strange how "Scriptural" a man can be when he wants to get rid of something he doesn't like? There are no "houses of God" in the New Testament, there are no Sunday Schools, there are no Daily Vacation Bible Schools, and Spurgeon's "Metropolitan Pulpit" is about as ]{.class_4938}[Scriptural ]{.class_4937}[as a novena.]{.class_4938}

[Note that instruments have "voice tones" exactly like speakers. The ]{.class_4938}["solemn sound" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 3) on a harp is in the lowest register. Cellos have rich, smooth, somber voices that pacify, sympathize, or "weep" with the listener. Oboes have a lonely, macabre sound which comes out even stronger on the bassoons. Bass fiddles are somber and disturbing and can produce an unmistakably tense atmosphere. The flutes are the most "airy" and "outdoors" of any of the instruments; they can weep and lament, but they usually soothe or cause the spirit to soar. The piano (like most percussion instruments) has no definite "sound" or "tone," but it creates moods by a variety of tones, volumes, and speeds. Trumpets speak definite statements in any mood. Trombones suggest chaos or majesty, depending upon the score, and the tuba is a jester or sardonic commentator, an amplifier to a crescendo of some kind. Violins and violas are much like the piano, and are capable of speaking lightly, gaily, soberly, somberly, tragically, in jest, in sympathy, or in pathos, tragedy, or sorrow. Of all the instruments, the harp is by far the most "celestial"; it has an ethereal, unearthly quality about it found in no other instrument. The tone of the clarinet is light and jovial. The tone of the French horn is lonesome and expansive. Music speaks: it is "the universal language."]{.class_4938}

[Inspirationally, ]{.class_4937}[we have here the joys of the ministry. God makes His servants, whom He called to preach, glad through His work. From a personal standpoint, after fifty years in the ministry, I must say that I have been "made glad" not only by seeing souls saved (over seven thousand) and converts growing in grace, but by the chance to enjoy real, human friendships, which is a rare thing in this world, the chance to redeem twenty seven years of an utterly selfish and self centered life, the opportunity to observe God Almighty actually fulfilling His words, and the opportunity to see something established (Dan. 12:3) that will last forever.]{.class_4938}

[God's works are ]{.class_4938}["great"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 5), and His thoughts are ]{.class_4938}["deep."]{.class_4939}[ They are so deep (Job 11:7) that a man can give up hope of finding out what they are unless they are revealed in Scripture (1 Cor. 2:16). There are some things revealed in Scripture which no man can know, if he is ]{.class_4938}["brutish" ]{.class_4939}[(see Ps. 94:8, 49:10). One of these things is given in verse 7; it is that ]{.class_4938}["when the wicked spring as the grass" ]{.class_4939}[(Jer. 5:28) ]{.class_4938}["it is that they shall be destroyed for ever." ]{.class_4939}[They will be ]{.class_4938}["scattered" ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["perish" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 9). We are back on the Second Advent again, and not one commentator could pick up the trail. The wicked ]{.class_4938}["spring as the grass and\...flourish" ]{.class_4939}[in Jude 4--13; 2 Peter 2:1--20; and 2 Timothy 3:1--6. They are referred to in Proverbs 30:11--14 as a specific ]{.class_4938}["generation." ]{.class_4939}[They are ]{.class_4938}["destroyed for ever" ]{.class_4939}[(see Rev. 14:9--11 and Ps. 140:10). The wicked are for fuel (Mal. 4:1--3; Isa. 5:24, 9:19, 33:12, etc.), but the righteous are for fruit (John 15:5; Rom. 6:22).]{.class_4938}

[If the Psalm is correct, and we are not ]{.class_4938}["brutish," ]{.class_4939}[we understand that America's cup is just about full (see Gen. 15:16). The wicked flourish in America, and they brag about their wickedness. They go beyond that: they teach their wickednesses to your children and recommend their sins as acceptable "life styles." They have sprung up in America ]{.class_4938}["as the grass"]{.class_4939}[ and have taken deep root. According to the text, this means they are about to be burned in the fire (Matt. 13:42, 50; 2 Sam. 23:6--7; Zeph. 3:8). ]{.class_4938}["But thou, Lord, art most high for evermore" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 8). No one is going to destroy Him, and no one is going to "scatter" Him, and He is not about to perish. It was Satan who said that He would be ]{.class_4938}["like the most High" ]{.class_4939}[(Isa. 14:12--14). Observe that the context of this ambitious statement is the end of the Antichrist and the close of the Tribulation (Isa. 14:16--20). All the commentators miss all of the references.]{.class_4938}

92:10 "But my horn shalt thou exalt like the horn of an unicorn: I shall be anointed with fresh oil.

11 Mine eye also shall see my desire on mine enemies, and mine ears shall hear my desire of the wicked that rise up against me.

12 The righteous shall flourish like the palm tree: he shall grow like a cedar in Lebanon.

13 Those that be planted in the house of the LORD shall flourish in the courts of our God.

14 They shall still bring forth fruit in old age; they shall be fat and flourishing;

15 To shew that the LORD is upright: he is my rock, and there is no unrighteousness in him."

[The Psalmist, speaking for the Lord Jesus Christ as well as himself, says that his horn, his eyes, his ears, and his desire will be worked upon by God. His ]{.class_4938}["horn" ]{.class_4939}[(see Ps. 18:2, 75:4, and comments) will be exalted; his eye will see the destruction of his enemies; his ear will receive the news of their destruction; and his desire to destroy them will be granted (Isa. 63:3). What follows (vss. 12--14) is a direct ]{.class_4938}[doctrinal ]{.class_4937}[reference to Millennial saints (see the matter of the ]{.class_4938}["courts"]{.class_4939}[ in Ps. 65:4). But much ]{.class_4938}[devotional ]{.class_4937}[material can be found here, since the word ]{.class_4938}["like"]{.class_4939}[ shows up. This is the key to Scriptural interpretation according to Hosea 12:10.]{.class_4938}

[As we have noted, the wicked are for fuel, while the righteous are for fruit. Now the fruit bearers show up. They are likened to two things: palm trees and cedar trees. Both of these trees were connected with the ]{.class_4938}["courts" ]{.class_4939}[of the Lord (vs. 13). One tree was carved on the walls of Solomon's temple (1 Kings 6:29), and the other was the actual building itself (1 Kings 6:9--10, 15, 18, 20). The Holy Spirit picks the palm tree for reasons that are apparent to no one unless they had read 1 Thessalonians 2:19; Matthew 13:23; and 2 Timothy 3:1--2. The palm tree referred to is the "tamar phoenix dactylifera," and it is a wonder to behold.]{.class_4938}

1\. As the soulwinning Christian, it was "once abundant, but now is very rare." It could supply all the needs for desert people, for it could be used to produce food for camels, rope, wine to drink, and timber for building. The Hindus claim it had 360 uses.

2\. It is tall and straight and grows in three sizes; thirty feet, sixty feet, and one hundred feet! (Think about that! Matt. 13:23.)

3\. Like the soulwinning Christian, it has his roots in water that is underground, and it remains verdant when everything else dries up in a drought (see Jer. 17:7--8).

4\. The best dates are produced after thirty years (see Gen. 41:46; Luke 3:23), and they are more productive in old age than before thirty. The heart of the palm tree tastes like an artichoke.

[The second similitude used for ]{.class_4938}["the righteous" ]{.class_4939}[(be sure and note the doctrinal application of the term in Psalm 5:12, 7:11, and 14:5) is the ]{.class_4938}["cedar."]{.class_4939}[ The cedar has its roots in the rocks. It is evergreen. It has a long life. It repels insects, and it is sound to the core. It is actually ]{.class_4938}["planted in the house of the Lord" ]{.class_4939}[in the sense that the temple was built out of cedar planks. The purpose for allowing ]{.class_4938}["the righteous" ]{.class_4939}[to bring forth fruit in old age is ]{.class_4938}["to shew that the Lord is upright" ]{.class_4939}[(]{.class_4938}["From me is thy fruit found," ]{.class_4939}[Hosea 14:8). The proof that ]{.class_4938}["there is no unrighteousness in him" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 15) is the fruit He produces (see Matt. 3:10, 7:17). We are the ]{.class_4938}[branches ]{.class_4937}[(John 15:1--5); we simply hold the fruit that the ]{.class_4938}["true vine" ]{.class_4939}[produces. Note in the commentary on Galatians--Colossians (Gal. 5:22), that one must work to produce the fruit of the flesh, but the ]{.class_4938}["fruit of the Spirit" ]{.class_4939}[does not have the word "works" attached to it. The fruit of the Spirit comes from "walking in the Spirit" and "minding the things of the Spirit." Billy Sunday led men to Christ within the month of his death. Lucius Eddy led men to Christ when he was eighty one years old; so did John Wesley. John the Apostle produced the book of Revelation after he was seventy; Dwight Moody and Charles Spurgeon had souls saved under their preaching to the very years of their deaths. Caleb and Joshua were still "Sturmtruppen" at the age of eighty, and Moses was entrusted to write a song that will be sung after 1991 (Rev. 15:3); he was given this commission when he was over one hundred years old (Deut. 31:19).]{.class_4938}

A final lesson is found in the fact that it is no good to grow TALL (the cedar), unless you grow STRAIGHT (the palm).

[We have given enough time to the ]{.class_4938}[devotional parts ]{.class_4937}[of the passage to suffice. However, notice that the Second Advent is about to pop up again (Ps. 93), and if we had not seen this, the term ]{.class_4938}["the righteous" ]{.class_4939}[should have warned us. There is (as we have said before on four dozen occasions) a difference between salvation in the Church Age and in Daniel's Seventieth Week. The term ]{.class_4938}["the righteous" ]{.class_4939}[can be applied to Tribulation saints exactly as the term ]{.class_4938}["sinner" ]{.class_4939}[was applied under the Law (see Luke 7:37 and John 9:16, 24).]{.class_4938}

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ1516.xhtml}

PSALM 93

93:1 "The LORD reigneth, he is clothed with majesty; the LORD is clothed with strength, wherewith he hath girded himself: the world also is stablished, that it cannot be moved.

2 Thy throne is established of old: thou art from everlasting.

3 The floods have lifted up, O LORD, the floods have lifted up their voice; the floods lift up their waves.

4 The LORD on high is mightier than the noise of many waters, yea, than the mighty waves of the sea.

5 Thy testimonies are very sure: holiness becometh thine house, O LORD, for ever."

[Trouble: ]{.class_4938}["The LORD reigneth." ]{.class_4939}[The ]{.class_4938}[present tense is ]{.class_4937}[going to destroy the commentators down to the last man. If ONE of them was a Premillennialist (like Kroll of Liberty University), verse 2 would have finished him off because it spoke of an ]{.class_4938}["everlasting" ]{.class_4939}[reigning. This would be coupled to ]{.class_4938}["Thy throne, O God, is forever and ever" ]{.class_4939}[(Heb. 1:8), and the deception would be complete.]{.class_4938}

Here, the "serious student of Scripture" (an Alexandrian Cult cliché used to seduce you into thinking that "serious" scholars would accept no book on this earth as holy and no book on this earth as the final authority) needs to notice two things completely overlooked by Kroll, Dummelow, Yates, Motyer, Hengstenberg, McCaw, Baethgen, Briggs, and company.

[1. The present tense is used for the future so many times that it would make a long list: ]{.class_4938}["This is my rest for ever: here will I dwell" ]{.class_4939}[(Ps. 132:14)---not in past and not NOW. ]{.class_4938}["Unto us a child is born, unto us a son is given" ]{.class_4939}[(Isa. 9:6). Not then or near then. ]{.class_4938}["Hell from beneath is moved for thee to meet thee at thy coming" ]{.class_4939}[(Isa. 14:9). Not at any time within 2,400 years of the time it was written. ]{.class_4938}["For, behold, I create new heavens and a new earth" ]{.class_4939}[(Isa. 65:17); no, at least not within 3,400 years of stating the matter. ]{.class_4938}["He smote his enemies in the hinder parts" ]{.class_4939}[(Ps. 78:66). Not yet, He hasn't. ]{.class_4938}["In Salem also is his tabernacle, and his dwelling place in Zion" ]{.class_4939}[(Ps. 76:2). Not when the next verse took place, no. God is not dwelling in Zion when He breaks the arrows, bows, shields, and ]{.class_4938}["battle" ]{.class_4939}[(Ps. 76:3) unless it is the Second Advent. God did not ARISE (Ps. 76:9) to save any ]{.class_4938}["meek of the earth" ]{.class_4939}[one time since Adam, but He WILL.]{.class_4938}

[2. Again, notice that all the descriptions in the following verses were written in the ]{.class_4938}[past tense, ]{.class_4937}[though they dealt with things anywhere from 450 to 2,000 years ]{.class_4938}[after ]{.class_4937}[they were written: Isaiah 53; Habakkuk 3; Psalm 68; 2 Samuel 2:4--8; Deuteronomy 32; Judges 5, etc.).]{.class_4938}

[3. Finally, notice that ]{.class_4938}["thy throne," ]{.class_4939}[in the passage, is not just God's throne; it is ]{.class_4938}[David's throne at Jerusalem ]{.class_4937}[(Jer. 3:17, 14:21, 17:12, 22:4). The trouble was the same as usual. The apostate Nicolaitans in the Scholar's Union, who wanted to have dominion over your faith, were so buried in "original autographs" and "the Hebrew" up to their noses, they couldn't smell a rat if he showed up.]{.class_4938}

[When Spurgeon and company hit Psalms 93, 97, and 99, they came apart like a paper bag going through a shredder. Here (vs. 1), we get "Whatever opposition may arise, His throne is unmoved; He has reigned, does reign, and will reign forever and ever." Neato! A perfect truism that doesn't tell you anything. "All things are ordered according to His eternal purposes, and His will is done." Well, Calvin, ole' son, you had to pray for His will to be done (Matt. 6:10), remember? And His will is NOT always done, even in the bodies of those in whom he worketh ]{.class_4938}["to will and to do of his good pleasure" ]{.class_4939}[(Phil. 2:13). That is, unless you consider the ]{.class_4938}["works of the flesh" ]{.class_4939}[(Gal. 5:19)---for which God will kill a Christian (Rom. 8:13; 1 Cor. 5:5)---to be his ]{.class_4938}["will and\...good pleasure.]{.class_4939}

Calvinists have a time of it, don't they?

[It is plain to see that God is an eternal Sovereign in the sense that ]{.class_4938}[He is in control. ]{.class_4937}[No one will ever be able to make a liar out of Him, although it has been tried on a number of occasions (Peter: Matt. 16:22; Paul: Acts 22:18--21; the Pharisees: John 8:13; Pilate: John 18:38; Ahab: 1 Kings 22:28--30; Arthur Farstad, Zane Hodges, A. T. Robertson, Philip Schaff, Bob Jones IV, John R. Rice, Curtis Hutson, Spiros Zodhiates, James Price, Stewart Custer, Lee Roberson, et al.). God is an everlasting ruler on an everlasting throne in the sense of Daniel 4:25 and Jeremiah 10:11--12; that is, ]{.class_4938}[He is in charge of the universe, ]{.class_4937}[so what He says will come to pass. His word is as good as His omnipotence. But when you begin to deal with God's]{.class_4938}[ "house" ]{.class_4939}[(see vs. 5), you are on another track. Heaven is His throne, and the earth is His]{.class_4938}[ "footstool,"]{.class_4939}[ so a ]{.class_4938}["house"]{.class_4939}[ in eternity is something else. God told Stephen and David that a ]{.class_4938}["house" ]{.class_4939}[was something on this earth (2 Sam. 7:5, 6; Acts 7:48--49), and it was at JERUSALEM, ]{.class_4938}[not out in eternity.]{.class_4937}

[Kroll flinches at the verse because he has been messing with "the Hebrew," which said "God is become KING," implying He had ]{.class_4938}[not ]{.class_4937}[been king before a certain time. We didn't need the Hebrew; we had Zechariah 14:9; Psalms 2:6, 10:16, and 24:7 to clear up "the Hebrew." The Psalmist here (Ps. 93) is praising a Lord who is on the throne ]{.class_4938}[at Jerusalem ]{.class_4937}[(Luke 1:30--33), where His "house" will be in the Millennium (Ezek. 40--44). Still, we may make some devotional applications.]{.class_4938}

[1. God has always been clothed with majesty and strength (vs. 1). He throws this into Job's face when that poor suffering saint is sitting on an ash heap, covered with sores and maggots (Job 7:5, 40:10). ]{.class_4938}["The world also is stablished, ]{.class_4939}[" in the sense of Isaiah 42:5, 45:12, and 45:18. ]{.class_4938}["It cannot be moved," ]{.class_4939}[in the sense that neither man nor the devil can move it, but it most certainly WILL be ]{.class_4938}["moved" ]{.class_4939}[(look at Isa. 24:19) and moved ]{.class_4938}["exceedingly." ]{.class_4939}[To get around this truth, the scholars decide that the word for ]{.class_4938}["world" ]{.class_4939}[is only the "inhabitable earth." Kroll makes it God's ]{.class_4938}[sovereignty ]{.class_4937}[that cannot be moved, although the text (in any set of manuscripts) says nothing of the kind. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown come up close to the truth when they note that the reference is to "the re establishment of the world, now disorganized by sin." If they had gone one step further they would have located the text ]{.class_4938}[doctrinally, ]{.class_4937}[but they stopped there. Fausset noted that the first three words of verse 1 were a formula that "proclaimed\...the accession of an EARTHLY MONARCH" (p. 304). Fausset, completely contradicting every commentator in Spurgeon's ]{.class_4938}[Treasury,, ]{.class_4937}[says that the "reference is not to the ordinary and constant government of God." Correct. But when pressed to locate the time this takes place, Fausset flips over to Revelation 21:1--4 and blows it. ]{.class_4938}[He omitted the Millennial Reign, and that is what the Psalm is about.]{.class_4937}

[2. We may say that the ]{.class_4938}["floods" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 3 refer to ]{.class_4938}["the floods of ungodly men" ]{.class_4939}[(Ps. 18:4) trying to overthrow God's plans (Ps. 2 or Rev. 20, for example) and that the]{.class_4938}[ "waters"]{.class_4939}[ and the]{.class_4938}[ "sea" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 4) are ]{.class_4938}["peoples, and multitudes, and nations" ]{.class_4939}[(Rev. 17:15), the sea is the ]{.class_4938}["wicked,"]{.class_4939}[ as in Isaiah 57:20, but this is "thin ice," as we have seen before (Ps. 24:2, 32:6).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 5 is much easier to handle. His testimonies are sure (see Ps. 119:2, 14, 22, 24, 31, 46, 59, 79). Testimonies are things that God has laid out for man to do. They are "testaments" with which God instructs man with; things that He "testifies" to be SO because they are so:]{.class_4938}[ "very sure."]{.class_4939}

PSALM 94

94:1 "O LORD God, to whom vengeance belongeth; O God, to whom vengeance belongeth, shew thyself.

2 Lift up thyself, thou judge of the earth: render a reward to the proud.

3 LORD, how long shall the wicked, how long shall the wicked triumph?

4 How long shall they utter and speak hard things? and all the workers of iniquity boast themselves?

5 They break in pieces thy people, O LORD, and afflict thine heritage.

6 They slay the widow and the stranger, and murder the fatherless.

7 Yet they say, The LORD shall not see, neither shall the God of Jacob regard it.

8 Understand, ye brutish among the people: and ye fools, when will ye be wise?

9 He that planted the ear, shall he not hear? he that formed the eye, shall he not see?

10 He that chastiseth the heathen, shall not he correct? he that teacheth man knowledge, shall not he know?

11 The LORD knoweth the thoughts of man, that they are vanity."

[The entire Psalm is from the mouth of an Israelite in Daniel's Seventieth Week, so you may expect a mass defection among the Scholar's Union. Note how clearly the Holy Spirit has pointed out the ]{.class_4938}[doctrinal ]{.class_4937}[location of the Psalm, which will completely "flabbergast" the expositors. Kroll stumbles through the first eleven verses like a blind man on an army obstacle course. He cannot locate ]{.class_4938}["thy people" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 5), he cannot find why God is called ]{.class_4938}["the God of Jacob" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 7), he doesn't know who the ]{.class_4938}["heathen" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 10) are, and he has the ]{.class_4938}["judge of the earth" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 2) up in the air judging nothing on earth.]{.class_4938}

"[Since God alone is able to righteously avenge those who are afflicted\...the question 'How long?' is a frequent lamentation in the Psalms\...such a cry is both one of impatience and one of faith\...here is a strong plea for divine intervention; for those afflicted by the wicked are none other than the elect of God\...even among God's people there were some who supposed God would not see or regard their sin."]{.class_4938}

[Who are the "elect"? ]{.class_4938}[He didn't ]{.class_4937}[say.]{.class_4938}

[Who are "God's people"? ]{.class_4938}[He didn't say.]{.class_4937}

[Who are]{.class_4938}[ "the wicked"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 3)? ]{.class_4938}[He didn't say.]{.class_4937}

[What happened to God's ]{.class_4938}["heritage"]{.class_4939}[ in verse 5? ]{.class_4938}[It vanished.]{.class_4937}

[Who are the ]{.class_4938}["heathen" ]{.class_4939}[in verse 10? Kroll hasn't got the "foggiest."]{.class_4938}

At Lynchburg, Virginia, there are three things that the "Champions for Christ" cannot do:

[1. They cannot ]{.class_4938}[understand ]{.class_4937}[the Scriptures.]{.class_4938}

[2. They cannot ]{.class_4938}[quote ]{.class_4937}[the Scriptures.]{.class_4938}

[3. They cannot ]{.class_4938}[expound ]{.class_4937}[the Scriptures.]{.class_4938}

[Look at the signposts: ]{.class_4938}["Vengeance\...shew thyself\...lift up thyself\...render a reward\...how long?" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 1--3). How could any Christian on God's earth look at that line up and forget Deuteronomy 32:35; Hebrews 10:30; 2 Peter 2:13; Zechariah 12:10, 13:6; Revelation 1:7; Psalms 7:6, 10:12; Revelation 22:12; Luke 17:22; Psalm 37:10; and John 16:16--20? In the last reference, the travail of Israel (as a nation) is mentioned in the context, with the cross reference to Luke 6:21 thrown in for good measure. We are now faced with a baleful and scandalous truth: the vast majority of Hebrew and Greek professors at Christian universities spend no time ]{.class_4938}[reading their Bibles. ]{.class_4937}[You couldn't be a daily reader of the Book and miss the ]{.class_4938}[doctrinal ]{.class_4937}[import of Psalm 94:1--10. Kroll, Wemp, Hindson, Dobson, Freerksen, Schrader, Towns, Jantz, and Willmington couldn't find a croquet ball in the bathroom sink.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 1. The prayer to the God of vengeance will be answered, ]{.class_4938}[literally, ]{.class_4937}[by the God of vengeance (Heb. 10:30). This God will render a reward to ]{.class_4938}["the proud" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 2) among His own people (Dan. 11:34; Hos. 5:5) as well as ]{.class_4938}["the heathen" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 10). The warnings to ]{.class_4938}["his people" ]{.class_4939}[(Israel) are given in Hebrews 3, 6, 10, and Matthew 24:13.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 3. The wicked will triumph until Revelation 11:15 and 19:11--17. They will speak the ]{.class_4938}["hard things" ]{.class_4939}[like Daniel 11:36 and Revelation 13:4--8; 16:9, 11. All those verses are literal, ]{.class_4938}[and all are in the future.]{.class_4937}

[Verse 5.]{.class_4938}[ "Thy people"]{.class_4939}[ here, as elsewhere, is clearly the nation of Israel, and is called ]{.class_4938}["his people"]{.class_4939}[ so many times in the Scriptures that only the Scholar's Union could fail to identify it. Here are Jacopo Sadolet, Barton Bouchier, John Milton, John Trapp, Charles Spurgeon, Joseph Caryl, Hugo Cardinalis, J. M. Neale, and Albert Barnes all expounding Psalm 94. Not ]{.class_4938}[one man in the lot ]{.class_4937}[could identify ]{.class_4938}["thy people." ]{.class_4939}[Not even Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown can handle it; the best they can dish out is that the second clause (]{.class_4938}["thine heritage"]{.class_4939}[) is a sample of what Pharaoh did to Israel. Spurgeon, so far out in left field that he is in the bleachers, tells you that God's people in verse 5 "marks out the election of the saints."]{.class_4938}

[Do you know the judgment that the Holy Spirit passed on such men? It is recorded in Romans 11:25. It says that men who think ]{.class_4938}["thy people" ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["his people" ]{.class_4939}[refer to ]{.class_4938}[Gentiles, ]{.class_4937}[in the Body of Christ, are IGNORANT and CONCEITED.]{.class_4938}

Verse 6. Observe the Tribulation saints again, as found in James 5:4--6 and Matthew 25:35--37.

[Verse 7. The ]{.class_4938}["they"]{.class_4939}[ in the passage are ]{.class_4938}["all the workers of iniquity" ]{.class_4939}[mentioned in verse 4. They will be found again in 2 Peter 2:1--10, Jude 4--12, and again in Proverbs 30:11--14. They are ]{.class_4938}[Gentiles]{.class_4937}[ (]{.class_4938}["the heathen" ]{.class_4939}[in vs. 10).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 8. The verse is aimed at the people of Israel, who are being seduced by the Son of Perdition in Daniel 11:34--35. They have become like the heathen, in that they have adopted a neuter magnetic force field for a ]{.class_4938}["god"]{.class_4939}[ (Dan. 11:38), who ]{.class_4938}[doesn't know what they are doing ]{.class_4937}[because he is not a ]{.class_4938}[person. ]{.class_4937}[The " god" of the heathen (1991) is the electronic and chemical components of the physical universe. (The brutes and fools were mentioned back in Ps. 92:6.)]{.class_4938}

[Verses 8--10. Five questions are asked. The first is, "When are you going to get smart"? (]{.class_4938}[The New Authorized New Revised New International New Living New Standard New Bible]{.class_4937}[). The second question is, "If God can construct a human ear, do you think He isn't capable of hearing"? The third question is, "If He could construct a marvel like the human eye, do you think He is blind"? The fourth question is, "Won't God correct His own people if He takes time out to correct the heathen"? And the last question is, "Since God imparted to man the ability to think, do you think that He doesn't know what is going on inside your head"? (]{.class_4938}["The Lord knoweth the thoughts of man, that they are vanity" ]{.class_4939}[vs. 11).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 11. This is what Paul quoted in 1 Corinthians 3:20 when dealing with the intellectual Greeks in Corinth. God gave Adam speech. There was never, at any time, on this earth, such a thing as a "gradual progression" from animal grunts to human communication. That is a fairy tale for adults, meticulously and lovingly treasured and guarded by the NEA (the ]{.class_4938}[Nutty Evolved Apes]{.class_4937}[). It was God who taught Adam how to plow, sow, plant, and reap crops.]{.class_4938}

[The entire high school and college curriculum, with its teachings on "man" is a non scientific FARCE (see ]{.class_4938}[The Christian's Handbook of Science and Philosophy, ]{.class_4937}[1989). It is ]{.class_4938}[imparted wisdom ]{.class_4937}[(not trial and error) that you see demonstrated by bees, birds, insects, fish, and mammals (see Job 12:7--8, and comments in that commentary), and the same is true of man. You can only find out what God allows you to find out, and beyond ]{.class_4938}[that]{.class_4937}[ you can find out nothing. That goes for all branches of chemistry, biology, botany, physics, astronomy, geography, zoology, geology, and electronics. God's note on this is in two installments: ]{.class_4938}["Many shall run to and fro, and knowledge shall be increased" ]{.class_4939}[(Dan. 12:4), and ]{.class_4938}["Ever learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth ]{.class_4939}[(2 Tim. 3:7).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 11. See 1 Chronicles 28:9. Although impossible to imagine, the Scriptures affirm over and over again (Ecc. 12:14; Rom. 2:16; Ps. 139:2, etc.) that God knows what 6,000,000,000 people are turning over in their minds. Notice that Paul deliberately misquotes the passage and inserts ]{.class_4938}["wise" ]{.class_4939}[for ]{.class_4938}["man" ]{.class_4939}[(1 Cor. 3:20). This time, not even Origen and his buddies (Symmachus, Aquilla, and Theodotian) could mess with the text. (We presume because a one-word change was too small for them to spot.) They did not alter the Hebrew Old Testament text ]{.class_4938}[here to ]{.class_4937}[match Paul, although you saw them do it in Psalm 95 and Psalm 8.]{.class_4938}

94:12 "Blessed is the man whom thou chastenest, O LORD, and teachest him out of thy law;

13 That thou mayest give him rest from the days of adversity, until the pit be digged for the wicked.

14 For the LORD will not cast off his people, neither will he forsake his inheritance.

15 But judgment shall return unto righteousness: and all the upright in heart shall follow it.

16 Who will rise up for me against the evildoers? or who will stand up for me against the workers of iniquity?

17 Unless the LORD had been my help, my soul had almost dwelt in silence.

18 When I said, My foot slippeth; thy mercy, O LORD, held me up.

19 In the multitude of my thoughts within me thy comforts delight my soul.

20 Shall the throne of iniquity have fellowship with thee, which frameth mischief by a law?

21 They gather themselves together against the soul of the righteous, and condemn the innocent blood.

22 But the LORD is my defence; and my God is the rock of my refuge.

23 And he shall bring upon them their own iniquity, and shall cut them off in their own wickedness; yea, the LORD our God shall cut them off."

[Note that although the verse has New Testament application to a child of God (Heb. 12:6--12), because it originated with a Gentile before the Law (Job 5:17), the context here is ]{.class_4938}["out of thy law" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 12) and ]{.class_4938}["his people\...his inheritance" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 14). ]{.class_4938}["The days of adversity" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 13) plainly refer to the days of Revelation 11--13, and the ]{.class_4938}["pit"]{.class_4939}[ that will be dug for the wicked is both figurative (the destruction of the Son of Perdition with his armies, Rev. 19:19--20) and ]{.class_4938}[literal ]{.class_4937}[(Rev. 20:1--3). Verse 14 is as clear as plate glass (see Rom. 11:2, 24--27; Isa. 51, 65--66).]{.class_4938}

"[Judgment shall return unto righteousness; and all the upright in heart shall follow it" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 15); i.e., they will agree with the judgment that is meted out when it shows up; and it will show up literally (Matt. 25:30--42). The judgment returns when the JUDGE returns (James 5:9); " heah come de judge!" (American, circa 1970).]{.class_4938}

The first coming was for RIGHTEOUSNESS (John 12:47); the second one is for JUDGMENT (John 12:48).

[Of course, there are all kinds of devotional things in this passage. In verse 11, Andrew Fuller has observed that vain thoughts include thoughts that seek satisfaction where it cannot be found, thoughts about the past which cannot be recalled or undone, thoughts that anticipate some evil that might befall us, thoughts that put great value on things of no account, thoughts that are conceited or self centered, and thoughts that exclude God from our plans for the future. Listen to two people talk and watch what happens in the way of ]{.class_4938}["vanity"]{.class_4939}[; the average conversation is just two egos comparing their own experiences with each other.]{.class_4938}

[In verse 12 there are all kinds of things. Biblical examples are Job (Job 42:6), Simon Peter (John 21:15--20), Manasseh (2 Chron. 33:12--13), and especially the Psalmist himself (Ps. 119:67). Chastening comes in a variety of forms: failure in business, sick or handicapped children, car wrecks, bills, low grades, divorces, broken engagements, lawsuits, diseases, broken bones, fevers, headaches, and in its most violent forms, whippings, stonings (Paul, 2 Cor. 11:23--30), imprisonments, and torture (see Heb. 11:36--38; the matters are ]{.class_4938}[literal]{.class_4937}[).]{.class_4938}

"[Who will rise up for me\...who will stand up for me\..."]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 16). This is one of the greatest challenges and questions in the entire canon of Scripture. PSALM 95

95:1 "O come, let us sing unto the LORD: let us make a joyful noise to the rock of our salvation.

2 Let us come before his presence with thanksgiving, and make a joyful noise unto him with psalms.

3 For the LORD is a great God, and a great King above all gods.

4 In his hand are the deep places of the earth: the strength of the hills is his also.

5 The sea is his, and he made it: and his hands formed the dry land.

6 O come, let us worship and bow down: let us kneel before the LORD our maker.

7 For he is our God; and we are the people of his pasture, and the sheep of his hand. To day if ye will hear his voice,

8 Harden not your heart, as in the provocation, and as in the day of temptation in the wilderness:

9 When your fathers tempted me, proved me, and saw my work.

10 Forty years long was I grieved with this generation, and said, It is a people that do err in their heart, and they have not known my ways:

11 Unto whom I sware in my wrath that they should not enter into my rest."

[The verses are not in chronological order, much to the consternation of the Alexandrian Cult. Verses 1 through 6 are addressed to a Millennial saint who has survived Daniel's Seventieth Week and has held fast his ]{.class_4938}["confidence stedfast unto the end"]{.class_4939}[ (Heb. 3:6, 14). Verses 7--11 are addressed to a Tribulation saint who must ]{.class_4938}["endure unto the end" ]{.class_4939}[(Matt. 24:13) and not ]{.class_4938}["draw back unto perdition"]{.class_4939}[ (Heb. 10:39; see extensive comments on these dispensational matters in ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Hebrews, ]{.class_4937}[1989). Four things are mentioned to show God's power, and they all have to do with creation.

1\. The dry land (see Gen. 1:9--10).

2\. The oceans (see Gen. 1:10).

3\. The heights of the earth (the hills and mountains).

4\. The depths of the earth (subterranean caverns and the core of the earth).

Once again (and, oh, how many times have we had to do this when studying Genesis, Exodus, Daniel, Job, Proverbs, Matthew, John, Acts, and Romans), let us pretend that Nicolaitans like Briggs, Duhm, Baethgen, Ewald, Perowne, Dummelow, Motyer, Kroll, Yates, and Delitzsch had never written a word about anything; and expositors like Davidson, Kay, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Ellicott, Lange, Clarke, and Hitzig never got into the commenting business.

[5. Their offspring were the]{.class_4938}[ "men of renown"]{.class_4939}[ of Genesis 6:4, and hence the Hebrew word for "mighty ones" is used for them time and time again: ]{.class_4938}[gibbor. ]{.class_4937}[In Psalm 8:5 the word for ]{.class_4938}["angels" ]{.class_4939}[is ]{.class_4938}[elohim: gods.]{.class_4937}

[Observe how convenient it is for the Alexandrian Cult to overlook "the Hebrew," or fail to cite it, if it proves anything they don't believe. Remarkable, isn't it, when you have dual authorities? You go to the English to make a liar out of God and to cover up your ignorance, and then, if the English doesn't perform that valuable function, you run to the "Hebrew" or the "Greek." Dual authorities: ]{.class_4938}[no absolute authority. ]{.class_4937}[We knew the ]{.class_4938}["gods"]{.class_4939}[ were a reference to angels, principalities, powers, and]{.class_4938}[ "spiritual wickednesses in high places" ]{.class_4939}[without consulting ]{.class_4938}[one]{.class_4937}[ copy of ]{.class_4938}[one ]{.class_4937}[Greek or Hebrew manuscript extant, or consulting ]{.class_4938}[one ]{.class_4937}[Hebrew or Greek teacher to get the information.]{.class_4938}

[These ]{.class_4938}["gods"]{.class_4939}[ will have to perish from under the heavens (Jer. 10:11) because they did not create the heavens. When the Lord says, ]{.class_4938}["Before me there was no God formed, neither shall there be after me," ]{.class_4939}[(Isa. 43:10), ]{.class_4938}["When there was no strange god among you," ]{.class_4939}[(Isa. 43:12), ]{.class_4938}["Is there a God beside me? yea, there is no God; I know not any" ]{.class_4939}[(Isa. 44:8), He is saying that He is the supreme God of creation. He is the Creator, and not only the Creator of heaven and earth (Isa. 40:12, 42:5, 43:15, 45:9), but the Creator of the ]{.class_4938}["gods,"]{.class_4939}[ who cannot compete with Him. Note: ]{.class_4938}["Being made so much better than the angels\...For unto which of the angels said he at any time\...let all the angels of God worship him\...thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever\...But to which of the angels said he at any time\..." ]{.class_4939}[(Heb. 1:4--14).]{.class_4938}

[Note that throughout God's first person singular descriptions of His own power and authority, that the "fill ins" all deal with ]{.class_4938}[carved idols and graven images ]{.class_4937}[(Isa. 40:19--20, 41:6--9, 42:17, 44:9--20, 45:20, 46:4--8, etc.).]{.class_4938}

[This explains everything for the believer, without one reference to any Greek or Hebrew text. The ]{.class_4938}[image ]{.class_4937}[of Dagon (1 Sam. 5:2--5) represented a "sure enough" fish god who was ]{.class_4938}[here ]{.class_4937}[before the flood. His fish head mitre---adopted by the archbishops of the Roman Catholic Church---indicates the offspring of a male angel and a fish. These monsters and mutations existed, just like on the "late, late show." Dagon was connected with ]{.class_4938}[statues ]{.class_4937}[of him. They were "aids to worship," and that is why there are more than 150 references in the Old Testament to warn against idolatry, idols, carved and graven images, and sacrifices to idols. It is impossible to understand the emphasis the Holy Spirit puts on this (it is the second commandment in the Decalogue, although that commandment was removed by the ]{.class_4938}[Roman Catholic Church]{.class_4937}[)]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4937}[because it turns out to be THE "bone of contention" between God and His chosen people (see 1 Kings 18:18, and Jer. 44:2--30).]{.class_4938}

"[Mary" is ]{.class_4938}[present ]{.class_4937}[in a spiritual sense when one of the "faithful" kneels before her image and twiddles his beads. Of course, the "Mary" that is present is not the Mary of Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John. She is ]{.class_4938}["the queen of heaven" ]{.class_4939}[mentioned in Jeremiah 44:17--19, 25, and she (like the popes) has a number of "aliases." Some of these are the Lorelei, the Elle Woman, Medusa, Venus, Diana, Astarte, Ashtoreth, Ishtar, and Minerva. "Doting" on a statue or idol "summons up" a resident spirit. This explains the most primitive forms of religious belief, called "animism": the teaching that different spirits reside in trees, lakes, rivers, mountains, doors, windows, animals, etc. The Catholics have a monopoly on this today, with a "saint" for hearths, bathrooms, hospitals, cars, and even dog houses and shrimping fleets. ]{.class_4938}[This idolatry draws demons. ]{.class_4937}[It attracts unclean spirits, so eventually a country (Spain, Italy, South Ireland, South America, or India, for example) becomes so infested with demons that God abandons it, ]{.class_4938}[exactly as He did His own nation ]{.class_4937}[(Amos 1--8; Hosea 4--9; Isa. 1; Jer. 1--20). In the Bible, God attributes this to ]{.class_4938}[idolatry: ]{.class_4937}[the use of idols as "an aid to worship."]{.class_4938}

[Modern man takes the credit from God and gives the credit to "Mother Nature"; then he claims she belongs to him. No wonder that, in order to save man, the Saviour has to be crucified between ]{.class_4938}[two thieves.]{.class_4937}

That is what was in Psalm 95:3 and Psalm 96:4--5.

Verse 6 will be done literally in the Millennium, right in front of Jesus Christ, who will be on David's throne (Luke 1:30--33; Isa. 2:1--5; Ps. 2:6).

"[For he is our God; and we are the people of his pasture." ]{.class_4939}[There is no doubt about the first application; he is talking about Israel (note Ps. 100:3, 44:11, 74:1).]{.class_4938}

"[To day if ye will hear his voice" ]{.class_4939}[is attributed to David in Hebrews 4:7. Origen---writing more than one hundred years ]{.class_4938}[after ]{.class_4937}[the completion of the New Testament---decided to manufacture a Greek Old Testament and called it a ]{.class_4938}[Septuagint]{.class_4937}[, so with Hebrews 3 and 4 sitting right on his desk in front of him, he alters ]{.class_4938}["heart" ]{.class_4939}[to "hearts" to make it match Hebrews 3:8, and then alters ]{.class_4938}["work" ]{.class_4939}[to "works" to make it match Hebrews 3:9. (Ninety-nine percent of the commentators to this day think that a B.C. LXX was quoted by the New Testament writers. Monkey shines; stuff and nonsense.) Feeling that he did not pervert the "original Hebrew autographs" enough to be convincing, Origen then transferred the whole phrase (]{.class_4938}["So I sware in my wrath, they shall not enter into my rest"]{.class_4939}[) from Hebrews 3:11 and forced it into Psalm 95:11, thus ]{.class_4938}[perverting ]{.class_4937}[the living words of the living God. The ]{.class_4938}["oracles"]{.class_4939}[ given to the Jews (Rom. 3:2), not the Greeks---said: ]{.class_4938}["Unto whom I sware ]{.class_4939}[(not "So I sware") ]{.class_4938}[that they should not enter" ]{.class_4939}[(not "they shall not enter"). You are to believe that the author of Hebrews is quoting an Old Testament in Greek, written ]{.class_4938}[before ]{.class_4937}[the time of Christ, that is more accurate than the Hebrew text. Kroll bought it, and so did Yates and Motyer. But as far as that goes, so did the chief honchos at Oxford, Yale, Bob Jones, Cambridge, Stuttgart, Chicago Divinity School, Tennessee Temple, Colgate Rochester, Wheaton, BBC, Fuller, Moody, and Louisville Theological Seminary. Intellectual Conservatives, Fundamentalists, and Liberals are some of the most gullible suckers on the face of this earth.]{.class_4938}

"[Harden not your heart, as in the provocation." ]{.class_4939}[All is clear here, for it is explained in the book of Hebrews. However (see lengthy doctrinal comments under Heb. 3 and 4), the Scholar's Union misses everything. The attempts of the Scofield Board of Editors to equate Hebrews 3 and 4 with Church Age salvation are simply tragic. Nothing fits.]{.class_4938}

[The "gospel" preached to the Jews of Numbers 13 and 14 had nothing to do with anyone getting out of hell or into heaven, having their sins forgiven, or getting eternal life (Heb. 4:2). Likewise, no one in this age had to hold anything ]{.class_4938}["stedfast unto the end" ]{.class_4939}[(Heb. 3:14). Those in Hebrews 3 and 4 are told to ]{.class_4938}[labor ]{.class_4937}[to get into God's]{.class_4938}[ "rest"]{.class_4939}[ (Heb. 4:11). You couldn't get further removed from Romans and Galatians if you went back to Leviticus. David is reaching out beyond 2002in this Psalm. Not one commentator, living or dead, could locate the application of the words. Bullinger has an intuition about it, so he says "the rest, thus lost, is to be yet found in the future." But he says this without telling you ]{.class_4938}[what ]{.class_4937}[the rest is or ]{.class_4938}[how ]{.class_4937}[it will be found. Kroll (Liberty University) just throws in the towel again and goes back to the shower room. He can't make any application: hyperventilation.]{.class_4938}

"[The day of temptation" ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["provocation" ]{.class_4939}[refers to ]{.class_4938}[Meribah ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[Massah ]{.class_4937}[as found in Numbers 20:10--13 and Exodus 17:3--6. Israel ]{.class_4938}["proved"]{.class_4939}[ God in those places in that they tested Him out and discovered what He would do. They certainly]{.class_4938}[ "saw"]{.class_4939}[ His work: the parting of the Red Sea, the destruction of Pharaoh, the healing of the waters of Marah, the appearance of quail and manna, etc. The details of verses 10 and 11 are the subject matter of Numbers 13--22. ]{.class_4938}[Prophetically, ]{.class_4937}[all of this means that there will be a repeat (see Ps. 78:2). The Sabbath of Millennial rest (Isa. 14:7), of which the seventh day is a type (Gen. 2:1--2), is the time when Israel is supposed to be in full possession of the land. The conditions for this are described in 1 Kings 4:25. As we have said before, Solomon's reign is THE picture of the Millennial reign of Christ.]{.class_4938}

"[To day,"]{.class_4939}[ in Psalm 95:7, is a reference to ]{.class_4938}[this present generation of Israelites ]{.class_4937}[(Matt. 24:34). They are being told to go enter the land now (see 2 Chron. 36:22--23). Once there, they will be preached to by Elijah and Moses (Rev. 11:3), and they will have to believe on Jesus Christ under this preaching. But then, in addition to ]{.class_4938}["the faith of Jesus" ]{.class_4939}[(Rev. 14:12), they will have to keep ]{.class_4938}["the commandments of God"]{.class_4939}[ (Rev. 14:12, 12:17), and this is what blows the minds of ]{.class_4938}[Baptists ]{.class_4937}[in this decade who are trying to pose as Christian educators and Bible teachers for the Body of Christ. (It just "eats 'em alive!") A Tribulation saint has to ]{.class_4938}["endure unto the end" ]{.class_4939}[(Matt. 24:13), and the period of time he is called to endure is when the]{.class_4938}[ "gospel of the kingdom shall be preached" ]{.class_4939}[(Matt. 24:14), ]{.class_4938}[not ]{.class_4937}[the ]{.class_4938}["gospel of the grace of God"]{.class_4939}[ (Acts 20:24).]{.class_4938}

PSALM 96

96:1 "O sing unto the LORD a new song: sing unto the LORD, all the earth.

2 Sing unto the LORD, bless his name; shew forth his salvation from day to day.

3 Declare his glory among the heathen, his wonders among all people.

4 For the LORD is great, and greatly to be praised: he is to be feared above all gods.

5 For all the gods of the nations are idols: but the LORD made the heavens.

6 Honour and majesty are before him: strength and beauty are in his sanctuary.

7 Give unto the LORD, O ye kindreds of the people, give unto the LORD glory and strength.

8 Give unto the LORD the glory due unto his name: bring an offering, and come into his courts.

9 O worship the LORD in the beauty of holiness: fear before him, all the earth.

10 Say among the heathen that the LORD reigneth: the world also shall be established that it shall not be moved: he shall judge the people righteously.

11 Let the heavens rejoice, and let the earth be glad; let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof.

12 Let the field be joyful, and all that is therein: then shall all the trees of the wood rejoice

13 Before the LORD: for he cometh, for he cometh to judge the earth: he shall judge the world with righteousness, and the people with his truth."

[This is the Millennial Reign of Christ. Poor Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown see enough to know that all of the Psalm is about the Second Advent (as is all of Ps. 97--100), but they will not ]{.class_4938}[locate ]{.class_4937}[the Advent in Revelation 19.]{.class_4938}

[Fausset picks Revelation 11, but does so in order to get the Second Advent situated at Revelation 20---the White Throne Judgment. Fausset believes in a literal return of Christ to Jerusalem to reign, but not for one thousand years beginning in Revelation 20:2. ]{.class_4938}[Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown are half amillennial. ]{.class_4937}[Spurgeon is ]{.class_4938}[postmillennial ]{.class_4937}[(at least in ]{.class_4938}[The Treasury]{.class_4937}[), so he is about to go into gyrations that would make a twist dancer look sedate.]{.class_4938}

Here is "the prince of preachers" making a perfect debacle of Holy Scriptures:

"[His salvation is His glory...the word of the Gospel glorifies Him\...the gospel is a mass of wonders\...a really living, self denying church would solemnly resolve that right speedily they shall all hear thereof\...Tell this out among the heathen\...His Son has assumed the throne\...already we are in His presence\...even now He is near; His advent should, therefore, be the cause of immediate rejoicing\...at this moment He is on the road\...the day shall come when every inhabited rood of ground\...shall contain a CHURCH."]{.class_4938}

[Do you know what THAT was supposed to be an exposition of? Well, bless my soul, you couldn't imagine it, but that was supposed to be an exposition of verses 1, 2, 10, 12, and 13. Absolute insanity. Even the ]{.class_4938}[devotional material ]{.class_4937}[in it is irrelevant. That was postmillennialism, the mark of the ]{.class_4938}[ignorant ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[conceited ]{.class_4937}[Gentile (Rom. 11:25). You say, "Well, back in Spurgeon's day people didn't have the light they have today." Go'wan! The light God gives ]{.class_4938}[today ]{.class_4937}[from the ]{.class_4938}[Authorized Version]{.class_4937}[ is rejected as firmly, constantly, and deliberately by Fundamentalists as Catholic priests rejected 1 Peter 2:5 in Luther's day.]{.class_4938}

[Hengstenberg: ]{.class_4937}["It is a missionary hymn for all ages of the Church." Go stick you head in a bucket three times and pull it out twice.]{.class_4938}

[David Dickson: ]{.class_4937}["It is part of the commission given to ministers of the Gospel\...nothing so wonderful\...as is the salvation of man by Christ\...."]{.class_4938}

[Mollerus: ]{.class_4937}["What he had before called salvation he now names ]{.class_4938}['GLORY' ]{.class_4939}[and afterwards 'WONDERS.' And since this salvation, whereby the human race is redeemed from eternal death and damnation is ]{.class_4938}[glorious ]{.class_4937}[and full of ]{.class_4938}[wonders, ]{.class_4937}[it is therefore worthy of admiration and praise." That is exactly what the Holy Spirit did ]{.class_4938}[not ]{.class_4937}[write nor intend nor record nor have in mind ]{.class_4938}[one time ]{.class_4937}[in the entire Psalm.]{.class_4938}

[Phillip Schaff ]{.class_4937}[(head of the ]{.class_4938}[ASV ]{.class_4937}[committee): "Tell it out among the heathen, that the Lord ]{.class_4938}[reigneth from the tree"]{.class_4937}[ (citing an old Roman Catholic Latin version).]{.class_4938}

[Legh Richmond: ]{.class_4937}["There is a commandment given us of the Lord\...to say to them in fact, ]{.class_4938}['the LORD reigneth']{.class_5033}[." That is exactly what the Lord never commanded you or anyone you know. When Richmond cites a proof text for the "command" he cites Mark 16:15. You couldn't find the New Testament gospel of salvation in Psalm 96 with the Mt. Wilson telescope.]{.class_4938}

[Kroll: ]{.class_4937}[being a Premillennialist, he has to mention "the coming King who will rule from the throne of David in the Millennium," but he doesn't open his mouth about the matter until verse 10. Kroll's proclivity for playing the dumbbell is obvious, for before verse 10 he says that the ]{.class_4938}["offering" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 8 is a Christian in ]{.class_4938}[this age, ]{.class_4937}[offering himself to God (Rom. 12:1). With all of the "advanced light we now have from the Scriptures," Kroll regresses one hundred years and xeroxes Spurgeon: "the ]{.class_4938}[wonder ]{.class_4937}[of the Gospel of God's redeeming grace is not to be published to Israel alone but unto the ends of the earth." Try that in the doctrinal context of Psalm 96, and you will be ]{.class_4938}[killed ]{.class_4937}[(see Zech. 13:3).]{.class_4938}

It is fortunate for Kroll that he has a Bible in his own language, from 1611, to keep him from getting killed in the Millennium.

[Watch this operation, and examine it closely, for it shows you how Biblical truths and Biblical revelations are squelched and stopped (Matt. 23:13) ]{.class_4938}[in every]{.class_4937}[ ]{.class_4938}[generation. ]{.class_4937}[Just as the evolutionary fanatics of 1850--1940 stopped scientific advancement for nearly eighty years by ignoring Mendel's Laws---which were contrary to the religious speculations of Darwin, Lyell, Paley, Haekel, Huxley, et al.---so the Conservative scholars of the nineteenth century and the Fundamental scholars of the twentieth century have kept the Body of Christ chained to A.D. 325 or A.D. 1909 (the Scofield notes). Note that Kroll, a Premillennial Baptist in the late twentieth century, cannot locate ]{.class_4938}["glory" ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["wonders" ]{.class_4939}[in verse 3 any more than ]{.class_4938}[Origen ]{.class_4937}[could in A.D. 200, ]{.class_4938}[Augustine ]{.class_4937}[in A.D. 415, ]{.class_4938}[Luther ]{.class_4937}[in A.D. 1520, ]{.class_4938}[Hort ]{.class_4937}[in A.D. 1880, ]{.class_4938}[Dummelow ]{.class_4937}[in A.D. 1940, or anyone else. Bullinger knows the context is the Millennium, but what the ]{.class_4938}["glory" ]{.class_4939}[and the ]{.class_4938}["wonders" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 3 are, he has no idea: consequently, he makes no comment---not even a marginal note. What is the problem? If the problem is not just rank ]{.class_4938}[unbelief, ]{.class_4937}[what could it be? Nothing in this Psalm was difficult: ]{.class_4938}[not one verse. ]{.class_4937}[Yet look what happens in the late twentieth century after one hundred years of "light" beyond Spurgeon:]{.class_4938}

"[The extent to which a missionary universalism had entered Old Testament thought. All the earth\...are summoned unto the church\...men must realize the Lord is King and that only in Him does the world become stabilized" (Motyer).]{.class_4938}

[What did one hundred years of "light" do for Dr. J. A. Motyer, the Hebrew scholar (1953)? Nothing. ]{.class_4938}[Absolutely nothing. ]{.class_4937}["Lack of light" was not Spurgeon's problem, nor was it Schaff's problem nor Dickson's problem nor Hengstenberg's problem (see above).]{.class_4938}

[Again: "The striking universalism running throughout demonstrates the enlarged outlook of the exiles as they returned from captivity." It does? You mean Ezra (Ezra 4:1--5) and Nehemiah (Neh. 13:20--28) had an "enlarged outlook" toward the nations? "In keeping with Israel's universal mission, all the nations are called upon to praise God\...perhaps this refers to a ceremonial enthronement which may have been a part of the New Year's celebration." What did one hundred years of "light" do for Dr. Yates, the Hebrew scholar who was invited to sit in on the ]{.class_4938}[RSV ]{.class_4937}[committee in 1952? Nothing: ]{.class_4938}[absolutely nothing. ]{.class_4937}[He cannot locate the ]{.class_4938}[city ]{.class_4937}[where the King is; he cannot locate ]{.class_4938}[how ]{.class_4937}[the King comes, ]{.class_4938}[where ]{.class_4937}[He comes, or ]{.class_4938}[when ]{.class_4937}[He comes; he cannot define the ]{.class_4938}["glory" ]{.class_4939}[or the ]{.class_4938}["wonders"]{.class_4939}[; he cannot tell you ]{.class_4938}[what ]{.class_4937}[happens to nature, ]{.class_4938}[when ]{.class_4937}[it happens, or ]{.class_4938}[why ]{.class_4937}[it happens; and he cannot even hazard a guess about ]{.class_4938}[who ]{.class_4937}[brings the ]{.class_4938}["offering"]{.class_4939}[ or when they bring it.]{.class_4938}

[Spurgeon's trouble was not lack of light. ]{.class_4938}[Neither is yours. ]{.class_4937}[Neither is Bob Jones IVs.]{.class_4938}

We have already commented at length on verses 4 and 5 under Psalm 95:3, which see. Psalm 97:7 and 9 deal with the same matters. Micah 4:5 is an interesting comment on Psalm 96:5, for the context is the Millennium.

"[Sing unto the LORD, all the earth." ]{.class_4939}[The King is on the ground (Rev. 20:4). The Maker of the ]{.class_4938}["earth" ]{.class_4939}[is on the earth. Not only are its ]{.class_4938}[inhabitants ]{.class_4937}[to sing, but IT is to sing (see Ps. 96:12 and 148:7--12). Offerings will be brought directly to Him at Jerusalem (vs. 8) where His ]{.class_4938}["courts"]{.class_4939}[ will be; Solomon (2 Chron. 9:1--9) is the type, which is fulfilled in Isaiah 2:3 and 66:23. The ]{.class_4938}["glory"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 3), that is to be declared, is NOT the ]{.class_4938}[gospel of the grace of God, ]{.class_4937}[nor is it the ]{.class_4938}[gospel of the Kingdom of Heaven, ]{.class_4937}[nor what Paul calls ]{.class_4938}["my gospel"]{.class_4939}[ (Rom. 2:16), nor is it ]{.class_4938}["the everlasting gospel" ]{.class_4939}[which is preached in the last half of the Tribulation (Rev. 14:6). The ]{.class_4938}["glory" ]{.class_4939}[and the ]{.class_4938}["wonders" ]{.class_4939}[that are to be declared among the people are that: ]{.class_4938}["the LORD reigneth" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 10), ]{.class_4938}["The LORD made the heavens" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 5), and ]{.class_4938}["he shall judge the people righteously" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 10).]{.class_4938}

[Prophesying is "out" (see Zech. 13:3), and faith as a means of salvation is out, for the earth's inhabitants will all be in the shoes of Thomas Didymus (John 20:29) who walked by sight, ]{.class_4938}[and not by faith ]{.class_4937}[(John 20:25). (Some more Baptist spasms and sensational shocks for the apostate teachers at BBC and Liberty University!) The "constitution" of this Millennial kingdom is given in Matthew 5--7. Salvation "by grace through faith"]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4939}[in the finished work of Christ is ]{.class_4938}[not ]{.class_4937}[in this Constitution. That explains why all unsaved religious, social, political, and educational leaders approve of "The Sermon on the Mount," even if they can't accept everything (see Matt. 5:22, for example) in it.]{.class_4938}

[When He ]{.class_4938}["cometh to judge the earth"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 13) ]{.class_4938}["with righteousness," ]{.class_4939}[the heavens and the earth are to rejoice (vs. 11), the cultivated fields are to rejoice, and the forests and woods are to rejoice (vs. 12). Their ]{.class_4938}[Maker ]{.class_4937}[has showed up. All the problems of the "ecologists" and "environmentalists" (i.e., International Socialists) are solved in twenty four hours, so "Earth Day" with its "New Age" and its "Global Citizens" will be commemorated once every one hundred years as "International FOOL'S Day."]{.class_4938}

[Observe that this tremendous event will take place according to a Book, and it is the words of that Book that the Judge uses when He judges ]{.class_4938}["the world with righteousness" ]{.class_4939}[(John 12:48). Verse 13 says that the world and ]{.class_4938}["the people" ]{.class_4939}[will be judged ]{.class_4938}["with his truth." ]{.class_4939}[Look at what that means in John 17:17 and Psalm 138:2---it means what Paul (Rom. 3:4) and David (Ps. 51:4) said it would mean.]{.class_4938}

[We know what ]{.class_4938}["moved" ]{.class_4939}[meant in Psalm 46:5 and 93:1, because all kinds of furniture are moved around before the Advent (see Ps. 18:7, 46:6, and 68:8). Then the earth cannot be moved because ]{.class_4938}["the righteous" ]{.class_4939}[can no longer be ]{.class_4938}["moved"]{.class_4939}[ (Ps. 55:22). ]{.class_4938}[Israel ]{.class_4937}[can no longer be moved (Ps. 14:7, 53:6), and ]{.class_4938}[Zion ]{.class_4937}[cannot be moved (Ps. 69:35). Look at Hebrews 12:22 for the interpretation, independent of all the commentators. Thus in verse 10, ]{.class_4938}["the world also shall be established that it shall not be moved." ]{.class_4939}[In Psalm 2:11, the kings of the earth were told to ]{.class_4938}["fear before him" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 9). They are to prostrate themselves at the feet of Jesus Christ---not some wine headed "Vicar"---and they are to kiss His feet, as the woman of Luke 7:38 did.]{.class_4938}

The whole Psalm was literal, and none of it was fulfilled before 1995. Let us go from the sublime (the Holy Scripture) to the ridiculous (the Scholar's Union).

[According to Spurgeon, verse 1 is "the essence of the gospel proclamation." Can you imagine that after reading Galatians 1:8--9, 12, and 1 Corinthians 15:1--4? Why, if that was the "gospel" you proclaimed, you would be ]{.class_4938}[cursed. ]{.class_4937}["The very globe itself may well be glad that its Maker and liege Lord has come to His ]{.class_4938}[own, ]{.class_4937}[and the whole race of man may also be glad, since to every willing ]{.class_4938}[subject ]{.class_4937}[Jesus brings untold blessings."]{.class_4938}

"[He came unto his own, and his own received him not" ]{.class_4939}[(John 1:11).]{.class_4938}

The "prince of preachers" was off his rocker.

"[States and kingdoms which stand out upon the world like mountains are utterly dissolved when he decrees their end (vs. 5). Systems as firmly rooted as the hills pass away when He doth but look on them."]{.class_4938}

He is off his rocker, but not far enough off to stop rocking.

"[In the Pentecostal Era and its subsequent age\...heathenism yielded at the glance of Jehovah \[Spain and Italy, perhaps?\]\...and the tyrannies based upon it melted like wax" (vs. 5). Iraq and Saudi Arabia, perhaps? Or was it Germany and Japan?]{.class_4938}

"[In the little world of our nature the presence of Jesus in reigning power is as a fire to consume our lusts and melt (vs. 5) our souls to obedience\...sometimes\...He is concealed with clouds (vs. 2), but we are again assured that He is within us\...."]{.class_4938}

[Fine. Good devotional. Now, will you comment on ]{.class_4938}[the text? ]{.class_4937}[No comment. Spurgeon commenting on Psalm 96 and 97 is like Curtis Hutson or John Rice commenting on Ezekiel 3:20 or Hebrews 10:30--31. Spurgeon "expatiating" on Psalm 99 is like James Combs or Bob Jones IV expounding Hebrews 6:1--6 or Revelation 22:14. But this time, the references are so obviously to the Second Advent that even Kroll, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown can find the application.]{.class_4938}

[Yates plays "Eusebius" for his readers (Eusebius, the church historian, took the "median way" between Arius and Athanasius at the Council of Nicea, A.D. 325). He posits, "the theocratic principle of God's kingship is acclaimed," which in itself---divorced from Isaiah 2; Joel 2; Isaiah 40; Revelation 19; Zechariah 14; Isaiah 11; etc.---doesn't mean anything. Instead of locating the text, Yates gets by with "All those\...are called to rejoice in the TRUTH of this eschatological dominion." Which ]{.class_4938}[is what? ]{.class_4937}[Which comes ]{.class_4938}[when? ]{.class_4937}[What is the truth about this dominion? What is the dominion like? "Eschatological" simply means "future." Yate's position is amillennial, postmillennial, and premillennial at the same time, because no truth was given about anything "eschatological."]{.class_4938}

Yate's approach is so typical of modern Christian education, and modern "Bible" teachers, that it could be set up as a requirement for the head of nearly every "Bible department" in the United States.

In the Millennium, there will be disobedient nations who will not obey the instructions of Psalm 96:7 and 9, so at the end of the Millennium, these people are "dupes" for Satan (see Rev. 20:8). The intimation is that, just as mankind made "gods" out of the fallen angels of pre Deluge days, so some survivors of the Tribulation will make "gods" out of angels who show up in the Tribulation with Satan (see Rev. 12:7--9).

[Devotionally, ]{.class_4937}[there are all kinds of things that may be said about the Psalm.]{.class_4938}

[1. All kings have wanted what the King of kings will have (vss. 6--9): ]{.class_4938}["honour\...majesty\...strength\...beauty\...glory\...worship." "Glory" ]{.class_4939}[is certainly God's ]{.class_4938}["due,"]{.class_4939}[ and 1 Corinthians 6:20 commands it to be paid (vs. 8). Holiness is said to be beautiful (vs. 9). ]{.class_4938}["Holiness" ]{.class_4939}[is beautiful because (a) it enables God to manifest Himself, (b) it proves that there is more to life than living for self, (c) it maintains fellowship with God, (d) it shows that man can do superhuman things by God's grace and help, (e) it pleases God.]{.class_4938}

"[A new song" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 1) could be a song that Stuart Hamblin wrote after he got saved or a song that Red Harper sang after he got converted or a song that Larry Richardson (world champion banjo player---Grand Ole Opry) sang after he received the Lord as Saviour. Russ Columbo, Rudy Valee, Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra, Nat "King" Cole, Helen Forrest, Peggy Lee, Helen O'Connell, Ray and Bob Eberle, Jo Stafford, Doris Day, Jussi Bjoerling, Perry Como, Mario Lanza, Bruce Springsteen, and all the rest, never learned how to sing it; and when Elvis Presley, Red Foley, Tex Ritter, Eddy Arnold, and Patti Page tried to sing it, it sounded like Caiaphas and Ananias serenading the Temple. Bill Manns used to sing as an entertainer with Abbot and Costello and Bob Hope. He was converted after seeing six of his crew (airmen) die in an exploding aircraft. Joe E. Brown gave him a ticket to go hear Jeanette McDonald sing a concert in the Hollywood Bowl; Jeanette was a Christian. Being informed, by Joe, that Manns was in the audience, she departed from her scheduled program and sang ]{.class_4938}[Blessed Assurance. ]{.class_4937}[Manns got the message and became a Christian soloist, singing for evangelists and pastors the rest of his life. After fifteen years of singing ]{.class_4938}["a new song," ]{.class_4939}[he got a telegram from Miss McDonald that said, "Stay close to God, and keep singing His praises" (vs. 4).]{.class_4938}

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ1742.xhtml}

PSALM 97

97:1 "The LORD reigneth; let the earth rejoice; let the multitude of isles be glad thereof.

2 Clouds and darkness are round about him: righteousness and judgment are the habitation of his throne.

3 A fire goeth before him, and burneth up his enemies round about.

4 His lightnings enlightened the world: the earth saw, and trembled.

5 The hills melted like wax at the presence of the LORD, at the presence of the Lord of the whole earth.

6 The heavens declare his righteousness, and all the people see his glory.

7 Confounded be all they that serve graven images, that boast themselves of idols: worship him, all ye gods."

[All is clear. Verse 1 is Christ on His throne in Jerusalem ]{.class_4938}["the throne of the LORD" ]{.class_4939}[(Jer. 3:17), ]{.class_4938}["the throne of thy glory" ]{.class_4939}[(Jer. 14:21). This throne moves from heaven to earth (Matt. 6:10), according to Daniel's prophesy (Dan. 2:44), so the things that accompany the Advent (vss. 2--5) are described. They were described like this in Deuteronomy 32:22; 2 Samuel 22:8--11; Joel 2--3; Nahum 1:5; Psalm 68:7--17, and a dozen other places. Charles Haddon Spurgeon couldn't find ]{.class_4938}[one reference. ]{.class_4937}[He was just as ignorant of prophesy, in 1880, as the faculty and staff of BBC are in 1990.]{.class_4938}

[The main theme of the Holy Bible is "the kingdom," and its chief character is "the King of kings." It follows therefore, as surely as sunset follows sunrise, that ]{.class_4938}[any ]{.class_4937}[Bible teacher ought to be better acquainted with that subject than any other subject in the entire Book. Any Bible teacher should be able to give you more details about the setting up of God's literal kingdom on earth (Matt. 25:31), than he could give you on the church, water baptism, the Lord's Supper, the plan of salvation, or Christian living. ]{.class_4938}["A false balance is abomination to the Lord ]{.class_4939}[(Prov. 11:1).]{.class_4938}

[What in the world happened to Spurgeon when he saw ]{.class_4938}["and]{.class_4939}[ ]{.class_4938}[all the people see his glory" ]{.class_4939}[in verse 6? Did he go to Isaiah 40:5 and glue it to Isaiah 40:6, without seeing the "right division" between Isaiah 40:2 and Isaiah 40:3? ]{.class_4938}[He had a paragraph mark in verse 3 to help him ]{.class_4937}[out. Did Spurgeon really believe that Jerusalem was through with ]{.class_4938}["warfare"]{.class_4939}[ (Isa. 40:2) ]{.class_4938}[after John the Baptist showed up ]{.class_4937}[(Isa. 40:3)? Did he really think that ]{.class_4938}[unbelieving Jews ]{.class_4937}[since the time of Christ were ]{.class_4938}["pardoned" ]{.class_4939}[after rejecting John's message (Isa. 40:6)? Evidently, for he exegetes Psalm 97:6 as though God's ]{.class_4938}["glory" ]{.class_4939}[was seen, in that the Gospel was "glorious" and became "well known."]{.class_4938}

Spurgeon must have chosen Origen for a mentor: this is the Alexandrian, allegorical exegesis.

[There is not ]{.class_4938}[one verse ]{.class_4937}[in the first six verses of Psalm 97 that are remotely connected with ]{.class_4938}[anything ]{.class_4937}[that has taken place before A.D. 1995.]{.class_4938}

[The ]{.class_4938}["clouds and darkness" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 2 are not just the clouds and darkness of Sinai back in 1500 B.C. They are Mt. Sinai again, after 1995 (see Deut. 33:2; Hab. 3:3--6; and Ps. 68), with the advent of the ]{.class_4938}["day of the LORD"]{.class_4939}[ (Ezek. 30:3). Note: ]{.class_4938}["A day of darkness and of gloominess, a day of clouds and of thick darkness" ]{.class_4939}[(Joel 2:2). How is it that Peter S. Ruckman---who never professed to be a scholar ]{.class_4938}[of any sort---]{.class_4937}[can find these Scriptural references---]{.class_4938}[which interpret the Scriptures in the light of the Scriptures]{.class_4937}[---while five thousand accredited, qualified, recognized Greek and Hebrew professors and exegetes cannot find any of them? I'm a non union "scab."]{.class_4938}

[Kroll, although he is premillennial, comes completely to pieces at verse 2. Without any knowledge of Joel 2 or Amos 5:18, 20 (]{.class_4938}["The day of the LORD is darkness, and not light\...the day of the LORD be darkness, and not light? even very dark, and no brightness in it?"]{.class_4939}[), he says that the ]{.class_4938}["clouds and darkness" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 2 is ]{.class_4938}[the Millennial reign ]{.class_4937}[of Christ! And you understand this is done with the Holy Spirit right in front of his face saying that, ]{.class_4938}["The Gentiles shall come to thy light, and kings to the brightness of thy rising" ]{.class_4939}[(Isa. 60:3), and ]{.class_4938}["The LORD shall be unto thee an everlasting light" ]{.class_4939}[(Isa. 60:19), and ]{.class_4938}["They shall walk, O LORD, in the light of thy countenance" ]{.class_4939}[(Ps. 89:15), and ]{.class_4938}["Arise, shine; for thy light is come" (Isa]{.class_4939}[. 60:1).]{.class_4938}

[Do you know how Liberty University justifies turning the Millennial Reign of Christ into ]{.class_4938}["clouds and darkness"]{.class_4939}[ (]{.class_4938}[Commentary,]{.class_4937}[ p. 1109)? "God's essential Deity must forever be shrouded from man."]{.class_4938}

Blow it out your stacking swivel.

[Don't count on that kind of pious drivel to fool those of us who believe the Book. ]{.class_4938}[That is nothing but hypocritical claptrap. ]{.class_4937}[That is "stocking filler" at Christmas time to cover up the fact that you couldn't buy any presents; in short, it is a sack of peanuts, with the emphasis on the "nuts."]{.class_4938}

[You see, the problem is real. God intended the Christians of A.D. 400 to build on the truths that their predecessors had learned from the Scriptures (2 Tim. 2:2). He intended for the Christians of A.D. 1000 not only to keep and treasure the truths that their forbearers had delivered to them, but to search the Scriptures (that is a commandment, not a suggestion) and study the Scriptures (that is a commandment found in one English version and ]{.class_4938}[only one ]{.class_4937}[English version) for more truth. The converts of Martin Luther were to believe the Scriptural truths that God had revealed to Martin, and likewise Calvin's converts; but at no time was any Christian to quit studying and searching the Scriptures for ]{.class_4938}[more truth. ]{.class_4937}[Whoever it was that caused the Body of Christ to doubt the Scriptures, low rate the Scriptures, ignore the Scriptures, or abandon the Scriptures was operating under the power and authority of Satan (Gen. 3:1).]{.class_4938}

[Advanced revelations ]{.class_4937}[have been given from the Scriptures, by the Author, ever since they were written. This means that "historic positions," from time to time, must be brought into line with Scripture as revelation advances. The "establishment" (professors, educators, and teachers in the Scholar's Union) will not do this. ]{.class_4938}[They want to maintain a Nicolaitan control over the Body of Christ. ]{.class_4937}[Each Alexandrian will stop at a certain point and then demand that you stop with him or be labeled as a "heretic." His intellectual egotism demands that you remain ]{.class_4938}[as stupid as he is. ]{.class_4937}[It is a sin for you to get ahead of him or find out anything that he doesn't know, for in his apostate and depraved imagination, he fancies that no one could know more than he knows because he has been ]{.class_4938}[educated. ]{.class_4937}[This is the key to understanding apostasy in any age.]{.class_4938}

[Kroll, and all his kind, are stupid for ]{.class_4938}[two ]{.class_4937}[reasons:]{.class_4938}

1\. They have rejected the Holy Bible as their final authority and have replaced it with the opinions and conjectures of scholars.

2\. They had a lobotomy performed on them by the Holy Spirit the first day they messed with His Book. This is what makes them stupid. It is not a lack of formal education or of a knowledge "of the original languages." The problem is much deeper.

Verse 1 is self explanatory (see Ps. 96:11).

[Verse 2 has been explained. The ]{.class_4938}["righteousness and judgment" ]{.class_4939}[were discussed under Psalms 33:5 and 94:15.]{.class_4938}

Verse 3 is Malachi 4:1--4 and 2 Thessalonians 1:7--9.

Verses 4 and 5 are more of the same.

[Verse 6 is when ]{.class_4938}["every eye shall see him" ]{.class_4939}[(Rev. 1:7) and God's righteousness (see Isa. 1:27, 10:22 specifically) is revealed from ]{.class_4938}["the heavens" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 6, Matt. 24:30), not on earth as at the First Advent (Rom. 10:1--4).]{.class_4938}

[There wasn't one difficult or obscure verse in the entire passage. Kroll was unhorsed at verse 2 because the second clause said ]{.class_4938}["habitation of his throne." ]{.class_4939}[He forgot that a comma can indicate a gap of two thousand years (see Gen. 49:11, 49:24, and Isa. 61:2).
