In this case, a colon stood for a separation of less than one year. Kroll couldn't "rightly divide" because he had no access to ]{.class_4938}["the scripture of truth" ]{.class_4939}[(Dan. 10:21). All he had was "the original Hebrew and Greek" or perhaps some "plenary, verbally inspired, original, infallible autographs."]{.class_4938}

97:8 "Zion heard, and was glad; and the daughters of Judah rejoiced because of thy judgments, O LORD.

9 For thou, LORD, art high above all the earth: thou art exalted far above all gods.

10 Ye that love the LORD, hate evil: he preserveth the souls of his saints; he delivereth them out of the hand of the wicked.

11 Light is sown for the righteous, and gladness for the upright in heart.

12 Rejoice in the LORD, ye righteous; and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness."

[At verse 9, Spurgeon again exhibits his great dexterity at slipping, masterfully, between ]{.class_4938}[three ]{.class_4937}[prophetic positions (a, pre, and postmillennial) to avoid criticism. After saying that the "literal Zion, so long forsaken" will joy in salvation, he says that this will be comparable to the "good days" coming again, like they did in Acts 2, "when the apostles first dwelt in Jerusalem." ]{.class_4938}[Christ was not on earth when the apostles "first dwelt in Jerusalem," and they were all from Galilee, before they came to Jerusalem, when Christ was on earth. ]{.class_4937}[Christ was not on David's throne at ]{.class_4938}[any time ]{.class_4937}[in Jerusalem, before or ]{.class_4938}[after ]{.class_4937}[the apostles were in either place. The ]{.class_4938}["daughters of Judah," ]{.class_4939}[who are "rejoicing," are not rejoicing in Christ's literal reign on David's throne at all (see Luke 1:30--33 and Jer. 3:17); in Spurgeon, they are rejoicing because of the "widespread reign of Jehovah\...through ]{.class_4938}[the gospel ]{.class_4937}[of His dear Son\..." (op cit., p. 197). Which, of course, is demented claptrap. That is the position of the National Council of Churches and the Roman Catholic Church. It is called "postmillennialism" and is the religious counterpart of Darwin's ]{.class_4938}[Origin of the Species ]{.class_4937}[and "uniformitarian geology." Rubbish.]{.class_4938}

[The master of this clumsy, irreverent, and blasphemous treatment of the Second Advent was the first "original" Roman Catholic---Aurelious Augustine (A.D. 354--430), whom Spurgeon cites (p. 203) as saying that the ]{.class_4938}["fire"]{.class_4939}[ going before God (vs. 3) was the "kindling blaze of the Holy Ghost" sent down at Pentecost, which was to "burn freely throughout the world for the destruction of obstinate unbelievers and the purifying of those who gladly receive the word." In ]{.class_4938}[application, ]{.class_4937}[this meant ]{.class_4938}[burning Christians at the stake ]{.class_4937}[if they didn't agree with Calvin's five point system, and burning Donatists at the stake, since they wouldn't baptize babies. Augustine's Second Advent is the "fire" of Ezekiel 39:6 coming to "every faithful soul as it kindles a longing for Him, etc."]{.class_4938}

[In short: blasphemous, insane, pagan ]{.class_4938}[nonsense.]{.class_4937}

[Spurgeon's ]{.class_4938}[Treasury ]{.class_4937}[tells you the ]{.class_4938}["clouds" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 2 are not literal---although ]{.class_4938}["he cometh with clouds"]{.class_4939}[---but simply mean that, in the Church Age, God governs "mysteriously." Rubbish. Pagan rubbish. Again, Spurgeon reprints the incoherent blathering of Martin Geier, who says that the ]{.class_4938}["lightnings" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 4 is a reference to the "rapid increase of the Kingdom of God," and according to A. R. Fausset, the "melting hills" of verse 5 are the "heights of man's self-exalting pride of intellect, wealth, and power." More rubbish for the ]{.class_4938}[dumpster. ]{.class_4937}[Gross out.]{.class_4938}

[Spurgeon himself gives the grossest exposition of the entire passage, for he says, back in verses 1--4, that the Christ is now reigning and that THIS is the essential "essence of the Gospel proclamation"---which, of course, it is NOT (see 2 Cor. 5:20--21 and 1 Cor. 15:1--4). After connecting the fire of verse 3 with the "coming of Christ"---amillennial at the White Throne---Spurgeon says, "even ]{.class_4938}[now ]{.class_4937}[where the gospel is preached in faith\...it burns its own way\...it is the Lord that reigneth and because of it let the earth rejoice\...." It is "States, kingdoms," and "systems" that "burn up" in the Church Age (p. 196) when "Jehovah Jesus" glances at them. "Christ in us" is concealed by clouds (ibid), so we doubt His reigning presence INSIDE of us (ibid), but the warmth of His "grace" is like "fire melting wax" (ibid).]{.class_4938}

Bunko.

Charles Haddon Spurgeon couldn't get one verse right in the first six verses and blew it again in verse 8.

[We had a nut come to PBI, one time, who made a living overcharging people for Spurgeon's books. His name was Bob Ross (Pasadena, Texas). This Five Point, TULIP, Hyper-Calvinist used to be an associate editor for ]{.class_4938}[The Baptist Examiner ]{.class_4937}[(John Gilpin, Ashland, Kentucky). When we pointed out how deficient Spurgeon was on Eschatology, this nut had a spasm and began to send out letters all over the country saying, "Ruckman had met his Waterloo," Ruckman was a "cowardly jackass," Ruckman was a "Jesuit plant" teaching Roman Catholicism. He (Bob Ross) would come to Pensacola and drop an "A Bomb" on Ruckman's "cowardly street preachers" who were "cloned wimps," and he \[Bob Ross\] had more respect for Catholic priests who were child molesters than "Ruckman." He said some other things I would not print, and, honey, if ]{.class_4938}[I]{.class_4937}[ wouldn't print it, it must be pretty rough!]{.class_4938}

[Well, we had hit this TULIP sniffer so hard in the pocket book---see above---he had to do something. So he agreed to come to PBI and conduct an "Open Bible Forum"; something which I had been doing for about thirty eight years. We invited him to come on April Fool's Day. We picked the right day. If Spurgeon couldn't have found a better advertising agent than Bob Ross to promote his books, he should ask God for permission to come back down from Glory and hire Gorbachev to do the job. We made a video of this farce, and you can obtain it. It is called "A ]{.class_4938}[Predestinated Failure,"]{.class_4937}[ by Bob Ross. When asked for one verse of Scripture to prove about twenty nine things he believed, Ross failed twenty three times out of twenty nine, and three of the other six times he could not turn to the verse he ]{.class_4938}[quoted. ]{.class_4937}[At PBI, that is a grade of ]{.class_4938}[20:]{.class_4937}[ 49 below flunking.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 8 is a reference to Psalm 48:11, which anyone could tell, at one glance at the verses. In that Psalm, ]{.class_4938}["Zion" ]{.class_4939}[has literal walls that you can walk around (48:12). It is a ]{.class_4938}[literal city ]{.class_4937}[established forever (48:8), and God is in it (48:3). The New Testament Gospel is as far removed from either passage as it is from Psalm 68 or Psalm 80. In the ]{.class_4938}[Song of Solomon, ]{.class_4937}[the ]{.class_4938}["daughters of Jerusalem"]{.class_4939}[ (1:5) are talking to the Bride of Christ (the Church). They are NOT in it (see Song of Sol. 2:7). In Isaiah 4:3, these same daughters get their ]{.class_4938}["filth" ]{.class_4939}[washed away at the END of the Tribulation (Isa. 4:1--4), and the Lord is present IN THE TEMPLE at that time (vs. 5). These same ]{.class_4938}["daughters" ]{.class_4939}[are told by Jesus Christ Himself that they had better start weeping for themselves at the ]{.class_4938}[beginning ]{.class_4937}[of the Church Age, because they are going to get it rougher at the ]{.class_4938}[end ]{.class_4937}[of this Age (Luke 23:29).]{.class_4938}

John Calvin, Charles Haddon Spurgeon, and Aurelious Augustine lost all the references in both Testaments.

[They didn't "disagree with Ruckman," who attacks "everyone who doesn't agree with him," etc. ]{.class_4938}[They attacked the Holy Bible and the Author of the Holy Bible, ]{.class_4937}[so God converted their "scholarship" into third grade "finger paintings."]{.class_4938}

[Verse 9 is a general statement, which could apply to any age, so the "spiritualizers" have no trouble with it. The part with which they had trouble they put in submission to ]{.class_4938}[this ]{.class_4937}[verse, and by the old gimmick of "contexting the truth out of sight," they used ONE verse to destroy eight verses (see comments on Ps. 12).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 10 is devotional and transparent. If you ]{.class_4938}[love, ]{.class_4937}[you must ]{.class_4938}[hate; ]{.class_4937}[you cannot love without hating (see 139:21 and 101:3). Verse 10 could be applied, spiritually, to the eternal security of the believer in this age (Rom. 8:37--39), but directly to Tribulation saints who are delivered from Satan (see Joel 2:32).]{.class_4938}

[The things that any child of God should HATE in this dispensation (vs. 10) are: 1. Evil ]{.class_4938}[writings ]{.class_4937}[(]{.class_4938}[Washington Post, New York Times, Life, Time, Newsweek, Detroit Free Press, ]{.class_4937}[etc.). 2. ]{.class_4938}[Evil pictures ]{.class_4937}[(CBS, NBC, ABC, CNN, National Art Gallery, ]{.class_4938}[Playboy, Hustler, ]{.class_4937}[etc.). 3. ]{.class_4938}[Evil speaking ]{.class_4937}[(Richard Pryor, Eddie Murphy, Madonna, Pope John Paul II, District Court Judges, daily and nightly newscasts by women "reporters," etc. 4. ]{.class_4938}[Evil thinking ]{.class_4937}[(pro gun control, pro integration, Affirmative Action, Socialism, Communism, Fascism, Nazism, Catholicism, Humanism, etc.).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 11 shows 2 Corinthians in application, where it touches the revelation of truth. Light rejected becomes lightning (2 Thess. 2:12), and light accepted (John 3:21) brings more light, if we are to believe Galatians 6:7. The ]{.class_4938}["upright in heart" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 11), who are literally ]{.class_4938}["righteous," ]{.class_4939}[in the Old Testament (see Ps. 24:3--6 and comments), have someone to do their "sowing for them" while they are sleeping, and it is not the gentleman of Matthew 13:25. It is the Lord. He plants a crop for you when you tend to his business. The fruit is LIGHT and GLADNESS. Again, ]{.class_4938}[the "righteous" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 12 is a reference, historically, to Jewish saints under the law. A Christian can rejoice "in" the Lord, in a literal sense, for he is literally]{.class_4938}[ "in"]{.class_4939}[ the Lord (see Eph. 1:1, 10).]{.class_4938}

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ1797.xhtml}

PSALM 98

98:1 "O sing unto the LORD a new song; for he hath done marvellous things: his right hand, and his holy arm, hath gotten him the victory.

2 The LORD hath made known his salvation: his righteousness hath he openly shewed in the sight of the heathen.

3 He hath remembered his mercy and his truth toward the house of Israel: all the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.

4 Make a joyful noise unto the LORD, all the earth: make a loud noise, and rejoice, and sing praise.

5 Sing unto the LORD with the harp; with the harp, and the voice of a psalm.

6 With trumpets and sound of cornet make a joyful noise before the LORD, the King.

7 Let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.

8 Let the floods clap their hands: let the hills be joyful together

9 Before the LORD; for he cometh to judge the earth: with righteousness shall he judge the world, and the people with equity."

[There is no let up in the subject matter, ]{.class_4938}[doctrinally; ]{.class_4937}[this is the Millennial Reign of the Lord Jesus Christ. We know, of course, what Spurgeon and the Calvinists are going to do with it: they are going to massacre it. They already told us that the ]{.class_4938}["fire"]{.class_4939}[ that went before Christ in Psalm 97:3 was "the kindling blaze of the Holy Ghost," that the ]{.class_4938}["lightnings" ]{.class_4939}[(Ps. 97:4) were "the rapid increase of the Kingdom of God," that the ]{.class_4938}["clouds and darkness" ]{.class_4939}[(Ps. 97:2) were the way God governs the world "mysteriously," and that all the power of men and all the thrones of men were "shattered to dust" back in 1000 B.C. Origen, Clement, Pantaneus, Augustine, and Philo couldn't have handled the Psalm any worse. These Alexandrians (who founded the Alexandrian Cult) laid down the format for Christian scholars to follow in the next fifteen centuries: (a) if you can't understand it or don't like it, CHANGE it (]{.class_4938}[ASV, RSV, NASV, NIV, NKJV, RSV ]{.class_4937}[in 2 Tim. 2:15; 2 Cor. 2:17; Rom. 1:18, 25; 1 Tim. 6:5, 10, etc.); (b) always bring the reader or student down to your level of infidelity and ignorance on the grounds that he can't understand it because he can't be any smarter than you. That is the quintessence of "Bible study" at Bob Jones University, Moody, Fuller, BBC, Grace and Louisville seminaries, Tennessee Temple, Colgate Rochester, Harvard, Yale, Liberty University, and Oxford. Keep the young man in ignorance as long as possible so he will think that Chris]{.class_4938}[tian scholarship]{.class_4937}[ is the final authority in "all matters of faith and practise." It has been going on since A.D. 200 (Origen at Alexandria, Egypt).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 1. Self explanatory: it matches Psalm 96:1 and Psalm 31:21. The ]{.class_4938}["right hand" ]{.class_4939}[is the one we discussed in Psalm 44:3. ]{.class_4938}["His holy arm" ]{.class_4939}[is also a reference to the Lord Jesus, for the ]{.class_4938}["hand" ]{.class_4939}[is on the ]{.class_4938}["arm" ]{.class_4939}[(see Ps. 44:3 and 77:15). Devotionally, note that a Christian can sing a song ]{.class_4938}["unto the LORD." ]{.class_4939}[There doesn't have to be anyone around to hear it, and even in a congregation it can be sung for the Lord's benefit instead of the people around you.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 2. Nothing could be worded more plainly. This is Isaiah 40:5, Revelation 1:7, and Zechariah 12:10 fulfilled to the letter. ]{.class_4938}["His righteousness" ]{.class_4939}[is Jesus Christ (Rom. 10:1--4, 1 Cor. 1:30), who never showed Himself ]{.class_4938}["openly\...in the sight of the heathen" ]{.class_4939}[one time in 1960 years. Not only have ]{.class_4938}["all the ends of the earth" ]{.class_4939}[never seen the salvation of God, Israel has not seen it either. Zechariah's prayer of victory (Luke 1:68--74) was put off for nineteen centuries. Nevertheless, watch the blockheads go:]{.class_4938}

[We now have a new song "because ]{.class_4938}[he has come, ]{.class_4937}[and seen and conquered" (]{.class_4938}[Treasury]{.class_4937}[, p. 210). We are told the Lord made known (past tense) His salvation by the coming of Jesus and the "outpouring of the Holy Ghost." Then we are told that since the Jews who got saved in the book of Acts were the "true Israel," that verse 3 has been fulfilled. It is "the covenant of grace" to the Christian in the Church that is referred to in verse 3. Absolute disregard for the words of God. Absolute rejection of the words of God, exactly as Curtis Hutson, John R. Rice, James Combs, Stewart Custer, Bob Jones IV, or Spiros Zodhiates would reject the words found in Hebrews 8:8--12 in any manuscript.]{.class_4938}

[What you have to realize is that these pious, Bible rejecting Nicolaitans would alter ]{.class_4938}[every word]{.class_4937}[ of what an "original autograph" said if they had access to it, and they didn't ]{.class_4938}[agree ]{.class_4937}[with it. Spurgeon's old Adamic nature was just like the Adamic nature in Torrey, Broadus, Scarborough, Norris, Criswell, Truett, Falwell, and Rice. With the words of the Holy Spirit right in front of him (]{.class_4938}["the house of Israel," ]{.class_4939}[vs. 3), he pretended it said "the true Israel." Every Catholic and Campbellite in the United States does the same thing with Hebrews 8:8--12, and every faculty member at PCC, Bob Jones, Santa Rosa, Tennessee Temple, BBC, and Liberty University follows them. In Hebrews 9 it is even stronger, for not only is the ]{.class_4938}["house of Israel" ]{.class_4939}[mentioned but also ]{.class_4938}["the house of Judah." ]{.class_4939}[Nonetheless, no modern commentator will take either expression as the word and words of God (see ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Hebrews, ]{.class_4937}[1989).]{.class_4938}

[What Spurgeon and his buddies are trying to tell you is that two thousand promises from the Old Testament, which are to be fulfilled ]{.class_4938}[in the future, ]{.class_4937}[were fulfilled ]{.class_4938}[1900 years ago. ]{.class_4937}[What little learning Spurgeon had in the "original Greek" and the "original Hebrew" shot his britches so full of infidelity that he simply threw two thirds of the Old Testament out the window. He would have done better to believe what he professed about the ]{.class_4938}[King James ]{.class_4937}[text when he was in his right mind.]{.class_4938}

[Verses 4--8 are going to take place ]{.class_4938}[literally. ]{.class_4937}[Nature will rejoice, and its elements will physically respond to Christ's presence, for He is their Creator (Col. 1:15--18). His coming liberates them from ]{.class_4938}["the bondage of corruption"]{.class_4939}[ (Rom.]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4939}[8:21). The commentators were so anxious to make everything figurative they forgot that Paul said ]{.class_4938}["the whole creation groaneth and travaileth in pain together" ]{.class_4939}[(Rom. 8:22). It is not just men and animals who are waiting for the]{.class_4938}[ "Deliverer"]{.class_4939}[ to come ]{.class_4938}["out of Sion" ]{.class_4939}[(Rom. 11:26--27) to lift the curse of Genesis 3:15--20. The flowers and bushes are waiting; the trees and rocks are waiting; the hills and mountains are waiting; the rivers and ponds are waiting; the seas and oceans are waiting; and the canyons, deserts, and polar caps are waiting. According to Spurgeon, they all got what they were waiting for, and all of them were "liberated" more than 1,900 years ago! He couldn't understand the word ]{.class_4938}["regeneration" ]{.class_4939}[as applied to the earth by Jesus Christ in Matthew 19:28. There isn't the slightest hint that the earth will be "regenerated" till the Second Advent. Simon Peter knew this as far back as A.D. 33. Where did Charlie go to school?]{.class_4938}

[It is the refusal to believe the words being studied that get the Conservative and Fundamental scholars in a "pickle." For example; in order to prove that verses 2 and 3 were fulfilled ]{.class_4938}[before ]{.class_4937}[the destruction of Jerusalem in A.D. 70, Adam Clarke lays the passage alongside Luke 1:49--54. But as far as that goes, why not lay it alongside Hannah (more than one thousand years before ]{.class_4938}[Mary ]{.class_4937}[was born) in 1 Samuel 2:4--9 and make that all past, when not one word of it has taken place yet?]{.class_4938}

[Clarke is more rabid than Spurgeon. He insists that since Mary's "song" was fulfilled before Acts 7 that it must be "ultimately understood" as the whole world getting converted to Christ by the proclamation of the gospel (]{.class_4938}[Treasury,]{.class_4937}[ p. 215). What a ghastly Catholic performance. What a hellish papal example of "recognized, qualified scholarship" from "godly" men. What God Almighty said to Abraham (see Mary in Luke 1:55) has not been totally fulfilled since 1900 B.C. Abraham hasn't received the land yet. God's "help" to ]{.class_4938}["his servant Israel," ]{.class_4939}[following Mary's "song" was the ]{.class_4938}[destruction of Jerusalem ]{.class_4937}[(A.D. 70), the end of Israel as a nation until 1949, and the ]{.class_4938}[wrath of God ]{.class_4937}[coming upon them ]{.class_4938}["to the uttermost" ]{.class_4939}[(1 Thess. 2:16). If that is "help," you can have it; I don't want it, and neither does any other sane man on the face of this earth. When Adam Clarke messed with the Book, God messed with his mind.]{.class_4938}

[Everywhere we encounter this phenomenon. It explains why the Bible is still a closed Book to thousands of saved people (see ]{.class_4938}[The Unknown Bible, ]{.class_4937}[1987). Christian scholars keep it shut.]{.class_4938}

[It is ]{.class_4938}["the King" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 6) who shows up in the open (]{.class_4938}["openly"]{.class_4939}[ vs. 2) to restore Eden, and this King appears ]{.class_4938}["in the sight of the heathen." ]{.class_4939}[Jesus Christ did not appear to any unsaved Jew or Gentile ]{.class_4938}[after ]{.class_4937}[His appearance to Paul (A.D. 68). Martin Luther threw an ink well at an apparition that professed to be Him. As if there could have been one doubt about the literalness of these matters, the following appears: ]{.class_4938}["He cometh to judge the earth: with righteousness shall he judge the world" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 9). That is what Jesus Christ said that He ]{.class_4938}[didn't come to do ]{.class_4937}[at His First Coming (John 3:17). He said judgment would take place later (John 5:28--29). He came to save the world the first time (John 3:17; Matt. 11:28--30; 1 Tim. 2:3--4), ]{.class_4938}[not ]{.class_4937}[to ]{.class_4938}[judge ]{.class_4937}[it. James says the Judge is ready to come (James 5:9), but this is long after Christ has gone back to glory. If He has to get up (see Ps. 3:7, 7:6, 9:19) to judge someone, He will judge ]{.class_4938}["the man of the earth" ]{.class_4939}[(see Ps. 10:18 and comments). When He comes for this judgment, He must take the government ]{.class_4938}["upon his shoulder" ]{.class_4939}[(Isa. 9:6), for Psalm 67:4 says He will ]{.class_4938}["govern the nations upon the earth" ]{.class_4939}[(see Rev. 11:15).]{.class_4938}

[To say that God is on the earth now, ]{.class_4938}[governing ]{.class_4937}[the White House, the British Parliament, the UN, Iraq, and Israel, is to encourage impeachment proceedings against Him. Any "governor" who is a ruler, actually governing what has taken place since A.D. 70, needs to be ]{.class_4938}[replaced. ]{.class_4937}[The Calvinists, as always, have confused the permissive will of God with the directive will of God. God will ordain a power (Rom. 13:1--6) like Nebuchadnezzar or like Saul (Jer. 27:6, 1 Sam. 8:5--18), but if God Himself is going to govern, He will do it according to the absolute qualifications He laid down in His own Scriptures (2 Sam. 23:3). God would not govern Germany the way it was governed between 1933 and 1945, and God has NEVER judged this earth for its conduct during the last two thousand years. He has let nations and individuals reap what they have sown. He has allowed Satan to have access to men and nations at times and He has intervened on rare occasions to save an individual or a nation from destruction, but that is not governing ]{.class_4938}[directly ]{.class_4937}[on earth. That is God's sovereignty over the Universe. When He comes, He will come as a King and will sit down on a throne to judge (Matt. 25:31). His apostles will judge with Him (Matt. 19:28), martyrs will judge with Him (Rev. 20:4), and he will ]{.class_4938}["judge the earth" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 9, Rev. 6:10).]{.class_4938}

[The Calvinists simply don't know what they are talking about. But then, again, they never did. (See ]{.class_4938}[The Other Side of Calvinism,]{.class_4937}[ Vance, 1991.)]{.class_4938}

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ1825.xhtml}

PSALM 99

99:1 "The LORD reigneth; let the people tremble: he sitteth between the cherubims; let the earth be moved.

2 The LORD is great in Zion; and he is high above all the people.

3 Let them praise thy great and terrible name; for it is holy.

4 The king's strength also loveth judgment; thou dost establish equity, thou executest judgment and righteousness in Jacob.

5 Exalt ye the LORD our God, and worship at his footstool; for he is holy.

6 Moses and Aaron among his priests, and Samuel among them that call upon his name; they called upon the LORD, and he answered them.

7 He spake unto them in the cloudy pillar: they kept his testimonies, and the ordinance that he gave them.

8 Thou answeredst them, O LORD our God: thou wast a God that forgavest them, though thou tookest vengeance of their inventions.

9 Exalt the LORD our God, and worship at his holy hill; for the LORD our God is holy."

[This is the Millennial Reign of Christ, ]{.class_4938}["the day" ]{.class_4939}[(2 Pet. 3:10) that is the " day of days" on God the Father's calendar. Naturally, He is going to give it more space, time, and attention than anything else in His Book. Our "day" is the day that God's Son laid down His life for us, but that is not the day that interests the Father. The day ]{.class_4938}[He ]{.class_4937}[wants to see is when His Son receives what rightfully belongs to Him: glory, honor, power, strength, dominion, and worship or as the heavenly hosts put it: ]{.class_4938}["Worthy is the Lamb that was slain to receive power, and riches, and wisdom, and strength, and honour, and glory, and blessing"]{.class_4939}[ (Rev. 5:12).]{.class_4938}

You can count on the Calvinists to foul the waters till a catfish couldn't survive in them:

1\. Mt. Zion is now the church because it is God's "favorite palace."

2\. The whole earth (in 1000 B.C. or A.D. 1880) should feel the "awe" of God sitting in heaven as a universal "Monarch."

[3. The "rule of God" can be perceived and "felt" by Christians in 1880 (Spurgeon, ]{.class_4938}[Treasury,]{.class_4937}[ p. 222).]{.class_4938}

[4. The subject of the Psalm is Christ's "mediatorial reign" and "those who are honored to lead the church of God" (]{.class_4938}[Treasury]{.class_4937}[, p. 222).]{.class_4938}

Pack N'Sack it.

"[He sitteth between the cherubims.]{.class_4939}[" M]{.class_4938}[otyer: ]{.class_4937}["Pass." Kroll: "Pass." ]{.class_4938}[Yates: ]{.class_4937}["Pass." ]{.class_4938}[Dummelow: ]{.class_4937}["Pass." The card table is empty. Somebody should have taken up poker (seven card stud) instead of bridge. Dummelow says ]{.class_4938}["between"]{.class_4939}[ should be "upon," but then, as far as that goes, he has recommended that you throw out your ]{.class_4938}[King James Bible ]{.class_4937}[for an ]{.class_4938}[RV ]{.class_4937}[in Psalm 95:4, 7, 8; 96:3, 9; 97:12, and earlier in Psalm 84:5--6, 9; 85:13; 86:1--3, 13; 87:4, 6--7; 88:3--6, 9, 11, 13, 17; 89:7--8, 14, 19, 25, 38--39, 47--48, 50; 90:10, 12, 14; 91:9, 13; 92:10, 11, 14, and Psalm 93:4. My, what a "recommendation." Dummelow was brainwashed by Westcott and Hort.]{.class_4938}

"[He sitteth between the cherubims." ]{.class_4939}[God is sitting there now (see Rev. 4:6--9). How did the "Champions" at Liberty University---who had a participant in the Miss America contest of 1990---miss ]{.class_4938}[that? ]{.class_4937}[With his ]{.class_4938}["affection on things above" ]{.class_4939}[(not "mind," as in the ]{.class_4938}[NKJV, ]{.class_4937}[Col. 3:1--3), how did Kroll fail to locate God and the cherubims ]{.class_4938}["above"]{.class_4939}[? Remarkable, isn't it? The ]{.class_4938}["mercy seat"]{.class_4939}[ was called a ]{.class_4938}["seat"]{.class_4939}[ for some reason (Num. 7:89), and since Satan intends to enter the Temple in Jerusalem and sit down (2 Thess. 2:4), why would he not sit down in the place reserved for God? After all, that was his intention when he was a cherub (Isa. 14:13; Ezek. 28:14).]{.class_4938}

[Didn't any of the commentators know that Satan has a ]{.class_4938}["seat" ]{.class_4939}[(Rev. 2:13)? God sat ]{.class_4938}["between the cherubims"]{.class_4939}[ in the Tabernacle in the wilderness, for that is the place from where He communed with Moses (Exod. 25:22). How did Kroll miss that? All he can mumble is, "God is represented as sitting between the cherubims, which were never heard of again after the Babylonian captivity." Awesome.]{.class_4938}

[When Jesus Christ returns, He is pictured as ]{.class_4938}[sitting ]{.class_4937}["between the cherubims"]{.class_4939}[ (Ezek. 1:20--28).]{.class_4938}

[Not one commentator from 1611 to 1992 could find the Scriptural application of the verse. Unbelief; ]{.class_4937}[pure infidelity, just as blatant and unabashed as Madalyn Murray O'Hair or the ACLU. All you have to do is dump in the supernatural, and out goes the bottom of the grocery bag.]{.class_4938}

[There are two phases to Christ's return. One of these is pictured as a man seated on a threshing machine of some kind, which has wheels and cherubims. The seated driver is on a machine that is ]{.class_4938}["excellent in working," ]{.class_4939}[and it grinds everything under it to pieces (Isa. 28:27--29). The other picture is a leader of Cavalry on a white Arabian charger composed of fire (Rev. 19:11). The references for these pictures of the Second Advent are in Joel 2:1--12; Habakkuk 3:9--16; Revelation 19:11--16; 2 Samuel 22; Isaiah 63:1--6; and Ezekiel 1:2--26. When these things take place, ]{.class_4938}["the people tremble" ]{.class_4939}[(literally), and the earth is ]{.class_4938}["moved,"]{.class_4939}[ literally (see Ps. 18:7, 46:6 and comments). For all that you and I know, the literal cherubims will be actually standing around the throne of David when Jesus Christ ]{.class_4938}[sits down on it ]{.class_4937}[in the Millennium. After all, the prayer was, ]{.class_4938}["thy kingdom come. Thy will be done in earth, as it is in heaven" ]{.class_4939}[(Matt. 6:10). Well, bless my soul, in heaven (Rev. 4:6--9), the cherubims are around the throne (Rev. 5:6)!]{.class_4938}

[The Scriptures that paralyzed Spurgeon, Gill, Tucker, Nicolson, Scofield, Custer, Baethgen, Hengstenberg, Kroll, Matthew Henry, Geir, Adam Clarke, John Peter Lange, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Dummelow, Perowne, Chanock, George Phillips, Richard Mant, and Joseph Thrupp simply described something they could not ]{.class_4938}[believe ]{.class_4937}[(along with Briggs, Hitzig, Ewald, Hupfeld, Motyer, Yates, Bullinger, Pentecost, Lindsay, Kirban, Rosenthal, Church, and Davies). This time not even Bullinger knows what the score is. All he can mutter is that verse 1 shows that the Psalm must have been written while the ark was still in existence. Why? God was sitting ]{.class_4938}["between the cherubims" ]{.class_4939}[before Genesis 1:2, and He is sitting there ]{.class_4938}[right now ]{.class_4937}[in 2002. Why would the ark of the covenant, built in 1500 B.C., have to be "in existence"?]{.class_4938}

They have a time of it, don't they?

[The, by now, familiar ]{.class_4938}["judgment and righteousness" ]{.class_4939}[show up again (vs. 4), as in Psalm 9:8, 33:5, 37:6, and 72:2. The ]{.class_4938}["equity" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 4 is the ]{.class_4938}["equity" ]{.class_4939}[of the preceding Psalm (Ps. 98:9). The word means "in a correct or upright fashion." That is, a fashion that is the exact opposite of court procedures in America today (see comments under Ps. 94:20).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 5. ]{.class_4938}["His footstool"]{.class_4939}[ is this ]{.class_4938}[earth. ]{.class_4937}[There is no reference to anything "heavenly," as the Calvinists put it (see Ps. 110:1--3 and Matt. 5:35).]{.class_4938}

[This footstool is located at a specific place on this earth: ]{.class_4938}["at his holy hill" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 9). His name is holy (vs. 3), ]{.class_4938}["he is holy"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 5), and His ]{.class_4938}[hill ]{.class_4937}[is holy (vs. 9). It is holy because He is sitting on it. When Spurgeon hits the ]{.class_4938}["holy hill," ]{.class_4939}[he contracts lockjaw and cannot make one comment. (It is a good thing he didn't; he never could have handled it.) Furthermore, his whole army of teachers, peers, and mentors (John Bengel, J. J. Perowne, Christopher Wordsworth, William Hill Tucker, Amryaldus, William Nicolson, John Gill, David Dickson, Thomas Wilson, Charles Standford, Augustine, Matthew Henry, Mollerus Hugo Cardinalis, Alfred Edersheim, George Phillips, W. Wilson, Richard Man, F. W. Hengstenberg, Bellarmine, Joseph Thrupp, George Home, Herbert Palmer, Thomas Goodwin, and John Collins) seem to have been bitten by a rabid dog. They cannot say one word about ]{.class_4938}["his holy hill." ]{.class_4939}[As soon as they heard about a specific place for worship---on a ]{.class_4938}[mountain, at that ]{.class_4937}[(John 4:20)---they came apart at the seams (Matt. 9:16). They knew they could not apply the New Testament precept given by Jesus Christ in John 4:21, so their Scrabble boards fell of the table, and all the letters with them.]{.class_4938}

[Three ]{.class_4938}[priests ]{.class_4937}[are mentioned (vs. 6). Moses is included because he, like David, was a prophet, priest, and king. It is Moses who offered the first sacrifice in the Tabernacle (Exod. 40:27), before Aaron ]{.class_4938}["called upon the Lord and he answered them" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 6). Furthermore, He forgave them (vs. 8). God did not speak to Samuel in the cloudy pillar, although the house of the Lord could be filled with a cloud at times (notice 1 Kings 8:10).]{.class_4938}

"[Though thou tookest vengeance of their inventions" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 8). The statement emphasizes that great New Testament truth which says, ]{.class_4938}["all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God" ]{.class_4939}[(Rom. 3:23). Moses, Aaron, and Samuel are no exceptions. There has been only one exception in six thousand years of recorded history, in spite of the Darwinistic pipe dream about "every day in every way, things are getting better and better." The world has never seen another man like Jesus Christ since He went back to glory. No one has seen one man who even ]{.class_4938}[resembled ]{.class_4937}[Him when it came to the matter of sinlessness. Moody and Spurgeon were gluttons who were forty pounds overweight; Norris would cuss on occasions; Billy Graham would soft soap the religious liberals; Billy Sunday (in absolute disregard for Jude 9) would challenge the devil to take him on; Luther would drink beer and joke about it; Peter cursed; Joseph lied about the cup; Noah got drunk; David committed adultery; and Paul (the worst in the lot) deliberately rebelled against the revealed will of God after being shown God's mind about the matter FIVE times (Acts 19--22). When it comes to sin, John Paul II can keep up with Adolph Hitler; Elvis Presley was just as rotten as Charles Manson; Mother Teresa can give Bloody Mary a "run for her money"; and there is no difference between Nelson Mandela and Jim Jones.]{.class_4938}

[Moses lost his temper, murdered a man, and then disobeyed a direct order from God (Exod. 2:12; Num. 20:11). These]{.class_4938}[ "inventions"]{.class_4939}[ prevented him from entering the Promised Land. Aaron---believing that "government of the people and by the people" should be for "the people"---succumbed to mob rule and built a golden calf (an "invention" if you ever saw one, Exod. 32:1--6), and consequently experienced what Moses experienced: both of them died without entering the Promised Land. Samuel, one of the most godly men who ever lived, appointed his sons as judges in Israel (1 Sam. 8:1) when they were not fit to be lawyers (1 Sam. 8:3). The bitter fruit of this was a demand from the people for a king, who in turn was one of the greatest types of the Son of Perdition found anywhere in the Scripture. They chose a murderer who was a suicidal maniac.]{.class_4938}

"[Thou tookest vengeance of their inventions."]{.class_4939}

We have commented already on verse 9. The last clause is perfectly apparent.

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ1863.xhtml}

PSALM 100

100:1 "Make a joyful noise unto the LORD, all ye lands.

2 Serve the LORD with gladness: come before his presence with singing.

3 Know ye that the LORD he is God: it is he that hath made us, and not we ourselves; we are his people, and the sheep of his pasture.

4 Enter into his gates with thanksgiving, and into his courts with praise: be thankful unto him, and bless his name.

5 For the LORD is good; his mercy is everlasting; and his truth endureth to all generations."

[We see the Millennium again. The ]{.class_4938}["we"]{.class_4939}[ of verse 3 are the Israelites, who at this point recognize the fulfillment of Ezekiel 34. Israel was literally a nation created by God (see Isa. 43:1).]{.class_4938}

[Of course we can make ]{.class_4938}[spiritual application. ]{.class_4937}[We can say that the New Testament believer is one of the Lord's sheep (as in John 10:1--26 and 1 Pet. 2:25), and he is led by the "Good Shepherd" (see Ps. 23 and comments), but here the context is ]{.class_4938}["come before his presence\...into his courts" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 2, 4). These are the familiar ]{.class_4938}["courts" ]{.class_4939}[of Psalms 65:4; 84:2, 10; and 92:13.]{.class_4938}

[Devotionally, ]{.class_4937}[three things are said about the Lord:]{.class_4938}

1\. He is the One who created us (vs. 3, so Mother Nature and "pools of proteins and amino acids" had nothing to do with it).

2\. He is good (vs. 5) despite His dealings, which, at times, seem to come from a malevolent despot (no irreverence: look at Job 9:24).

[3. ]{.class_4938}["His mercy is everlasting"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 5), in the sense that those to whom it is promised will receive it eventually.]{.class_4938}

[4. His truth (see Ps. 12:6--8 and 138:2) ]{.class_4938}["endureth to all generations" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 5) because it is ]{.class_4938}["true from the beginning" ]{.class_4939}[(Ps. 119:160) and ]{.class_4938}["settled in heaven" ]{.class_4939}[forever (Ps. 119:89).]{.class_4938}

The saint is admonished to do six things:

[1. ]{.class_4938}["Make a joyful noise."]{.class_4939}

[2. ]{.class_4938}["Serve the Lord."]{.class_4939}

3\. Come to Him singing.

4\. Come to Him with thanksgiving.

5\. Come to Him with praise.

[6. ]{.class_4938}["Bless his name."]{.class_4939}

[If ]{.class_4938}["we are his people, and the sheep of his pasture" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 3), we can be thankful for the ability to do all these things. There are actually seven items in the list, but the last is often overlooked because it is not connected with "worship services." The seventh item is in verse 3. There, we are admonished to know something; we are to ]{.class_4938}["know ye that the Lord\...is he that made us." ]{.class_4939}[That is a great truth confirmed by Isaiah 45:12 and Psalm 74:17. Perhaps your great great grandfather was fired by the pack for taking too many banana breaks, but ]{.class_4938}[ours ]{.class_4937}[wasn't. (Two monkeys were launched in a rocket. After a while one of them said, "Well, anyway, it beats the cancer clinic.")]{.class_4938}

[Some of you ought to buy two tickets when you go to the zoo alone: one to get in and one to get out. You may be a bankrupt monkey, but I am not; and there are no tarsiers, lemurs, or orangutans in my family line. I am a "child of the King." I am one of the Good Shepherd's sheep. I don't believe in evolution, but I must confess that I have been tempted to believe it at times when I have seen photos of Mike Tyson or Louis Armstrong. (No offence! I didn't judge them by their color; I judged them by their photographs. Strictly individual judgment, on an ]{.class_4938}[individual basis, ]{.class_4937}[man.)]{.class_4938}

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ1890.xhtml}

PSALM 101

101:1 "I will sing of mercy and judgment: unto thee, O LORD, will I sing.

2 I will behave myself wisely in a perfect way. O when wilt thou come unto me? I will walk within my house with a perfect heart.

3 I will set no wicked thing before mine eyes: I hate the work of them that turn aside; it shall not cleave to me.

4 A froward heart shall depart from me: I will not know a wicked person.

5 Whoso privily slandereth his neighbour, him will I cut off: him that hath an high look and a proud heart will not I suffer.

6 Mine eyes shall be upon the faithful of the land, that they may dwell with me: he that walketh in a perfect way, he shall serve me.

7 He that worketh deceit shall not dwell within my house: he that telleth lies shall not tarry in my sight.

8 I will early destroy all the wicked of the land; that I may cut off all wicked doers from the city of the LORD.

[Historically, this could be David assuming his position as ]{.class_4938}["captain"]{.class_4939}[ of the Lord's people (1 Sam. 13:14). He is meditating on what kind of a king he is going to be. But throughout the Psalm, one can sense the presence of the coming ]{.class_4938}["King of kings"]{.class_4939}[ who will ]{.class_4938}[literally ]{.class_4937}[perform the vows mentioned in verses 5, 6, 7, and 8. David is not this great of a king. He ]{.class_4938}[does ]{.class_4937}[set wicked things before his eyes (2 Sam. 11:2--3), and they get the best of him. He does ]{.class_4938}[not ]{.class_4937}[always walk within his house ]{.class_4938}["with a perfect heart" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 2), and no one who read 2 Samuel would think that he did. No heart can be ]{.class_4938}["perfect"]{.class_4939}[ where New York, Chicago, Los Angeles, Miami, and Las Vegas are constantly ]{.class_4938}["set" ]{.class_4939}[before the eyes (vs. 3). The main source of sin in America, since 1960, has been in the living room. The images of Ezekiel 8:10--12, and much more, are in full color and in motion, infiltrating the ]{.class_4938}["chambers of ]{.class_4939}[\[man's\]]{.class_4938}[ imagery"]{.class_4939}[ (see Ezek. 8:12).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 1 is self-explanatory. Judgment and mercy have to be combined, for judgment is essential, and unless it is tempered with mercy, no man could stand the judgments of God. ]{.class_4938}["The throne is established by righteousness"]{.class_4939}[ (Prov. 16:12), but it is ]{.class_4938}["upholden by mercy"]{.class_4939}[ (Prov. 20:28). The history of the individual and mankind is the history of judgment, affliction, severity, and sorrow, tempered with mercy. ]{.class_4938}["O when wilt thou come unto me?"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 2) is in the sense of answered prayer or in the sense of a greater awareness of God's presence in]{.class_4938}[ "my house."]{.class_4939}

The ideal king resolves to do a number of things:

1\. Hate some things (vs. 3).

2\. Avoid contact with some things (vs. 3).

3\. Avoid bad company (vs. 4).

4\. Cut off slanderers (vs. 5).

5\. Not put up with proud people (vs. 5).

6\. Get rid of liars (vs. 7).

[7. Kill ]{.class_4938}["wicked doers"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 8).]{.class_4938}

[One can see, at a glance, that these rules could not be carried out; neither by an individual Christian, nor by a leader in government. An Oriental monarch could do it, for he could decide for himself who was ]{.class_4938}["wicked"]{.class_4939}[ and who wasn't. Daniel was a "wicked doer" in the eyes of his contemporary magistrates (Dan. 6:4, 10--12). Jesus Christ was a "wicked doer" that needed to be ]{.class_4938}["cut off"]{.class_4939}[ (see Isa. 53:8), according to the leading religious lights of His day. Some ]{.class_4938}[historical application ]{.class_4937}[can be made in David's case. Shimei slandered his neighbor (2 Sam. 16:7--8).]{.class_4938}[ "Sheba, the son of Bichri"]{.class_4939}[ (2 Sam. 20:1) had a ]{.class_4938}["high look and a proud heart"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 5). Mephibosheth would fit verse 6, and Saul's sons would fulfill verse 8 (see 2 Sam. 21:5--10). Still, the doctrinal reference must go to the supreme Oriental Potentate (1 Tim. 6:15), the Lord Jesus, who will fulfill everything in verses 4-8 to the letter. This will be done through a ]{.class_4938}["council"]{.class_4939}[ (see Matt. 5:22) and with the banishment of some sinners into a literal lake of fire (Matt. 5:29--30) into which the body as well as the soul can be cast (Matt. 10:28). ]{.class_4938}[None of the commentators can find any of the references.]{.class_4937}

[There will be no ]{.class_4938}["wicked doers"]{.class_4939}[ in ]{.class_4938}["the city of the Lord"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 8) in the Millennium. Devotionally, we may say that certain things are ]{.class_4938}["wicked,"]{.class_4939}[ no matter what the NEA teaches in the federal school system (vs. 3), and that if you don't hate "wicked things" you are no kin to David or Jesus Christ (Ps. 97:10; 119:113, 163; 139:21). Every Christian has to be a good hater, but unfortunately, that Biblical virtue is passing from most of the Christians in America today. Strangely enough, the love mongers, who talk the most about "love," are the ones who do not love the Book and do not tremble when the Book speaks (see Isa. 66:2). They tremble when the press speaks or when their kinfolk and supporters speak.]{.class_4938}

[It is usually easier to behave yourself ]{.class_4938}["wisely"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 2) when in public---where everyone is watching you---than ]{.class_4938}["within"]{.class_4939}[ your house (vs. 2). One commentator (]{.class_4938}[Treasury]{.class_4937}[, p. 245) says, "How many are as meek as lambs among others, when at home they are wasps and tigers." That was perfectly true when Adam Clarke wrote those words, but since 1940 all of that has changed. The normal condition in America, now, is that the man out in the world is a big, bold, aggressive, positive thinking, domineering "straw" boss, but at home, with the "little lady," he is a poodle on a leash.]{.class_4938}

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ1917.xhtml}

PSALM 102

102:1 "Hear my prayer, O LORD, and let my cry come unto thee.

2 Hide not thy face from me in the day when I am in trouble; incline thine ear unto me: in the day when I call answer me speedily.

3 For my days are consumed like smoke, and my bones are burned as an hearth.

4 My heart is smitten, and withered like grass; so that I forget to eat my bread.

5 By reason of the voice of my groaning my bones cleave to my skin.

6 I am like a pelican of the wilderness: I am like an owl of the desert.

7 I watch, and am as a sparrow alone upon the house top.

8 Mine enemies reproach me all the day; and they that are mad against me are sworn against me.

9 For I have eaten ashes like bread, and mingled my drink with weeping,

10 Because of thine indignation and thy wrath: for thou hast lifted me up, and cast me down.

11 My days are like a shadow that declineth; and I am withered like grass."

[The prayer is commonly taken to have been composed by Hezekiah on his death bed (see Isa. 38:1--3), but Bullinger refers us to the "Messiah's humiliation." Most of the commentators allow this double application, although some of them guess that the Psalm was written by Jeremiah or Daniel. Be that as it may, we have learned enough already from studying the Psalms to know that the standard lament is coming from an individual in deep distress, Christ Himself in deep distress, or a Tribulation Israelite in deep distress. For this reason, we will confine our remarks largely to the devotional and inspirational aspects of the Psalm. The doctrinal stuff does not come in strong until verse 13, and then the Second Advent shows up immediately (vss. 16, 21).]{.class_4938}[ "The destitute"]{.class_4939}[ in verse 17 are praying in verses 1--11. Verses 1--11 are ]{.class_4938}["the groaning of the prisoner"]{.class_4939}[ in verse 20.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 2. You need help right now, not later, and unless God answers ]{.class_4938}["speedily,"]{.class_4939}[ you are a dead duck. That moment when you are placed under arrest, that moment when the papers are served, that moment when the bilge pump doesn't work, that moment when both engines conk out, and so forth. I have had a chain-saw bounce off a wire hidden in the branches and shave my upper lip; the wing of a plane graze the frozen landing strip at 120 miles an hour; the engine of a freight train total my car after pushing me 230 feet down the track, etc. Trouble comes in all kinds of packages, delivered by all kinds of express companies. Sometimes if the prayer is not answered ]{.class_4938}["speedily,"]{.class_4939}[ it can never be answered.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 3. The sufferer is like Job, who said his bones were]{.class_4938}[ "burned with heat"]{.class_4939}[ (Job 30:30). A wasted form from ]{.class_4938}[fasting ]{.class_4937}[is presented in verse 5 (see Job 19:20). The sufferer is completely ]{.class_4938}[alone ]{.class_4937}[(vs. 7) and likens himself to two unclean birds (vs. 6). ]{.class_4938}[Unclean birds]{.class_4937}[ in the Scripture (Gen. 8:7; Deut. 14:14; Isa. 34:11) are types of demons (Rev. 18:2; Isa. 34:14; Mark 4:4, 15). Thus, Job calls his companions ]{.class_4938}["dragons"]{.class_4939}[ (Job 30:29) and]{.class_4938}[ "owls."]{.class_4939}[ There is no doubt about it: verses 3--6 are the laments of a Tribulation saint.]{.class_4938}

[The figure of a man feeding on ashes (vs. 9) is given in Isaiah 44:20. The description is partly literal, for it was a habit for penitent sufferers in deep depression to cover themselves with ashes (Job again, Job 2:8, 30:19). When bread is put into the mouth, it gets ashes sprinkled on it. Again, when the drink is lifted to the lips, the tears are streaming down the face, and this makes the ]{.class_4938}["drink" ]{.class_4939}[briny. ]{.class_4938}["Thou hast lifted me up, and cast me down"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 10). Here the Messianic similarities appear. Compare this with the descriptions of Christ's sufferings in Psalm 89:38--46 and Psalm 88:7. Note especially the references to God's wrath and the shortening of life. Christ is]{.class_4938}[ "cut off"]{.class_4939}[ before His time (Isa. 53:8). The]{.class_4938}[ "enemies"]{.class_4939}[ of verse 8 can refer to the scribes, Pharisees, Sadducees, Pilate, Herod, and so forth. ]{.class_4938}["My days are like a shadow\...But thou, O LORD, shalt endure for ever"]{.class_4939}[ (vss. 11--12). Review the remarks under Psalm 21:4 on Christ's humanity and the promise made to Him "before the foundation of the world" (John 17:24).]{.class_4938}

102:12 "But thou, O LORD, shalt endure for ever; and thy remembrance unto all generations.

13 Thou shalt arise, and have mercy upon Zion: for the time to favour her, yea, the set time, is come.

14 For thy servants take pleasure in her stones, and favour the dust thereof.

15 So the heathen shall fear the name of the LORD, and all the kings of the earth thy glory.

16 When the LORD shall build up Zion, he shall appear in his glory.

17 He will regard the prayer of the destitute, and not despise their prayer.

18 This shall be written for the generation to come: and the people which shall be created shall praise the LORD.

19 For he hath looked down from the height of his sanctuary; from heaven did the LORD behold the earth;

20 To hear the groaning of the prisoner; to loose those that are appointed to death;

21 To declare the name of the LORD in Zion, and his praise in Jerusalem;

22 When the people are gathered together, and the kingdoms, to serve the LORD."

[Verse 12 is self-explanatory. Verse 12 shows why God will be remembered long after the Psalmist is dead. No generation can outlive God; even ]{.class_4938}[the universe ]{.class_4937}[can't outlive Him (vs. 26), although every unsaved evolutionist in every university in the world thinks it will. From a ]{.class_4938}[devotional standpoint]{.class_4937}[, the sufferer has temporarily forgotten his own personal troubles as he contemplates the fact that God is going to deliver Zion. Spurgeon interprets this to mean that the church is going to\
conquer the world by "spreading the kingdom" (]{.class_4938}[Treasury]{.class_4937}[, p. 254--255), which is fox-fire (or snipe hunting, I don't know which). The ]{.class_4938}["set time"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 13) has no more to do with the church spreading the kingdom than it does with a countdown on a space shuttle at Cape Canaveral. The ]{.class_4938}["set time" ]{.class_4939}[for God to favor Zion (vs. 13) is the Second Advent (Zech. 14; Joel 2--3).]{.class_4938}

"[When the Lord shall build up Zion, he shall]{.class_4939}[ ]{.class_4938}[appear in his glory"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 16). This should finish off every post- and amillennial expositor from Origen to Phillip Mauro and John Bray. Christ appearing in ]{.class_4938}["glory" ]{.class_4939}[(2 Pet. 1:16--19) is Matthew 17:1--6. He is accompanied by Moses and Elijah, as in Malachi 4:4--5 and Revelation 11:2--6. You couldn't find a reference to the Church Age anywhere near either scene. The Lord will ]{.class_4938}["build"]{.class_4939}[ something when He returns see Acts 15:16, and His praise has nothing to do with being "outside the camp" at Calvary. It will be ]{.class_4938}[inside the camp]{.class_4937}[ at Jerusalem (vs. 21):]{.class_4938}[ "the name of the LORD in Zion; and his praise in Jerusalem." "All the kings of the earth" ]{.class_4939}[certainly do not]{.class_4938}[ "fear the name of the LORD"]{.class_4939}[ in this age (vs. 15), and they know no more about the ]{.class_4938}["glory"]{.class_4939}[ of the Lord (vs. 15) than the postmillennial expositors who try to make it refer to the "glorious gospel." The literal stones and dust of the city of Jerusalem (vs. 14) will be considered beautiful when the Lord lands there. The comforts given in verses 17 and 20 are thus clearly applicable to Tribulation Jews awaiting ]{.class_4938}["the set time."]{.class_4939}[ In verse 19, He hasn't landed yet; note, ]{.class_4938}["he hath looked down."]{.class_4939}[ The prisoners are ]{.class_4938}[literal ]{.class_4937}[(Rev. 6:9), for they are back in Abraham's bosom in an intermediate state, and those ]{.class_4938}["appointed to death"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 20) are ]{.class_4938}[literal ]{.class_4937}[(see Rev. 6:11), for they are ]{.class_4938}["counted as sheep for the slaughter"]{.class_4939}[ (see Ps. 44:22). All the commentators miss all the references.]{.class_4938}

102:23 "He weakened my strength in the way; he shortened my days.

24 I said, O my God, take me not away in the midst of my days: thy years are throughout all generations.

25 Of old hast thou laid the foundation of the earth: and the heavens are the work of thy hands.

26 They shall perish, but thou shalt endure: yea, all of them shall wax old like a garment; as a vesture shalt thou change them, and they shall be changed:

27 But thou art the same, and thy years shall have no end.

28 The children of thy servants shall continue, and their seed shall be established before thee.

[Verses 23 and 24 deal with Christ's prayer (Heb. 5:7), as we mentioned before. Of course, they could apply to anyone in the condition in which Hezekiah was (Isa. 38).]{.class_4938}[ "Of old thou hast laid the foundations of the earth; and the heavens\...they shall perish\...shall wax old like a garment\...as a vesture shalt thou change them, and they shall be changed"]{.class_4939}[ (vss. 25--26). This passage is quoted in Hebrews 1:10--12, and there is so much that can be said about it that not even our work on Hebrews (]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Hebrews]{.class_4937}[, 1989) could cover it adequately.]{.class_4938}

[1. God wears clothes (Ps. 104:6). These "clothes" are His ]{.class_4938}["glory"]{.class_4939}[ (Ps. 19:1--4). He made them "by hand" (Ps. 8:3), for they are called His ]{.class_4938}["handywork"]{.class_4939}[ (Ps. 19:1).]{.class_4938}

2\. These clothes show up on Jesus Christ as literal, earthly apparel that a human being wears, but the Holy Spirit indicates that when He is stripped of these clothes, the outer garment is a poncho with a hole in the top where the head protrudes (John 19:23). In shape, this would match a pyramid-shaped universe. As a pyramid, the headstone (where the head protruded through the poncho) is missing until Christ shows up (Matt. 21:42).

[3. These clothes are denoted by the Holy Spirit as ]{.class_4938}["vesture," "garment," "raiment," "clothes,"]{.class_4939}[ but never (no, not ever!) "robes" or "robe." Lloyd Douglas' ]{.class_4938}[The Robe]{.class_4937}[---like all Satanic counterfeits of Biblical narratives (]{.class_4938}[Ben Hur, The Gladiator, The Exodus, The Bible, David and Bathsheba,]{.class_4937}[ etc.)---was a Roman Catholic blasphemy to the name of Jesus Christ. Catholics always have to make something "magical" about a spear that touched Him, a cup from which He drank, a shroud in which He was buried, the cross on which He died, or (in this case) a Roman robe draped on Him by the Roman soldiers who nailed Him to the cross (John 19:2). It was the gang who murdered Him ("murder" as in]{.class_4938}[ "murderers"]{.class_4939}[: Acts 7:52) who wore ]{.class_4938}[robes ]{.class_4937}[(Luke 20:46). Don't you know an ecumenical, compromising, left-wing infidel like Lloyd Douglas would get it balled up?]{.class_4938}

[4. When Christ died, a curtain is rent (the veil to the holy of holies was classified with the ]{.class_4938}["curtains" ]{.class_4939}[of the Tabernacle in 2 Sam. 7:2), and this (Matt. 27:51) is said to match the tearing of Christ's ]{.class_4938}["flesh"]{.class_4939}[ (see Heb. 10:20). This rending of cloth (see Josh. 7:6; Judg. 11:35; 1 Sam. 4:12; 1 Kings 21:27; 2 Kings 5:7, etc.) appears over and over again in the Old Testament as a picture of grief over someone's ]{.class_4938}[death]{.class_4937}[. Thus it happens "true to form" when the only begotten Son dies: the ]{.class_4938}[veil ]{.class_4937}[is rent, the ]{.class_4938}[rocks ]{.class_4937}[are rent, and the ]{.class_4938}[high priest rends his clothes]{.class_4937}[ (Matt. 26:65, 27:51).]{.class_4938}

[5. When the veil was rent in the Temple, something was being rent in the ]{.class_4938}[north end of the universe]{.class_4937}[. ]{.class_4938}["The door"]{.class_4939}[ (John 10:7) is now open so a man can enter into it (Heb. 10:19; John 10:1--7). The ]{.class_4938}["door"]{.class_4939}[ is Jesus Christ: He is ]{.class_4938}["the head"]{.class_4939}[ in the poncho (Eph. 5:22--27). What you are looking at in Psalm 102 is ]{.class_4938}[God changing His clothes]{.class_4937}[ (vs. 26). He will put on a ]{.class_4938}["new heavens"]{.class_4939}[ (2 Pet. 3:8--12, Rev. 21:1--3), just like a man goes to Newberrys, Sears, Monkey Ward, Pelletiers, Wannamakers, or Hart, Schaefner, and Marx, and dons a new suit. All the Fundamentalists, Liberals, Conservatives, Modernists, Neo-Evangelicals, Catholics, agnostics, atheists, and "godly" men missed all the references in ]{.class_4938}[both ]{.class_4937}[Testaments. Par for the course.]{.class_4938}

6\. Some day (oh, my soul!), God will reveal His nakedness to His creation. And when He takes off His clothes (oh, my soul!), the impure heavens and earth cannot bear the sight, so they go up in flames (Prov. 20:8--9; 2 Pet. 3:10--13; Rev. 20:11).

[What a day for the sinners who stripped Him of His clothing and gazed ]{.class_4938}[at His naked body]{.class_4937}[ (Luke 23:35--37) and ]{.class_4938}[mocked him]{.class_4937}[ (oh, my soul!).]{.class_4938}

Gaze on Him now, ye scribes and Pharisees! Ogle Him now with your eyeballs, you Roman soldiers, under your Roman governor, with your Roman "Caesar" as your "holy father"!

[My God, what a day, when sinners are required to face Someone who loved them enough to die for them, and His naked glory is so glorious that the blazing sun would seem like a fly speck on a curtain by comparison! ]{.class_4938}["Bless the Lord, O my soul"]{.class_4939}[ (Ps. 103:1). I sure am glad I am on the right side (Isa. 42:14, Rom. 10:11).]{.class_4938}

[In their haste to be moderate, temperate, and cool (so as not to offend anyone with "bad language"), all of the commentators forgot that the Lord Jesus was naked when He was crucified. Being completely duped by nineteenth- and twentieth-century ]{.class_4938}["science falsely so called"]{.class_4939}[ (1 Tim. 6:20), all of the commentators, scholars, and exegetes failed to perceive the ]{.class_4938}["breadth\...length\...depth, and height"]{.class_4939}[ of "Mt. Zion," even though Paul prayed that they would comprehend it (Eph. 3:18). This refusal to believe Genesis 1:6--8 literally (see ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis]{.class_4937}[, 1970) cut them out of one of the greatest advanced revelations in the archaic, Elizabethan English that God ever revealed to mankind.]{.class_4938}

[Faced with the sinless purity of a holy God, ]{.class_4938}["who can say I have made my heart clean, I am pure from my sin?"]{.class_4939}[ (Prov. 20:9).]{.class_4938}

What leader of the NAACP will step forward and lecture the Almighty about slavery (Exod. 21:6) and segregation (Neh. 13:20--26; Lev. 22:10--11)? Do I see a volunteer? Where are the "civil rights" and "pro-choice" people peddling their pornographic literature and drinking their liquor on Sunday? Will anyone step forward from the ranks? Come, come, this won't do! Isn't man "the measure of all things"? Where, then, are all those NEA people who wanted to demonstrate sex perversion in the middle schools as an "alternate lifestyle"? What, no volunteers? C'mon, Eddie Murphy, give us one of your filthy jokes now! Don't be bashful, Dean Martin, you and Sinatra, pour the Lord of heaven and earth a drink; mix Him a cocktail.

[Hey Einstein, Bertrand Russell, Mohammed. Hey, Marx, Darwin, and Freud! YOO-HOO! Where are you? Come, come gentlemen, cat got your tongue? C'mon Pope John Paul II, tell us all about what Christianity has in common with Buddhism and Islam. Come, come gentlemen, let's not be bashful. ]{.class_4938}[Speak up!]{.class_4937}[ After all, down on that old burned up planet all we got from you idiots for 150 years was Women's Lib, man's rights, Civil Rights, Gay Liberation, evolution, no judgment, no hell, no resurrection, "unlocking the secrets of outer space," no accountability to God, free love, "liberated lifestyles," group affirmation of self, celebrating diversity, "coping with stress," affirmative action, liberty, fraternity, equality, self realization, global citizenship, saving the environment, and "relating to imperatives." Why is everyone so quiet now?)]{.class_4938}

I'll tell you why: Romans 3:19.

[I'll tell you why: ]{.class_4938}["He that sitteth in the heavens shall laugh"]{.class_4939}[ (Ps. 2:4).]{.class_4938}

I'll tell you why. "He who laughs last laughs best" (Prov. 1:26).

[Wait till God changes His clothes! ]{.class_4938}["Thou art the same, and thy years shall have no end"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 27). Mankind changes as fast as the hands on a clock, so man will ]{.class_4938}["perish"]{.class_4939}[ and ]{.class_4938}["wax old"]{.class_4939}[ like his clothing (vs. 26). This is the substance of Isaiah 51:6, which matches our Psalm. Observe that in Isaiah 51:6, when the garments ]{.class_4938}["wax old"]{.class_4939}[ the inhabitants who ]{.class_4938}["move"]{.class_4939}[ in God and have their ]{.class_4938}["being"]{.class_4939}[ in Him (Acts 17:28), perish unless they have God's righteousness (Isa. 51:5--8).]{.class_4938}

"[Their seed shall be established before thee"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 28).]{.class_4938}[ "The generation to come"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 18) is the Millennial generation we discussed back in Psalm 87.]{.class_4938}[ "The people which shall be created"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 18) are created in this age by a spiritual birth (Ps. 22:31), for they are the Lord's ]{.class_4938}["seed"]{.class_4939}[ (Isa. 53:10 and Ps. 89:29). They outlive the present heavens and earth (2 Pet. 3:12).]{.class_4938}

[This is what lay concealed in Psalm 102. Liberty University in Lynchburg gave verses 25 and 26 ]{.class_4938}[five lines]{.class_4937}[ of printed comment. ]{.class_4938}[This is the depth]{.class_4937}[ ]{.class_4938}[of Fundamentalism in 1991]{.class_4937}[. At any appearance of the supernatural, they come apart like hysterical old women. From the standpoint of "science":]{.class_4938}

1\. Jesus Christ, as Moses and Isaiah, was a creationist (Mark 13:19; Matt. 19:4).

2\. The second law of thermodynamics forbids any planet, star, moon, or earth from continuing indefinitely or improving.

3\. The Big Bang theory that hypothesizes the universe will open and close like an accordion, indefinitely, is just pagan mythology.

4\. The first law of thermodynamics shows the universe has NOT always been here.

[We are left with only one really scientific conclusion: the ultimate reality is ]{.class_4938}[God Himself.]{.class_4937}[ What can be seen will perish (Isa. 51:6); what cannot be seen will endure (Heb. 12:27). Moses ]{.class_4938}["endured, as seeing him who was invisible"]{.class_4939}[ (Heb. 11:27). Change is not evolution---contrary to the teaching of every teacher connected with the NEA. ]{.class_4938}[Change ]{.class_4937}[is nothing but entropy destroying what is there. Growth, development, maturation, and improvement can take place temporarily, but eventually ]{.class_4938}[change ]{.class_4937}[will bring decay, randomness, deterioration, and destruction. Proof? Jog through a graveyard. Any one will do just fine.]{.class_4938}

The ultimate reality is God, not man.

The only true and lasting thing is GOD: not "things."

"[Thou art the same, and thy years shall have no end." ]{.class_4939}[If you can get "hooked up" with God (1 John 5:13), you will live forever (1 John 5:20). That's the trick.]{.class_4938}

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ1985.xhtml}

PSALM 103

103:1 "Bless the LORD, O my soul: and all that is within me, bless his holy name.

2 Bless the LORD, O my soul, and forget not all his benefits:

3 Who forgiveth all thine iniquities; who healeth all thy diseases;

4 Who redeemeth thy life from destruction; who crowneth thee with lovingkindness and tender mercies;

5 Who satisfieth thy mouth with good things; so that thy youth is renewed like the eagle's.

6 The LORD executeth righteousness and judgment for all that are oppressed.

7 He made known his ways unto Moses, his acts unto the children of Israel."

[This Psalm has the same number of verses as the letters in the Hebrew alphabet, and the word ]{.class_4938}["bless" ]{.class_4939}[occurs in it seven times. It begins with ]{.class_4938}["Bless the Lord, O my soul" ]{.class_4939}[and ends that way (vs. 22). The Psalm is about ]{.class_4938}[mercy. ]{.class_4937}[It shows the quality of mercy (vs. 4), the measure of mercy (vs. 8), the extent of mercy (vs. 11), and the duration of mercy (vs. 17). ]{.class_4938}[Devotionally, ]{.class_4937}[we may say that salvation in the Lord Jesus Christ gives the sinner help in the court room (vs. 2), help in the hospital (vs. 3), help in the slave market (vs. 4), help in the throne room (vs. 4), and help in the banquet hall (vs. 5). That'll preach, brother!]{.class_4938}

[Everything within me that CAN praise God should do so. ]{.class_4938}["In my flesh, dwelleth no good thing"]{.class_4939}[ (Rom. 7:18), so]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4937}[the Holy Spirit (within me) should control my soul, not the other way around. The saints should bless the Lord because He: forgives (vs. 3), heals (vs. 3), redeems (vs. 4), crowns (vs. 4), satisfies (vs. 5), and renews (vs. 6). A knowledge of ]{.class_4938}["his ways" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 7) is better than just a knowledge of ]{.class_4938}["his acts."]{.class_4939}

"[Soul music" and "soul food" are ]{.class_4938}[not ]{.class_4937}[what is meant in verse 1. Nor are "soul brothers" the subject of blessing. It was the "soul brothers" in Viet Nam who introduced the entire nation (via the armed forces) to heroin and cocaine. ]{.class_4938}["All that is within me" ]{.class_4939}[means that the Spirit (Rom. 8:26) is to summon up my affections, imaginations, conscience, and desires and cause them to ]{.class_4938}["bless the Lord."]{.class_4939}

[At verse 3, all of the commentators flip again. They all bent over backward to prove they were not "Holiness" or "Charismatics" who believed that physical healing was in the atonement---which it is. Kroll craftily works the second clause (vs. 3) into the first clause, so that he doesn't have to tell you what either clause means. But all the writers in Spurgeon's ]{.class_4938}[Treasury ]{.class_4937}[spiritualize the]{.class_4938}[ "diseases"]{.class_4939}[ and force them to be diseases of the soul---]{.class_4938}[which they are not. ]{.class_4937}[In the Millennium, not one saint from this age will contract a disease; at the Second Coming of Christ, any saint can say that God has healed all his diseases---literally and ]{.class_4938}[physically ]{.class_4937}[(see Rom. 8:21; 1 John 3:1--3; and Phil. 3:21). The commentators failed to observe the great Biblical truth that all of the benefits of the blood atonement are not available in this age. Three of these benefits---which were certainly purchased at Calvary---were the redemption of the dirt and clay that make up the earth's surface (Matt. 13:44), the redemption of the animals who are on the earth's surface (Isa. 11:6--9), and the redemption of the physical body of corruptible flesh, which is subject to infirmities (Rom. 8). You don't get any of these things till Jesus Christ returns, but all three of them were taken care of at Calvary. For the animals, it was a Lamb that was born in a stable (no, honey, not a "cave" like Papias suggested); for the ground, it was a crown of thorns which came from Genesis 3:18 and for you it was the body of a man ]{.class_4938}["made of a woman" ]{.class_4939}[(Gal. 4:4).]{.class_4938}

All of the commentators did "you know what."

[Just as the Lord has not executed ]{.class_4938}["judgment for all that are oppressed" ]{.class_4939}[yet (vs. 6), so verse 3 has not been fulfilled YET.]{.class_4938}

[The children of Israel saw God's]{.class_4938}[ "acts"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 7) in Exodus and Numbers, but Moses knew about His ]{.class_4938}["ways" ]{.class_4939}[(see Exod. 31--34).]{.class_4938}

[There is more trouble at verse 5. David's ]{.class_4938}["mouth" ]{.class_4939}[has suddenly been "transmogrified" (Calvin and Hobbes, circa 1992) into his soul. The ultra spiritual Calvinists and superspiritual commentators evidently do not think that God can be the author of a plate full of good food. This is done with Acts 2:46; 1 Timothy 4:3--6; and 1 Timothy 6:17 right in front of their "godly" snoots. "Or rather," says Spurgeon, "filling with good thy soul." ]{.class_4938}["Rather,"]{.class_4937}[ your foot. David lost his ]{.class_4938}["mouth"]{.class_4939}[ in the ]{.class_4938}[New International Version ]{.class_4937}[(but kept his "desires"), he lost his mouth again in the ]{.class_4938}[RSV ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[NRSV, ]{.class_4937}[then he lost it again in the ]{.class_4938}[New English Bible, ]{.class_4937}[and again in ]{.class_4938}[The Living Bible. ]{.class_4937}[How David is going to ]{.class_4938}["bless the LORD" ]{.class_4939}[without his mouth is a little hard to say.]{.class_4938}

Crafty old Kroll , the cautious, careful critic, gives you this brilliant exegesis: "Regardless of our age, when we find joy and happiness in blessing the name of the Lord and recounting all His benefits to us\...."

"[Sorry, Mario, our princess is in another castle!"]{.class_4938}

Instead, he quotes the second clause. That is a "Champion for Christ" if you ever saw one. What is the problem? Well, let's face it; let us face up, face off, square off, and "shape up" to the problem.

[The problem is that these fellows, to the last man, want to appear to be ]{.class_4938}[spiritual giants, ]{.class_4937}[and they want to do this after misreading the Book, ]{.class_4938}[lying ]{.class_4937}[about the Book, ]{.class_4938}[misinterpreting ]{.class_4937}[the Book, ]{.class_4938}[misunderstanding ]{.class_4937}[the Book, and ]{.class_4938}[perverting ]{.class_4937}[the Book. At the same time, they devote their lives to bringing you into their "Union" to increase ]{.class_4938}[their own number. ]{.class_4937}[That is the problem, and that has been the problem since 2 Peter 2:1 and Revelation 2:15.]{.class_4938}

[If they had dared let you think for a minute that the meat and potatoes that you put in your physical mouth came from God, and for ]{.class_4938}[that ]{.class_4937}[God should be "blessed," you would have thought they were "materialistic" or "carnally minded." So just like their gnostic, ascetic forefathers (Philo, Origen, Eusebius, Valentius, Basilides, Saturnius, et al.), they put on a spiritual show for your benefit. They did this in pure ignorance and conceit because the Jew in the Tribulation has to pray for bread to put into his mouth (Matt. 6:11), and this bread is miraculously provided (Micah 7:14). David is in the wilderness, as the Jews will be in the wilderness, running from Saul (2 Sam. 17:27--29), as the Jew will be running from the Son of Perdition (Matt. 24:16; Rev. 12:13--17), and he puts good things in his mouth that were providentially supplied by God. The ]{.class_4938}["good things" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 5) that go in your mouth---for heaven's sake don't let the Scholar's Union take your mouth from you as they took it from David---are ]{.class_4938}["honey," "milk," "butter," "grapes," "kidneys of wheat," "bread," "fat of lambs," "rams," "apples," "goats," "barley," "flour," "parched corn," "beans," "strong meat," "lentils," "parched pulse," "honeycomb," "broiled fish." ]{.class_4939}[If you are too lazy to look up the references, you deserve to lose your mouth (]{.class_4938}[NRSV, NEB]{.class_4937}[).]{.class_4938}

103:8 "The LORD is merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and plenteous in mercy.

9 He will not always chide: neither will he keep his anger for ever.

10 He hath not dealt with us after our sins; nor rewarded us according to our iniquities.

11 For as the heaven is high above the earth, so great is his mercy toward them that fear him.

12 As far as the east is from the west, so far hath he removed our transgressions from us.

13 Like as a father pitieth his children, so the LORD pitieth them that fear him.

14 For he knoweth our frame; he remembereth that we are dust.

15 As for man, his days are as grass: as a flower of the field, so he flourisheth.

16 For the wind passeth over it, and it is gone; and the place therof shall know it no more."

[What follows are some statements about God's "communicable" attributes: the things about Him that man can understand. These things are also mentioned in Exodus 34:6, which see. Among them are mercy, grace, patience, and leniency (vss. 8--10). These are based on His knowledge of His creation. ]{.class_4938}["He knoweth our frame" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 14), and knows that it was framed from dirt (see Job 10:9). He knows that our ]{.class_4938}["days are as grass; as the flower of the field" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 15, see Isa. 40:6 and Ps. 37:2, 90:5). We can not only be blown about ]{.class_4938}["with every wind of doctrine" ]{.class_4939}[(Eph. 4:14), we can be blown clean off the map. ]{.class_4938}["For the wind passeth over it, and it is gone" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 16). Hurricanes, tornadoes (see Job 1:19), and typhoons can remove us from our ]{.class_4938}["place"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 16); and the germs of typhus, fever, and poxes borne in the air can kill us. The big thing coming up is chemical warfare; it is the ]{.class_4938}[wind ]{.class_4937}[that spreads the destruction. The east wind destroys Pharaoh (Exod. 10:13, 14:21).]{.class_4938}

"[The LORD is merciful" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 8, see comments on Ps. 118).]{.class_4938}

"[The LORD is\...gracious." ]{.class_4939}[He allowed a whole generation of Israelites, who should have been killed eight days after they were born (see Gen. 17:10 and Josh. 5:3--5), to live. He let Samson violate every rule in the book, and after Samson committed suicide, God registered him with the heroes of faith (Heb. 11:32). He let David live after he had committed two sins for which no Old Testament sacrifice could atone (Ps. 51:16); David died of old age. The Lord is gracious.]{.class_4938}

"[The LORD is\...slow to anger." ]{.class_4939}[He put up with Israel for forty years in the wilderness (Acts 13:18), He put up with idolatry for four hundred years in the book of Judges, He gave Israel nearly forty years to get right after they had crucified and rejected His only begotten Son, He put up with Manasseh (2 Chron. 33:1--13) until the old sinner got right, although God could have killed him for breaking the law (ditto Ahab: see 1 Kings 21:25--29). God has taken more from America in the last forty years than He took from Sodom, Gomorrah, Tyre, or Sidon in one hundred years. The Lord is ]{.class_4938}["slow to anger."]{.class_4939}

"[Neither will he keep his anger for ever" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 9). The Jews got back to Palestine after 1,875 years; He let Germany reunite after forty years of slavery to Russia; He promised restoration to Jerusalem while Nebuchadnezzar was in the act of destroying it (Jer. 32:5--10); even Egypt and Assyria finally get a blessing (Isa. 11:16, 19:23--24).]{.class_4938}

"[He will not always chide." ]{.class_4939}[He rebukes at times, but at other times He smiles on His children. He scolded David (2 Sam. 12:10--12) and chastened him (Ps. 119:67), but when the sunset of life came (1 Chron. 29:28), David died a rich and honored man with a secure throne, and God's blessing on his life (Acts 13:36). He scolded Moses (Num. 20:12) but let him into the Promised Land 1,500 years later (Matt. 17:1--6). If God had ]{.class_4938}["dealt with us after our sins" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 10), we would be dead and in hell. Cain was not rewarded ]{.class_4938}["according" ]{.class_4939}[to his]{.class_4938}[ "iniquities." ]{.class_4939}[Neither were you. ]{.class_4938}["As far as the east is from the west" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 12) is pretty far, you will have to admit, for the globe spins, and ]{.class_4938}["east"]{.class_4939}[ never catches up with ]{.class_4938}["west."]{.class_4939}

[Doctrinally, ]{.class_4937}[the ]{.class_4938}["our"]{.class_4939}[ is Israel, for the ]{.class_4938}["covenant" ]{.class_4939}[and the ]{.class_4938}["commandments" ]{.class_4939}[are in evidence (see vs. 18, which all of the commentators ignored). However, we can make some application, for verse 15 says ]{.class_4938}["As for man," ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["them that fear him" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 11) are found in the New Testament as Gentiles who are attending the services in the synagogue (see Acts 13:16). A mother ]{.class_4938}["comforteth" ]{.class_4939}[(Isa. 66:13), whereas a father ]{.class_4938}["pitieth" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 13). The distinction goes across the grain of all "equal rights" advocates and "women's libbers," for it clearly shows that men are not only different from women in physical structure, emotional makeup, outlook on life, self sufficiency, stability, and reproductive powers, but also in their ability to respond to a child's need. No one can ]{.class_4938}[comfort ]{.class_4937}[like a mother. When the quarterback gets "red dogged" by a linebacker and has to be carried off the field, "Mamma" (up in the stands) is saying, "Oh! my poor boy!" The "old man" is muttering, "Nice try, son!" There is a difference.]{.class_4938}

[A boy with a bruised finger comes into the house, hollering bloody murder. "Daddy" takes one look at it---it isn't even bleeding---and says, "Shoot, you ain't hurt. Run on!" Fifteen minutes later, the boy appears in the door with Mamma, who says, "Papa, why didn't you help his finger?" "Help his finger? What in the world are you talking about?
