I couldn'na helped his finger none" (]{.class_4938}[The Living Bible,]{.class_4937}[ 1990).]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4937}["You could so of," says the boy ("The Brand New Spanking New New Living Bible," ]{.class_4938}[1999½). ]{.class_4937}["I'd like to know how," says the old man. "Well," says the boy, "you coulda said 'Ooooh'!" You see, that is what Mamma said: she comforted the boy. To Daddy, the hurt wasn't big enough to fool with.]{.class_4938}

[We are "dust to dust" and "ashes to ashes." Anyone of us can topple over and fall flat on our faces, never to get up again, and we are liable to do this at any time in our lives. I know of scores of cases where people under thirty died of heart attacks. General Rommel survived three major battles and thirty smaller ones, but he didn't get to live to be sixty. Elvis Presley fell flat into his coffin before he was forty three years old. Errol Flynn dropped into hell before his sixtieth birthday. Franz Peter Schubert couldn't get through thirty five years, and neither could David Brainerd. Alexander the Great didn't live as long as Elvis Presley, and Jeroboam's son didn't live long enough to learn how to read (1 Kings 14:17). A stroke, a clot, or a coronary can "do you in" before the sun goes down tonight, and God knows this. All He requires, in order to give mercy and pity, is that the culprit ]{.class_4938}["fear him"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 13). The mark of the God fearing man in the Old Testament is keeping the commandments (see Ecc. 12:13 and Ps. 103:17--18). Micah 6:8 sums it up so well that to this day every unsaved, hell bound religious person in America quotes it like he thought it was the plan of salvation. God pities those who fear Him (vs. 13), and His mercy is to them that fear Him (vs. 17). ]{.class_4938}["He that feareth God shall come forth of them all" ]{.class_4939}[(Ecc. 7:18).]{.class_4938}

As someone has once said, "The day has a very brief life: its heart beats twenty four times, and it is dead."

103:17 "But the mercy of the LORD is from everlasting to everlasting upon them that fear him, and his righteousness unto children's children;

18 To such as keep his covenant, and to those that remember his commandments to do them.

19 The LORD hath prepared his throne in the heavens; and his kingdom ruleth over all.

20 Bless the LORD, ye his angels, that excel in strength, that do his commandments, hearkening unto the voice of his word.

21 Bless ye the LORD, all ye his hosts; ye ministers of his, that do his pleasure.

22 Bless the LORD, all his works in all places of his dominion: bless the LORD, O my soul."

[We have already commented on verses 17 and 18. The remaining verses have two applications, primarily to God's general power and authority over the universe at any time, and secondarily to His reign at Jerusalem in the Millennium. The phrase ]{.class_4938}["the LORD hath prepared his throne in the heavens"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 19) will be enough to prevent the Scholar's Union from making any future prophetic application. They read it as "the Lord hath been sitting on His throne in the heavens and will stay there." But the throne]{.class_4938}[ "prepared\...in the heavens"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 19) shows up on this earth as the Lord's throne (Jer. 3:17, 14:21, 33:16--17). ]{.class_4938}["His kingdom ruleth over all"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 19) in the sense of Daniel 4:17, Daniel 4:32, as we have remarked before. The writer of Hebrews knows where we are, so he cites Psalm 104:4 in Hebrews 1:7 since it is attached to the angels of Psalm 103:20. They are called ]{.class_4938}["ministering spirits, sent forth to minister"]{.class_4939}[ in Hebrews 1:7, 14, and he got that expression from Psalm 103:21 (]{.class_4938}["ye ministers of his"]{.class_4939}[).]{.class_4938}

[The angels ]{.class_4938}["excel in strength,"]{.class_4939}[ which is evidenced by the fact that one of them is quite capable of killing 185,000 men in less than twelve hours (2 Kings 19:35). They are God's Green Berets or SS. They hearken to His voice and ]{.class_4938}["do his commandments" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 20). G. R. (in Spurgeon's ]{.class_4938}[Treasury,]{.class_4937}[ p. 300) outlines as follows:]{.class_4938}

[1. The Center of praise (]{.class_4938}["Bless the Lord"]{.class_4939}[).]{.class_4938}

2\. The Concert of praise (angels, the redeemed, ministers, and the surrounding creation).

[3. The Climax of praise (]{.class_4938}["Bless the Lord, 0 my soul"]{.class_4939}[).]{.class_4938}

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ2042.xhtml}

PSALM 104

104:1 "Bless the LORD, O my soul, O LORD my God, thou art very great; thou art clothed with honour and majesty.

2 Who coverest thyself with light as with a garment: who stretchest out the heavens like a curtain:

3 Who layeth the beams of his chambers in the waters: who maketh the clouds his chariot: who walketh upon the wings of the wind:

4 Who maketh his angels spirits; his ministers a flaming fire:

5 Who laid the foundations of the earth, that it should not be removed for ever.

6 Thou coveredst it with the deep as with a garment: the waters stood above the mountains.

7 At thy rebuke they fled; at the voice of thy thunder they hasted away.

8 They go up by the mountains; they go down by the valleys unto the place which thou hast founded for them.

9 Thou hast set a bound that they may not pass over; that they turn not again to cover the earth.

10 He sendeth the springs into the valleys, which run among the hills.

11 They give drink to every beast of the field: the wild asses quench their thirst.

12 By them shall the fowls of the heaven have their habitation, which sing among the branches.

13 He watereth the hills from his chambers: the earth is satisfied with the fruit of thy works."

The Psalm is on the majesty of God. "How Great Thou Art" is the song that best describes the Psalm (see Ps. 86:10). As you can see at a glance, the Psalm describes the "clothing" we talked about under Psalm 102:25--26. One of the ancient commentators called Psalm 104 an "Oratorio of Creation." The "cosmogony," of course, will not fit into the Darwinian-Einsteinian opium dream.

[Verse 1. ]{.class_4938}[Spiritually]{.class_4937}[ speaking, God is clothed with ]{.class_4938}["honour and majesty" ]{.class_4939}[(see Prov. 31:25---]{.class_4938}["strength and honour are HER clothing"]{.class_4939}[). God tells Job to deck himself ]{.class_4938}["with majesty" ]{.class_4939}[(Job 40:10) if he wants to impress everyone how righteous he is.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 2. ]{.class_4938}["God is light" ]{.class_4939}[(1 John 1:5), so He can put on light like a garment. He is the ]{.class_4938}["light" ]{.class_4939}[that is present before sunlight shows up (see Gen. 1:3). The ]{.class_4938}["heavens"]{.class_4939}[ (notice the plural) are a ]{.class_4938}["curtain," ]{.class_4939}[as we noted under Psalm 102, and therefore the measurements of the universe will not be found in Genesis 1. The real ]{.class_4938}["breadth\...length\...depth, and height" ]{.class_4939}[(Eph. 3:18) will be found in Exodus 25--31: the tabernacle. ]{.class_4938}["The beams of his chambers" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 3) will be the foundations of the earth if you make ]{.class_4938}["his chambers" ]{.class_4939}[the planet, but the chances are that the Lord is speaking about more than Genesis 1:9--10. The ]{.class_4938}["chambers" ]{.class_4939}[are probably the]{.class_4938}[ "stories"]{.class_4939}[ of Amos 9:6 that are ]{.class_4938}[contrasted ]{.class_4937}[with the earth. The three heavens of 2 Corinthians 12:2 are typified by Noah's three-storied ]{.class_4938}["ark" ]{.class_4939}[(Gen. 6:16), so the commentators are probably out of the race already before we have gone three verses. What else did you expect? All of them (as Kroll) went into spasms about how "poetic" the Psalm was before they began to comment. Not a man in the union thought it was "scientific" for a minute. Do you think the Author of the Book doesn't know this? ]{.class_4938}["The Lord knoweth the thoughts of man, that they are vanity" ]{.class_4939}[(Ps. 94:11).]{.class_4938}

[The Lord often uses something visible in nature (see Rom. 1:20) to show you the ]{.class_4938}[invisible. ]{.class_4937}[If you really want a proof text for what we have said about the curtains and the tabernacle, then go up to Alaska and watch the Northern Lights. They look like the hanging drapes of a curtain. Do you have to guess from which direction they would appear after reading Psalm 75:6--7? Well, you might if you had followed the blockheads who messed up that passage, but not if you took it at face value with Psalm 48:2. The ]{.class_4938}["waters" ]{.class_4939}[are above the solar system (see Ps. 148:4).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 3. The clouds were His chariot in Ezekiel 1:4, ]{.class_4938}[literall]{.class_4937}[y, and the clouds will be His chariot in Revelation 14:14, ]{.class_4938}[literally]{.class_4937}[. There are clouds that no telescope has picked up yet; they are part of God's covering, along with ]{.class_4938}["honour and majesty" ]{.class_4939}[(see Job 22:13, 26:9, 36:29, and comments in that commentary). The light with which God is clothed is covered with ]{.class_4938}["clouds"]{.class_4939}[ (Job 36:32), so that ]{.class_4938}["men see not the bright light which is in the clouds" ]{.class_4939}[(Job 37:21). All of the Alexandrians had the clouds of earth in mind when they wrote, for the supernatural, again, unhinged their materialistic, carnal minds. The passage in Job 37 matched Psalm 104 so closely that Job says, in conjunction with a bright light in the clouds, that ]{.class_4938}["fair weather cometh out of the\..." ]{.class_4939}[guess where. You get one guess! ]{.class_4938}["Out of the north" ]{.class_4939}[(Job 37:22). Then Job adds the word ]{.class_4938}["majesty"]{.class_4939}[ from Psalm 104:1 so you couldn't miss it. (You know what happened; you don't have to be told. ]{.class_4938}[They all missed it]{.class_4937}[.)]{.class_4938}

[Verse 4. The angels are ]{.class_4938}["ministers"]{.class_4939}[ because they "minister" (see Mark 1:13; Matt. 4:11, 28:2, etc.). The angels can move in fire, for they are spiritual beings like ]{.class_4938}["flaming fire"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 4, see Manoah's case, Judg. 13:20 and the case with the Hebrew children, where the Angel of the Lord shows up, Dan. 3:25). An angel can produce fire by touching something (Judg. 6:21). Elijah's ]{.class_4938}["chariot of fire, and horses of fire" ]{.class_4939}[(2 Kings 2:11) are, in that sense, "angelic" (note Joel 2:3--8). England calls its head of state a "Prime Minister" (George--1916, Baldwin--1923, MacDonald--1929, Chamberlain--1937, Churchill--1940, Atlee--1945, Eden--1955, MacMillan--1957, Thatcher--1987, and so forth).]{.class_4938}

[Now we come "down to earth" (vs. 5), where the apostate Scholar's Union has been the whole time. Observe that this "descent" is indicated in several places in the Bible, but if a man doesn't get Genesis 1:3--14 down right---in regards to water ]{.class_4938}[above ]{.class_4937}[the solar system---then he can never get the order right anywhere else. Note, in particular, Psalm 148. Verses 1--2 deal with a locality beyond Alpha Draconis; verses 3--6 deal with the solar system and the waters above it; verses 7--8 deal with the first heavens and the things that take place there; and verses 9--11 deal with the surface of the earth. The ]{.class_4938}["dragons" ]{.class_4939}[in verse 7, by the way, are connected with "deeps" that are on the earth (]{.class_4938}["the fountains of the great deep," ]{.class_4939}[Gen. 7:11), but by now we have left the "historic positions" of the "majority of Conservative scholars" so far behind they couldn't catch up with the caboose of an Amtrack train.]{.class_4938}

Genesis 1:3--5 is not on this earth and is nowhere near the solar system; it is in the third heaven. Genesis 1:6--8 is not on this earth and is nowhere near the earth; it is above the Milky Way. You descend. You begin at the mercy seat (Exod. 25:8--10) and work your way outward. It is the "science" that the commentators learned in high school that destroyed their belief in the Book.

"[The foundations of the earth" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 5) were laid in Job 38, and they were covered with a flood in Genesis 1:2 ]{.class_4938}["as with a garment" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 6, see 2 Pet. 3:5). ]{.class_4938}["The waters stood above the mountains" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 6) long before Noah's great grandfather was born (see 2 Pet. 3:5). God "rebuked" them in Genesis 1:2--5. At verse 8, you move forward from the first flood to the second one, for the Noahic promise of Genesis 9:11 is found here in verse 9 (]{.class_4938}["that they turn not again to cover the earth"]{.class_4939}[).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 8. The waters evaporate and]{.class_4938}[ "go up"]{.class_4939}[ (see Gen. 8:3). They ]{.class_4938}["go down" ]{.class_4939}[into the ocean basins formed by the flood. From Noah's time on, the boundaries are set (vs. 9). The globe will never be covered with water again; the next time it is covered, it will be with flames (2 Pet. 3:10--12).]{.class_4938}

[The results of the drying of the earth in Genesis has been the creation of ]{.class_4938}["springs" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 10), and the water now runs ]{.class_4938}["among the hills" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 10) and gives drink ]{.class_4938}["to every beast of the field" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 11). The second animal need after air is water. Thus the animals (vs. 12) and the earth itself are sustained by water. The tree ]{.class_4938}["branches" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 12) can't show up unless there is water, for water sustains trees. ]{.class_4938}["The earth is satisfied with the fruit of thy works" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 13). ]{.class_4938}[Absolutely ]{.class_4937}[in the Millennial sense; ]{.class_4938}[temporarily ]{.class_4937}[in the Church Age. Just as we have noticed that the outstanding cause of any nation "going to pot" has been the use of images and "idols" as "aids to worship" (see comments under Psalms 95:3 and 96:4), we must also notice that the first major judgment that God brings on a country in the Near East is drought. Armies and pestilences may follow, but a lack of water is what produces the famine and pestilence (read carefully 2 Sam. 21:1; 1 Kings 8:37, 18:2; 2 Kings 6:25, and 25:3). There are twelve famines recorded in the Bible, with the last one in Acts 11:28. Note the tremendous emphasis the Old Testament puts on DROUGHT as a punishment for the sins of a nation (Lev. 26:4; Deut. 11:11, 11:17, 28:12; 1 Kings 8:35, 36; 17:1, 7; 2 Chron. 6:26--27; Jer. 3:3; 14:4, 22; Amos 4:7; Zech. 14:17--19). Stupid Americans, back in 1989, actually hired an Indian "rain dancer" to try to drum up some rain for Toledo, Ohio, during a drought. (You understand, this was in the "New Age"---the "Age of Enlightenment"---where "man is the measure of all things" because "through his vast learning he has\...etc.") I was raised in Kansas, between 1921 and 1939; tell me all about it! When America voted Prohibition out (1933), God took her gold with it and gave her "nine old men" (see Ps. 94:20 and comments) who turned the country into a pornographic hellhole of crime that made Africa, Asia, and Europe blush. America kills more babies in one year than Hitler killed between 1935 and 1945.]{.class_4938}

[What could be funnier on earth than someone, today, singing ]{.class_4938}[America the Beautiful, ]{.class_4937}[and getting to that ridiculous line, "Thine alabaster cities gleam, undimmed by human tears." New York, perhaps? How about Miami? Oh, I know! How about Houston or Chicago, for an "alabaster city," and how about Washington, D.C. and St. Louis for "undimmed by human tears"? How about the capital of sex perversion: San Francisco? Okay, let's settle for Key West. Now there is an "alabaster city, undimmed by human tears" if you ever saw one!]{.class_4938}

In 1933, we tied the noose around our neck and kicked the stool out from under our feet.

104:14 "He causeth the gram to grow for the cattle, and herb for the service of man: that he may bring forth food out of the earth;

15 And wine that maketh glad the heart of man, and oil to make his face to shine, and bread which strengtheneth man's heart.

16 The trees of the LORD are full of sap; the cedars of Lebanon, which he hath planted;

17 Where the birds make their nests: as for the stork, the fir trees are her house.

18 The high hills are a refuge for the wild goats; and the rocks for the conies.

19 He appointed the moon for seasons: the sun knoweth his going down.

20 Thou makest darkness, and it is night: wherein all the beasts of the forest do creep forth.

21 The young lions roar after their prey, and seek their meat from God.

22 The sun ariseth, they gather themselves together, and lay them down in their dens.

23 Man goeth forth unto his work and to his labour until the evening. "

All is clear; all is anti evolutionary; all is completely contrary to the federal school jungle (formerly "system") from first grade to post grad. The Bible gives God credit for the following things:

1\. Quenching the thirst of birds and animals (vss. 11--12).

2\. The production of apples, oranges, pears, tangerines, satsumas, grapefruit, avocados, cherries, bananas, mangos, figs, and peaches (vs. 13).

3\. Spices, wheat, corn, oats, rice, barley, potatoes, carrots, okra, broccoli, asparagus, rye, tomatoes, celery, brussel sprouts, lettuce, cabbage, peas, and beans (vs. 14).

4\. The time set up daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly (vs. 19).

5\. Night and day (vs. 20).

6\. The growth of cedars, pine trees, box trees, fir trees, elms, oaks, ashes, poplars, birch trees, redwoods, dogwoods, maples, tamarisks, bamboo, and aspens (vs. 16).

[If God were to "die" (like Altizer of Emory University said in 1955) "nature's heart would cease to beat, and we could only stand orphaned on its mighty corpse and wait without hope until we were buried in the same eternal grave of rayless light" (Talmage). This is the doctrinal and religious conviction of the NEA and UNESCO and every major "recognized" scientist on the North American continent. The animals know where their sustenance comes from, if man doesn't. It is]{.class_4938}[ "the]{.class_4939}[ ]{.class_4938}[hearts of the children of men" ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["hell and destruction" ]{.class_4939}[(Prov. 15:11) that are never satisfied; ]{.class_4938}["the earth is satisfied"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 13), although this full satisfaction will not be complete until Romans 8:19 and Isaiah 11:1--11.]{.class_4938}

[The faces of Stephen and Moses literally ]{.class_4938}["shine"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 15) when the oil of the Holy Spirit (see John 3:34; Heb. 1:9) anoints them (Exod. 34:29 and Acts 6:15). ]{.class_4938}[Devotionally speaking, ]{.class_4937}[the ]{.class_4938}["wine"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 15) pictures the blood of Christ (see John 2:3--4 and Deut. 32:14). This is what fixes the ]{.class_4938}["heart." ]{.class_4939}[The]{.class_4938}[ "oil"]{.class_4939}[ ]{.class_4937}[is obviously a type of the Holy Spirit, and the ]{.class_4938}["bread" ]{.class_4939}[is so easy to locate that only "you know who" would fail to locate it (see Luke 4:4; Isa. 55:2, 10--11, and Ecc. 11:1--6). Poor, blind Kroll, at Liberty University, gives us this deplorable explanation: "Such oil was used for various purposes: i.e., to avoid intoxication, to improve the health, to perfume the body\...God sees to it that man has all he needs\...the Promised Land is said to have produced in abundance these three: wine, oil, and bread."]{.class_4938}

You say "Is that it?" That's it! "That's all she wrote."

[With the blood of Christ, the Holy Spirit of Christ, and the Holy Scriptures of Christ before his eyes, the "Champion" from Liberty can only find ]{.class_4938}[one dead, historical explanation ]{.class_4937}[that happened 3,400 years ago. Kroll has been "clocked" out.]{.class_4938}

[The subject had nothing to do with the Jews and the Promised Land; it was addressed to ]{.class_4938}["man"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 15). Evidently the "Champions" at Liberty University are not very well acquainted with the Holy Spirit or the Holy Scriptures.]{.class_4938}

[Verses 16--18 are simple statements of fact. The ]{.class_4938}["conies"]{.class_4939}[ in verse 18 cause some grief among the "educated class," whose "refined sensibilities" make them correct the Bible every time they don't like it: they don't like it ]{.class_4938}[here. ]{.class_4937}[The ]{.class_4938}["conies"]{.class_4939}[ are from Leviticus 11:5 and Deuteronomy 14:7. A ]{.class_4938}["coney,"]{.class_4939}[ according to Webster, is "a rabbit; a quadruped of the genus Lepus which has a short tail and naked ears. In a wild state, the fur is brown, but the color of the domestic rabbit is various." Kroll is just drooling at the mouth to tell you that it can mean "marrots." (That is the chief thrill that a destructive critic gets in life: the opportunity to show they know something that the ]{.class_4938}[AV ]{.class_4937}[translators did not know.) Following suit, the ]{.class_4938}[RSV ]{.class_4937}[cannot wait to tell you that the coney is not really a "rabbit" or a ]{.class_4938}["coney"]{.class_4939}[: it is a "rock badger." But John Wilson (Spurgeon's ]{.class_4938}[Treasury]{.class_4937}[) is literally dying to tell us that it wasn't a "rock badger," but "rather" it was a "shaphan," which is mistaken for a rabbit. But James Hamilton says it is actually a "damon" or "hydrax." (And by the way, it doesn't have any tail if you go by Spurgeon's ]{.class_4938}[Treasury ]{.class_4937}[\[p. 323\].)]{.class_4938}

[Put it this way: ]{.class_4938}[it is a coney]{.class_4937}[, and these (vs. 18) are]{.class_4938}[ "conies."]{.class_4939}

"[The sun knoweth his going down" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 19). The sun, like all the planets, knows its function. The Psalmist uses sunrise and sunset exactly as they are used in every daily newspaper in the United States and Europe. It "goes down" from the human standpoint, and it knows when to go down. Jesus is the ]{.class_4938}["bright and morning star"]{.class_4939}[ (Rev. 22:16) and ]{.class_4938}["the dayspring from on high" ]{.class_4939}[(Luke 1:78), so He knows His ]{.class_4938}["going down" ]{.class_4939}[(Eph. 4:9; Acts 2:27) and His "rising" (Eph. 4:9). God makes darkness (vs. 20) by letting the globe spin at the proper rate.]{.class_4938}

[The lion is picked because he is the king of the beasts and pictures both Jesus Christ (Rev. 5:5) and the Devil (1 Pet. 5:8). Verse 23 shows that the original intention was for man to work from sunup to sundown: a twelve hour day (6 a.m. to 6 p.m.). This would make "wake up" time at 5 a.m. and would make "bed time" somewhere between 8 and 9 p.m., giving every man eight hours of sleep a night. Modern civilization has completely changed all this, and with the change have come a thousand complications including tension, nervous disorders, hyperventilation, heart attacks, high blood pressure, nervous exhaustion, shingles, clots, clogged arteries, phlebitis, hemorrhoids, gastritis, colitis, upset stomach, etc., which are produced by ]{.class_4938}[artificial light]{.class_4937}[ (anything after sundown), ]{.class_4938}[artificial air]{.class_4937}[ (any modern office building or school building), ]{.class_4938}[artificial foods ]{.class_4937}[(any grocery store), and ]{.class_4938}[artificial pain relievers, appetite depressants, ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}["sleeping" pills.]{.class_4937}

[Man has ]{.class_4938}["sought out many inventions" ]{.class_4939}[(Ecc. 7:29). The original plan was a six day week of twelve hours a day. What modern man wants is a five day week of eight hours a day. Some of them don't want even ]{.class_4938}[that.]{.class_4937}

104:24 "O LORD, how manifold are thy works! in wisdom hast thou made them all: the earth is full of thy riches.

25 So is this great and wide sea, wherein are things creeping innumerable, both small and great beasts.

26 There go the ships: there is that leviathan, whom thou hast made to play therein.

27 These wait all upon thee; that thou mayest give them their meat in due season.

28 That thou givest them they gather: thou openest thine hand, they are filled with good.

29 Thou hidest thy face, they are troubled: thou takest away their breath, they die, and return to their dust.

30 Thou sendest forth thy spirit, they are created: and thou renewest the face of the earth.

31 The glory of the LORD shall endure for ever: the LORD shall rejoice in his works.

32 He looketh on the earth, and it trembleth: he toucheth the hills, and they smoke.

33 I will sing unto the LORD as long as I live: I will sing praise to my God while I have my being.

34 My meditation of him shall be sweet: I will be glad in the LORD.

35 Let the sinners be consumed out of the earth, and let the wicked be no more. Bless thou the LORD, O my soul. Praise ye the LORD."

[God's wisdom is so manifest (see Job 12:7--10 in that commentary) that the only sinners who can believe evolution with a clear conscience are sinners who want a religion of FAITH without works or ]{.class_4938}[evidence. ]{.class_4937}[All the evidence points to a Divine Designer with knowledge beyond the pale of human understanding.]{.class_4938}

1\. If the earth rotated at one tenth the speed that it does, all the vegetation that didn't burn up during the day would freeze solid at night.

2\. If the earth was one tenth closer to the sun than it is, everything on it would roast in a solar furnace.

3\. If the moon were fifty thousand miles over your head, instead of 240,000 miles, the tides would go clean over Oklahoma, Nebraska, Colorado, and North Dakota twice a day.

4\. If the diameter of the earth was 7,200 miles instead of 8,000 miles, the whole earth would be ice and snow.

5\. If the average temperature of the earth went up twenty degrees, the Polar caps would melt, and all the cities from Boston to Miami would be drowned out. Ain't "mother nature" wonderful?

"[O LORD\...the earth is full of thy riches" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 24).]{.class_4938}

6\. If the ozone layer (forty miles up) were compressed, it would only be one quarter of an inch thick, yet it "accidently" knocks out eight lethal rays from the sun. Isn't "evolution" terrific?

7\. A female mackerel lays 500,000 eggs at one time. A ten year progeny of this laying, if all survived, would fill the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans with mackerel to a depth of 20,000 feet. If the same thing happened to herring, in forty years the fish would outweigh the earth: 338,000,000,000,\
000,000 tons.

8\. Migratory birds who fly from Alaska to Hawaii to join their parents, who left there the year before, simply take off and fly over two thousand miles without a chart, maps, compass, or any instruction by anyone on where to go. "Mother Nature" is it, that gets them into the same nest with their parents? You wouldn't try to pull our leg, would you, Doc? ("Aeeaye, what's up, Doc?")

9\. The asteroids in the belt between Mars and Jupiter are so small that the gravitational pull from something as large as the moon ought to suck them in. The largest planet in the solar system, right next to them, can't attract them, and some of them are no bigger than an automobile. "Physics," is it, doctor? The whole mess got slung out of the sun did it, doctor? When the earth is the only one on which man could crop up, and after spending more than \$400,000,000,000 to find something like a man somewhere else you haven't found one yet? Do you know what you are, doctor? You are a religious fanatic; a whirling dervish with all the accretions and trappings of a voodoo witch doctor.

Monkey men are "unholy rollers." Their religion has two elements in it: fiction and faith in fiction.

"[Oh LORD, how manifold are thy works!"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 24).]{.class_4938}

[Verses 25--30. The direct subject ]{.class_4938}[is marine animals. ]{.class_4937}[Hence ]{.class_4938}["leviathan" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 26) this time shows up as a whale (never a crocodile). Notice how God constantly confuses men with Scripture for the purpose of destroying the ones who have ]{.class_4938}["idols in their heart"]{.class_4939}[ (Ezek. 14:3). This time, Leviathan has only ]{.class_4938}[one head, ]{.class_4937}[and he is listed as a regular marine animal. Compare this with a seven headed, supernatural "serpent," Revelation 12:3, and regular]{.class_4938}[ "serpents"]{.class_4939}[ (Num. 21:6), and you will see what is going on. The commentators can't see their noses in front of their faces.]{.class_4938}

[Observe, also, that ships are mentioned; this is important, for a little later the crew is said to ]{.class_4938}["return to their dust" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 29). Here the seamen are classified as ]{.class_4938}[part ]{.class_4937}[of the ocean population; observe the same thing again in Psalm 107:30, where the sailors are identified with the very waves that nearly drown them (Ps. 107:25--28). Seafarers and seafaring men have an affinity and a communion with the ocean that is not found in other people. This is only natural, as the first life came from water (Gen. 1:20), and a man's first earthly birth is a water birth (John 3:5). ]{.class_4938}[Moby Dick ]{.class_4937}[is a white whale (see Job 41:32) and represents an "Angel of Light." In Jonah, the whale is a type of ]{.class_4938}[Satan ]{.class_4937}[(see Matt. 12:40; Jonah 2:2; and Jer. 51:34).]{.class_4938}

"[Wherein are things creeping innumerable" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 25) is the right way to put it. The oceans are alive. This is where phosphorus comes from. Here, completely contrary to evolutionary logic, are an innumerable variety of animals with absolutely no reason for the variety and no explanation for the diversity. No evolutionist in two hundred years could explain it. Here are fish who do not blend in with their backgrounds. Here are fish that catch other fish by blowing, by butting them, by squeezing them, and by sticking them; and there is one fish that actually has a projection growing out of the top of his head with a "line" on it and "bait."]{.class_4938}

[Here is a "doctor fish," a "deep sea toro," and a "John Dory." Different tails, different fins, different mouths, different colors. ]{.class_4938}[Why?]{.class_4937}[ Why is "evolution" so random and extravagant? Here is a spade fish and a porcupine fish, that looks like a "puffer." Here is a sergeant major, a chub, a tile fish, a blue parrot fish, a burr fish, eight kinds of "snapper," five kinds of file fish, a bat fish, a jackknife, a frog fish, a flying fish, a lump fish, a Jew fish, seven different "jacks," eleven different kinds of "grouper," four "grunts," a bluefish, a red fish, a lady fish, a bone fish, and then snook, salmon, mullet, mackerel, spots, bass, perch, cod, sharks, whales, dolphins, swordfish, barracuda, cobia, "permits," "lookdowns," "pomanos," "drums," etc. What is going on here?]{.class_4938}

[Is anyone dumb enough to think that ]{.class_4938}[evolution ]{.class_4937}[would go to this much trouble when it would do to simply make a half dozen different fish? Or for that matter, why not only one kind of fish? There is only one kind of ]{.class_4938}[man]{.class_4937}[, according to every humanist on the face of this earth. No fish on earth has a gizzard but a mullet. Do you know of any men who have gizzards? Do you know of any men who have their legs growing out of their torso, so that their feet are at right angels, front and back? Porpoises have tails that are horizontal instead of vertical. The green wrasse's tail is vertical; so are those of the bluefin tuna, the blackfin tuna, the bonito, the albacore, the herring, the salmon, the crevalle, the black margate and the "sailor's choice." If a catfish has "whiskers" and the rest of them don't, are you telling us that only ]{.class_4938}[one ]{.class_4937}[nationality of men should be able to grow whiskers? How come the scamp and the rays don't have any scales? Do you know any race of man whose skin texture is that different from their fellow men that you can scrape off the skin without killing the man?]{.class_4938}

You have to face it, eventually. EVOLUTION EXPLAINS NOTHING. Forth thousand species of plants, fifty thousand kinds of butterflies, two hundred varieties of birds, ten thousand different fish and sixteen thousand animals happened by accident?

[Where are the varieties of ]{.class_4938}[men? ]{.class_4937}[There are only three races (Mongoloid, Negroid, and Caucasian), and all of them have the same blood, and except for rare individual exceptions, occurring only occasionally, they all have the same shaped arms, legs, heads, backs, ears, noses, eyes, mouths, feet, and hands.]{.class_4938}

Whoever saw a man with a nose three feet long?

[A swordfish has a nose that long, and he is a ]{.class_4938}[fish.]{.class_4937}

Whoever saw a man who could breath water?

[A porpoise can breath air, and he is ]{.class_4938}[in ]{.class_4937}[the water. Why isn't there a man, in the air, who can breathe water?]{.class_4938}

Fish vary from two inches long to over twelve feet. Do you know any two inch men, or any twelve foot men? Why not?

[A "flying fish" can glide one hundred feet through the air, while ten thousand of his buddies can't handle more than three to ten feet. You never saw a porpoise in your life that could glide eighty feet over the top of the water. Where are the ]{.class_4938}[men ]{.class_4937}[who can "glide" one hundred feet in the air using just their arms? You say man wasn't ]{.class_4938}[made ]{.class_4937}[to do that. Neither were the fish: ]{.class_4938}[they were made to swim.]{.class_4937}

[Somebody has a problem, and it's not ]{.class_4938}[me. ]{.class_4937}[I know old "Mother Nature" never gave birth to "nothin'," and evolution is about as stable and rational a belief as the perpetual virginity of Mary or faith in Jim Jones' settlement in Guyana. ]{.class_4938}["Thou sendest forth thy spirit, they are created" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 30). THAT is what is going on. They gather what God gives them (vs. 28); from plankton and shrimp and minnows to menhaden and mullet, "fish eats fish." Those on ships (vs. 26) have to wait on God for a catch (see John 21:3--6), and when God hides his ]{.class_4938}["face, they are troubled" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 29). When God opens His hand (vs. 28) the net is full (Luke 5:6). ]{.class_4938}["Thou takest away their breath, they die, and return to their dust" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 29). This ]{.class_4938}[is literally ]{.class_4937}[true of those in the ships who are men, and it is ]{.class_4938}[figuratively ]{.class_4937}[true of the fish in the water who originally were made from dust (see Gen. 2:19), which is usually forgotten in reading Genesis 1:20. ]{.class_4938}["Thou renewest the face of the earth" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 30) is a reference to two renewals, both of them occurring after a flood. The first of these is in Genesis 1:2--3, and the second takes place in Genesis 8--9.]{.class_4938}

A personal note: if you have been raised by the ocean, you don't ever really get it out of your blood. A retired thirty year navy man or "merchant marine" does not settle more than twenty miles inland, for if he does he will be miserable until the day of his death. I was raised in Topeka, Kansas, but every summer for fourteen years (1926--1940) we spent the summers at Rehoboth Beach, Delaware. I wore no shoes three months a year, for fourteen years; consequently, my feet came out 9EEE. As a "beach boy" I was handling umbrellas from fifteen to seventeen; I was a lifeguard from seventeen to twenty. You never get the saltwater out of your clothes. I have lived five miles from the Gulf of Mexico for the last thirty years, and if the Lord tarries I will die here.

[There is something about the ocean that affects a man like his ]{.class_4938}[mother ]{.class_4937}[affects him. It is indescribable, but it is real. I have body surfed the waves of the Atlantic in a "northeaster"; I have slept on the moonlight washed deck of a liberty ship in the South Pacific. I have rolled on the pounding swells off the coasts of Washington and British Columbia till half of the passengers were sick; I have fished the Gulf of Mexico in the morning, at noon, in the evening, and after midnight. I have swum in Manila Bay, Chesapeake Bay, and Pearl Harbor and have gone underwater to retrieve keys, glasses, fishing nets, rods and reels, and billfolds, in waters eight to fifteen feet deep, both in sunlight and moonlight. I have had manta rays come at me when I was waist deep in the water, sharks dive for me when I was in two feet of water, stingrays strike me in muddy water, bluefish bite my shirt sleeve and bust their teeth out chomping down on a knife blade jammed in their mouth to retrieve a hook; and I have stood in schools of "spots" and "L Ys" at night that were so thick they literally brushed against my bare feet and arms, passing through. The water is alive;]{.class_4938}[ life]{.class_4937}[ ]{.class_4938}[comes from water ]{.class_4937}[(Gen. 1:20). I have never been more aware of the ]{.class_4938}["honour and majesty" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 1) and ]{.class_4938}["wisdom" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 24) of God than when out on the ocean at night or in the ocean or the bays at night. ]{.class_4938}["O LORD, how manifold are thy works!" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 24).]{.class_4938}

[Up pops the Second Advent again. ]{.class_4938}["He looketh on the earth, and it trembleth: he toucheth the hills, and they smoke" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 32). The reference is to Psalm 68 and Psalm 18:7. This is easy to see when one reads the prayer of verse 35. The ]{.class_4938}["sinners"]{.class_4939}[ and the ]{.class_4938}["wicked" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 35 are NOT ]{.class_4938}["consumed out of the earth" ]{.class_4939}[until the Lord lands on the earth. The "Washouts for Christ," at Lynchburg, can only find a "poetic reference" in the first clause; which is literal, and not poetic. All they can get in the second clause is a reference to dead history fulfilled in the past at Mt. Sinai in 1500 B.C. You could expect as much. Kroll, a Premillennialist, is dead set on removing every reference in the Psalms to the Second Coming of Christ that he can possibly remove.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 33 is self explanatory. It can be Christ Himself saying it, or it can be any saint in any age, or it can have a direct application to a thankful Israelite who has "endured to the end" and entered ]{.class_4938}["the kingdom prepared\...from the foundation of the world" ]{.class_4939}[(Matt. 25:34). We may suppose that ]{.class_4938}["in the LORD" ]{.class_4939}[can fit verse 34 to us, although the expression here would be more like Revelation 14:13 and not like the ]{.class_4938}["in Christ" ]{.class_4939}[which Paul uses (Rom. 16:3, 7). While a Christian has his ]{.class_4938}["being" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 33), he should make it a habit to praise the Lord and sing unto Him (see Ps. 7:17; 9:2, 11; and 13:6). ]{.class_4938}["Praise ye the LORD" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 35).]{.class_4938}

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ2144.xhtml}

PSALM 105

105:1 "O give thanks unto the LORD; call upon his name: make known his deeds among the people.

2 Sing unto him, sing psalms unto him: talk ye of all his wondrous works.

3 Glory ye in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the LORD.

4 Seek the LORD, and his strength: seek his face evermore.

5 Remember his marvellous works that he hath done; his wonders, and the judgments of his mouth;

6 O ye seed of Abraham his servant, ye children of Jacob his chosen.

7 He is the LORD our God: his judgments are in all the earth.

8 He hath remembered his covenant for ever, the word which he commanded to a thousand generations.

9 Which covenant he made with Abraham, and his oath unto Isaac;

10 And confirmed the same unto Jacob for a law, and to Israel for an everlasting covenant:

11 Saying, Unto thee will I give the land of Canaan, the lot of your inheritance.

12 When they were but a few men in number; yea, very few, and strangers in it.

13 When they went from one nation to another, from one kingdom to another people;

14 He suffered no man to do them wrong: yea, he reproved kings for their sakes;

15 Saying, Touch not mine anointed, and do my prophets no harm."

[We will not spend a lot of time in detail with what is about to follow, because we have already written more than one thousand pages on these matters (see ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[Exodus]{.class_4937}[). The subject matter in this Psalm, from verse 6 to verse 41, is discussed in those two commentaries and is discussed in great detail along with the critical notes of the commentators and the modern translations (which corrupt more than fifty verses in each book).]{.class_4938}

[This Psalm is the first in a series of five Psalms that feature the expression ]{.class_4938}["O give thanks unto the LORD." ]{.class_4939}[The others are Psalm 106, 107, 118, and 136. This Psalm, with Psalm 106, reviews the history of Israel as Psalm 78 reviewed it, and Psalm 106 ends the fourth collection of the Psalms.]{.class_4938}

Verses 1--5 contain nine commandments for a saint in any age. We are to:

[1. ]{.class_4938}["Give thanks" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 1).]{.class_4938}

[2. ]{.class_4938}["Call upon his name" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 1).]{.class_4938}

[3. ]{.class_4938}["Make known his deeds" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 1).]{.class_4938}

[4. ]{.class_4938}["Sing unto him" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 2).]{.class_4938}

[5. ]{.class_4938}["Talk\...of\...his wondrous works" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 2).]{.class_4938}

[6. ]{.class_4938}["Glory\...in his holy name" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 3).]{.class_4938}

[7. ]{.class_4938}["Rejoice"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 3).]{.class_4938}

[8. ]{.class_4938}["Seek his face" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 4).]{.class_4938}

[9. ]{.class_4938}["Remember his marvelous works" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 5).]{.class_4938}

[These admonitions are specifically addressed to the ]{.class_4938}["seed of Abraham" ]{.class_4939}[and the children of ]{.class_4938}["Jacob his chosen" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 6). Nonetheless, they are good spiritual advice for anyone.]{.class_4938}

[The word]{.class_4938}[ "seek"]{.class_4939}[ occurs three times in the first four verses. Spurgeon says, properly, that "to seek His face is to desire His presence, His smile, His favor consciously enjoyed." This is fine ]{.class_4938}[spiritual application ]{.class_4937}[because actually (and ]{.class_4938}[doctrinally]{.class_4937}[) there is no Lord left for the born again child of God to ]{.class_4938}["seek"]{.class_4939}[: Christ dwells ]{.class_4938}[inside ]{.class_4937}[his body (1 Cor. 3:16). Spurgeon says: "First we seek Him, then His strength, then His face\...this seeking must never cease" (]{.class_4938}[Treasury,]{.class_4937}[ p.]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4937}[337). Still, the doctrinal part is plainly Jewish, for behold: ]{.class_4938}["He is the LORD our God" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 7). The admonitions were not to the spiritual seed of Abraham (as Spurgeon tries desperately to prove a number of times), but they go to Isaac (vs. 9) and then to Jacob (vs. 10), and then to the twelve tribes: Israel (vs. 10). They do not have to do with getting to heaven or getting into Christ; they have to do with getting ]{.class_4938}[the land ]{.class_4937}[that the PLO and the Catholic popes want given to the Arabs: ]{.class_4938}["the land of Canaan, the lot of your inheritance," ]{.class_4939}[vs. 11.]{.class_4938}

"[The word which he commanded to a thousand generations" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 8). Clarence Larkin (1929) took this ]{.class_4938}[literally, ]{.class_4937}[and then by assuming that a generation was thirty three years---the length of Christ's earthly life---he constructed an age ("the ages of the ages") that preceded eternity. Larkin applied this to 1 Corinthians 15:28 and believed that the real eternity will not start---that is, the timeless ages that are unmarked by months or days (Isa. 66:23)---until 33,000 years after Revelation 21:1--20. At this time, he has Christ returning into one essence and one substance with the Father (1 Cor. 15:28), so ]{.class_4938}["that God may be all in all." ]{.class_4939}[This would duplicate the conditions that existed before Genesis 1:1, where the Lord Jesus would go back into the Trinity as "the Word" (1 John 5:7--8). (You can work on it. I have for about forty one years, and I haven't got it all figured out by a "long shot.")]{.class_4938}

[The ]{.class_4938}["covenant"]{.class_4939}[ of verse 8 is not the "elect" covenant about which Spurgeon talks. Further, it has nothing to do with New Testament salvation. This covenant is the one God gave to Abraham three times: in Genesis 14, 15, and 22. It is a land grant with a clear title deed. Bullinger insists the ]{.class_4938}["Israel" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 10 is the spiritual seed, but it is ]{.class_4938}[not. ]{.class_4937}[It is true that only saved Israelites will finally wind up possessing the land, but Israel possessed the land in Solomon's day when ]{.class_4938}[none of them ]{.class_4937}[were "spiritual seed."]{.class_4938}

[Jacob gets this land promise in Genesis 28:13, after Isaac got it again in Genesis 26:3. The ]{.class_4938}["everlasting covenant" ]{.class_4939}[(vs . 10) is the land of Canaan. ]{.class_4938}["When they were but a few men in number" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 12) holds true even to Jacob's day (see Gen. 34:30). The Hebrews were the "least of all people" in the days of Abraham and Isaac (see Deut. 26:5) because Abraham had no children for one hundred years, and then, he only had one with Sarah. (He had eight total.) Isaac and Rebekah were barren for twenty years, and in the remaining years of their lives, they had only two children. The "Hebrews" (from ]{.class_4938}["Eber," ]{.class_4937}[1 Chron. 1:25--27) were not a great people until they came out of Egypt. God protected Abraham in his dealings with Pharaoh (Gen. 12:17) and Abimelech (Gen. 20:3--6), and in his military excursions (see Gen. 14:15--16). We have discussed these matters in great detail in ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis, ]{.class_4937}[which see. God protected Isaac in dealing with Abimelech (Gen. 26:11); both about his wife and about the wells (Gen. 26:13--22). ]{.class_4938}["He suffered no man to do them wrong" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 14). Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob are all called ]{.class_4938}["prophets" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 15), so we immediately run into the faculties and staffs of BJU, BBC, Liberty University, Tennessee Temple, etc., "strutting their stuff" (that they got from unsaved German rationalists) and telling us that a prophet is not really a "foreteller" as much as a "forthteller." (Ever hear that baloney before? That is "standard" at Grace Theological Seminary, Louisville, Denver, New Orleans, and Ft. Worth.)]{.class_4938}

[We will allow the Holy Spirit to correct the slobbering fanaticism of the Scholar's Union. For Abraham ]{.class_4938}[prophecies, ]{.class_4937}[see Genesis 22:8 and Genesis 24:7. But that is a small part of it. Can you imagine Abraham not discussing the future with anyone after God told him the words you find recorded in Genesis 12:1--3; 13:14--17; 15:5--17; 17:19--21, and 22:16--18? Abraham was given enough prophetic material to hold a two week "Conference on Prophecy." In his last few years, Jacob does nothing but foretell the future (look at Gen. 48 and 49). You have to humor people like Harold Willmington, James Price, Fred Afman, Stewart Custer, Woodrow Kroll, and Elmer Towns. They just aren't too bright. If Isaac was not telling the future (and I don't mean "forthtelling") in Genesis 27:28--29, and 27:39--40, what was he doing? Reading the ]{.class_4938}[New York Times?]{.class_4937}

[Verse 15 is a favorite verse for some pastors to prove that no one in the congregation had better lay a hand on him. This may be "all very well" in America in the twentieth century; but it didn't work anywhere in England, France, Italy, or Germany in the Dark Ages, and don't think that it will work in Mexico or South Ireland now. I don't think you could apply it to five hundred pastors in China and Russia between 1920 and 1960. In the New Testament, all of the saints are ]{.class_4938}["anointed"]{.class_4939}[ (1 John 2:27), and there is no class that has any special protection. Some preachers may get it, but this is not because they are ]{.class_4938}["anointed." ]{.class_4939}[All ]{.class_4937}[of the Lord's people are anointed, including the deacons (]{.class_4938}[if they are saved]{.class_4937}[), even if they are threatening you with physical violence, cutting off your salary, and lying about you.]{.class_4938}

I've always had a lot of fun with the verse because I played ice hockey, soccer, and touch football up into my late sixties. I was "roughed up" by young Christian men about once a week. Nothing would be funnier than for me to call out from the bottom of a pile of hockey players, "Stop kicking me! I am the Lord's anointed!"

[At sixty nine, I have had my helmet knocked off my head twice playing ice hockey and have had a two hundred-pound man crash into the "crease" with one skate going over my right shoulder and one over my left, while he hit the "gate" so hard he drove it back to the boards. I have stepped out of swimming pools (over the age of fifty) after playing "blood ball" with young men in their teens and their twenties and had enough bloody stripes on my back to qualify for ]{.class_4938}["forty stripes save one" ]{.class_4939}[(2 Cor. 11:24). On some occasions, I have come out with one whole arm black from the armpit to the wrist. In defending the goal in bloodball, I have had fists and elbows in the mouth so many times that I was missing two front teeth (after I was forty five) and had to wear a rubber mouthpiece (after fifty five) to keep from getting some more knocked out. I can see myself---trying to hang on to the cement wall, by the goal with one hand, fending off two young men with the other, while some thirty year old former college football player is butting me with his head---we wore no equipment---hollering]{.class_4938}[ "touch not mine anointed, and do my prophets no harm"]{.class_4939}[!]{.class_4938}

"[Touch not" ]{.class_4939}[is a clear warning that there are some things you had better "keep your hands off of." One is another man's wife (Prov. 6:29). Christ said ]{.class_4938}["let them alone" ]{.class_4939}[(Matt. 15:14) in regards to the outstanding orthodox religious leaders of His day. There are some other things that you had better not "mess with."]{.class_4938}

1\. God's glory.

2\. The temple of the Holy Ghost.

3\. The poor and needy.

4\. Orphans and widows.

5\. The Jews.

6\. The Holy Bible.

[Keep your dirty, cotton pickin', unbelieving hands off our Book. If you don't believe it, leave it alone. We don't revise any of your college textbooks, even yearly, although we don't believe ]{.class_4938}[three fourths ]{.class_4937}[of the crap that is in them to start with.]{.class_4938}

105:16 "Moreover he called for a famine upon the land: he brake the whole staff of bread.

17 He sent a man before them, even Joseph, who was sold for a servant:

18 Whose feet they hurt with fetters: he was laid in iron:

19 Until the time that his word came: the word of the LORD tried him.

20 The king sent and loosed him; even the ruler of the people, and let him go free.

21 He made him lord of his house, and ruler of all his substance:

22 To bind his princes at his pleasure; and teach his senators wisdom."

[Nothing is difficult. The famine was ]{.class_4938}["called for" ]{.class_4939}[in Genesis 41:27, and Joseph was down in Egypt (vs. 17) before the famine came. Here, we learn that for some time Joseph lay ]{.class_4938}["in iron" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 18), although this may have been only a nighttime precaution, as he was a "trustee" in the prison (Gen. 39:21--22) and given freedom in the daytime in a way the other prisoners were not. ]{.class_4938}["Until the time that his word came: the word of the LORD tried him" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 19). The ]{.class_4938}["his"]{.class_4939}[ and the ]{.class_4938}["him"]{.class_4939}[ are both references to ]{.class_4938}[Joseph. ]{.class_4937}[It is the word of the Lord that puts Joseph to the test, and then when the test is up (Gen. 40:8), the word of the Lord comes to him (Gen. 40:12), and ]{.class_4938}[he ]{.class_4937}[can give it out: which he does (Gen. 41:16, 28--36).]{.class_4938}

[The essential thing to notice is that there was not one ]{.class_4938}[written statement ]{.class_4937}[anywhere in the Bible about God intending to "try" Joseph. No decree or prophesy is ]{.class_4938}[recorded; ]{.class_4937}[still, credit is given to ]{.class_4938}["the word of the LORD" ]{.class_4939}[for the testing. Was it unspoken? It certainly was ]{.class_4938}[unwritten. ]{.class_4937}[Observe that the ]{.class_4938}["words"]{.class_4939}[ of God's mouth in the Old Testament (Job 23:12) were sometimes ]{.class_4938}[spoken ]{.class_4937}[without anyone setting them on paper (or stone). Again, note that God can sometimes decree a thing with spoken words (1 Kings 22:19--23), that are ]{.class_4938}[unheard ]{.class_4937}[by human beings. The decree of Daniel 4:13--17 wasn't heard by anyone. Nebuchadnezzar heard it in a dream (Dan. 4:5) and didn't know what it was when he heard it. In Hosea 10:10, God tells Hosea that He desires to do something with Israel, and He does it.]{.class_4938}

[Edward Luscombe Hall (Spurgeon's ]{.class_4938}[Treasury]{.class_4937}[) has a great sermon on ]{.class_4938}["the word of the Lord" ]{.class_4939}[trying the saints, but throughout his outline he is talking about what God said ]{.class_4938}[as He recorded it in the Scriptures. Joseph had no Scriptures. ]{.class_4937}[The best anyone could say is that after Joseph heard the interpretation of his dream (Gen. 37:7), he believed he would be some kind of a king someday. Being chained in a prison is not exactly reigning on a throne.]{.class_4938}

[Verses 20--22 are self explanatory. See ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis: ]{.class_4937}[Genesis 39--43.]{.class_4938}

105:23 "Israel also came into Egypt; and Jacob sojourned in the land of Ham.

24 And he increased his people greatly; and made them stronger than their enemies.

25 He turned their heart to hate his people, to deal subtilly with his servants.

26 He sent Moses his servant; and Aaron whom he had chosen.

27 They shewed his signs among them, and wonders in the land of Ham.

28 He sent darkness, and made it dark; and they rebelled not against his word.

29 He turned their waters into blood, and slew their fish.

30 Their land brought forth frogs in abundance, in the chambers of their kings.

31 He spake, and there came divers sorts of flies, and lice in all their coasts.

32 He gave them hail for rain, and flaming fire in their land.

33 He smote their vines also and their fig trees; and brake the trees of their coasts.

34 He spake, and the locusts came, and caterpillars, and that without number.

35 And did eat up all the herbs in their land, and devoured the fruit of their ground.

36 He smote also all the firstborn in their land, the chief of all their strength.

37 He brought them forth also with silver and gold: and there was not one feeble person among their tribes."

[We have covered nearly all of this material in ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus, ]{.class_4937}[which see.]{.class_4938}

[Notice that it is God who "turns the heart" of the Hyksos kings to hate Israel (Exod. 4:21): see the comments under Psalm 90:3, and see why we corrected 100 percent of the Nicolaitans in their private interpretation of that passage. ]{.class_4938}["He turned their heart to hate his people" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 28). Verse 26 is described in the commentary on Exodus, as are the details of verses 27--37. ]{.class_4938}["They rebelled not against his word" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 28) is a reference to Israel obeying the Lord's commandments regarding the Passover. Somewhere in his reading, Spurgeon got the wild idea that Moses and Aaron didn't "rebel against God's word" when he commanded them to bring about the darkness. But this is "over the hill." You don't find one time that God commanded Moses to bring a plague that Moses hesitated about it long enough to cough. John Gill thinks the "they" is a reference to the signs and wonders of verse 27; i.e., the signs and wonders didn't rebel. God's word to Moses and Aaron to bring plagues wasn't the kind of a thing against which either man was tempted to rebel. However, Moses ]{.class_4938}[does ]{.class_4937}[rebel against God's word in his first encounter with Pharaoh, because he refuses to say the words God told him to say (see Exod. 4:23 and compare it with Exod. 5:1--2). Notice that this disobedience sets the children of Israel back several weeks from their deliverance (Exod. 5:5--18). A much better interpretation would be that the children of Israel did not rebel against God's word one time between Exodus 4:29 (where Moses showed up) and Exodus 12:51 (when they left). They obeyed the instructions found in Exodus 4:31; 9:19--21; 11:2; and 12:1--28.]{.class_4938}

"[Caterpillars" ]{.class_4939}[are added here, so we know they accompanied the locusts (Exod. 10:4--14).]{.class_4938}

[Verses 36 and 37 are discussed in ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus.]{.class_4937}

105:38 "Egypt was glad when they departed: for the fear of them fell upon them.

39 He spread a cloud for a covering; and fire to give light in the night.

40 The people asked, and he brought quails, and satisfied them with the bread of heaven.

41 He opened the rock, and the waters gushed out; they ran in the dry places like a river.

42 For he remembered his holy promise, and Abraham his servant.

43 And he brought forth his people with joy, and his chosen with gladness:

44 And gave them the lands of the heathen: and they inherited the labour of the people;

45 That they might observe his statutes, and keep his laws. Praise ye the LORD."

[All of the verses (38--45) are discussed in detail in the commentary on Exodus. Verse 44 takes you into the times of Joshua. ]{.class_4938}["They inherited the labour of the people" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 44), in the sense that they became possessors of houses, vineyards, lands, and cattle that were not originally theirs (see Deut. 6:10--12). These unearned blessings and luxuries were so ]{.class_4938}["that they might observe his statutes, and keep his laws" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 45).]{.class_4938}

[Spiritually, we are delivered from Egypt to serve God. We are delivered from Pharaoh by the blood of the Lamb. We are baptized into Christ through the "Red Sea" of His own blood. We then start our wilderness journey to the Promised Land. We have divine guidance the whole trip (vs. 39), and our "needs" are met (vss. 40--41). When we prosper we must give God credit for it and not forget our "former estate" and the ]{.class_4938}["pit" ]{.class_4939}[from whence we were ]{.class_4938}["digged" ]{.class_4939}[(Isa. 51:1). We are saved to serve and to obey.]{.class_4938}

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ2234.xhtml}

PSALM 106

106:1 "Praise ye the LORD, O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.

2 Who can utter the mighty acts of the LORD? who can shew forth all his praise?

3 Blessed are they that keep judgment, and he that doeth righteousness at all times.

4 Remember me, O LORD, with the favour that thou bearest unto thy people: O visit me with thy salvation;

5 That I may see the good of thy chosen, that I may rejoice in the gladness of thy nation, that I may glory with thine inheritance."

[Spurgeon believes the author is David, while others (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown) attribute it to a writer after 600 B.C. Kroll takes a middle position, and A. J. Motyer sides with Spurgeon by saying that verse 47 could refer to "local captivities" of "some" of the Israelites during the books of Judges or 2 Samuel. We think that David for an author (at least for the whole Psalm) is extremely far fetched. A prayer for God to deliver the nation of Israel (]{.class_4938}["us,"]{.class_4939}[ vs. 47) is hardly a theme for David to "harp on," even ]{.class_4938}[before ]{.class_4937}[he is a king. Israel wasn't ]{.class_4938}["captives"]{.class_4939}[ among the ]{.class_4938}["heathen" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 46--47) until the times of Sennacherib and Shalmaneser (2 Kings 17--18).]{.class_4938}

[This is the first in a small collection of "Halleluia" Psalms---the others being Psalms 111, 112, 113, 135, 146, 147, 148, 149, and 150. Eight of those Psalms end with ]{.class_4938}["Praise ye the LORD."]{.class_4939}

Verse 1 is self explanatory.

[Verse 2. No one can utter all of ]{.class_4938}["the mighty acts of the LORD" ]{.class_4939}[because no one has ever seen one tenth of them. John said that if you wrote down just what Jesus did in one lifetime, the world could not contain the record of it (John 21:25). Who among us can describe what took place in Genesis 1:1? Or for that matter, Genesis 1:3? Who among us has seen the glory that God gave Jesus Christ before the foundation of the world (John 17:24)? What fifty thousand men, operating 500,000 computers, could tell you what God did through six thousand years for individual men, at opportune times, when they were about to be shot, tortured, stabbed, imprisoned, starved to death, robbed, drowned, burned to death, or have their lungs collapse on them? If nature is the creation of God (]{.class_4938}[and it is]{.class_4937}[) and nature obeys Him (]{.class_4938}[and it does]{.class_4937}[), how could three thousand journalists tell you in three thousand years what God actually did when Krakatoa and Mt. Pelee and Vesuvius "blew their stacks"?]{.class_4938}

It is God who sends hurricanes, typhoons, floods, earthquakes, tornadoes, droughts, and monsoons, if the material in Psalms 147 and 148 is correct.

[Do you know why God destroyed certain houses, trailers, huts, highways, roads, cars, and trucks and left others? Do you know why one person drowned and the one right next to him didn't? Do you know why four survived a plane crash in which two hundred were killed? Or why 150 survived when two were killed? Do you know why one man fell 3,000 feet without a parachute and lived, while another man fell off a ten foot step ladder and died? Of course you don't. One survivor of the Bataan Death March (1942) got killed three years later, when he was being liberated by American troops, ]{.class_4938}[after the war stopped. ]{.class_4937}[A case of tomatoes, dropped by a Red Cross plane, hit him on the head while he was still in a Japanese prison camp. One of the Americans who set up the flag at "Iwo" (an American Indian) came home unwounded but died of pneumonia in less than three years, after being drunk most of that time.]{.class_4938}

[Do you realize how impossible these things are to ]{.class_4938}["utter"]{.class_4939}[? They are so unutterable that intellectuals throw in the towel and insist that there can't be any God, because nothing can be explained when it comes to death, handicaps, tragedy, pain, and sorrow. ]{.class_4938}[The intellectuals have nothing to offer.]{.class_4937}

[If those of us who believe the Book cannot ]{.class_4938}["utter the mighty acts of the Lord,"]{.class_4939}[ could some lame brained, self-righteous idiot like Albert Einstein, Karl Marx, Sigmund Freud, Bertrand Russell, William Glasser or the leaders of the National Education Association ever ]{.class_4938}["utter" ]{.class_4939}[them? No way, Jose.]{.class_4938}

[Again, if the heavenly hosts in God's very presence (Rev. 4:8) cannot ]{.class_4938}["shew forth all his praise," ]{.class_4939}[who ]{.class_4938}[down here ]{.class_4937}[can do it?]{.class_4938}

[Observe the legal character of Old Testament salvation (and in the Tribulation, under faith and works) given clearly in verse 3---to the consternation of all the milksops at BBC and Liberty University: ]{.class_4938}["He that doeth righteousness at all times." ]{.class_4939}[This is hardly what Carroll, Truett, Broadus, Haldemann, Spurgeon, Norris, and Falwell teach when it comes to salvation. It is the salvation of Romans 2:6--7, not Romans 10:9--13. ]{.class_4938}["O visit me with thy salvation" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 4) is not a salvation that comes "by grace through faith. " It is a salvation that comes through keeping the judgments (vs. 3), the covenant (Ps. 25:10, 44:17, 78:10, 37), and the commandments (Ps. 78:7, 103:18, 111:10; Rev. 12:17, 14:12, and 22:14).]{.class_4938}

[Subtly (and without being detected by the Scholar's Union), a Gentile has inserted his own prayer right in the middle of the opening paragraph. The ]{.class_4938}["I"]{.class_4939}[ of verse 5 and the ]{.class_4938}["me" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 4 are in absolute contrast to ]{.class_4938}["thy people," "thy chosen," "thy nation," ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["thine inheritance." ]{.class_4939}[One hundred percent of the scholars (Baethgen, Bullinger, Dummelow, Motyer, Yates, Ewald, Briggs, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Hengstenberg, et al.), to the last man, breeze by the reference because of the ]{.class_4938}["we"]{.class_4939}[ that shows up in verse 6. ]{.class_4938}["Rightly dividing the word of truth" ]{.class_4939}[(2 Tim. 2:15) has never been the strong suit of Conservatives and Fundamentalists who mess with the Book. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown say that the Psalmist is "representative of Israel." How this happens only God knows, for how could Israel want to glory with ]{.class_4938}[Israel ]{.class_4937}[(vs. 5) and Israel rejoice in the gladness ]{.class_4938}[of Israel ]{.class_4937}[(vs. 5)? Nonsense. The ]{.class_4938}["we"]{.class_4939}[ of verse 6 is the Psalmist representing Israel, and nothing could be plainer than that. But who is this ]{.class_4938}["I"]{.class_4939}[ and ]{.class_4938}["me"]{.class_4939}[ of verses 4 and 5? Why, don't you know? It is someone from Deuteronomy 32:43 rejoicing in the restoration of Israel during the Millennium. (Ps. 68:3, 70:4, 97:1, 98:4; Isa. 65:18, 66:10, 61:11; Jer. 3:17, 4:2, etc.)]{.class_4938}

[The wording of the passage simply confounded the "historicizing historicists" with their "historic positions" who wouldn't leave "history" to believe the Book. That explains why the command to rightly divide the word of truth (2 Tim. 2:15) has been removed from every other English Bible in the world---including all the Bibles recommended by Bob Jones IV, John MacArthur, James Combs, A. V. Henderson, Jerry Falwell, Gary Hudson, and the National Council of Christian Churches. There is no command to ]{.class_4938}[study ]{.class_4937}[the Bible in the ASV, ]{.class_4938}[NASV, RSV, NRSV, NIV, NKJV, ]{.class_4937}[or any other English version. The last fifty "updated versions" were translated by Nicolaitans who wanted the "preeminent place," and as surely as God made little green apples, they didn't want any Christian studying his Bible and finding the proper divisions, ]{.class_4938}[so they erased the command. ]{.class_4937}[This sabotage reveals the real moral character of Bob Jones III, Bob Jones IV, James Combs, Arthur Farstad, Ankerberg, Thieme, MacArthur, Swindoll, Curtis Hutson, John R. Rice, and any fifty "celebrities" just like them. Each of them recommended some version of the Bible that did away with the commandment to study the Bible (2 Tim. 2:15). If you are a cultic heretic who believes in "Dead Duck Otherism" (instead of "King James Onlyism"), you are guilty of one of the most blasphemous, treacherous, and flagrant acts of carnality that a Christian could commit. As a fire in the interior of the image was ventilated, the whole image would become red hot. At the proper moment, the children (usually babies under five years old) were placed on the open palms of the image. To drown out their screaming, the drums beat. 41 And he gave them into the hand of the heathen; and they that hated them ruled over them.

SECTION FIVE

INTRODUCTION

[Because of some similarities with Psalms 106 and 105, Dummelow believes Psalm 107 was originally in the collection of ]{.class_4938}[the fourth section]{.class_4937}[. However, the similitude is not that fitting. A much more universal theme is found in Psalm 107 than in either of the Psalms that precede it. A great deal more of Psalm 107 can be applied to the entire human race than the material in Psalms 105 and 106. True, there are some references to Israel (vss. 4, 7, 33, 34, 36, and 39), but the ]{.class_4938}["whoso is wise"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 43) is aimed at anybody who is wise. There is no Jew, Gentile, or Church distinction (see 1 Cor. 10:32).]{.class_4938}

Fifteen Psalms in this final collection are said to be David's. A group in the collection is called the "Hallel Psalms" (Ps. 113--118), another group is the Songs of Ascents (or "Degrees," Ps. 120--134), and then there is the Hallelujah group (Ps. 145--150). The fifteen "songs of degrees" are supposed to match the fifteen years that God added to Hezekiah's life in Isaiah 38:5. Bullinger says ten were written by Hezekiah to match the "sun dial" (Isa. 38:8), and the other five were written by someone like David or Solomon. Tradition says these songs were sung on the steps of the Temple (assuming there were fifteen steps). This is where Dummelow got the idea of erasing the word "degrees," and converting it to "ascents." However, Isaiah 38:20 would indicate that the degrees are "degrees" (as in the original English) and refer to Hezekiah's experience. These fifteen Psalms (120--134) are arranged in five groups of three each.

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ2362.xhtml}

PSALM 107

107:1 "O give thanks unto the LORD, for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.

2 Let the redeemed of the LORD say so, whom he hath redeemed from the hand of the enemy;

3 And gathered them out of the lands, from the east, and from the west, from the north, and from the south.

4 They wandered in the wilderness in a solitary way; they found no city to dwell in.

5 Hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted in them.

6 Then they cried unto the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them out of their distresses,

7 And he led them forth by the right way, that they might go to a city of habitation.

8 Oh that men would praise the LORD for his goodness, and for his wonderful works to the children of men!"

Verse 1 is self explanatory.
