PSALM 112

112:1 "Praise ye the LORD. Blessed is the man that feareth the LORD, that delighteth greatly in his commandments.

2 His seed shall be mighty upon earth: the generation of the upright shall be blessed.

3 Wealth and riches shall be in his house: and his righteousness endureth for ever.

4 Unto the upright there ariseth light in the darkness: he is gracious, and full of compassion, and righteous.

5 A good man sheweth favor, and lendeth: he will guide his affairs with discretion.

6 Surely he shall not be moved for ever: the righteous shall be in everlasting remembrance.

7 He shall not be afraid of evil tidings: his heart is fixed, trusting in the LORD.

8 His heart is established, he shall not be afraid, until he see his desire upon his enemies.

9 He hath dispersed, he hath given to the poor; his righteousness endureth for ever; his horn shall be exalted with honour.

10 The wicked shall see it, and be grieved; he shall gnash with his teeth, and melt away: the desire of the wicked shall perish."

[The Psalm is describing the "blessed man" already mentioned in Psalm 1. He is called ]{.class_4938}["a good man" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 5), and the words used to describe him and his kind are ]{.class_4938}["upright" ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["righteous" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 2, 4). Paul says, ]{.class_4938}["There is none righteous, no, not one"]{.class_4939}[ (Rom. 3:10), and Solomon says, ]{.class_4938}["There is not a just man upon earth, that doeth good, and sinneth not" ]{.class_4939}[(Ecc. 7:20), so the devotional "spiritualizers" are going to have a problem cramming a Christian into the passage; at least doctrinally. We can preach from the Psalm, however, because good doctrine is not always good preaching (take Psa. 74:14 or Psa. 59:14), and good preaching does not always make good doctrine (for example, the way Heb. 9:22 and Rev. 3:20 are used in soulwinning).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 1. A man who is ]{.class_4938}["blessed"]{.class_4939}[ is a man who has learned ]{.class_4938}["the beginning of wisdom," ]{.class_4939}[for that was the preceding statement in the last Psalm. We may take Romans 7:22 to prove that a New Testament Christian "delighteth" in God's commandments, but the context is the Old Testament law. The best way to handle the man described, doctrinally, is simply to apply it to Jesus Christ:]{.class_4938}

[1. He was ]{.class_4938}["heard in that he feared" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 1 with Heb. 5:7).]{.class_4938}

[2. He certainly was, and is, ]{.class_4938}["upright" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 2, 4).]{.class_4938}

[3. He begat "a seed" (vs. 2), and it will be ]{.class_4938}["mighty upon earth."]{.class_4939}

4\. He is now the most wealthy individual in the entire universe (vs. 3); see John 17:5, 22.

[The Lord Jesus ]{.class_4938}["ariseth" ]{.class_4939}[in the Tribulation to the ]{.class_4938}["upright" ]{.class_4939}[who ]{.class_4938}["look for him" ]{.class_4939}[(see Heb. 9:28), and we have discussed this literal "light" (vs. 4) many times (see Ps. 4:6, 18:28, 43:3, and 89:15). Our Lord is a loan agent (not a loan shark), and He loans us (vs. 5) our houses, lands, families, ministries, and other things, expecting the investment to be returned with interest (see Matt. 25:27 and Luke 19:23).]{.class_4938}

[Christ was never ]{.class_4938}["afraid of evil tidings" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 7). Notice His response to the news of the death of John the Baptist (Matt. 14:11--13), the death of Lazarus (John 11:1--7), and the hatred of the Pharisees (Matt. 15:12--14) and of Herod (Luke 13:32). Although ]{.class_4938}["he hath given to the poor" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 9) is referring to alms and clothing passed out, the connection is beautiful, for ]{.class_4938}["his righteousness" ]{.class_4939}[is the next clause. This brings up a remarkable ]{.class_4938}[devotional ]{.class_4937}[truth. Observe that "po' folks" (Isa. 55:1--4), who have no money to buy anything, can get God's righteousness free. Observe that the context of Isaiah 55:1--3 is David. ]{.class_4938}["He hath given to the poor." ]{.class_4939}[Yes, He certainly has. We could not have bought His righteousness with all the gold in New Jerusalem.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 10, ]{.class_4938}[doctrinally]{.class_4937}[, is a reference to ]{.class_4938}["that Wicked" ]{.class_4939}[of 2 Thessalonians 2:1--8, which see. All of the commentators did "you know what."]{.class_4938}

[The thing that saves the Psalm from staying in the past, as a historical picture of an Old Testament saint, is verse 4, for this is quoted in a New Testament Pauline epistle written to the Body of Christ (2 Cor. 9:9). Men like Bullinger, Stam, Baker, O'Hair, Watkins, Greaterex, Moore, Sharpe, et al., don't care too much for this kind of thing, but since ]{.class_4938}["all"]{.class_4939}[ Scripture is profitable not only for ]{.class_4938}["doctrine" ]{.class_4939}[(2 Tim. 3:16) but for ]{.class_4938}["reproof\...correction\...instruction," ]{.class_4939}[we always need to look a little deeper into the Scriptures than the Dry Cleaners (Hyper-Dispensationalists).]{.class_4938}

[Watching Perowne, Spurgeon, Gesner, Kroll, and others trying to make themselves "righteous" by applying all of the Psalm to the Body of Christ is like watching Larry, Moe, and Curly trying to decorate a Christmas tree. The funniest of these is Kroll (Liberty University), who tells us that Psalm 112 "sets forth MAN, his CHARACTER and work before God." When Kroll hits verse 4 he says---after mangling more than two dozen passages in the Psalms so far---"The man who delights greatly in the commandments of God and in His word is never ]{.class_4938}[in the dark ]{.class_4937}[about dispersing the wealth he has accumulated." Kroll has been in the DARK so long in regard to "His word" that only the Rapture will give him any real light. Kroll puts the everlasting righteousness of verse 3 down as the personal holiness of the Christian: "When the godly man prospers, he does not forsake righteousness. Prosperity does not destroy his holiness."]{.class_4938}

[Perowne says that the righteousness of MAN is "imperishable" because it is connected to the righteousness of God (]{.class_4938}[Treasury]{.class_4937}[, p. 21). And that is as ghastly an exegesis as you ever picked up. That classifies lost men with men who have Christ's imputed righteousness. (Boy, do you get in one mess when you try to jam the Psalms into the Pauline epistles at every point!) Gesner is more tricky. He makes the righteousness that ]{.class_4938}["endureth for ever" ]{.class_4939}[human righteousness, but it is a righteousness that is "before men" instead of God. (He got this capital idea from Rom. 4:2.) Ezekiel 18:24 was written to make a liar out of the whole crew of expositors. Not even the righteousness of an Old Testament saint was "imperishable."]{.class_4938}

[Speaking ]{.class_4938}[inspirationally, ]{.class_4937}[we may sum up the things that mark the truly "devout" or "godly" man in any dispensation.]{.class_4938}

[1. He will fear God beyond the shadow of a doubt, and when God gives a commandment he will delight in it simply because of the SOURCE. He may not relish its ]{.class_4938}[content ]{.class_4937}[(vs. 1).]{.class_4938}

2\. God will bless his children, and they will serve God (vs. 2). (Note the problems in this spiritualizing: David had two sons that weren't "worth a Continental," Samuel had two sons that weren't worth a dime, and Eli had two sons that God had to kill.)

[3. He will be wealthy in regards to money and material goods (vs. 3). Note again, the difficulty when you try to apply everything to a New Testament Christian. Paul, after his conversion, didn't have enough material wealth to purchase a grave plot, and thousands of God's saints have lived and died without ever owning their own homes or being able to pay their hospital bills. Reality is reality. You can't just go hop, skip, and jump through the Psalms taking what you like and dumping what you don't like. John 9:2 and Luke 18:25 show you that verse 3 in this Psalm applies to Old Testament saints; not New Testament saints. David, Abraham, Job, and others die wealthy because of their own righteousness (see Ezek. 3:20, 14:14); ]{.class_4938}[not the imputed righteousness of Jesus Christ. ]{.class_4937}[The disciples understood this even if the stupid, twentieth-century Conservatives don't.]{.class_4938}

[4. He will be gracious and full of compassion on those less fortunate than himself (vs. 4), and he will have ]{.class_4938}["light" ]{.class_4939}[on problems and trials that the wicked do not have (vs. 4).]{.class_4938}

[5. He will have wisdom in handling financial, social, and family matters (vs. 5), and will loan money to people who need it. He will show them favor by not requiring ]{.class_4938}["usury" ]{.class_4939}[(see Exod. 22:25).]{.class_4938}

[6. He may be ]{.class_4938}["moved" ]{.class_4939}[emotionally from time to time (1 Thess. 4:13), or even moved out of his "place" on occasions (Job certainly was, Job 19:10), but not ]{.class_4938}["for ever" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 6). Eventually, he will be ]{.class_4938}["rooted and grounded"]{.class_4939}[ (Eph. 3:17), established and settled (1 Pet. 5:10). Once in New Jerusalem, he will never be "evicted" again.]{.class_4938}

7\. The real saint will live to see the destruction of the NAACP, NEA, ACLU, United Nations, Roman Catholic Church, European Common Market, AMA, NAAA, and the college curriculums (vs. 8). Armageddon and the Millennium will fix all that mess.

8\. The saint will be exalted and honored (vs. 9) by becoming exactly like God's Son (see Rom. 8:29--30; Phil. 3:20--21; and 1 John 3:1--3).

[9. Being wise (according to the definition given in Ps. 111: 10), he knows what to fear and what not to fear (vs. 7) so ]{.class_4938}["evil tidings"]{.class_4939}[---such]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4939}[as the destruction of a crop or piece of property or the death of a relative or friend or a depression or a war or a plague---do not scare him into doing, thinking, or saying anything foolish. The crowning example of this in the Bible is not found in the New Testament. The acme and very epitome ("apotheosis") of the verse is Job's double responses (Job 1:20--21, 2:10) to ]{.class_4938}["evil tidings" ]{.class_4939}[that would cause the "bravest of the brave" to collapse in despair. Joshua, who was never defeated in battle, fell flat on his face (Josh. 7:6--8) upon receiving ]{.class_4938}["evil tidings" ]{.class_4939}[that would look like "Good News for Modern Man" beside what Job was told.]{.class_4938}

[Job's heart was ]{.class_4938}["fixed" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 7).]{.class_4938}

[The up to date, twentieth-century language of the ]{.class_4938}[AV ]{.class_4937}[is remarkable at this point, for it is this word that is chosen by the zippies, hippies, yippies, baby boomers (and other dope heads) to describe a "reefer," a "joint," "crack," or heroin. They needed a "FIX." With this great, twentieth-century truth clearly revealed in 1611, you don't have to guess what the "modern, up to date" versions in the "language of the people" (so folks "could understand them") did with the word. They threw it out as quickly as Bob Jones III threw ]{.class_4938}["science" ]{.class_4939}[out of 1 Timothy 6:20, and as quickly as Curtis Hutson threw]{.class_4938}[ "study"]{.class_4939}[ out of 2 Timothy 2:15, and as quickly as any unsaved Liberal in the NCCC got rid of ]{.class_4938}["corrupt" ]{.class_4939}[from 2 Corinthians 2:17.]{.class_4938}

[Shall we just ditch the ]{.class_4938}[NKJV, ASV, NASV, RSV, NRSV, NIV, ]{.class_4937}[and the next five? Let us do just THAT! Let us go back 380 years for some ]{.class_4938}[advanced revelation ]{.class_4937}[on the twentieth century. (Oh how an apostate Fundamentalist hates those two words! Oooo! Brrrr!)]{.class_4938}

1\. Everybody in a "fix" needs a "fix."

2\. Everything broken, or "out of order," needs fixing.

3\. Man's trouble is that his HEART needs fixing.

4\. A modern "fix" (drugs) can't fix anything.

5\. The ultimate FIXER is God.

[There! How is that for "spiritualizing" a passage? You see, those of us who really know twentieth-century America and twentieth-century American slang can exegete a text so that a teenager can get it without referring to any English version on the face of this earth other than a ]{.class_4938}[King James Bible. ]{.class_4937}[The ]{.class_4938}[NIV ]{.class_4937}[said "stedfast," the ]{.class_4938}[RSV ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[NRSV ]{.class_4937}[said, "firm," and the Living Quack said "settled." The ]{.class_4938}[NASV ]{.class_4937}[took "trusting," and the ]{.class_4938}[NKJV ]{.class_4937}[(with Curtis Hutson, A. V. Henderson, Truman Dollar, James Price, Elmer Towns, and Harold Ockenga presiding) altered it to "stedfast" to match the ]{.class_4938}[New International Version.]{.class_4937}

They all lost "the meat of the text." What else would you expect?

6\. The heart is never FIXED until it is fixed on God.

"[Come quickly, doctor! He took his vitamin pill, his ulcer pill, his heart pill, and his antihistamine; then he swallowed his tranquilizer and appetite depressant pill, and then, just before he lit his cigarette, he took a dash of Benzedrine, and the whole kitchen blew up!" This is not the "Pepsi Generation"; this is the ]{.class_4938}[pill popping generation. ]{.class_4937}[It is the main theme of television and has been for forty years. Headache? Pill. Stomachache? Pill. Want to sleep? Pill. Want an abortion? Pill. Don't want to get pregnant? Pill. Want to lose weight? Pill. Want to stay awake? Pill. Want to relax? Pill. The pill is deified: it has become "god."]{.class_4938}

[As far as the strong drugs are concerned, the definitive work is called ]{.class_4938}[Heroes and Heroin, ]{.class_4937}[and it traces the path of our modern, dope headed society ("drug culture" in news media doublespeak) from its inception in Vietnam, where black "soul brothers" introduced it into the United States Army, which brought it back to the colleges and high schools, from whence it quickly entered the middle schools and grade schools via the ]{.class_4938}[Civil Rights Act ]{.class_4937}[of 1964. There are 60,000 heroin addicts in Washington, D.C. (1991), there are 2,000,000 opium users in the U.S., and every rock star and every rock band that teenagers have adored, since 1950, was composed of fornicating dopeheads who couldn't even perform without a "fix." The drug industry is more powerful than U.S. Steel or General Motors, and even has its own trade vocabulary: acid, snow, snowbirds, hop heads, hypes, acid heads, speed freaks, barbs, weed, grass, mesc, goof balls, jolly beans, scags, dollies, dexies, rainbows, bombitas, uppers, downers, flashes, blasts, bindles, sleighrides, tracks, blanks, decks, smack, happy dust, crack, etc. Two generations of Americans have been "out of order," and neither of them thought God could "fix" what was out of order. ]{.class_4938}[They went to the pill.]{.class_4937}

[The gnashing of teeth (vs. 10) will take place at the Second Advent, according to Jesus Christ (Matt. 8:12, 13:42, 50; 22:13, and 24:51). It is at this time that the ]{.class_4938}["wicked" ]{.class_4939}[physically ]{.class_4938}["melt" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 10). See Psalm 58:7 and compare it with Malachi 4:1 and Ezekiel 22:20. He does NOT ]{.class_4938}["melt" ]{.class_4939}[at Liberty University; instead he "will perish in pity and poverty" (]{.class_4938}[Liberty Commentary,]{.class_4937}[ p. 1139). He does not ]{.class_4938}["melt" ]{.class_4939}[in Spurgeon's ]{.class_4938}[Treasury ]{.class_4937}[(p. 25); instead, his eyes and his heart "pine away." It is the "sun of God's providence" that causes "the heat of his passions to melt him like wax" (Spurgeon). The only place where the wicked melts physically is in a ]{.class_4938}[King James ]{.class_4937}[1611 ]{.class_4938}[AV]{.class_4937}[. He "slinks away" and "wastes away" in the Nutty Idiotic Version (]{.class_4938}[NIV]{.class_4937}[) and the Lousy Boo Boo (]{.class_4938}[The Living Bible]{.class_4937}[). (This time the ]{.class_4938}[ASV ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[NKJV ]{.class_4937}[had enough sense to go back to 1611 for the right readings. Bully for them.)]{.class_4938}

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ2777.xhtml}

PSALM 113

113:1 "Praise ye the LORD. Praise, O ye servants of the LORD, praise the name of the LORD.

2 Blessed be the name of the LORD from this time forth and for evermore.

3 From the rising of the sun unto the going down of the same the LORD's name is to be praised.

4 The LORD is high above all nations, and his glory above the heavens.

5 Who is like unto the LORD our God, who dwelleth on high,

6 Who humbleth himself to behold the things that are in heaven, and in the earth!

7 He raiseth up the poor out of the dust, and lifteth the needy out of the dunghill;

8 That he may set him with princes, even with the princes of his people.

9 He maketh the barren woman to keep house, and to be a joyful mother of children. Praise ye the LORD."

[The entire Psalm, with the exception of verses 4--6, refers to the Millennial reign of Christ after the ]{.class_4938}["poor\...and\...needy" ]{.class_4939}[have been lifted up from ]{.class_4938}["the dust, and\...the dunghill" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 7) and have been placed on thrones (literally: see Rev. 20:2--4) ]{.class_4938}["with the princes" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 8). Spurgeon wavers back and forth a few minutes on verse 8, and then (after saying that "we shall reign for ever and ever"---which is NOT a reference to reigning on earth in the Millennium: Rev. 22:5) says that all the Christians are princes now in this age (]{.class_4938}[Treasury]{.class_4937}[, p. 31). (I guess that is why he was called "The prince of preachers.") To prove that some of the beggars are ]{.class_4938}["princes" ]{.class_4939}[in this dispensation, Spurgeon cites John Bunyan and Paul. This is fine preaching, but Paul the prince gets his head cut off, and Bunyan "the prince" gets thrown into "slam city" for twelve years. Psalm 113:8 is not talking about that or anything like that. It is talking about princes sitting on ]{.class_4938}[thrones ]{.class_4937}[(Rev. 20:2--4) and judging ]{.class_4938}[angels ]{.class_4937}[(1 Cor. 6:3). Spurgeon doesn't know where he is or with what he is dealing. James Bats (1703--1775) says that this Psalm "appears to be wholly written with a view to Christianity" (]{.class_4938}[Treasury, ]{.class_4937}[p. 33).]{.class_4938}

Guess again.

[John Gill, Job Orion (1717--1783), Matthew Henry, John Boys, Richard Watson, and Valentine Nelso write some beautiful homilies on the expression ]{.class_4938}["he raiseth up the poor\...and lifteth the needy," ]{.class_4939}[but there is no doctrinal content in one word from any of them.]{.class_4938}

[What is involved is Hannah's prayer in 1 Samuel 2:8. E. W. Hengstenberg grasps this truth, but can't do anything with it. Furthermore, he makes Hannah a type of the church and goes on his ]{.class_4938}[conceited ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[ignorant ]{.class_4937}[way, forgetting Romans 11:25, which warned characters like him (Hebrew scholars) not to "play the fool" (1 Sam. 26:21). Hannah is talking about the deliverance of Israel by the Messiah almost from start to finish (]{.class_4938}["The bows of the mighty men are broken\...they that were full have hired out themselves for bread\...the Lord killeth, and maketh alive\...the Lord maketh poor, and maketh rich\...he raiseth up the poor out of the dust, and lifteth up the beggar from the dunghill, to set them among princes, and to make them inherit the throne of glory\...the wicked shall be silent in darkness\...The adversaries of the Lord shall be broken to pieces\...the Lord shall judge the ends of the earth; and he shall give strength unto his king" ]{.class_4939}[(1 Sam. 2:4--10). There is no ]{.class_4938}[king ]{.class_4937}[over Israel when Hannah makes this prophesy.]{.class_4938}

[With this in front of the faculty and staff of Liberty University in Lynchburg, Virginia, their Associate Professor of Religion (with ]{.class_4938}[three ]{.class_4937}[seminary educations in his background) says, "God takes the castaways from the ash heap of life and makes them CHAMPIONS!"]{.class_4938}

Buddy, you talk about using the word of God to promote a school! "Champions?" The word doesn't occur anywhere in the Book of Psalms. The only "champion" mentioned in the entire Bible is Goliath---one of the greatest types of the Antichrist in the Old Testament. What kind of an education do you suppose Kroll got in THREE seminaries? Do you know what those seminaries were? They were Gordon Conwell Theological Seminary, Westminister Theological Seminary, and Dallas Theological Seminary. That is what is being produced in America in the last quarter of the twentieth century: functional illiteracy in the Body of Christ.

[Verse 2 should have alerted anyone who could read third-grade English that neither the Old Testament nor the Church Age is the doctrinal position for verses 7 and 8. God's name (vs. 2) has certainly NOT been ]{.class_4938}["blessed" ]{.class_4939}[from the time of Christ until now, and it will be less ]{.class_4938}["blessed" ]{.class_4939}[in the next three to seven years. God's name was blasphemed by ]{.class_4938}[Christians ]{.class_4937}[(Acts 26:11) and ]{.class_4938}[unsaved Israelites ]{.class_4937}[from the time of the Crucifixion to Acts 9. Someone lost their ability to read: they lost it at three "recognized, accredited Christian SEMINARIES." ]{.class_4938}["The Lord's name" ]{.class_4939}[is going to be praised daily (vs. 3) in the Millennium; not the Church Age (see Mal. 1:11; Psa. 61:8, 72:15). ]{.class_4938}["Sound doctrine" ]{.class_4939}[(2 Tim. 4:3) is evidently a course that has been carefully omitted from the curricula of Grace Theological Seminary, Bob Jones University, California Graduate School of Theology, Baptist Bible College, Dallas Theological Seminary, Trinity Evangelical Divinity School, Pensacola Christian College, and Garrett Theological Seminary.]{.class_4938}

[Devotionally: ]{.class_4937}[All of God's servants should praise Him (vs. 1). God's name is blessed forever, even if no one blesses it (vs. 2). God should be praised at all hours of the day (vs. 3). God's power is the determining power in the course of any nation (vs. 4; see Rom. 13:1). His glory is above the universe (vs. 4, see John 17:24). He ]{.class_4938}["dwelleth" ]{.class_4939}[in eternity (vs. 5, see Isa. 57:15), and the ]{.class_4938}["high," ]{.class_4939}[in the verse, is so high that you would die of old age before you got to it, even if you traveled twice as fast as the speed of light. That is pretty ]{.class_4938}["high" ]{.class_4939}[(Note how the word is used by the "drug culture"---Psa. 112:7 and comments---for "feeling good." They say, "I got a high," or "I was high." The old drunkards' expression was "higher than a kite.")]{.class_4938}

[Of verse 6, we may say that God ]{.class_4938}["humbleth himself" ]{.class_4939}[in two senses. First of all, One of such power and majesty is under no obligation whatsoever to deal with man (Rom. 9:20). David marveled at this in Psalm 8. Mary recognizes that she is a woman of ]{.class_4938}["low estate"]{.class_4939}[ (Luke 1:48). God actually takes time to instruct man, rebuke man, help man, deliver man, chasten man, heal man, and encourage man. The references are much too numerous to list. Why would a Being who can fill heaven and earth take time out to deal with something as insignificant as a microbe on the bosom of the universe? Easy: He is ]{.class_4938}[humbling Himself. ]{.class_4937}[Secondly, He puts humbling into literal, physical practice: He ]{.class_4938}["made himself of no reputation, and took upon him the form of a servant\...And being found in fashion as a man, he humbled himself" ]{.class_4939}[(Phil. 2:6--8).]{.class_4938}

[Devotionally, ]{.class_4937}[verse 9 is a simple statement of truth which every mother should consider before griping about her household duties and responsibilities (see ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Titus; ]{.class_4937}[Titus 2:5). ]{.class_4938}[Doctrinally, ]{.class_4937}[the woman is Hannah, and you know, by now, what to make of that (1 Sam. 2). Spurgeon and company wrongly apply Isaiah 54:1 to the Church as the ]{.class_4938}["joyful mother," ]{.class_4939}[but everywhere in the Old Testament it is the Israel of the Tribulation who travails and brings forth children (Isa. 66:7--8; 37:3; 66:9, etc.). The woman who bore "seven" (1 Sam. 2:5) was Israel at the First Advent.]{.class_4938}

PSALM 114

114:1 "When Israel went out of Egypt, the house of Jacob from a people of strange language;

2 Judah was his sanctuary, and Israel his dominion.

3 The sea saw it, and fled: Jordan was driven back.

4 The mountains skipped like rams, and the little hills like lambs.

5 What ailed thee, O thou sea, that thou fleddest? thou Jordan, that thou wast driven back?

6 Ye mountains, that ye skipped like rams; and ye little hills, like lambs?

7 Tremble, thou earth, at the presence of the Lord, at the presence of the God of Jacob;

8 Which turned the rock into a standing water, the flint into a fountain of waters."

The Psalm needs little or no comment. It is a recitation of Exodus and Joshua, and as we have seen, these things will take place again. Spurgeon calls it "true poetry"---meaning that he doesn't believe much of it (literally) because he cannot visualize it.

[Verse 1, see Psalm 81:5. Verse 2 indicates that God already intended to place the Tabernacle in Judah before they got into the Promised Land. It was placed in Shiloh (Ephraim) until the days of David, but long before the Tabernacle was built, Jacob told his sons that the scepter would come from Judah---not Ephraim (Gen. 49:10). Verse 3 is what happened at the Red Sea (Exod. 15) and Jordan (Josh. 4:1). Verse 4: If a ]{.class_4938}[literal earthquake ]{.class_4937}[did not attend the crossing of Jordan, one certainly will attend the earth opening up and swallowing a ]{.class_4938}["flood" ]{.class_4939}[(Rev. 12:16--17) that Behemoth trusts he can take up ]{.class_4938}["into his mouth" ]{.class_4939}[(Job 40:23) when Israel flees to the wilderness (Matt. 24:16--18).]{.class_4938}

[Verses 5 and 6 are questions asked to the elements; compare Psalm 68:16, which is the same prophetic context. Again, Psalm 68:8 and 16 give you the prophetic context of verse 7. The speaker there is NOT referring to anything in the past. He is talking about ]{.class_4938}["the presence of the God of Jacob." ]{.class_4939}[All of the commentators miss all of the future applications. Even verse 8 will take place again; see Psalm 105:41 and 107:35 and compare with Psalm 49:3--4.]{.class_4938}

PSALM 115

115:1 "Not unto us, O LORD, not unto us, but unto thy name give glory, for thy mercy, and for thy truth's sake.

2 Wherefore should the heathen say, Where is now their God?

3 But our God is in the heavens: he hath done whatsoever he hath pleased.

4 Their idols are silver and gold, the work of men's hands.

5 They have mouths, but they speak not: eyes have they, but they see not:

6 They have ears, but they hear not: noses have they, but they smell not:

7 They have hands, but they handle not: feet have they, but they walk not: neither speak they through their throat.

8 They that make them are like unto them; so is every one that trusteth in them."

[The ]{.class_4938}["us"]{.class_4939}[ in verse 1 is Israel again; the contrast is with the Germans, Russians, Italians, Spaniards, Englishmen, Japanese, Irishmen, Mexicans, and Chinese mentioned in the next verse (]{.class_4938}["the heathen"]{.class_4939}[).]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4939}[Again, ]{.class_4938}["our God" ]{.class_4939}[is the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, who is not Thor, Wotan, Freia, Siva, Krishna, Vishnu, Bramha, "Mary," Venus, Apollo, Zeus, Jupiter, or some pope. Again, the doctrinal references can be to silver shrines honoring someone that ]{.class_4938}["fell down from Jupiter" ]{.class_4939}[(see Acts 19:35 and comments in ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Acts]{.class_4937}[). They will apply to images made of "THE" image (Rev. 13:15; Acts 19:24, 38) when all the world worships the beast.]{.class_4938}

[In the Psalms, you simply cannot replace Israel with the Church, although this seems to be the aim of most of the commentators. We need to remind the reader that "the day" on God's calendar is not the Feast of the Passover in A.D. 33; "the day" on God's calendar is not Pentecost in 33 A.D.; and "the day" is not the Church Age. "The day," on God's calendar, is the day that His Son returns to the city where sinners murdered Him ]{.class_4938}[and takes over the earth and gets the honor, recognition, glory, and worship due Him. ]{.class_4937}[Since this glorious Advent is preceded by the near annihilation of the nation that brought forth God's Son, Daniel's Seventieth Week and the Millennium will be the main subjects of the Psaltery from Psalm 1 to Psalm 150.]{.class_4938}

[Devotionally: ]{.class_4937}[God can glorify His own name (John 12:28) by "getting glory" on a man (Exod. 14:17). Verse 2, every time Satan speaks in Scripture he speaks with a question mark (Gen. 3:1; Job 1:9) or with an]{.class_4938}[ "if" ]{.class_4939}[(see Luke 4:3; Matt. 27:40).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 3. God is the only One who can do what He pleases ]{.class_4938}[all the time ]{.class_4937}[and get away with it (see Gal. 1:15). Further, He is the only One who has both the right and the power to do as He pleases ]{.class_4938}[all the time, ]{.class_4937}[and finally, He is the only one who always does right every time that He does what He pleases. The idols of the heathen (vs. 4) are their "gods" (see Ps. 82). They are ]{.class_4938}["the work of men's hands"]{.class_4939}[; i.e., computers, slot machines, cars, planes, houses, toys, boats, weapons, TV sets, radios, word processors, electric ranges, washing machines, refrigerators, etc. Computers are mechanical and electrical; they have no "living soul" and cannot perform human functions as humans do. They cannot even figure until a human being "programs" them. It is their circuits that see, hear, smell, move, act, think, and perform functions. They have all the physical equipment: ]{.class_4938}["mouths\...eyes\...ears\...noses\...hands\...feet\...throat" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 5--7), but they do not have souls, and the ]{.class_4938}["spirit of man" ]{.class_4939}[(1 Cor. 2:11) is not in them. There is no ]{.class_4938}["breath of life" ]{.class_4939}[(Gen. 2:7) from God in the contraptions, for ]{.class_4938}["the spirit of man is the candle of the Lord" ]{.class_4939}[(Prov. 20:27), not "Gulf Power Company." Electricity, from lightning, might be the source of life for Frankenstein or Darwin---remember the little itty bitty "pool" that accidently formed the "polypeptide chain of amino acids"?---but it is not the source of life for man. (Darwin may not have been an ape; he certainly ]{.class_4938}[looked ]{.class_4937}[like one.)]{.class_4938}

"[They that make them are like unto them" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 8), so the remark about Charlie (above) was not too far "out of line." The convert of a long robed, blood drinking ]{.class_4938}["father"]{.class_4939}[ (Matt. 23:9; Luke 20:46) is even a little better than his "maker," for he is ]{.class_4938}["twofold more child of hell"]{.class_4939}[ (Matt. 23:15) than the priest who created him out of water. The rule is, a man is as stupid, or as wise, as his creator. As a "straw boss" said to one of his construction gangs in Georgia, "Don't take your helmet off when a woodpecker is in the area." ]{.class_4938}["So is every one that trusteth in them." ]{.class_4939}[Put your faith in one of the "gods" of the heathen (vs. 2), and you will be the work of man's hands (vs. 4), not the work of God. This explains how a man can have eyes and not see, and ears and not hear (see Matt. 13:14, 16; John 12:40; Acts 28:26; Rom. 11:8, etc.). The physical equipment is present---just as it is in a robot---but it ]{.class_4938}[can't function, ]{.class_4937}[for the man is dead (]{.class_4938}["dead," ]{.class_4939}[Eph. 2:1--5, as in D E A D, ]{.class_4938}[dead, ]{.class_4937}[Col. 2:13).]{.class_4938}

115:9 "O Israel, trust thou in the LORD: he is their help and their shield.

10 O house of Aaron, trust in the LORD: he is their help and their shield.

11 Ye that fear the LORD, trust in the LORD: he is their help and their shield.

12 The LORD hath been mindful of us: he will bless us; he will bless the house of Israel; he will bless the house of Aaron.

13 He will bless them that fear the LORD, both small and great.

14 The LORD shall increase you more and more, you and your children.

15 Ye are blessed of the LORD which made heaven and earth.

16 The heaven, even the heavens, are the LORD'S: but the earth hath he given to the children of men.

17 The dead praise not the LORD, neither any that go down into silence.

18 But we will bless the LORD from this time forth and for evermore. Praise the LORD."

[2. The ]{.class_4938}["house of Aaron"]{.class_4939}[: Levitical priests who are about to serve in the Millennial temple (Ezek. 40--42) under the Messiah but, at the present, are being called upon to supervise Baal worship (Rev. 2:14) in the Temple, with fornication (Rev. 2:20--21) and cannibalism as part of the "religious happening" (see Ps. 14:4 and 53:4). The commentators (100%) naturally miss all the passages and all of the prophetic doctrines.

[Verse 16 gives you God's mind on Houston Space Center and Cape Kennedy. Man's dominion, according to the Holy Spirit, is up to where the eagle flies (see Gen. 1:26). PSALM 116

116:1 "I love the LORD, because he hath heard my voice and my supplications.

2 Because he hath inclined his ear unto me, therefore will I call upon him as long as I live.

3 The sorrows of death compassed me, and the pains of hell gat hold upon me: I found trouble and sorrow.

4 Then called I upon the name of the LORD; O LORD, I beseech thee, deliver my soul.

5 Gracious is the LORD, and righteous; yea, our God is merciful.

6 The LORD preserveth the simple: I was brought low, and he helped me.

7 Return unto thy rest, O my soul; for the LORD hath dealt bountifully with thee.

8 For thou hast delivered my soul from death, mine eyes from tears, and my feet from falling.

9 I will walk before the LORD in the land of the living."

[The Psalm is Messianic without a shadow of a doubt, but due to unbelief and due to the Alexandrian Cult constantly messing with words like "sheol" and "hades," we are told that "today not many scholars hold to the Messianic applications" of verses 1--3 and 8--17. Naturally, the apostates attribute this rejection to "newer light" on the "original Hebrew," and all the rest of the claptrap, bag and baggage. The problem is an UNBELIEF in Christ's humanity (see comments under Titus 1:1--2 and Heb. 5:7--8 in those commentaries). The super-pious boys don't want to impugn the Deity of Christ. (They are, by the way, the same boys who threw His Deity out of Acts 4:27, 1 Timothy 3:16, and Luke 2:33 in the ]{.class_4938}[ASV, RV, NASV, RSV, NRSV, NIV, ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[NKJV.)]{.class_4937}

[Devotionally, ]{.class_4937}[we may observe that God loves us without a good reason (vs. 1), and we loved Him only ]{.class_4938}["because he first loved us" ]{.class_4939}[(1 John 4:19). Verse 2, if it worked once it will work again (]{.class_4938}["he hath inclined his ear unto me"]{.class_4939}[). Prayer is the most scientific method on earth to use for inductive proof. Any man can find out if God exists by calling on His name for wisdom and truth. Christ said it would be revealed to him (John 7:17). Prayer is a life long occupation, and there is no time in life when it is not needed and not helpful. Verse 3, the ]{.class_4938}["sorrows of death" ]{.class_4939}[begin to encompass a man before he dies, sometimes many years before he dies. Hell begins to take hold of a sinner before he dies. Hell can get a hold on you before you get there. As ]{.class_4938}["Christ in you" ]{.class_4939}[is ]{.class_4938}["the hope of glory" ]{.class_4939}[(Col. 1:27), so Satan in the unsaved man is his hope of damnation: it is his "foretaste of damnation divine." This explains why self-righteous sinners think "your heaven or your hell is just here on earth." As Dwight Moody wisely said: "This earth is all of 'heaven' that an unsaved man will ever see, and this earth is all of 'hell' that a saved man will ever see."]{.class_4938}

The Lord's salvation is revealed in order between verses 5 and 7:

1\. The Lord is gracious (vs. 5), indicating that He gives sinners a chance to be saved.

[2. The Lord is ]{.class_4938}[righteous]{.class_4937}[ (vs. 5), indicating that He demands righteousness for salvation.]{.class_4938}

[3. The Lord is ]{.class_4938}[merciful]{.class_4937}[ (vs. 5), indicating that He has relieved you of the burden and will become YOUR righteousness for you in your stead.]{.class_4938}

[4. The Lord ]{.class_4938}[preserves ]{.class_4937}[the saved sinner (vs. 6).]{.class_4938}

[6. The Lord ]{.class_4938}[helps ]{.class_4937}[the sinner with His word (vs. 6).]{.class_4938}

[7. The Lord often ]{.class_4938}[blesses ]{.class_4937}[the saved sinner materially, as well (vs. 7).]{.class_4938}

[In verse 8, the soul has been ]{.class_4938}[delivered ]{.class_4937}[from eternal death in hell, the body has been saved from the sorrow of reaping a sinful life, and the man's spirit has been guided to save him from falling into more sins or into a disaster.]{.class_4938}

[Doctrinally ]{.class_4937}[speaking, verse 3 is a reference to Jesus Christ as found in Acts 2:27, 31 and Psalm 16:10. 116:10 "I believed, therefore have I spoken: I was greatly afflicted:

11 I said in my haste, All men are liars.

"[The Gospel brings everlasting life to all that believe" says Kroll, which is a safe statement to make at anytime. Unfortunately, it has absolutely nothing to do with the text. The text is not talking about anyone enduring (Kroll says "Jew and Gentile alike") for ever; it is talking about ]{.class_4938}["the truth of the LORD endureth for ever." ]{.class_4939}[Isn't it amazing how many times these super pious Bible critics confound themselves with ]{.class_4938}["the truth"]{.class_4939}[? This is a little better, for it constitutes about ]{.class_4938}[one tenth ]{.class_4937}[of the truth. But the whole truth is that ]{.class_4938}["the truth of the Lord" ]{.class_4939}[is anything that is SO that God has revealed to man. This would include the following items:]{.class_4938}

1\. The Lord Jesus Christ.

2\. Everything He said while on the earth.

3\. The Biblical accounts of the creation of man, the earth, and the heavens.

4\. All of the Biblical prophecies for and against all the nations mentioned in Scripture (Assyria, Babylon, Persia, Greece, Rome, Edom, Moab, Ammon, Syria, Israel, Egypt, etc.).

5\. All the Biblical prophecies concerning the First Coming of Christ.

6\. All the Biblical prophecies concerning the Second Coming of Christ.

7\. All the commandments, statutes, and judgments given to Israel.

8\. All of the ethical and moral instructions contained in Proverbs, Psalms, Romans--Titus, and James--3 John.

PSALM 118

118:1 "O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: because his mercy endureth for ever.

2 Let Israel now say, that his mercy endureth for ever.

3 Let the house of Aaron now say, that his mercy endureth for ever.

5 I called upon the LORD in distress: the LORD answered me, and set me in a large place.

6 The LORD is on my side; I will not fear: what can man do unto me?

PSALM 119

This Psalm is an "alphabet Psalm" because eight verses are allotted to each letter in the Hebrew alphabet. Each line, in each section, begins with the letter that heads its section.

[The Psalm is obviously the longest "chapter" in the Bible, so it is appropriate that its main theme would be the Bible itself. This is neatly camouflaged (and then done away with by the modern, apostate Fundamentalists who use Barth and Brunner's famous "double speak," called "the Word of God"). No modern, apostate Fundamentalist\
believes ANY Bible (a "Bible" is a book, you understand), but rather he believes that a series of ]{.class_4938}[translations]{.class_4937}[---which he can ]{.class_4938}[correct]{.class_4937}[---contain a "message" from God, which he ]{.class_4938}[calls ]{.class_4937}["the Word of God." This is the Neo orthodoxy that has been taught at Bob Jones University for sixty years; ditto BBC, TTU, LU, etc. To us, the "Bible" is Scripture, and we have "the Scriptures" which are "the word of God" and contain "the words of God."]{.class_4938}

[Do you get the difference? There are ten words used in Psalm 119 that describe "the word of God"---which we call "the Scriptures" or "the Holy Bible."

[1. ]{.class_4938}["The way": ]{.class_4939}[hereafter, when this word shows up you can take it to mean "the way a man should go on his feet;" that is, it describes a "journey." (Genesis 3:24 is the first occurrence.)]{.class_4938}

[2. ]{.class_4938}["Testimonies": ]{.class_4939}[This word will occur twenty three times, and it is primarily a reference to advice, instruction, or reproof that needs to be reviewed, or gone back over again.]{.class_4938}

[3. ]{.class_4938}["Precepts": ]{.class_4939}[this word shows up twenty one times; it means some command that is to be passed on to others as a mandate.]{.class_4938}

[4. ]{.class_4938}["Commandments" ]{.class_4939}[will be found twenty-two times, and it simply means what it says: "Befehl ist Befehl."]{.class_4938}

[5. ]{.class_4938}["Word"]{.class_4939}[ shows up nineteen times and, in this case, deals with something brought forth to light by means of speech. (See 2 Pet. 1:21 for example, and notice now all of the apostate Fundamentalists---100 percent from every seminary in America---will use the verse to try to prove the Holy Ghost moved ]{.class_4938}[pens ]{.class_4937}[instead of ]{.class_4938}[mouths]{.class_4937}[.)]{.class_4938}

[6. ]{.class_4938}["Law"]{.class_4939}[ appears twenty five times, and although the word precedes the Mosaic Law (see Gen. 26:5), the references here are to Genesis through Deuteronomy.]{.class_4938}

[7. ]{.class_4938}["Judgments": ]{.class_4939}[These are God's decisions on matters. It is the "sentence" of a Richter (German for "judge") on a matter.]{.class_4938}

[8. ]{.class_4938}["Righteousness," ]{.class_4939}[in the sense of proper balance in matters, as well as God's own personal righteousness.]{.class_4938}

[9. ]{.class_4938}["Statutes" ]{.class_4939}[are fixed decrees or ordinances; they occur twenty two times here.]{.class_4938}

[10. ]{.class_4938}["Word" ]{.class_4939}[or ]{.class_4938}["words": ]{.class_4939}[Here is the final designation (which is the one chosen by the apostates to make you think they believe in some non-existent "Book." They use only the singular, and when they do they capitalize it so it can refer to Christ (John 1:1--3) and, therefore, a "message from God" and not God's WORDS. This "word" is from ]{.class_4938}["dabar"]{.class_4937}[ (דבר) in distinction from the "word" of No. 5, which comes from ]{.class_4938}["amar"]{.class_4937}[ (אמר). There is no distinction when it comes to what God said, and that is what we are interested in.]{.class_4938}

[We will not do much commenting after giving you this orientation. These definitions will give you the content of the verse in which they appear. Most scholars believe the Psalm is Davidic; others put it after the exile. The tone of the poem is Messianic, for with the exception of one or two places (vss. 6, 36, 60, 67, 71, 120, 176), the writer is "mighty nigh" ]{.class_4938}[sinless. ]{.class_4937}[Note that you could hardly apply verses 8, 11, 55--56, 60, 93, 101--102, 111, 129, 157, 167--168, etc., to David in his dealings with Uriah, Bathsheba, Absalom, Achish, or his numbering of the people. Undoubtedly, we have the situation in which David is often found; speaking for himself one time and the Lord another time.]{.class_4938}

The "salient" verses are verses 9, 11, 18, 46, 67, 89, 98--100, 103, 105, 127--128, 130, 160, 162, so we'll give them most of our attention.

[א]{.class_4938}[ALEPH]{.class_4941}

119:1 "Blessed are the undefiled in the way, who walk in the law of the LORD.

2 Blessed are they that keep his testimonies, and that seek him with the whole heart.

3 They also do no iniquity: they walk in his ways.

4 Thou hast commanded us to keep thy precepts diligently.

5 O that my ways were directed to keep thy statutes!

6 Then shall I not be ashamed, when I have respect unto all thy commandments.

7 I will praise thee with uprightness of heart, when I shall have learned thy righteous judgments.

8 I will keep thy statutes: O forsake me not utterly."

[Aleph: ]{.class_4937}[The letter signifies "an ox."]{.class_4938}

Verse 1. A man is clean on his journey if he obeys the Law (spiritually, John 15:3).

Verse 2. Old Testament, as in Isaiah 55:6.

Verse 3. Wesley's teaching: while a man is consciously in fellowship with God (prayerful communion) and obeying God, that much of the time he is sinless.

[Observe that ]{.class_4938}[negative ]{.class_4937}[produces ]{.class_4938}[positive.]{.class_4937}

[Verse 4. Old Testament, but ]{.class_4938}[spiritually ]{.class_4937}[applicable to Luke 6:46.]{.class_4938}

Verse 5. A prayer for God's direction in the right way. We need directions as well as commands.

Verse 6. He will not be put to shame if God directs his "ways." The trouble is lack of respect, and this can only be taught by power and punishment.

[Verse 7. He will praise God at all times, but the ]{.class_4938}["uprightness of heart"]{.class_4939}[ cannot be obtained without learning the Lord's judgments.]{.class_4938}

Verse 8. Self explanatory.

[The ]{.class_4938}["undefiled" ]{.class_4939}[man in the context is the man who:]{.class_4938}

1\. Walks in the right way (vs. 1).

2\. Keeps the right advice (vs. 2).

3\. Seeks the right authority (vs. 3).

4\. Abstains from the wrong way (vs. 3).

[ב]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4940}[BETH]{.class_4941}

119:9 "Wherewithal shall a young man cleanse his way? by taking heed thereto according to thy word.

10 With my whole heart have I sought thee: O let me not wander from thy commandments.

11 Thy word have I hid in mine heart, that I might not sin against thee.

12 Blessed art thou, O LORD: teach me thy statutes.

13 With my lips have I declared all the judgments of thy mouth.

14 I have rejoiced in the way of thy testimonies, as much as in all riches.

15 I will meditate in thy precepts, and have respect unto thy ways,

16 I will delight myself in thy statutes: I will not forget thy word."

[Beth: ]{.class_4937}[The letter means a house or a "tent," and is used in Hebrew for the numeral 2.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 9. The verse is aimed directly at a ]{.class_4938}["young man," ]{.class_4939}[like the young man addressed in Proverbs 1--6. He can only clean his way by taking heed to his way, according to what God says in His word about that way. According to God's word, the cleansing way is:]{.class_4938}

1\. By the blood of Christ on Calvary's cross (1 John 1:7).

2\. By the words of God, meditated upon (John 17:17).

3\. By the blood applied daily "in the way" (Heb. 9:14).

4\. By judging and confessing sin, daily, in the life (1 Cor. 11:31--32).

5\. Assembling with God's people and exhorting them daily (Heb. 3:13; Acts 2:46--47).

A man's thought life is pure only as it is related to his exposure to the word of God. This means that 90 percent of the men in America have an "inner life" that looks like a cesspool overflowing into a garbage dump.

Verse 10. In the Old Testament sense.

[Verse 11.]{.class_4938}[ A salient verse. ]{.class_4937}[The purpose behind "Bible memorization" is primarily to get sin out of our lives, not to demonstrate ]{.class_4938}[our brains ]{.class_4937}[(see vs. 16). Kroll immediately demonstrates his brains by telling you to chuck out the ]{.class_4938}["word" ]{.class_4939}[as a means of overcoming sin and adopt instead, "promise," so it just means the promises of God---not the words in THE BOOK. Isn't that the most beautiful piece of humanistic positivism you ever saw in your life? What about the ]{.class_4938}[threats ]{.class_4937}[in the Bible against sin? Is Kroll trying to tell you that those won't keep you from sin because they are not "promises"? "You got it, honey; right on the money." Modern fundamentalism is LIBERAL in practice. Christ answers Satan from Scripture (Luke 4), ]{.class_4938}[and not one Scripture He quotes is a "promise." ]{.class_4937}[When the ]{.class_4938}["sword of the Spirit" ]{.class_4939}[is drawn to put down imaginations and every thought (Eph. 6:17; 2 Cor. 10:5), ]{.class_4938}[promises ]{.class_4937}[are secondary considerations unless you are an unsaved Modernist in the National Council of Churches. Tell me, how do you hide His ]{.class_4938}["word" ]{.class_4939}[in your heart if you don't memorize it?]{.class_4938}

[The word is to be hidden in the ]{.class_4938}[heart, ]{.class_4937}[not the closet or under a pile of newspapers or in a night stand somewhere. Scripture memorization is a lost art in most Fundamental churches, and as far as unsaved educators in America are concerned, they are as ignorant and unenlightened about the Scripture as the pope and the College of Cardinals.]{.class_4938}

[The]{.class_4938}[ "way"]{.class_4939}[ of verse 9 should match the ]{.class_4938}["word" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 11.]{.class_4938}

Verses 12 and 13 are self explanatory for any Old Testament saint.

[Verse 14 shows a great achievement for a Christian, in view of Colossians 3:1--3 and 1 Timothy 6:5--10. Both passages have been altered in the ]{.class_4938}[NKJV, NIV, NRSV, ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[NASV ]{.class_4937}[so you cannot hide the word of God in your heart. Par for the Laodicean course.]{.class_4938}

Verse 15 is plain.

[Verse 16 is a great resolution: ]{.class_4938}["I will not forget thy word." ]{.class_4939}[Pity the poor beknighted heathen (Doug Kutilek, Zane Hodges, Gary Hudson, Donald Waite, Bob Jones IV, Sherman, Afman, Dell, Martin, Dobson, Hindson, Hobson, Swindoll, MacArthur, Price, Farstad, Walker, Hymers, and Curtis Hutson) who never had it to remember!]{.class_4938}

[ג]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4940}[GIMEL]{.class_4941}

119:17 "Deal bountifully with thy servant, that I may live, and keep thy word.

18 Open thou mine eyes, that I may behold wondrous things out of thy law.

19 I am a stranger in the earth: hide not thy commandments from me.

20 My soul breaketh for the longing that it hath unto thy judgments at all times.

21 Thou hast rebuked the proud that are cursed, which do err from thy commandments.

22 Remove from me reproach and contempt; for I have kept thy testimonies.

23 Princes also did sit and speak against me: but thy servant did meditate in thy statutes.

24 Thy testimonies also are my delight and my counselors."

[Gimel ]{.class_4937}[means "camel," and it is used for the numeral 3 in Hebrew.]{.class_4938}

Verse 17. The purpose for staying alive is to keep God's word (see Ps. 118:17 and comments).

Verse 18 is a salient verse; it stands with six other verses that explain why higher Christian education rarely produces any kind of a Bible student, let alone a "scholar." There are exceptions, of course, but even in the exceptions the student pays the price in unbelief, at some point, for not obeying Psalm 119:18. You see, this is a prayer, and it is prayed by someone who wants to receive advanced light from the Bible. Study these seven verses carefully, because by ignoring these verses an international apostasy in the Body of Christ took place in 1880---through "orthodox" scholars who all graduated from "bastions of orthodoxy" and believed in the "fundamentals of the faith," exactly like any "World Congress of Fundamentalists" ever did.

[I. Men and women must have open hearts, and ]{.class_4938}[then ]{.class_4937}[have their eyes opened, before they can understand the Scriptures (Luke 24:32, 45; Acts 8:30--31). A man with physical eyes, reading literal print on material paper, can fail to see what is on the paper even when it is written in sixth-grade language and grammar. Is that clear? (It had better get clear if it is not clear now.)]{.class_4938}

[II. The ]{.class_4938}[interpreter ]{.class_4937}[of the words of God, whether written or spoken, is the Holy Spirit (Gen. 40:8, 41:16; Dan. 2:18--23), ]{.class_4938}[not ]{.class_4937}[the Catholic church or the "majority of godly scholars" or "the vast majority of recognized scholars" or some jerk with thirty years teaching Hebrew or Greek. The interpreter is ]{.class_4938}[the Holy Ghost.]{.class_4937}

[III. All true revelations---including advanced revelations---come from ]{.class_4938}[believing ]{.class_4937}[what God said, ]{.class_4938}[praying ]{.class_4937}[for God to show you what He meant when He said it, and ]{.class_4938}[trusting ]{.class_4937}[that God can reveal it to you (1 Cor. 2:10) if you need to know it. This is the prayer of Psalm 119:18. The ]{.class_4938}["wondrous things" ]{.class_4939}[which are revealed in the ]{.class_4938}["law"]{.class_4939}[ are not revealed by "word studies" in ]{.class_4938}[any ]{.class_4937}[language. They are not revealed by revising ]{.class_4938}[any ]{.class_4937}[Bible; they are not revealed by accepting "historic positions"; they are not revealed by going to the "originals" to correct passages you can't understand; and they are not revealed by believing any dunce who thinks he is smart enough to "play God." They are revealed by comparing Scripture with Scripture (1 Cor. 2:12--13) and praying for God to open your eyes.]{.class_4938}

[Is ]{.class_4938}[that ]{.class_4937}[clear?]{.class_4938}

[Well, it has never been clear to professional teachers, revisors, translators, and commentators who want to lord it over the Body of Christ and make you think that you are dependent on men like ]{.class_4938}[themselves ]{.class_4937}[for revelation. You are not. Tell them to "Pack 'N Sack" it, and give it to Santa Claus so he can dump it down somebody's chimney.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 19. The Psalmist takes God's side (Psa. 39:12) as to ]{.class_4938}[citizenship. ]{.class_4937}[Both he and God (2 Sam. 7:6) deny the creed of the UN and America. Neither one was a "Global Citizen."]{.class_4938}

Verse 20. He has an extreme desire to learn what God has ruled on various issues (see introduction).

Verse 21 can be applied to a number of cases, but the Second Advent is the best case, by far.

Verse 22. Possibly Messianic, and answered. Here is an Old Testament situation where the speaker can claim deliverance on the grounds of WORKS, not faith. Note the second clause. This is not a New Testament type of thing.

[Verse 23. Especially true of Jeremiah (see Jer. 36:12, 37:15, 38:4), but also of Ezekiel (Ezek. 33:30--33) and Christ. If the ]{.class_4938}["princes" ]{.class_4939}[are converted to kings, bishops, councilmen, new officials, college professors, and preachers, the verse could apply to Martin Luther, John Knox, Billy Sunday, Harlan Popov, Richard Wurmbrand, Christian young people in middle school and high school, street preachers all over America, and several score pastors between 1964 and 2000.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 24 is self explanatory (see]{.class_4938}[ "testimonies"]{.class_4939}[ in the introduction).]{.class_4938}

[ד]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4940}[DALETH]{.class_4941}

119:25 "My soul cleaveth unto the dust: quicken thou me according to thy word.

26 I have declared my ways, and thou heardest me: teach me thy statutes.

27 Make me to understand the way of thy precepts: so shall I talk of thy wondrous works.

28 My soul melteth for heaviness: strengthen thou me according unto thy word.

29 Remove from me the way of lying: and grant me thy law graciously.

30 I have chosen the way of truth: thy judgments have I laid before me.

31 I have stuck unto thy testimonies: O LORD, put me not to shame.

32 I will run the way of thy commandments, when thou shalt enlarge my heart."

[Daleth ]{.class_4937}[means "a door" and is used for the numeral 4.]{.class_4938}

Verse 25 could apply to any saint about to die. It can be Messianic.

[Verse 26 is the confession of a man who has confessed his sins and is asking for instruction. If he is a ]{.class_4938}["stranger" ]{.class_4939}[(as in vs. 19), then he will need what a stranger needs in a strange country: 1. Information. 2. Direction. 3. Orders. This is especially true if he is an ambassador (2 Cor. 5:20) representing a foreign country (Col. 3:1--4). Again, we must declare ]{.class_4938}[our ways ]{.class_4937}[before we can declare His.]{.class_4938}

Verse 27 is a prayer to be allowed to reveal what God showed him back in verse 18.

[Verse 28 is like verse 25. Notice, it is not only that the word of God "quickens" a sinner (see Col. 2:13; 1 Pet. 4:5), but also that it "strengthens" him (see Acts 20:32). This quickening is ]{.class_4938}[physical, ]{.class_4937}[not spiritual, as in Ephesians 2:1--5 and 1 Peter 4:5.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 29. ]{.class_4938}["The way of lying" ]{.class_4939}[is a "way of life," which is now called "a lifestyle" (NEA cliché). Regarding the faculties and staffs of Christian schools, this "lifestyle" is described from seventeen different angles in our work on ]{.class_4938}[The Christian's Handbook of Biblical Scholarship ]{.class_4937}[(chapter 7). It is how they make their living: it is their "bread and butter." In America, this "lifestyle" is best exemplified by CBS, NBC, ABC, ]{.class_4938}[Time, Newsweek, ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[Life ]{.class_4937}[magazines, who have been habitual and pathological liars for so long a lie detector test would not get a rise in blood pressure out of them if they were asked to explain their editorial policies. For David, it would be oath breakers like Shimei (1 Kings 2:42) and liars like Gehazi (2 Kings 5:25).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 30. ]{.class_4938}["The way of truth," ]{.class_4939}[in the Old Testament is described in Micah 6:8 and Ecclesiastes 12:13. The judgments are not only to be ]{.class_4938}["laid before" ]{.class_4939}[the saint in the sense of thinking about them, but they are to be opened in writing and placed before his "snoot" (see Deut. 17:18).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 31. A confession of personal righteousness which expects an answer to prayer. This is not New Testament praying, no matter what the shallow milksops at BBC and Liberty think about such matters. ]{.class_4938}[Stick to i-tive-ness]{.class_4937}[ is the idea of the first clause.]{.class_4938}

Verse 32. He could already WALK (vs. 1), but he needed to learn how to RUN (see Heb. 12:14 and 1 Cor. 9:26).

[ה]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4940}[HE]{.class_4941}

119:33 "Teach me, O LORD, the way of thy statutes; and I shall keep it unto the end.

34 Give me understanding, and I shall keep thy law; yea, I shall observe it with my whole heart.

35 Make me to go in the path of thy commandments; for therein do I delight.

36 Incline my heart unto thy testimonies, and not to covetousness.

37 Turn away mine eyes from beholding vanity; and quicken thou me in thy way.

38 Stablish thy word unto thy servant, who is devoted to thy fear.

39 Turn away my reproach which I fear: for thy judgments are good.

40 Behold, I have longed after thy precepts: quicken me in thy righteousness."

[He ]{.class_4937}[means a "lattice" or a "window" (see Song of Sol. 2:9 and Mal. 3:10), and is the numeral 5 in Hebrew.]{.class_4938}

Verse 33 is clear; there is some reference to Matthew 24:13.

Verse 34 is a prayer; all is clear.

Verse 35 is like verse 16.

[The theme of the next five verses is the first two words: ]{.class_4938}["Make me."]{.class_4939}

[Verse 36 is a prayer to be delivered from covetousness, as verse 29 was a prayer to be delivered from lying. Observe that where a sin is abandoned (like lying or covetousness) something must "fill the vacuum." Hence, ]{.class_4938}["grant me thy law" ]{.class_4939}[in verse 29, and ]{.class_4938}["incline my heart" ]{.class_4939}[in verse 36. We must be "made to go" (vs. 35) in the very thing we really delight in, for the new man "delights" in ]{.class_4938}["the law of God" ]{.class_4939}[(Rom. 7:22), but only the "inner" man. The "outer" man is "somethin' else"!]{.class_4938}

Verse 37 is so he can fulfill the covenant that Job made with his eyes (Job 31:1). This is a good prayer for anyone with a boob tube in the living room.

[Verse 38 is self explanatory. (For ]{.class_4938}["fear" ]{.class_4939}[see Ps. 107:11.)]{.class_4938}

[Verse 39. He confesses that he is afraid of something he should not be afraid of (see extended comments under Psa. 107:11), but at the same time, he confesses that he knows he should not fear it, for if the reproach falls upon him (see John 2:17 for a perfect example), it is one of God's ]{.class_4938}["judgments," ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["thy judgments are good."]{.class_4939}

Verse 40 matches verses 4, 20, 25, and 37.

[ו]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4940}[VAU]{.class_4941}

119:41 "Let thy mercies come also unto me, O LORD, even thy salvation, according to thy word.

42 So shall I have wherewith to answer him that reproacheth me: for I trust in thy word.

43 And take not the word of truth utterly out of my mouth; for I have hoped in thy judgments.

44 So shall I keep thy law continually for ever and ever.

45 And I will walk at liberty: for I seek thy precepts.

46 I will speak of thy testimonies also before kings, and will not be ashamed.

47 And I will delight myself in thy commandments, which I have loved.

48 My hands also will I lift up unto thy commandments, which I have loved; and I will meditate in thy statutes."

[Vau ]{.class_4937}[denotes a "nail" or a "hook," and is used for the numeral 6.]{.class_4938}

Verse 41 can be physical salvation, as a mercy or the salvation of Romans 2:7 promised on a works conscience basis. The first case is the most fitting, as is apparent by the next verse.

Verse 43 would be to allow him to keep preaching and proclaiming the word.

[Verse 44, again, shows the Old Testament dispensation. No one ever kept God's law ]{.class_4938}["continually for ever and ever" ]{.class_4939}[but Jesus Christ. The permanently redeemed in the New Heavens (Rev. 21--22) can qualify, but that is a long way off.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 45. ]{.class_4938}[Spiritually, ]{.class_4937}[the idea is that a man is at liberty who keeps the commandments. Notice how all of this is true in the ]{.class_4938}[spiritual sense ]{.class_4937}[of Galatians 5 and Romans 6, but in those passages the ]{.class_4938}["law"]{.class_4939}[ David speaks of is BONDAGE. True liberty is found in an undefiled walk, not a licentious walk, but you cannot equate the two when comparing David with Paul.]{.class_4938}

Verse 46. This would be a good verse for Paul, for it is part of his original commission (Acts 9:15). Verse 47 could be spiritually applied to Romans 7:22, but again, Romans 13:8--10 forbids all of this from contributing to salvation in the New Testament.

A Dark Age preacher is told by a king, "If you don't shut your mouth and stop that preaching, I'll have you drowned in this river." The answer he got was, "If you do, your majesty, my soul will arrive in glory, in the palace of the King of kings, before you can get your horse back to its stable."

A king said to a Dark Age preacher, "And what did you preach on today, since today is my birthday?" "I preached," said the witness, "that kings should have kingly thoughts."

[A king said to a famous medieval Bible preacher: "You must stop preaching, or I will have you thrown in prison." He replied, "I was set free by Jesus Christ, and a prison is a palace with Him near." "All right," said the king, "I will confiscate your goods and property." "I have treasures laid up in heaven," replied the witness. About to burst a blood vessel, the king cried, "I will have you exiled!" "I have no continuing city here," was what he got back. In a fit of rage, the king roared, "I'll have your head cut off!" He got this back, ]{.class_4938}["For me to live is Christ, and to die is gain."]{.class_4939}[ His "majesty" gave up and, turning to one of his barons, he said, "What in hell are you going to do with a scoundrel like that?" You can't do ]{.class_4938}[anything ]{.class_4937}[with him. He is not ]{.class_4938}["ashamed"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 46).]{.class_4938}

Verse 48. The hands, lifted up, accompany prayer (Ps. 141:2; Neh. 8:6; 1 Tim. 2:8) to be able to keep the commandments and statutes.

[ז]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4940}[ZAIN]{.class_4941}

119:49 "Remember the word unto thy servant, upon which thou hast caused me to hope.

50 This is my comfort in my affliction: for thy word hath quickened me.

51 The proud have had me greatly in derision: yet have I not declined from thy law.

52 I remembered thy judgments of old, O LORD; and have comforted myself.

53 Horror hath taken hold upon me because of the wicked that forsake thy law.

54 Thy statutes have been my songs in the house of my pilgrimage.

55 I have remembered thy name, O LORD, in the night, and have kept thy law.

56 This I had, because I kept thy precepts."

[Zain ]{.class_4937}[means "a weapon" and is used for the numeral 7. Verse 49. The ]{.class_4938}["word," ]{.class_4939}[this time, IS a promise (see Kroll under vs. 9), for he has been caused to ]{.class_4938}["hope" ]{.class_4939}[on it. No word from God, no hope. And then, in verse 50, no word from God, no comfort and no "quickening." The Scriptures were written for our hope and comfort (Rom. 15:4). It is the incorruptible ]{.class_4938}["word" ]{.class_4939}[that quickens the sinner.]{.class_4938}

Verse 51 is Messianic and has many other applications.

[Verses 52--56 could all be claimed by Jesus Christ, to the letter. Paul and Silas could claim verses 54--55. (See ]{.class_4938}["songs in the night," ]{.class_4939}[Job 35:10; Ps. 33:3, 40:3, and 42:8).]{.class_4938}

[ח]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4940}[CHETH]{.class_4941}

119:57 "Thou art my portion, O LORD: I have said that I would keep thy words.

58 I intreated thy favour with my whole heart: be merciful unto me according to thy word.

59 I thought on my ways, and turned my feet unto thy testimonies.

60 I made haste, and delayed not to keep thy commandments.

61 The bands of the wicked have robbed me: but I have not forgotten thy law.

62 At midnight I will rise to give thanks unto thee because of thy righteous judgments.

63 I am a companion of all them that fear thee, and of them that keep thy precepts.

64 The earth, O LORD, is full of thy mercy: teach me thy statutes."

[Cheth ]{.class_4937}[means a "hedge" or a "fence," and is used for the numeral 8.]{.class_4938}

[Observe that it is not enough to keep the "word" (or the statutes, judgments, precepts, commandments, etc.); ]{.class_4938}["I would keep thy words" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 57, see 2 Tim. 1:13 in ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on the Pastoral Epistles, ]{.class_4937}[1990). Verse 58 is self explanatory, and so are verses 59--60. The writer did a little introspection (see Ps. 4:4) and made haste to get on the right road (see Heb. 12:12--13).]{.class_4938}

Verse 61 is strongly Messianic.

Verse 62, if literal, is some real fanaticism by modern standards, but David probably did it more than once.

Verse 63 is a general confession of faith that could be said by Moses, Joshua, Caleb, Gideon, Barak, Hezekiah, Joseph, Joash, Josiah, or a dozen others.

[Verse 64. ]{.class_4938}[Doctrinally, ]{.class_4937}[this will be the Millennium (Ps. 33:5, 65:9, 104:24, etc.). In a spiritual sense, we can say that on earth throughout the centuries, God has shown mercy to sinful men many, many times (see Ps. 33:18, 22; 57:10; 85:10; 86:5, etc.). You may have noticed that verse 60 doesn't match Proverbs 19:2. You don't have to worry about sinning when you make haste to ]{.class_4938}["come down" ]{.class_4939}[(Zacchaeus, Luke 19:5), or when you hasten to obey God.]{.class_4938}

Your feet can take you places (vs. 59) where God's testimonies are not and will not be kept.

[ט]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4940}[TETH]{.class_4941}

119:65 "Thou hast dealt well with thy servant, O LORD, according unto thy word.

66 Teach me good judgment and knowledge: for I have believed thy commandments.

67 Before I was afflicted I went astray: but now have I kept thy word.

68 Thou art good, and doest good; teach me thy statutes.

69 The proud have forged a lie against me: but I will keep thy precepts with my whole heart.

70 Their heart is as fat as grease; but I delight in thy law.

71 It is good for me that I have been afflicted; that I might learn thy statutes.

72 The law of thy mouth is better unto me than thousands of gold and silver."

[Teth is ]{.class_4937}[something rolled or twisted together, and is generally interpreted as a "serpent." It stands for 9.]{.class_4938}

Verse 65 is a simple acknowledgment of blessings; he has tried out some promises and found them to be so.

Verse 66. By coincidence, there are sixty six books in the canon, and believing has to precede all teaching. God will teach a man who believes (1 Thess. 2:13), whereas a man who does not believe cannot be taught anything (see Ps. 32:9).

Verse 67. The verse is not Messianic. The writer had to have God deal with him (Heb. 12:4--10) to bring him back into the way. Throughout his problems he had never forgotten the word. The wicked may have robbed him of a great deal (vs. 61), but they couldn't steal his Bible; he had it in his heart (vs. 11).

[Verse 68. There are good people who do "badly"; there are bad people who do "goodly," but God is good and ]{.class_4938}[does ]{.class_4937}[good (see Luke 18:19 and Rom. 8:28). In that the writer was afflicted, we learn from what follows that affliction is good (vs. 68), it is faithful (vs. 75), and it produces good results (vs. 71) because it is part of God's doing (vs. 65).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 69 is plain. ]{.class_4938}["Fat"]{.class_4939}[ around the heart (vs. 70) is the condition of ]{.class_4938}["the proud." ]{.class_4939}[The idea is that they are stuffed with the world, the flesh, and the devil and ]{.class_4938}["have more than heart could wish" ]{.class_4939}[(Psa. 78:7; Job 21:7--13). A man who delights in the law (vs. 70) is not going to be very ]{.class_4938}["fat" ]{.class_4939}[in the sense of Psalm 22:29 and 92:14, at least in the sight of the world.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 71 is an honest confession after a "whuppin'." Moses learned the statutes by suffering ]{.class_4938}["affliction with the people of God" ]{.class_4939}[(Heb. 11:25).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 72 should be compared with verses 127 and 162, and note, in those cases, it is not merely the ]{.class_4938}["law"]{.class_4939}[ but also ]{.class_4938}["thy word." ]{.class_4939}[Many years before this, Job said that he esteemed the WORDS of God's mouth more necessary than his food (Job 23:12).]{.class_4938}

From a Messianic standpoint, notice what has accumulated in Psalm 119, since verse 20, in regard to the ENEMIES of Jesus Christ.

Verse 21: They are cursed.

Verse 23: They spoke against Him.

Verse 53: They forsake God's law.

Verse 61: They robbed Him.

Verse 69: They forged lies against Him.

If we continue this study we find that:

[Verse 78: ]{.class_4938}["They dealt perversely"]{.class_4939}[ with Him.]{.class_4938}

Verse 85: They digged a pit for Him.

Verse 86: They persecuted Him wrongfully.

Verse 95: They waited to destroy Him.

[Verse 110: They ]{.class_4938}["laid a snare"]{.class_4939}[ for Him.]{.class_4938}

Verse 139: They forgot God's words.

Verse 150: They followed after mischief.

The "Champions for Christ" at Lynchburg seem to have a terrible time finding their Saviour in a Book that Christ said testified of Him (John 5:39).

119:73 "Thy hands have made me and fashioned me: give me understanding, that I may learn thy commandments.

74 They that fear thee will be glad when they see me; because I have hoped in thy word.

75 I know, O LORD, that thy judgments are right, and that thou in faithfulness hast afflicted me.

76 Let, I pray thee, thy merciful kindness be for my comfort, according to thy word unto thy servant.

77 Let thy tender mercies come unto me, that I may live: for thy law is my delight.

78 Let the proud be ashamed; for they dealt perversely with me without a cause: but I will meditate in thy precepts.

79 Let those that fear thee turn unto me, and those that have known thy testimonies.

80 Let my heart be sound in thy statutes; that I be not ashamed."

[Yod ]{.class_4937}[means "hand," or "right hand." It signifies 10.]{.class_4938}

Verse 74 is like verse 63. People who fear God like to be around people who fear God, and people who fear men cannot stand the fellowship of those that fear God (see Ps. 107:11 and comments).

Verse 75 is like verse 71 and 67. Having visited the "woodshed," he now is entitled to claim some love and comfort (vss. 76--77). We know that God is right (vs. 75) even when we don't agree with Him. (Do I hear an "Amen"? Hello? Hello?)

Verse 78 is Messianic; see Psalm 109.

Verse 79 can be Messianic also (see Paul in Acts 13:16), for those that fear the Lord (Ps. 103:17, 107:43) are candidates for New Testament salvation; see this illustrated doctrinally in Acts 10.

119:81 "My soul fainteth for thy salvation: but I hope in thy word.

82 Mine eyes fail for thy word, saying, When wilt thou comfort me?

83 For I am become like a bottle in the smoke; yet do I not forget thy statutes.

84 How many are the days of thy servant? when wilt thou execute judgment on them that persecute me?

85 The proud have digged pits for me, which are not after thy law.

86 All thy commandments are faithful: they persecute me wrongfully; help thou me.

87 They had almost consumed me upon earth; but I forsook not thy precepts.

88 Quicken me after thy lovingkindness; so shall I keep the testimony of thy mouth."

[Caph ]{.class_4937}[signifies a "wing" or the "hollow of the hand" (the palm). It stands for the numeral 20.]{.class_4938}

Verse 81 is like verses 20 and 28.

[Verse 82 is more of the same. He is waiting to hear from God. The ]{.class_4938}["bottle in the smoke," ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 83) would be a leather bottle hanging by a fireplace, that has dried out and has cracked. Travelers tell us that in Oriental rooms, with no chimneys, the room gets so filled with smoke that after a few months the leather bottles are shrivelled, "smoke dried," and good for nothing. Thomas Harmer says that as far as he is concerned, ]{.class_4938}["a bottle in the smoke" ]{.class_4939}[is just any bottle that happens to hang in any Arab's tent (]{.class_4938}[Treasury]{.class_4937}[, p. 310).]{.class_4938}

Bibles must be available in classrooms, and if they are not, they must be made available in cells; that is, if you are a sincere "do gooder" who wants to "help your fellow man."

Verse 85 and 86 are Messianic. A lawful pit would be one dug to bury bodies (2 Sam. 18:17; Jer. 41:9), garbage, cisterns (Isa. 30:14), or a pit that God Himself prepares for the wicked (see Num. 16:30; Isa. 14:15). The pits here are hidden holes in the pathway for the purpose of catching a man or an animal.

Verses 86 and 87 are self explanatory.

Verse 88 is a prayer like verses 25, 37, and 40.

119:89 "For ever, O LORD, thy word is settled in heaven.

90 Thy faithfulness is unto all generations: thou hast established the earth, and it abideth.

91 They continue this day according to thine ordinances: for all are thy servants.

92 Unless thy law had been my delights, I should then have perished in mine affliction.

93 I will never forget thy precepts; for with them thou hast quickened me.

94 I am thine, save me; for I have sought thy precepts.

95 The wicked have waited for me to destroy me: but I will consider thy testimonies.

[Lamed ]{.class_4937}[signifies 30 as a numeral and means "an ox goad."]{.class_4938}

[I love pure things. ]{.class_4937}[Do you know why I love them? Because I have been so IMPURE most of my life that I appreciate what has been so unattainable for me. I didn't enroll at BJU, in 1949, to learn the Bible or even to get into the ministry; I enrolled there because a farm woman ("Mom" May, of Sawyerville, Alabama) told me that no one there smoked or drank or attended the movies. 119:145 "I cried with my whole heart; hear me, O LORD: I will keep thy statutes.

146 I cried unto thee; save me, and I shall keep thy testimonies.

147 I prevented the dawning of the morning, and cried: I hoped in thy word.

148 Mine eyes prevent the night watches, that I might meditate in thy word.

149 Hear my voice according unto thy lovingkindness: O LORD, quicken me according to thy judgment.

150 They draw nigh that follow after mischief: they are far from thy law.

151 Thou art near, O LORD; and all thy commandments are truth.

152 Concerning thy testimonies, I have known of old that thou hast founded them for ever."

[Koph ]{.class_4937}[stands for 100 and denotes "the hole of an axe"; that is, the hole in the axe head into which the handle is stuck.]{.class_4938}

Verses 145--148 are plain. The Psalmist:

1\. Cried with his heart (vs. 145).

2\. Cried with an undivided heart (vs. 145).

3\. Cried to God (vs. 146).

4\. Cried to God early (vs. 148).

5\. Cried to God in the night (vs. 148).

[The ]{.class_4938}["night" ]{.class_4939}[plays a great part in the Psalms (see Ps. 78:14). You can have LIGHT in the night (auroras, a pillar of fire, house lights, street lights, moonlight, starlight, fireflies, phosphorus, vehicle lights). You can have ALARMS in the night (Gideon's attack, the coming of the Bridegroom, burglaries, fire alarms, the reports of "listening posts" in World War I, the Lord speaking to you (1 Sam. 3:1--6) and warning you (Abimelech; Gen. 20:3). You can have REST in the night (Ps. 4:8, 127:2). You can have WATCHFULNESS in the night (night prayer vigils, sentries, "security guards" on patrol, and night watchmen going about the city \[Song of Sol. 3:3\], and waiting for the Second Coming \[1 Thess. 5:1--10\]). You can have SONGS in the night (like Paul and Silas sang, or Christ Himself---Matt. 26:30---or like Schubert and Mozart composed when they were sick and dying). You can have DESTRUCTION in the night (Belshazzar's feast, the bombings of London and Hamburg).]{.class_4938}

119:161 "Princes have persecuted me without a cause: but my heart standeth in awe of thy word.

162 I rejoice at thy word, as one that findeth great spoil.
