[Schin ]{.class_4937}[is 300, and it means "to be high, or spread out." It also can mean "to spread out, or intertwine." This twenty-first letter has a vowel point on the right stem if it is pronounced "SH" (Schin), but a point on the left stem for "Sin." See Judges 12:6 for the trouble this caused the Ephraimites. Technically, Schin (right stem) means "tooth."]{.class_4938}

David stands in awe of the Scriptures because:

1\. They are good enough for kings and princes.

2\. They bring joy to the heart.

3\. They deliver a man from worry and fear.

4\. They bring a real peace to the inner man.

5\. They impart a holy hatred for sin.

6\. They sustain hope and cause a man to praise God.

7\. They are indestructible within themselves, though the best and most refined intellects of six thousand years have attacked them, without one let up.

No wonder the Psalmist stood in awe of such a Book.

119:169 "Let my cry come near before thee, O LORD: give me understanding according to thy word.

170 Let my supplication come before thee: deliver me according to thy word.

171 My lips shall utter praise, when thou hast taught me thy statutes.

172 My tongue shall speak of thy word: for all thy commandments are righteousness.

[Tau ]{.class_4984}is the twenty second, and last letter in the alphabet (but twenty three if you make a distinction between Schin and Sin). Tau stands for 400 and means "a sign." The perversion of this letter is what Constantine and his Catholic followers called "the sign of the cross," because the name of this letter in Phoenicia (and on Maccabean coins) is given as a CROSS. The Greeks and Romans took both the name and the form of the letter from the Hebrew and got a "T." A "sign mark" is called a "cruciform." At the synod of Chalcedon, the apostate Greek and Romans bishops who could not sign their names, signed them with "the sign of the cross"; that is, an "X." The custom has been preserved for fifteen centuries. The doctrinal and prophetic significance of this is staggering, so naturally Dummelow, Kroll, Baethgen, Hengstenberg, Yates, Motyer, Lange, Matthew, Ellicott, Williams, Clark, Kutilek, Keil, Gesenius, Delitzsch, Borland, Dobson, Chapman, and Hawkins are going to miss it by 300,000 miles, at least.

1\. "X" is the Son of Perdition's letter (see [The Mark of the Beast, ]{.class_4984}1960), and "X marks the spot" (see Jer. 13:23 and Rev. 13:2--3). As in "Malcom X."

2\. Both Messiahs---the right one (Luke 2:26) and the wrong one (Ezek. 28:14)---are connected with this letter, because the Lord smites the earth with a CURSE (Mal. 4:6) when the false messiah is here (2 Thess. 2:1---6), but He also caused the right Messiah to become a ["curse for us" ]{.class_4982}(Gal. 3:13) when He came.

3\. "The sign of the cross," then, becomes the sign of a curse ([The Mark of the Beast, ]{.class_4984}1960), for it finishes the Jewish nation [spiritually ]{.class_4984}at the First Advent, and nearly annihilates them at the Second Advent (Lam. 5--6). No Christian has any business with such a "sign." The SIGNS are for [the Jew ]{.class_4984}(1 Cor. 1:22). (All of the commentators miss all the connections: too many "original autographs.")

Nothing is difficult in the Psalm. Verse 169 is self-explanatory. Note the prayer for ["understanding," ]{.class_4982}as in verse 18. Verses 170--172 are self-explanatory. Observe again, that ["thy word" ]{.class_4982}is not the fictitious, fragmentary, obscure "Word of God" that they talk about at BBC in Springfield. ["Thy word" ]{.class_4982}(vs. 169) consists of ["statutes\...commandments\...precepts\...and judgments" ]{.class_4982}(vss. 171--173, 175) that constitute a ["law"]{.class_4982} (vs. 174). You can READ "God's word." You can hide it in your heart (vs. 11), you can cleanse your way by observing it (vs. 9), and you can get things from it (vs. 162) that are more valuable than the contents of the Chase Manhattan Bank. Verse 175 is a prayer for physical life as in Genesis 19:20 and Ezekiel 3:19--21.

Verse 176 is a national prayer of Israel (see Ezek. 34:2--16). Christ refers to this at His First Advent (Matt. 10:6), but this is plainly the Second Advent, for the ["lost sheep of the house of Israel" ]{.class_4982}in Matthew 10:6 and 15:24 in Christ's day are represented by the prodigal son and the publicans and harlots. The older brother (Luke 15:29--30), who did not forget God's ["commandments" ]{.class_4982}(Ps. 119:176) was NOT a "lost" sheep. At the Second Advent, [the whole nation is lost, ]{.class_4984}and they are keeping ["thy commandments," ]{.class_4982}which is plain from Revelation 12:17 and 14:12, even if not one faculty member of one Christian school in America can find ANYTHING that has to do with this.

A man who thinks that Tribulation salvation matches Romans 10; Galatians 3; Ephesians 2; and Titus 3 has not been reading the Scriptures. (He probably has been fooling with a "reliable translation.")

Inspirationally, David went astray, and yet in a backslidden condition (2 Sam. 12:5--6), he still knew more about God's commandments (see Exod. 22:1) than the Scofield Board of Editors knew in 1909, although they would have professed to be innocent of anything like adultery or murder.

The Psalm closes at verse 176, which is 8 times 22 (the number of letters in a Hebrew alphabet). The number is not divisible by 3, 5, 6, 7, 9, or 10.

PSALM 123

123:1 "Unto thee lift I up mine eyes, O thou that dwellest in the heavens.

The entire Psalm takes place in the Tribulation. Whatever the historical associations may have been at the time it was written (Hezekiah's time, Josiah's time, Ezra's time, etc.), it will be fulfilled in our near future. This is a Tribulation Jew waiting for the Messiah. All is evident.

There is no doubt about the ]{.class_4938}["our"]{.class_4939}[ and ]{.class_4938}["us"]{.class_4939}[ and ]{.class_4938}["we"]{.class_4939}[ (vss. 2--3): they are Israelites. The stupefying thing about the Liberty Commentary is that it erased the words ]{.class_4938}["our"]{.class_4939}[ and ]{.class_4938}["us"]{.class_4939}[ from verse 2,]{.class_4938}[ "us"]{.class_4939}[ (two times) and ]{.class_4938}["we"]{.class_4939}[ from verse 3, and then removed ]{.class_4938}["our"]{.class_4939}[ from verse 4. Kroll's comments confine the entire Psalm to ]{.class_4938}[one ]{.class_4937}[man, in trouble, trying to get help for ]{.class_4938}[himself. ]{.class_4937}[Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown note a plural in the Psalm but never ]{.class_4938}[identified ]{.class_4937}[who they were; ]{.class_4938}[they were all Israelites. ]{.class_4937}[Look at the next Psalm and tell me how in the world any sane Christian could have missed who ]{.class_4938}["our," "us," ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["we"]{.class_4939}[ were. Look at it: ]{.class_4938}["now may Israel say," ]{.class_4939}[Psalm 124:1, was the answer to the petitions given in the preceding Psalm. How does a "Fundamental" Baptist, who believes in "verbal, plenary, inspired, original autographs," fail to find Biblical truth every time it shows up? Would you hire a place kicker to play on an NFL team if he averaged eight yards per punt and missed nine extra points out of ten tries? Well, that is what they hire at Christian colleges and seminaries.]{.class_4938}

[Israel is literally waiting on God in the Tribulation; this explains the figures of a ]{.class_4938}["maiden"]{.class_4939}[ and ]{.class_4938}["servants" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 2). They are completely at His mercy. A slave looks to his master's ]{.class_4938}["hand" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 2) for three things:]{.class_4938}

1\. What the master wants done, which he will indicate with a gesture.

2\. What the master will give him, which is passed from "hand to hand."

3\. How the master will punish him (see Exod. 21:20).

Devotionally we may say that:

1\. A man will not look up until he wants to "see" God.

PSALM 124

124:1 "If it had not been the LORD who was on our side, now may Israel say;

2 If it had not been the LORD who was on our side, when men rose up against us:

3 Then they had swallowed us up quick, when their wrath was kindled against us:

4 Then the waters had overwhelmed us, the stream had gone over our soul:

5 Then the proud waters had gone over our soul.

6 Blessed be the LORD, who hath not given us as a prey to their teeth.

7 Our soul is escaped as a bird out of the snare of the fowlers: the snare is broken, and we are escaped.

8 Our help is in the name of the LORD, who made heaven and earth."

To refer to Liberty University and their "Bible Correspondence Courses," at this point, would be the equivalent of going to a press conference at the White House in order to get information on Martin Luther King: there is nothing available. At verse 1 there is a traffic jam (Motyer, Yates, Dummelow, Lange, Clarke, Henry, Spurgeon, Kroll, et al.), where the cars are stalled bumper to bumper as far as the eye can see.

[The whole Psalm describes the literal deliverance of Israel from a literal flood, and from being literally ]{.class_4938}[eaten and swallowed ]{.class_4937}[(see Isa. 6:13; Rev. 6:9--11; Ps. 35:25, 44:22; Rev. 20:4, 12:15; Dan. 9:26, and Jer. 51:34).]{.class_4938}

[All the Bible correcting Alexandrians in the Scholar's Union, who turned up their noses at "King James Onlyism," missed all the references in ]{.class_4938}[both Testaments. ]{.class_4937}[Par for you know what.]{.class_4938}

[This Psalm gives the clearest description of the restoration of Israel in the entire Psalter. The Jews are talking in verses 1--2 from the standpoint of someone who realizes that an ]{.class_4938}["if"]{.class_4939}[ determined their fate, and that ]{.class_4938}["if"]{.class_4939}[ was ]{.class_4938}["If it had not been the LORD." ]{.class_4939}[The Lord is prejudiced; He takes sides. He takes the side of Shem (Gen. 9:26), in a showdown, and the other races turn out to be "inferior." You can't always tell whose side He is on now, but bless God, in the Tribulation!]{.class_4938}

[When someone wins a war they think it was because God was "on their side"---see the Civil War in America, for example. The South won the Civil War, for ]{.class_4938}[they ]{.class_4937}[came out with the Book, while the Northerners came out with the Vatican. "[Now may Israel say," ]{.class_4939}[and not before]{.class_4938}[ "now,"]{.class_4939}[ ]{.class_4937}[because ]{.class_4938}["now"]{.class_4939}[ is when Jesus Christ has returned to sit down on the throne of David. Kroll, Yates, Motyer, Ewald, Hitzig, Perowne, Delitzsch, Hengstenberg, Duhm, Clarke, and Lange couldn't locate ]{.class_4938}["now."]{.class_4939}[ Why not? That isn't the half of it. The words in italics in the ]{.class_4938}[AV ]{.class_4937}[all appear in regular print in the Masoretic Society's Hebrew Bible in English. How is it that the ]{.class_4938}[AV ]{.class_4937}[italics line up with the Masoretic Hebrew? You'll find out at the judgment.]{.class_4938}

[The ]{.class_4938}["waters" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 4) were ]{.class_4938}["waters"]{.class_4939}[ (Rev. 12:15), as in]{.class_4938}[ "waters,"]{.class_4939}[ spelled W A T E R S. Note how the original English of the ]{.class_4938}[King James ]{.class_4937}["Onlyism" clears up the muddled obscurity of "Dead Duck Otherism." The]{.class_4938}[ "snare"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 7) is the snare of Ecclesiastes 9:12, and the ]{.class_4938}["fowlers"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 7) are the servants of the]{.class_4938}[ "fowler"]{.class_4939}[ (see Ps. 91:3). The Jews have been saved from accepting ]{.class_4938}["the idol shepherd"]{.class_4939}[ (Zech. 11:17), whom Christ said they would receive (John 5:43). These have rejected the false messiah and lived to receive the One, but only with God's divine intervention. ]{.class_4938}["If it had not been the LORD\...."]{.class_4939}

[The sterile shallowness of Kroll's comments on the Psalm are so pitiful I will not even quote them. No expositor in the fifteenth or sixteenth century had ]{.class_4938}[less light ]{.class_4937}[on the Psalm than those at Lynchburg, Virginia, in 1991.]{.class_4938}

1\. The electromagnetic force field of the universe made nothing.

[2. Our help is not in science, man, philosophy, the church, the sacraments, religion, psychiatry, the UN, or education. Our ]{.class_4938}["help"]{.class_4939}[ is in one name (vs. 8); the name ]{.class_4938}["above every name" ]{.class_4939}[(Phil. 2:9), ]{.class_4938}["not only in this world, but also in that which is to come" ]{.class_4939}[(Eph. 1:21). ]{.class_4938}["The world to come" ]{.class_4939}[(Heb. 2:5) is the Millennium.]{.class_4938}

[All of the commentators lost the ]{.class_4938}[doctrinal ]{.class_4937}[reference again. When they carved the Millennium out of verse 1, they lost Jesus Christ out of verse 8 in every way except the spiritual sense of prayer.]{.class_4938}

PSALM 125

125:1 "They that trust in the LORD shall be as mount Zion, which cannot be removed, but abideth for ever.

2 As the mountains are round about Jerusalem, so the LORD is round about his people from henceforth even for ever.

3 For the rod of the wicked shall not rest upon the lot of the righteous; lest the righteous put forth their hands unto iniquity.

4 Do good, O LORD, unto those that be good, and to them that are upright in their hearts.

5 As for such as turn aside unto their crooked ways, the LORD shall lead them forth with the workers of iniquity: but peace shall be upon Israel."

[The Psalm deals with the beginning of the Millennium; note ]{.class_4938}["from henceforth even for ever" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 2) stuck right in front of the critic's face so he would know where he is. See Psalm 131:3; Isaiah 9:7, and 52:1, laid right out where any fool with a sixth-grade education could get his bearings. The Scholar's Union cannot get them; they cannot get them with a quadrant, a compass, a map, the north star, and a shortwave radio set. When Kroll hits this "highly visible" (news media cliché, 1980s) truth, found in three places in Isaiah---add Isaiah 59:21, plus Ezekiel 36:12 and Micah 4:7---he sinks into a "blue funk" and pretends that five words (]{.class_4938}["from henceforth even for ever"]{.class_4939}[) are simply not in the text. No comment. Didn't he look at the next Psalm? ]{.class_4938}["When the LORD turned again the captivity of Zion"]{.class_4939}[ (Ps. 126:1)?]{.class_4938}

This situation is the mount of Olives on the east (representing prayer), the east ridge on the other side of the valley of Jehoshophat (representing judgment), mount Scopas on the northern side, and the "Hill of Evil Council" on the south side of the city, representing, respectively, the crucifixion and betrayal. There is no peace on earth till the "city of peace" has peace.

[The real mount Zion (Heb. 12:22) will abide even after the heavens and earth have melted with a fervent heat (2 Pet. 3:10), and the earthly mount Zion will probably be renovated so that it is still there on the ]{.class_4938}["new earth" ]{.class_4939}[(Rev. 21:1--6). God remains "around" His people from Revelation 19 to Revelation 22, for at the final revolt of Gog and Magog (Rev. 20:7--9), He destroys ]{.class_4938}["the rod of the wicked" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 3). Bullinger is correct in locating the doctrinal reference to the]{.class_4938}[ "wicked"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 3) as the Son of Perdition in 2 Thessalonians 2.]{.class_4938}

[Notice the WORKS element in the Millennium, totally missing from the Church Age. In this age, Paul says ]{.class_4938}["there is none that doeth good" ]{.class_4939}[(Rom. 3:12), but in the Millennium there are ]{.class_4938}["good"]{.class_4939}[ folks who do ]{.class_4938}["good."]{.class_4939}[ The "upright in heart," in the passage (vs. 4), have had the Spirit poured out on them (Acts 2:17), and the world will be under a set of regulations (Matt. 5--7) that demand more righteousness than that of the Pharisees. The Sermon on the Mount is the Constitution for the kingdom found in Psalm 125. There are two classes found here that are found nowhere in this age: ]{.class_4938}["the righteous" ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["the wicked" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 3), exactly as you had them in the Old Testament, under the Law. Millennial salvation is NOT "by grace through faith. " And if 500,000 Baptists from A.D. 40 to A.D. 2002 thought otherwise, it would no more affect the truth than a bag full of gummy bears.]{.class_4938}

[Of course, there are always some ]{.class_4938}[devotional ]{.class_4937}[things we may glean from any passage.]{.class_4938}

1\. Saints, obviously, can yield to pressure (vs. 3). "Peer pressure" is what does it in the high schools, and watching successful crooks in the business world is what does it after college. The influence of people like Michael Jackson, John Lennon, Madonna, Elvis Presley, Bing Crosby, Hank Williams, Frank Sinatra, Pete Rose, and Joe Namath on teenagers is absolutely incalculable.

[2. The prayer is not for those who just do ]{.class_4938}["good," ]{.class_4939}[but those who do good with the ]{.class_4938}[right motive ]{.class_4937}[for doing good (vs. 4).]{.class_4938}

[Observe that, ]{.class_4938}[doctrinally, ]{.class_4937}[verse 5 refers to Luke 12:46, which again---for the fifth time---fixes the doctrinal position of the Psalm. A great deal could be said about verse 3, in relation to Proverbs 29:2, 12. In a country where the news media reports everything, and digs into every kind of corruption, fraud, deceit, lying, embezzling, fornicating, perversion, etc., for "news value," their readers quickly lose faith in ALL virtue, goodness, honesty, loyalty, bravery, patriotism, and hard work, for they quickly learn that immoral degenerates can make a million dollars by simply magnifying sex or by gambling. There are not five "role models" in Congress that fit the Biblical pattern for an upright man. There are not five in the soap operas, there are not ten in Hollywood, and there are not a dozen in professional basketball or football. In the popular music world, there are not two.]{.class_4938}

[Finally, to show you how really barren and pointless the commentators are on this Psalm, observe that verse 5 is the subject matter of the following verses in ]{.class_4938}[one Old Testament book. ]{.class_4937}[Proverbs 2:22; 3:25; 10:3, 7, 24, 25, 28, 30 (look at that last one); 11:7, 10, 21, 23, 31 (look at that last one); 12:7; 14:11, 19, 32; 16:4; 20:26; 21:12, 18; 24:20; 25:5 (get that one). This will give you some idea of the sterile, impotent, ]{.class_4938}[non Biblical ]{.class_4937}[perspective from which Kroll, Motyer, Dummelow, Lange, Clarke, Hengstenberg, and others view the Holy Bible. Any time you want to "ditch" the whole crew, just refer to the FUTURE, SATAN, or the STRUCTURE OF THE UNIVERSE, and you will successfully "bail out" the pilot, copilot, navigator, gunner, bombardier, and flight planner. No Bible critic who ever lived could handle one fourth of the verses.]{.class_4938}

PSALM 126

126:1 "When the LORD turned again the captivity of Zion, we were like them that dream.

2 Then was our mouth filled with laughter, and our tongue with singing: then said they among the heathen, The LORD hath done great things for them.

3 The LORD hath done great things for us; whereof we are glad.

4 Turn again our captivity, O LORD, as the streams in the south.

5 They that sow in tears shall reap in joy.

6 He that goeth forth and weepeth, bearing precious seed, shall doubtless come again with rejoicing, bringing his sheaves with him."

The very first verse should have told the Scholar's Union how to handle Psalm 125, but it showed them nothing. Their "historical" tendencies to "historicize" everything into "historic positions" (i.e., relegate it to the past) "benches" them. Good, old Kroll can always be depended upon for a double-tongued, two faced flim flam that will get him by with the postmillennialists and the premillennialists. In typical Bob Jones-Eusebian fashion: "Of much greater consequence is the question of interpretation. Should we with the majority of interpreters understand the Psalm to be a message of praise to God for the first wave of immigration back to Palestine after the Babylonian captivity? Or should we see\...this Psalm to have a reference to any one of a number of occasions where Jerusalem was besieged? Or is the Psalm Messianic\...with the call for a restoration from captivity in verse 4 to be understood as the national restoration of Israel when the Messiah returns in power and great glory?"

[Hold the phone, bud, that ain't no "call to restoration" in verse 1. Those folks in verse 1 have been ]{.class_4938}[restored]{.class_4937}[ so good, they aren't ever going to get over it (see the previous Psalm 125:1--2). What blew Kroll off the landscape? Simple: he couldn't understand anything if it wasn't in chronological order. Watch the Lord mess with his gray matter at this point: "Each interpretation presents us (speak for your own blind guides, Kroll; quit including those of us who believe the Book\]\...The Messianic interpretation appears forced. Those\...must wrestle with the question of whether Zion is used of the captives themselves or of the city of Jerusalem. All things being considered, perhaps \[Matt. 7:29\] the most likely interpretation is that this Psalm records the joy of those Israelites who have returned from the Exile."]{.class_4938}

[He threw out Biblical prophecy on the grounds that he couldn't understand how one word could apply to a city and the ]{.class_4938}[population ]{.class_4937}[of that city. Can you imagine such a thing from any Christian who had read the Bible through at least five times? Impossible. He made you think that YOU had the same problem HE had because he had given up on the Psalm ]{.class_4938}[doctrinally.]{.class_4937}[ Look at Job 42:10, Ezek. 16:53, 55 and tell us how anyone could be that fouled up in his head. Zion is a hill in Psalm 2. People are ]{.class_4938}["daughters of Zion"]{.class_4937}[ in Psalm 9. Zion is a ]{.class_4938}[city ]{.class_4937}[in Psalm 14. It is a mountain that can rejoice in Psalm 48. And in Psalm 53:6, the prayer that was made is now answered in Psalm 126: ]{.class_4938}["Oh that the salvation of Israel were come out of Zion! When God bringeth back the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad." ]{.class_4939}[Compare this with 126:1--2 and tell me that "All things considered, perhaps the most likely interpretation is\...."]{.class_4938}

That is Baptist Fundamentalism (Falwell brand) in 1991.

[Those in verse 4 are still in the Tribulation; the Lord just messed up the chronology like He has a habit of doing (see Ps. 89:2 for example). ]{.class_4938}["Then said they among the heathen," ]{.class_4939}[has no historical precedent anywhere in Ezra or Nehemiah, and Kroll knew it, ]{.class_4938}[so he wisely struck out all six words and made you think the last words came from Israelites. ]{.class_4937}[They didn't; they came from ]{.class_4938}["the heathen."]{.class_4939}

This is "Baptist Fundamentalism" in 1991.

[Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown followed Kroll to verse 2, but when they hit ]{.class_4938}["the heathen," ]{.class_4939}[they refer you to Dr. De Burgh who says that verse 4 is ]{.class_4938}[future fulfillment. ]{.class_4937}[You bet your booties it is. Watching the commentators Syriacking and Hebrewizing and Coptically working on the expression ]{.class_4938}["turn again our captivity" ]{.class_4939}[is reminiscent of watching Spiros Zodhiates, A. T. Robertson, or Kenneth Wuest stumbling through Hebrews 6:1--6 like a peg legged, drunken pirate on a rolling deck in high seas.]{.class_4938}

[The heathen say exactly what Psalm 126:2 said, and you couldn't think it was ]{.class_4938}[Israel ]{.class_4937}[saying verse 2, for in verse 3 the same expression is repeated with the substitution of ]{.class_4938}["us" ]{.class_4939}[for ]{.class_4938}["them."]{.class_4939}[ That is, you couldn't think the two were ]{.class_4938}[identical ]{.class_4937}[unless you went to Bob Jones or Liberty University, where no one has yet learned that]{.class_4938}[ "them"]{.class_4939}[ is a third person plural and ]{.class_4938}["us" ]{.class_4939}[is a first person plural. Kroll should have learned this in the fourth grade.]{.class_4938}

"[As the streams in the south" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 4). The Jews are praying for a "gulley washer." If you want to know what this is, then go to the south in America (specifically Texas) and watch what happens to a dry river bed when a real downpour comes. What is prayed for is a quick and sudden return from captivity; that is, a powerful return from captivity. A real "gully washer" will fill a ten foot dry ditch to the brim with water in less than two hours, and turn it into a raging, roaring cataract that will sweep trees, cars, and houses before it. The soulwinners of the Psalm (vss. 5--6) are those of Ecclesiastes 11:1--7, which see. They are turning ]{.class_4938}["many to righteousness"]{.class_4939}[ in Daniel's Seventieth Week (see Dan. 12:3 for the exact ]{.class_4938}[doctrinal ]{.class_4937}[reference).]{.class_4938}

[Devotionally, ]{.class_4937}[much can be said. Verses 5 and 6 are famous soulwinning texts and can be used with Matthew 9:37, John 4:36, 37, and 1 Corinthians 3:6--7 in the New Testament. The Tribulation references are in John 16:16--21, exactly, and in Matthew 5:4, exactly. No commentator could find any of the passages, although ]{.class_4938}["a little while" ]{.class_4939}[is repeated seven times in John 16 so you will spot the seven years of Daniel's Seventieth Week. The ]{.class_4938}[precious seed]{.class_4937}[ is the word of God (i.e. God's WORDS, not "God's Word"). The crop to be harvested is sinners (Matt. 10:38), and they are likened to wheat in a field (John 4:35) or grapes in a vineyard (Matt. 21:28).]{.class_4938}

[Devotionally ]{.class_4937}[speaking, when a man travails in prayer over lost souls (see Paul in Rom. 9:3; 1 Cor. 2:3; and Acts 20:19), God honors his burden and compassion and "puts fish on his stringer," which may be a rather crude way of putting Mark 1:17, but in this age who could offend anyone with ]{.class_4938}[crudeness? ]{.class_4937}[The Christians to whom Swindoll, Ankerberg, MacArthur, Bob Jones IV, Robert Schuller, and Dobson talk are watching half-dressed belly dancers doing the "bumps and grinds" in their living rooms weekly, and see more than fifty people (weekly) stabbed, shot, hacked, pierced, burned, blown up, or beaten to death, right in their living room. (Don't bug me with this "crude" bit, or this "uncouth" bit.)]{.class_4938}

[I have returned from many a trip to Memphis, Detroit, Los Angeles, Rochester, Dallas, San Antonio, Seattle, Atlanta, Mobile, etc., "bringing my sheaves" with me. I have also returned from some trips to Key West, Jacksonville, Cincinnati, Columbus, Toledo, Kansas City, Fort Worth, Gastonia, Charleston, Dryden, Grand Rapids, and Panama City without any sheaves. "In season, out of season," is how it goes. I have had to sow the precious seed when the Cold War was going on, the Iranian crisis, the Watergate crisis, the landing on the moon, the war in Vietnam, the Oliver North Crisis, the Iraqi crisis, the Civil Rights marches, and assassinations of Jack Kennedy and King, the "ministries" of Presley and the Beatles, the Super Bowl games, the Bakker and Swaggart scandals, the impeachment of Nixon, the end of the Berlin Wall, "double digit" inflation, the "domino effect," the end of the silver quarters and half dollars, the plague of AIDS, the "baby boomers," and the Federation of Catholic European economists. ]{.class_4938}[I didn't look up from the field long enough to see if it was going to rain or not]{.class_4937}[ (Ecc. 11:1--8). What the news media calls a "crisis" is just a news media orgy invented, amplified, exaggerated, and then developed to take up all of your time and attention, so you will neglect the ]{.class_4938}[real ]{.class_4937}[crisis: the crisis with which every individual on earth is faced: dying alone, in the world ]{.class_4938}["having no hope, and without God" ]{.class_4939}[(Eph. 2:12). "Fixing up the world" has always been a useless and stupid waste of time. You have to get the prodigal OUT of the hog pen; redecorating it won't help him or anyone else.]{.class_4938}

[The ]{.class_4938}[reaping ]{.class_4937}[of verse 5 goes beyond the rejoicing you experience on leading a soul to Christ, for a ]{.class_4938}["crown of rejoicing" ]{.class_4939}[shows up at the Judgment Seat of Christ (1 Thess. 2:19) when you "reap" eternal life (see Gal. 6:8).]{.class_4938}

Verse 3 can certainly be applied to a New Testament Christian in the sense that:

1\. He has spared our lives.

2\. He has given us food and raiment.

3\. He has showed us our true spiritual condition.

4\. He has revealed the solution for this condition.

5\. He has spared our lives long enough for us to choose this solution.

6\. He has prepared a mansion for us and predestinated for us a perfect, sinless body.

[If those are not ]{.class_4938}["great things" ]{.class_4939}[that the Lord has done for us, I don't know what are.]{.class_4938}

[A famous soulwinner said, "You can't win them with your big mouth if you don't use your big feet" (]{.class_4938}["goeth forth"]{.class_4939}[). You are to go forth with your ]{.class_4938}[testimony ]{.class_4937}[as to your own salvation, your ]{.class_4938}[tracts ]{.class_4937}[(with the plan of salvation on them), your ]{.class_4938}[tears ]{.class_4937}[(vs. 5), and your ]{.class_4938}[Testament ]{.class_4937}[(see Ps. 149:6).]{.class_4938}

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ3800.xhtml}

PSALM 127

127:1 "Except the LORD build the house, they labour in vain that build it: except the LORD keep the city, the watchman waketh but in vain.

2 It is vain for you to rise up early, to sit up late, to eat the bread of sorrows: for so he giveth his beloved sleep.

3 Lo, children are an heritage of the LORD: and the fruit of the womb is his reward.

4 As arrows are in the hand of a mighty man; so are children of the youth.

5 Happy is the man that hath his quiver full of them: they shall not be ashamed, but they shall speak with the enemies in the gate."

The prophetic theme slackens, and we have some good advice which states general truths. The commentators will not have much trouble. Skim milk doesn't give them gas on the stomach. It is the barbecued pork (Heb. 5:12--14) that upsets their little tummies.

Verse 1 is a great preaching verse. The Germans say, "An Gottes segen is alles gelegen," which means "Everything depends on God's blessing." Two things are involved: a house (which can be a household, as in Heb. 11:7) and a city (Dan. 5:30--31, for example). The Bible-believing position is that God Himself must take care of households and cities, as well as individuals and nations. When the preacher says, "What God hath joined together, let no man put asunder," it means very little if God has NOT joined them together. This, among other factors (TV, the Civil Rights Act, the public schools, the NEA, etc.), explains one divorce out of two marriages in America. Young couples, working to set up households these days, are faced with a dozen handicaps that were unknown to families before the time of Franklin Delano Roosevelt. Their need for God and God's blessing is ten times as great today as it was in 1933.

[The price of groceries went up 400 percent while their "dependent exemptions" only went up 100 percent. Their taxes went up 400 percent while the minimum wage only went up 180 percent. Pornographic pictures were made available to their children in their own living rooms; cocaine and heroin were made available to their children before they were fourteen years old; divorces are so easy now they can be purchased for \$200 and executed in less than a week without one appearance in court; convicted murderers and rapists are roaming the streets after serving less than a quarter of their sentences in jail; the government can force you to hire people who will destroy your business; your children are not allowed to pray in the schools or even carry a Bible to class; and if that were not enough, the "do gooders" on CBS, ABC, and NBC want you ]{.class_4938}[disarmed ]{.class_4937}[so that when the Federal judges order your property confiscated and your family imprisoned you will not be able to defend them or yourself. How will any young couple establish a household with ]{.class_4938}[that kind of thing ]{.class_4937}[going on? They won't, apart from a miracle of God Almighty.]{.class_4938}

[No set of radar systems or SAC forces can keep any city in America from being bombed to a pile of cinders. God protects a nation, or He doesn't protect a nation. ]{.class_4938}["At what instant I shall speak concerning a nation, and concerning a kingdom, to pluck up, and to pull down, and to destroy it. If that nation\...turn from their evil, I will repent of the evil that I have thought to do unto them\...At what instant I shall speak concerning a nation\...if it do evil in my sight\...I will repent of the good, wherewith I said I would benefit them" ]{.class_4939}[(Jer. 18:7--10). Can you get it any clearer? Babylon fell in less than four hours (Dan. 5:30--31), and it had watchmen all over its walls, which were the broadest and highest of any city in the ancient world. Jerusalem collapsed with its watchmen starving to death along with the populace (A.D. 70). No "wide awake watchman" could have saved Hiroshima or Nagasaki.]{.class_4938}

"[It is vain for you to rise up early, to sit up late, to eat the bread of sorrows" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 2). It is a waste of time to get up earlier than you should and work yourself to death (Ecc. 1:3, 14; 2:17). It is a waste of time to sit up all night worrying about the future (Ecc. 5:12), and sorrowing as those who ]{.class_4938}["have no hope" ]{.class_4939}[(1 Thess. 4:13). It is also a waste of time because you cannot bring back the dead or remedy some of the evils that befall you. Fix what you can and let the rest go. The Southerners have a much better grasp of this than Yankees. In Dixie, we say, "Never worry about two things: what you can fix and what you can't fix."]{.class_4938}

"[He giveth his beloved sleep" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 2), as in Psalm 4:8 and Proverbs 3:24. The ]{.class_4938}["sleep of a labouring man is sweet" ]{.class_4939}[(Ecc. 5:12), but ]{.class_4938}["the abundance of the rich" ]{.class_4939}[(same verse) makes him ]{.class_4938}["sit up late" ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["rise up early." ]{.class_4939}[It ]{.class_4938}["will not suffer him to sleep" ]{.class_4939}[(same verse).]{.class_4938}

"[Children are an heritage of the LORD\...the fruit of the womb\...so are children of the youth\...quiver full of them\..." ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 3--5). In agreement with Genesis 9:1 and Exodus 1:7, the Bible is for large families. In the Bible, children are from God, at least children born of lawful marriage (Isa. 57:3). Products of rape, incest, fornication, and adultery are "something else" in the Bible, but modern America (Sodom and Gomorrah) simply calls all sex acts, "having sex" or (in the language of SIECUS) "human sexuality." Legitimate children are from God, if you believe the Book. Shadduck has a masterpiece on this subject called ]{.class_4938}[Stopping the Stork. ]{.class_4937}[Rachel and Hannah know where children come from (Gen. 30:1; 1 Sam. 1:10--11).]{.class_4938}

[In this passage, they are likened to ARROWS which a man can use to defend himself against his enemies (vs. 5). This is because "blood runs thicker than water." Normally, a good man's sons are not ashamed of him and will stand up for him in lawsuits or other types of controversies (vs. 5). The next Psalm, though slightly prophetic, has some more to say about these family matters. The leaders of Israel made sure that they had plenty of sons to ]{.class_4938}["speak with the enemies in the gate" ]{.class_4939}[(see Gideon, Judg. 8:30; David, 2 Sam. 5:13; Rehoboam, 2 Chron. 11:21). These sons are called ]{.class_4938}["arrows" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 4) because they can be straight, strong, polished, and sharp. They will go where they are sent and will hit the target. They protect their owner and wound his enemies. Arrows are what ]{.class_4938}[you ]{.class_4937}[make them; ]{.class_4938}[there ]{.class_4937}[is a thought!]{.class_4938}

"[So are children of the youth" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 4). This would mean that the best time to start having children is somewhere around twenty or twenty one years old, at least according to what Numbers 1:3, 18 lays down as "draft age." The first child at twenty one would be ideal. Dr. Guthrie (]{.class_4938}[Treasury]{.class_4937}[, p. 94) used to say, "I am rich in nothing but children." He had eleven of them. I had ten; six boys and four girls: David, Michael, Peter, Jeremy, Brian, and Mike; Diana, Priscilla, Laura, and Rachel. Someone said to a preacher "You have just as many children as Jacob." To which he replied "Yes, and I have Jacob's God to provide for them."]{.class_4938}

[May I give a word of testimony? Not one of my children, from birth to maturity (twenty one years old), was ever a day without food, shelter, and clothing; and all ten children, between them, did not spend three months in a hospital in twenty-one years. May I give a word of testimony? Seven of my children were raised in the public school system, and all of them came out believing the ]{.class_4938}[King James Bible, ]{.class_4937}[and none of them got hooked on liquor or drugs. All of them got saved, and seven of them attend church regularly.]{.class_4938}

Yes, they disappoint you. Yes, at times they break your heart. Yes, they are a responsibility, and many times a liability. Yes, they cause you some tears and sleepless nights. Yes, you worry about them, and yes, they do reject your good advice, and you have to watch them suffer. In short, they affect you just like you affect your Father---GOD.

Remarkable, isn't it?

[Personally, I think life---at least from the natural, humanistic standpoint---is eighty five percent "kids and dogs." I really believe that; it is "an historic fundamental of the faith" with me. I admire the old farm women in America who think a ]{.class_4938}[small ]{.class_4937}[family is four children. One of the reasons for having children when you are under thirty is because when you get to be sixty or seventy, they will be thirty five to forty five years old and will be able to take care of you; that is, if they are good kids.]{.class_4938}

America today is no barometer for these matters. The last two generations (beginning with six year olds in 1964) have been raised in an electronic jungle where it is "dog eat dog," while talking about "co existing" and "affirmative action." They care for no one but themselves, in the main.

In closing accounts, notice in verse 1 that men build in vain because:

[1. ]{.class_4938}["He that built all things"]{.class_4939}[ was God (Heb. 3:4).]{.class_4938}

2\. No man can lay any other foundation than the One God laid (1 Cor. 3:10).

[3. Without Him you ]{.class_4938}["can do nothing"]{.class_4939}[ (John 15:5).]{.class_4938}

You can't stop AIDS, or the Black Death, from killing everyone in your household or your city. You cannot stop a volcano, or a flood, from killing everyone in your household or your city. You may have an enemy inside your household, or your city, that will betray you and sell you out no matter how watchful you are or how many security guards act as watchmen. Verse 1 means exactly what it says, exactly the way it says it.

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ3830.xhtml}

PSALM 128

128:1 "Blessed is every one that feareth the LORD; that walketh in his ways.

2 For thou shalt eat the labour of thine hands: happy shalt thou be, and it shall be well with thee.

3 Thy wife shall be as a fruitful vine by the sides of thine house: thy children like olive plants round about thy table.

4 Behold, that thus shall the man be blessed that feareth the LORD.

5 The LORD shall bless thee out of Zion: and thou shalt see the good of Jerusalem all the days of thy life.

6 Yea, thou shalt see thy children's children, and peace upon Israel."

[The Psalm is devotional and practical in the main, but the Holy Spirit again warns the milksops (Kroll, Yates, Motyer, Dummelow, Clarke, Lange, Hengstenberg, et al.) that the]{.class_4938}[ "blessed" ]{.class_4939}[people in the text are ]{.class_4938}[saved Jews living in Jerusalem during the Millennium ]{.class_4937}[(note vss. 5--6). The ]{.class_4938}["children's children" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 6 are the matchmeet of Job's ]{.class_4938}["sons, and his son's sons"]{.class_4939}[ (Job 42:16--17), and God placed the expression ]{.class_4938}["turned the captivity" ]{.class_4939}[into Job 42:10 so the milksops could locate Psalm 126:4 and 128:6. Job is the greatest type of the Tribulation saint found anywhere in either Testament (see ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Job]{.class_4937}[).]{.class_4938}

The fear of the Lord shows up again (vs. 1) and is promptly perverted by Robert Nisbet to mean love instead of fear. Nisbet carries on as follows: "It is the fear which a child feels towards an honored parent, a fear to offend\...a fear to act unworthily of his kindness\...a pardoned and grateful rebel in the presence of a venerated sovereign\...a fear lest he should forget his goodness\...gratitude for mercies, dread of displeasure\...desire for approval\...the fear, not of sorrow, but of love which shrinks with instinctive recoil from doing aught that would tend to grieve, or from denying aught that would tend to honour\...."

Just as "pretty as a picture," ain't it?

[No ]{.class_4938}[trembling ]{.class_4937}[(Phil. 2:12), no ]{.class_4938}[repentence ]{.class_4937}[(Jonah 3:5), no ]{.class_4938}[quaking ]{.class_4937}[(Heb. 12:21), no ]{.class_4938}[terror ]{.class_4937}[(2 Cor. 5:11), and no ]{.class_4938}[sweat. ]{.class_4937}[Just nice, wonderfully motivated, clean, sweet, pure adoration (Nisbet, ]{.class_4938}[The Songs of the Temple Pilgrims, ]{.class_4937}[1863, cited by Spurgeon, p. 101).]{.class_4938}

[Observe that the context is ]{.class_4938}["peace upon Israel" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 6), and the man blessed is blessed according to WORKS (vs. 1): ]{.class_4938}["that walketh in his ways." ]{.class_4939}[The words "faith" and "grace" occur nowhere in the Psalm. In fact, the word "faith" doesn't occur ]{.class_4938}[one time ]{.class_4937}[in 150 Psalms, and the word "grace" only occurs twice. One of these times it is a direct reference to Jesus Christ (Ps. 45:2) and has no reference to any sinner getting saved; in the other one (Ps. 84:11), grace is only promised to a sinner who does enough works to earn it. He has to ]{.class_4938}["walk uprightly," ]{.class_4939}[exactly as the man here in verse 1. The Baptist milksops simply cannot handle this kind of passage because it "bumps noses" with their "historic positions."]{.class_4938}

"[Thy wife\...thy children\...thy life" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 3, 5). The word "husband" comes from a ]{.class_4938}["husbandman" ]{.class_4939}[for a vine (John 15:1 and 2 Tim. 2:6), which means that your wife is part of your body; she is fragile (Ezek. 15:2--5) and needs to be cleansed (Eph. 5:26; Lev. 12), and she is by your side (Gen. 2:21--22), not over you as a roof or under you as a floor.]{.class_4938}

[The ]{.class_4938}["olive plants" ]{.class_4939}[are the children sitting at the table:]{.class_4938}

1\. They spring up out of the roots of olive trees, even if the roots are gnarled with age.

[2. They are new life in the family. Olive oil is the symbol of the Holy Spirit, and the ]{.class_4938}["tree of life" ]{.class_4939}[(Gen. 3:22) was an olive tree (see ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis, ]{.class_4937}[Gen. 2:9).]{.class_4938}

[3. Every plant has the potential of becoming a full grown tree (Gen. 1:11--12). These blessings are promised in the Millennium not only to the native Jew in Jerusalem, but to ]{.class_4938}["every one that feareth the Lord" ]{.class_4939}[(see Ps. 118:4).]{.class_4938}

[If you tend to flinch at our constant references to the future instead of the present "church age," consider for a moment what Kroll, James Combs, John R. Rice, Curtis Hutson, B. H. Caroll, Charles Haddon Spurgeon, James Price, Motyer, Yates, and Hengstenberg were unwilling to consider. How would you apply verses 2 and 5 to Paul and Stephen? ]{.class_4938}[You can't. ]{.class_4937}[You cannot apply them to Ignatius or Polycarp, and you cannot apply them to William Tyndale or two million Christians who were tortured to death or burned at the stake (A.D. 400--1800). Paul feared God, and he had no wife and no children, and he did not see ]{.class_4938}["the good of Jerusalem" ]{.class_4939}[one day in his life.]{.class_4937}

[If you will not STUDY so that you can "rightly divide ]{.class_4938}['the word of truth,']{.class_4939}[" all you can do is give the student a botched-up, stinking exegesis that is as untrue as ]{.class_4938}[Thus Spake Zarathustra ]{.class_4937}[(Neitzsche). Watch Spurgeon, for example, simply talking through his stack: "It is not to be inferred that all blessed men are married and are fathers, but that this is the way in which the Lord favors godly people who are placed in domestic life. He makes their relationships happy and profitable."]{.class_4938}

[So Harlan Popov is ]{.class_4938}[taken from his wife ]{.class_4937}[for thirteen years; Balthasar Hubmaier's wife is ]{.class_4938}[drowned ]{.class_4937}[after watching him being burnt at the stake; Job loses ]{.class_4938}[all ten children in one day; ]{.class_4937}[a mother of four (in the Johnstown flood) lost all four children in four hours; Adoniram Judson ]{.class_4938}[buries ]{.class_4937}[his wife in Burma; Talmage ]{.class_4938}[buries ]{.class_4937}[his wife in New York; whole families of missionaries in Columbia (1950--1960) are butchered; in Russia (1944--1945) 10,900 Christians lose their homes and 2,000 of them are put to death (1944--1945).]{.class_4938}

[In 1945, two native Mexican pastors, who "feared God and walked in His ways," were each cut into four pieces by bolo-wielding Catholics. Luis Potosi, in Mexico, was found dead with eighty five knife wounds in him. He was a Bible-believing pastor who "feared God and walked in His ways." Twenty missionaries, each with his ]{.class_4938}["olive plants" ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["fruitful vine," ]{.class_4939}[were slaughtered in Rhodesia in 1978, thanks, to financial assistance that the NCCC in America gave to Communist guerillas. Peter Wyss and David Schweitzer were murdered in Thailand; Dick and Sandy Watson were buried alive by the Viet Cong; and Walter Erikson and Edward Tritt were murdered in New Guinea.]{.class_4938}

"[Behold, that thus shall the man be blessed that feareth the LORD" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 4)!]{.class_4938}

Spurgeon doesn't know what he is talking about at this verse, any more than Woodrow Kroll.

[If you don't know what you are talking about, keep your big trap closed. Enough damage is done by Catholic priests and liberal Modernists without some stupid Fundamentalist (or Baptist) trying to "sermonize" on something to impress people with his spirituality. Imagine some lamebrain, who has been correcting the ]{.class_4938}[King James Bible ]{.class_4937}[for twenty years trying to "minister" to someone in this age who "feared God and walked in His ways," and lost his family in a car wreck or a plane crash. If you haven't got the Millennial dispensation separated from the Church Age and the Tribulation, don't try to teach the Bible where it deals with anything more than the plan of salvation ]{.class_4938}[in this age ]{.class_4937}[(and you'd better stay out of Matthew, Acts, and Hebrews, if you are going to do ]{.class_4938}[that]{.class_4937}[).]{.class_4938}

[Spiritually]{.class_4937}[, you can gather that:]{.class_4938}

1\. If you fear God you will walk in His ways.

2\. To walk in His ways, you certainly are going to have to know Him.

3\. To walk in His ways, you certainly will have to know how He walked (1 John 2:6).

PSALM 129

129:1 "Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth, may Israel now say:

2 Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth: yet they have not prevailed against me.

3 The plowers plowed upon my back: they made long their furrows.

4 The LORD is righteous: he hath cut asunder the cords of the wicked.

5 Let them all be confounded and turned back that hate Zion.

6 Let them be as the grass upon the housetops, which withereth afore it groweth up:

7 Wherewith the mower filleth not his hand; nor he that bindeth sheaves his bosom.

8 Neither do they which go by say, The blessing of the LORD be upon you: we bless you in the name of the LORD."

With the scourging of His Saviour right in front of his face, Woodrow Kroll (the "Champion" at Liberty) cannot find one verse to help him out. With Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, as far back as 1900, to show him the way, he still goes astray as a lost sheep. Again, the next Psalm was inserted for his benefit so Kroll could find where he was. The Psalm began with a reference to Christ's death (see Ps. 22 and comments).

[Kroll's problems are always man made breakdowns in mental processes. Once he saw ]{.class_4938}["Israel" ]{.class_4939}[in verse 1, he threw in the towel and closed whatever he had on the table. (I was going to say "Bible," but Kroll, by his own profession of faith, has never read the Bible. Neither has Curtis Hutson, according to his published statement of faith. See ]{.class_4938}[The Last Grenade]{.class_4937}[.) What is wrong with these apostate Baptists? Don't they know that Israel and Jesus Christ are both the "suffering SERVANTS of Jehovah"? After recommending the ]{.class_4938}[NKJV, ]{.class_4937}[doesn't anyone at Lynchburg know that they (Hutson, Dollar, Towns, Henderson, Ockenga, Farstad, Price, et al.) converted God's ]{.class_4938}["child" ]{.class_4939}[to His "servant" in Acts 4:27, on the basis of a theory that the reference was to the "servant" in Isaiah? Did they look at Isaiah?]{.class_4938}

[Look at Isaiah 37:35; ]{.class_4938}[David ]{.class_4937}[is the servant. Isaiah 41:8; ]{.class_4938}[Israel ]{.class_4937}[is the servant. Isaiah 42:1, 19; ]{.class_4938}[Jesus Christ]{.class_4937}[ is the servant. Isaiah 43:10;]{.class_4938}[ Jesus Christ]{.class_4937}[ is]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4937}[the servant. Isaiah 44:1; ]{.class_4938}[Jacob ]{.class_4937}[is the servant. Isaiah 44:21; ]{.class_4938}[Israel]{.class_4937}[ is the servant. Isaiah 48:20; ]{.class_4938}[Jacob ]{.class_4937}[is the servant. Isaiah 49:5; ]{.class_4938}[Jesus Christ]{.class_4937}[ is the servant.]{.class_4938}

[What is rotten at Lynchburg, Virginia? I'll tell you what is rotten. The same thing that has been rotten at Bob Jones University since 1948 and Tennessee Temple since 1950 and BBC since 1960, and any other outlet where the Alexandrian Cult controls the classrooms and the minds of the students: ]{.class_4938}[unbelief and rejection of the words of God in the Holy Bible. ]{.class_4937}[Israel is a type of Jesus Christ and is said to be the first time Israel appears as a unit (Exod. 4:22). This is why every orthodox Jew in 2000 would tell you that the ]{.class_4938}["servant"]{.class_4939}[ of Isaiah 53 (whose back was plowed) is Israel, ]{.class_4938}[not Jesus Christ ]{.class_4937}[(Isa. 52:13, 53:11). Kroll couldn't find one clue to the application of verse 3 in the Psalm. Verse 3 is Messianic beyond any shadow of a doubt.]{.class_4938}

"[May Israel now say" ]{.class_4939}[is a vivid road sign again. Verses 1--4 is the Millennium as far as Israel is concerned; their sufferings are in the ]{.class_4938}[past. ]{.class_4937}[The cords that were ]{.class_4938}["cut asunder" ]{.class_4939}[were the cords that bound Israel. The Son of Perdition and his United Nations had thought to cast off the cords that bound humanity to God and Christ (]{.class_4938}["their cords," ]{.class_4939}[Ps. 2), but God cast off some cords Himself.]{.class_4938}

[Immediately, the Psalmist steps out of order and goes back into Daniel's Seventieth Week at verses 5 and 6 (see the same thing in Ps. 126:4). This is what blows the minds of the commentators. (We will not waste your time recording their bum guesses.) The prayer of verses 5 and 6 is as imprecatory as the one found in Psalm 109, which see. They will be confounded, and they will be turned back. Their cords will be ]{.class_4938}["cut asunder." ]{.class_4939}[These cords were probably rope cords; like cords used for whipping people (vs. 3). ]{.class_4938}["The grass upon the housetops" ]{.class_4939}[can't really grow up; it has no depth for roots (see Matt. 13:6). No]{.class_4938}[ "mower"]{.class_4939}[ is going to fill his hands with housetop grass because there won't be enough to make one sheaf. Furthermore, ]{.class_4938}["The Lord be with you" ]{.class_4939}[(Ruth 2:4) will not accompany the harvest because there won't be enough to harvest. (See vs. 8: ]{.class_4938}["Neither do they which go by say\...") ]{.class_4939}[There is no blessing on withered grass. It is burned in the oven (Matt. 6:30). Those that hate Zion (vs. 5) go to hell (Matt. 13:42).]{.class_4938}

PSALM 130

130:1 "Out of the depths have I cried unto thee, O LORD.

2 Lord, hear my voice: let thine ears be attentive to the voice of my supplications.

3 If thou, LORD, shouldest mark iniquities, O Lord, who shall stand?

4 But there is forgiveness with thee, that thou mayest be feared.

5 I wait for the LORD, my soul doth wait, and in his word do I hope.

6 My soul waiteth for the Lord more than they that watch for the morning: I say, more than they that watch for the morning.

7 Let Israel hope in the LORD: for with the LORD there is mercy, and with him is plenteous redemption.

8 And he shall redeem Israel from all his iniquities."

Verse 1 is Messianic, as in Psalms 71:20, 42:7, 69:14, 17. Any Biblical writer has a clearer grasp, by divine revelation, of the composition of the universe and the underworld, than any twentieth-century astronomer or physicist. "Modern man" just likes to pretend that these matters are poetic and figurative because he doesn't believe them and can't imagine anyone more than three years ago, knowing more about it than he does. Typical: absolutely 1 Corinthians 3:18--20 to a "T."

[Verses 2--4 can be applied almost anywhere. We have already found verse 2 a number of times in the first collection of the Psalms. Verse 3 implies that nobody would stand. Proverbs 20:9 and Romans 2:6 have this in another form. This is why the wicked cannot ]{.class_4938}["stand in the judgment" ]{.class_4939}[of Psalm 1:5, which see. If our ]{.class_4938}["secret sins" ]{.class_4939}[are ]{.class_4938}["in the light of thy countenance" ]{.class_4939}[(Ps. 90:8), and He marked them (look at Jer. 17:1 and Job 10:14, 13:26), nobody is going to stand. Our standing has to be in Jesus Christ (1 John 3:6), or we will fall. A God who says you need forgiveness must know some pretty bad things about you (vs. 4). Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown are careful to tell you that the ]{.class_4938}["feared"]{.class_4939}[ of verse 4 has nothing to do with ]{.class_4938}["trembling"]{.class_4939}[ (inspite of the Holy Spirit making a liar out of him in Phil. 2:12). It is rather the fear of a "loving son." "Tough apples" for whoever wrote the Psalm: ]{.class_4938}[none of the saints in the Old Testament were "sons of God."]{.class_4937}[ Blew it again, didn't they?]{.class_4938}

[Observe, in verse 5, that the Psalmist is not just waiting on ]{.class_4938}[God; ]{.class_4937}[he is hoping in ]{.class_4938}["his word." ]{.class_4939}[The two are closely connected, and in view of the Messianic reference in verse 1 and the future reference to the Messiah in verse 8 (which fulfills Rom. 11:26), ]{.class_4938}["his word" ]{.class_4939}[has some marvelous applications. At the permanent restoration of Israel ]{.class_4938}[spiritually ]{.class_4937}[(Acts 15:16; Rom. 11:26; Jer. 33:8) as well as ]{.class_4938}[politically ]{.class_4937}[(Isa. 2, 11, 65, 66), Kroll's solenoid gives way, his clutch plate burns out, his brake drums crack, and the drive shaft drops out of the bottom of the wreck. He can't find one reference to Psalm 130:8, anywhere in either Testament. Does he have Ezekiel 36:24--31 in his "Bible"---whatever THAT is. The one who waits, in verses 5--6, is waiting in the Tribulation for the real Messiah to appear, and he knows what ]{.class_4938}["the morning" ]{.class_4939}[is, even if every apostate Baptist at BBC and Tennessee Temple doesn't. It is Malachi 4:1--5; Matthew 13; Hosea 3:5; and Exod. 19:11 as surely as you're reading print. It is not just that he longs to "have fellowship with God more than he wants to see daylight." He wants to see the Lord more than those Israelites who are looking for the restoration of the Kingdom to Israel, which will take place in ]{.class_4938}["the morning" ]{.class_4939}[(look at Ps. 30:5). The attitude of the "waiter," here, is the proper attitude for the child of God in this age, who is awaiting the Rapture. The real Bible believer is longing to see HIM, not just ]{.class_4938}["one of the days of the Son of man" ]{.class_4939}[(Luke 17:22). We are not looking for the Antichrist or for Russia to attack Israel or for the plagues of the first six trumpets like Kirban, Lindsay, Pike, Rosenthal, John Bray, and others do. These backsliders have given up hope in the Rapture, and are trying to get you ready to go into ]{.class_4938}["the time of Jacob's trouble"]{.class_4939}[ (Jer. 30:7), even though you have nothing to do with Jacob. They took their eyes off of "Him" (see Titus 2:13 and 1 John 3:1--3). We ]{.class_4938}["look for\...the Lord Jesus Christ" ]{.class_4939}[(Phil. 3:20)---not the Son of Perdition.]{.class_4938}

Because of the Messianic content, saved Gentiles can be involved in the speeches of verses 5--7, for there Israel is addressed in the third person, as though the speaker were giving Israelites instructions.

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ3901.xhtml}

PSALM 131

131:1 "LORD, my heart is not haughty, nor mine eyes lofty: neither do I exercise myself in great matters, or in things too high for me.

2 Surely I have behaved and quieted myself, as a child that is weaned of his mother: my soul is even as a weaned child.

3 Let Israel hope in the LORD from henceforth and for ever.

[Like Psalm 127, this Psalm is devotional with just the slightest hint of the Millennium in verse 3, where the familiar ]{.class_4938}["from henceforth and for ever" ]{.class_4939}[shows up again. Verse 1 is simple but profound, and it stands as a rebuke to almost every Greek and Hebrew scholar in the world, every intellectual and pseudo intellectual in the world, the National Association for the Advancement of Science, and most politicians, doctors, and lawyers. In the New Testament it is to be read with Romans 12:16; 1 Corinthians 1:25, 27, 31; 2:1, 3--4, 13--14; 3:18--20. There is more of it in Luke 10:21. ]{.class_4938}["Great swelling words" ]{.class_4939}[(2 Pet. 2:18; Jude 16) are the earmarks of this class of sinners. The ]{.class_4938}["simple" ]{.class_4939}[of Romans 16:18 are often seduced by these bloated windbags, who often pass for wise and pious people by having access to tradesmen's terms and an impressive vocabulary. To exercise yourself in great matters, theologically speaking, is to exercise yourself with Bullinger, Stam, O'Hare, Watkins, Greaterex, Moore, et al., on "When did the Body of Christ start?" or "Is water baptism for this age?" or "What is the difference in being justified ]{.class_4938}[through ]{.class_4937}[faith and ]{.class_4938}[by ]{.class_4937}[faith?" or "What is the difference between being baptized ]{.class_4938}[by ]{.class_4937}[the Spirit and ]{.class_4938}[with ]{.class_4937}[the Spirit?"]{.class_4938}

[Other excursionists and tourists (Kuyper, Dabney, Gill, Hodge, Calvin, Berkhof, Pink, Shelton, Ross, Gilpin, et al.) like "sovereign grace," "eternal election," "supralapsarianism," "predestinated to slip on the ice, February 5th, 1948," etc. The real bull shooters were the dead orthodox apostates who met at Nicea in A.D. 325 and took up hours discussing "Heterusios and homoiousios versus anomoios and katoousian," "Could a person be eternally begotten without a birth taking place?" "Do you endorse ousia, or phusis with unum in numero, or ens unum in multis? " and "Are hypostatses and triousian like proprietas personalis, or otherwise?" T. Robertson, Immanuel Kant, or Plato. F. Bruce, from the works of Hermann Cremer and Julius Kogel (1883). Ready?

"[In the New Testament ]{.class_4938}[haima ]{.class_4937}[(Greek) achieves its greatest theological significance in relation to the death of Christ: haima tou Christou (1 Cor. 10:16; Eph. 2:13; Heb. 9:14), ]{.class_4938}[Iesou ]{.class_4937}[(Heb. 10:19; 1 John 1:7), ]{.class_4938}[Iesou ]{.class_4937}[Christou (1 Pet. 1:2, etc.). The interest of the New Testament is not in the material blood of Christ but in His shed blood as the life is violently taken from Him\...the blood of Christ is simply another and even more graphic phrase for the ]{.class_4938}[death ]{.class_4937}[of Christ\...these varied expressions include simple references to the fact of Christ's ]{.class_4938}[death, images ]{.class_4937}[taken from the sphere of law, and ]{.class_4938}[concepts ]{.class_4937}[which belong to the ]{.class_4938}[language ]{.class_4937}[of sacrifice\...the early Christian representation of the blood of Christ as sacrificial blood is simply the ]{.class_4938}[metaphorical garment ]{.class_4937}[clothing the ]{.class_4938}[thought ]{.class_4937}[of the self offering\...since the latter is simply a pregnant verbal ]{.class_4938}[symbol ]{.class_4937}[\[Hello 1 Pet. 3:21\] for the saving work of Christ" (Vol. I, pp. 174--175).]{.class_4938}

Do you see who got John MacArthur (1987) and Dr. Thieme (1980) as screwed up as a corkscrew?

[Who else but a bunch of backslidden, stupid, apostate "Conservatives" (Mac and Thieme both swear by "verbal, plenary inspired, infallible, original autographs"), whose haughty hearts (vs. 1) got them involved in things too high for them (vs. 1), and by exercising themselves in ]{.class_4938}["great matters" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 1), they denied the substance of blood that had eternal life in it because it was literally God's blood (see Acts 20:28) and ]{.class_4938}["the life of the flesh is in the blood" ]{.class_4939}[(Lev. 17:11).]{.class_4938}

Seven thousand pages in ten volumes of Greek studies to prove a LIE.

More than one hundred scholars consulted, or referenced, in a work that is as rotten as anything the Watchtower Society put out.

[A spiritual man doesn't place his eyes, his heart, or his mind on high things. But that is where it will be found.

"[A child that is weaned of his mother," ]{.class_4939}[in the New Testament sense would be a "babe in Christ" (1 Pet. 2:2) who needs milk (Heb. 5:12), and its mother is ]{.class_4938}["new Jerusalem" ]{.class_4939}[(Gal. 4:26), not "the church," "the faith," or any other Roman Catholic horselaugh. (If the reader would really like to see what has happened since 4000 B.C. among the leading intellectuals of Asia, Africa, and Europe, let him purchase ]{.class_4938}[The Christian's Handbook of Science and Philosophy. ]{.class_4937}[That will demonstrate, beyond telling, what is meant by Psalm 131.)]{.class_4938}

"[Let Israel hope in the LORD from henceforth and for ever" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 3). The theme is familiar to you by now, that great theme of "HOPE." Our ]{.class_4938}[hope]{.class_4937}[ is Jesus Christ (see Rom. 8:20, 24; 1 John 3:1--3; and Heb. 6:19). This is the ]{.class_4938}["blessed hope"]{.class_4939}[ of Titus 2:13. "My hope is built on nothing less than Jesus' blood \[and I don't mean "death"\] and righteousness. On Christ the solid Rock I stand, all other ground \[art, music, man, humanism, socialism, Catholicism, education, philosophy, sacraments, space exploration, and science\] is SINKING SAND." The quicksands of science, philosophy, religion, and humanism have swallowed the Adamic race in this century at a rate exceeding the casualties of both World Wars. The ]{.class_4938}["foolish man\...built his house upon the sand"]{.class_4939}[ (Matt. 7:26).]{.class_4938}

"[Let Israel hope in the LORD." ]{.class_4939}[This means drop "Judaism" as quickly as you would drop a red hot poker. Israel will in the time of "Jacob's Trouble."]{.class_4938}
