PSALM 132

132:1 "Lord, remember David, and all his afflictions:

2 How he sware unto the LORD, and vowed unto the mighty God of Jacob;

3 Surely I will not come into the tabernacle of my house, nor go up into my bed;

4 I will not give sleep to mine eyes, or slumber to mine eyelids,

5 Until I find out a place for the LORD, an habitation for the mighty God of Jacob.

6 Lo, we heard of it at Ephratah: we found it in the fields of the wood.

7 We will go into his tabernacles: we will worship at his footstool.

8 Arise, O LORD, into thy rest; thou, and the ark of thy strength.

9 Let thy priests be clothed with righteousness; and let thy saints shout for joy.

10 For thy servant David's sake turn not away the face of thine anointed."

[By now, the reader should be able to spot the Millennial references with ease. Everything in verses 11, 13--18 is a Millennial reference, which is to be taken ]{.class_4938}[literally.]{.class_4937}[ Kroll and Bullinger can find them this time, but Kroll carefully avoids making any ]{.class_4938}[direct application of ]{.class_4937}[verses 14--18 to any literal fulfillment in time. He throws in someone being "in Christ," just to screw things up. The ]{.class_4938}["poor" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 15) remain unidentified, and the ]{.class_4938}["priests" ]{.class_4939}[are not priests at all, but anyone who is saved. Spurgeon ]{.class_4938}[spiritualizes ]{.class_4937}[everything and cannot locate ]{.class_4938}["Zion" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 13), where God will ]{.class_4938}["dwell" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 14), nor does he know who the poor are, what the bread is, who the priests are, or much else.]{.class_4938}

Verse 1 is a plea for God to remember someone else so that a prayer will be answered. It is like Moses pleading with God to remember Abraham (Exod. 32:13) when dealing with Israel. David's afflictions are mentioned at this time because the Psalmist is about to ask for the restoration of David's throne and the Temple which his son built. This is apparent by 1 Chronicles 22:14. David's vow (vs. 2) is not recorded in 1 and 2 Samuel, but he certainly made some kind of a vow or resolution before he talked with Nathan (2 Sam. 7). The thing that contributed to David making this vow was his own hand to-mouth existence as a fugitive for somewhere nearly seven years under Saul. Now that David "enters his rest," he is living "high on the hog and low on the chicken," and his thoughts are going back to the time when he was sleeping in caves (1 Sam. 22:1, 24:3) and trying to get a handout (literally, 1 Sam. 25:8). Undoubtedly, from 2 Samuel 5 on, David resolved to do something for the One who had done so much for him.

"[Jacob" ]{.class_4939}[is mentioned twice in the Psalm because of "the law of first mention" which Kroll, Bullinger, Dummelow, Motyer, Yates, Abraham Wright, Herman Venema, Martin Luther, Henry Hurst, and Arthur Jackson miss. You see, the first mention of ]{.class_4938}["God's house"]{.class_4939}[ was in Genesis 28:22, when Jacob was running for his life from Esau. Hence ]{.class_4938}["the mighty God of Jacob."]{.class_4939}

[The Psalmist either exaggerates David's vow, or else David could be speaking figuratively, for he did lay down on his bed many nights before he found a habitation ]{.class_4938}["for the mighty God of Jacob." ]{.class_4939}[It can be taken literally, if one remembers that David set up a tabernacle for the ark before he had a real palace in which to live, for he no sooner whipped the Jebusites (2 Sam. 5:8--12) than he had to take on the Philistines (2 Sam. 5:17--25). After that battle, David goes to fetch the ark and bring it up to a tabernacle (see 2 Sam. 6:17). This is why David calls his own palace ]{.class_4938}["the tabernacle of my house" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 3). He found a place for the ark before he had his own house, although it was not brought to that place till nearly three months later (2 Sam. 6:11).]{.class_4938}

"[Ephratath: we found it in the fields of the wood" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 6). David was raised in Bethlehem, which is in ]{.class_4938}["Ephratah" ]{.class_4939}[(see Gen. 35:19) so his family had heard about the ark many times. However, the ark was never in Bethlehem, for it was taken from Shiloh and carried into battle in 1 Samuel 4:3--8, whence it was captured by the Philistines, and then returned to the house of Abinadab in Gibeah, a hill in Kirjath jearim; 1 Sam. 7:2; 2 Sam. 6:3. The ark stayed there for twenty years before Saul was anointed king (1 Sam. 9). This would mean that when David attempted to bring it up, it had been in Kirjath-jearim for nearly sixty years. However, the writer of 1 Samuel 7:2 is probably writing from the standpoint of someone who has seen the ark returned to Jerusalem under David, in which case the twenty years would ]{.class_4938}[begin ]{.class_4937}[when David was ten years old, for as Joseph, he begins to reign at thirty.]{.class_4938}

[The ark is ]{.class_4938}["found\...fields of the wood." ]{.class_4939}[Henry Law (]{.class_4938}[Treasury]{.class_4937}[, p. 156) says this means someone found the church! But George Rogers (1883) says that you find ]{.class_4938}[Christ ]{.class_4937}[in the ]{.class_4938}["fields of the]{.class_4939}[ ]{.class_4938}[wood"]{.class_4939}[ (Kirjath jearim means "city of the woods"). Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown rightly comment that the ark was virtually lost because it remained hidden and useless for the nation and the Levitical priesthood.]{.class_4938}

"[We will go into his tabernacles" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 7). We will have to part company with the commentators, who now proceed to write a page and a half of small print on the ark coming up and becoming the ]{.class_4938}["footstool" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 7. The verse that flips the commentators out is verse 8, where we read ]{.class_4938}["thou, and the ark of thy strength." ]{.class_4939}[They have been following dead history (vss. 1--6) in order to locate the ]{.class_4938}[past application. ]{.class_4937}[They now are determined to continue in this fashion, but it won't work. Note ]{.class_4938}["Arise, O LORD." ]{.class_4939}[There it goes again. Do you remember those nuts back in Psalms 3:7, 7:6, 9:19, 10:12, 17:13, 44:23, and 68:1 telling you that it was a figurative expression picturing a judge "standing up to give sentence"? (See Kroll on Ps. 82:8.) The ark, this time, is the one in Revelation 11:19, at the Second Advent (see Rev. 11:15--18). The ]{.class_4938}["footstool"]{.class_4939}[ never was the ark in either Testament. The footstool is this earth (Matt. 5:35) or ]{.class_4938}[Jerusalem ]{.class_4937}[in the Millennium (see Ps. 99:5).]{.class_4938}

[The commentators all confounded the presence of God over the mercy seat, on the earthly ark, with the person of God sitting between the cherubims in a literal temple, in the third heaven: Psalms 80:1 and 99:1. The priests of verse 9 are real priests in a real temple (Ezek. 44--46), and the saints are Jerusalem saints at Zion, for in verse 16 they are called ]{.class_4938}["her priests\...and her saints." ]{.class_4939}[Once again the dead orthodox apostates were so carried away with recorded history that they buried the prophetic truth. We have seen this now time and time again, since Psalm 1. The scholars want a ]{.class_4938}[dead book ]{.class_4937}[that can be kept in a cemetery, while ]{.class_4938}[they ]{.class_4937}[hold the keys to the cemetery. All they can see is David bringing up the ark and the dedication of the temple by Solomon.]{.class_4938}

Just as blind as a bat.

[Not even Bullinger can locate anything. When Solomon says ]{.class_4938}["Arise, O LORD God, into thy resting place" ]{.class_4939}[(2 Chron. 6:41), and the Psalmist quotes the rest of the verse in this Psalm (vs. 9), the matter is "clinched" for the commentators. They throw the whole passage backwards three thousand years. Shall we try a little "King James Onlyism" to further aggravate the apostates at Bob Jones University? Let's do just that!]{.class_4938}

[1. Solomon dedicated his temple on the day of the Second Advent (the Feast of Tabernacles). This is why the temple is called ]{.class_4938}["the tabernacle of David" ]{.class_4939}[(Acts 15:16), ]{.class_4938}[not Solomon. ]{.class_4937}[The Feast of Tabernacles is the date of the Advent in Matthew 17:1--6 and Psalm 19:4--5, which see.]{.class_4938}

[2. This ]{.class_4938}["tabernacle" ]{.class_4939}[will be ]{.class_4938}[rebuilt ]{.class_4937}[(Acts 15:16) according to the exact specifications given in Ezekiel 40--44.]{.class_4938}

[3. This rebuilt ]{.class_4938}["tabernacle" ]{.class_4939}[will be in the ]{.class_4938}["city of the great King" ]{.class_4939}[(Matt. 5:33--35) because the Great King will be there (Zech. 14:1--17), and he is called ]{.class_4938}["the son of David."]{.class_4937}

4\. It therefore follows, as the moon follows the earth, that Solomon's reign is the definitive type of the Millennial reign of Christ. If you take types lightly---and at this point one hundred percent of the scholars DID---you had better remember that Moses lost his entrance into the Promised Land because he violated a type (1 Cor. 10:4; Num. 20:10--11).

[5. During Solomon's reign, Israel had the land grant given to Abraham, which they will have in the Millennium. In Solomon's reign, the Gentiles came to Jerusalem for wisdom, as they will in the Millennium. There was peace on earth during Solomon's reign, which there will be in the Millennium. The ]{.class_4938}["son of David" ]{.class_4939}[(Matt. 21:15) was ruling, which he will be in the Millennium. There was a revolt at the end of Solomon's reign, which there will be in the Millennium. ]{.class_4938}["Arise, O LORD," ]{.class_4939}[then, with the accompanying ]{.class_4938}["thou, and the ark of thy strength: let thy priests, O LORD God, be clothed with salvation, and let thy saints rejoice in goodness" ]{.class_4939}[(2 Chron. 6:41) cannot be consigned to the Museum of Natural History for curiosity seekers to take snapshots of it.]{.class_4938}

[Everything in the Psalm, from verse 8 to verse 18, will be fulfilled literally in the Tribulation. ]{.class_4938}["Thine anointed" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 10) are the ]{.class_4938}["anointed" ]{.class_4939}[of Psalm 105:15, over which the pastors made such a fuss. Historically, it would be the anointed kings who followed David, and by such a reckoning it could have an indirect application to our Lord Jesus Himself, who was anointed ]{.class_4938}["with the oil of gladness above thy fellows,"]{.class_4939}[ (Ps. 45:7), for He certainly will come to reign as a king in David's "line."]{.class_4938}

132:11 "The LORD hath sworn in truth unto David; he will not turn from it; Of the fruit of thy body will I set upon thy throne.

12 If thy children will keep my covenant and my testimony that I shall teach them, their children shall also sit upon thy throne for evermore.

13 For the LORD hath chosen Zion; he hath desired it for his habitation.

14 This is my rest for ever: here will I dwell; for I have desired it.

15 I will abundantly bless her provision: I will satisfy her poor with bread.

16 I will also clothe her priests with salvation: and her saints shall shout aloud for joy.

17 There will I make the horn of David to bud: I have ordained a lamp for mine anointed.

18 His enemies will I clothe with shame: but upon himself shall his crown flourish."

[All should be clear to a Premillennialist, for the promise of David's throne to Christ was given to Mary in Luke 1:31--33 before He was born, but He hasn't gotten it yet. If God said ]{.class_4938}["Of the fruit of thy body will I set upon thy throne," ]{.class_4939}[then He lied if He meant it ]{.class_4938}[literally. ]{.class_4937}[The commentators do not wish to accuse God of lying (at least not in so many words), so they ]{.class_4938}[spiritualize ]{.class_4937}[the promise and pretend that David's throne isn't really ]{.class_4938}[David's throne ]{.class_4937}[(although Simon Peter says it is, Acts 2:30). They convert David's throne in Jerusalem, ]{.class_4938}["the city of the great King"]{.class_4939}[ (on God's ]{.class_4938}["footstool,"]{.class_4939}[ the earth), to a]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4939}[heavenly throne, ]{.class_4937}[not on this earth. Spurgeon does this just as neatly as every Campbellite and Catholic on earth (p. 149). He has the glorious, victorious "peace on earth, good will to men" reign of Christ beginning at A.D. 33, and continuing until now. In so doing, he denied one fourth of the Old Testament (Rom. 11:25). In Spurgeon's ]{.class_4938}[Treasury]{.class_4937}[, the last clause in verse 11 (which is quoted in Acts 2:30) is not even exegeted. No mention is made of Jesus Christ on ]{.class_4938}[any ]{.class_4937}[throne, and ]{.class_4938}[David's ]{.class_4937}[throne is connected only with the kings of Judah, who were wiped out more than 590 years before John the Baptist. Someone doesn't want Jesus Christ on this earth, running things (see Matt. 21:38).]{.class_4938}

How do you like the reign? "Neat," uh? Hitler, Napoleon, Charlemagne, Bloody Mary, the Inquisition, the Crusades, Baibars, Genghis Khan, Tamerlane, Hannibal, Charlemagne, World War I, World War II, the French Revolution, the American Revolution, the Black Hole of Calcutta, the War of 1812, the Crimean War, the War of the Roses, the Thirty Years War, the Bataan Death March, the American Civil War, Vietnam, Korea, Afghanistan, Iraq, Iran, the Black Death, AIDS, one divorce out of two marriages, 1,500 prisoners waiting for capital punishment that won't be executed, eight million heroin addicts and ten million cocaine addicts in one country, seventy million chronic alcoholics, and three world wars coming up!

"[Joy to the world, the Lord is come, let earth receive her king! "]{.class_4938}

(You don't have to be a good, "godly," dedicated, recognized, qualified, Conservative scholar to be slap out of your skull, but it evidently helps.)

[The "fruit of David's body," literally and physically, is MARY (Luke 1:27). No male seed can be involved, for the "sons of David," who succeeded him on his throne, stopped at Jeconiah (Jer. 22:29--30). ]{.class_4938}["Thine anointed" ]{.class_4939}[could not have been born by the ]{.class_4938}[male ]{.class_4937}[seed of David; it was the seed of the woman (Gen. 3:15) that did the job. By now, we have given the cross references for the throne of David so many times as to be superfluous, but since all the commentators missed them for three hundred years, it will do us good to review.]{.class_4938}

[1. The throne of David is ]{.class_4938}[on the ground ]{.class_4937}[at Jerusalem (Ezek. 43:7).]{.class_4938}

[2. The throne of David is called ]{.class_4938}["the throne of the LORD" ]{.class_4939}[(1 Chron. 29:23).]{.class_4938}

[3. The throne of David is called ]{.class_4938}["the throne of his glory" ]{.class_4939}[(Matt. 25:31).]{.class_4938}

[4. The throne of His glory is on the ground, ]{.class_4938}[in Palestine ]{.class_4937}[(Matt. 19:28). Jesus Christ has never ascended that throne one time since God made the first whale.]{.class_4938}

[5. The Son of David has this throne ]{.class_4938}[in the Millennium ]{.class_4937}[(Isa. 9:7).]{.class_4938}

[6. David, or one of his descendants (literally), has this throne ]{.class_4938}[in eternity ]{.class_4937}[(Jer. 33:17).]{.class_4938}

That is what is in verses 11--12. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown almost say it, but not quite. They do say, "When the condition shall be fulfilled through the repentance and obedience of Israel, the promise (2 Sam. 7:14, 15) resumes its unabated force\...Jehovah's choice of Zion\...involves as a consequence the restoration of David's race and kingdom through which He shall then impart salvation to His people." Unfortunately, he leaves both statements open so anyone could apply them to the First Advent. He never says "Millennium" one time, he never says "the throne of his glory" one time, and he never explains what Millennial salvation is one time.

[Verse 13. This ]{.class_4938}[is literal. ]{.class_4937}[Jesus Christ will dwell on Mount Zion (Isa. 1:27, 2:3, 4:3--5, 8:18, 12:6, and 14:32). ]{.class_4938}["This is my rest for ever" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 14), so then this is the seventh day, Millennial Sabbath ]{.class_4938}["rest"]{.class_4939}[ of Hebrews 3:18 into which the present generation of Hebrews are to enter ]{.class_4938}["after so long a time" ]{.class_4939}[(Heb. 4:7--9). The Lord is not removed from Zion when Gog and Magog show up (Rev. 20:8). He burns them out. Verse 15 is literal; it follows a nation that has been starved during the time of Jacob's troubles, and this explains the constant repetition of the phrase ]{.class_4938}["poor and needy" ]{.class_4939}[(Ps. 9:18; 10:2, 9; 12:5; 35: 10; 37:14; 40:17; 68:10; 69:29; 70:5; 72:4, 12, etc.). The connection between ]{.class_4938}[poverty ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[belief in the Bible ]{.class_4937}[has been carefully hidden from every Bible correcting Greek and Hebrew scholar since 1611. A third of Matthew 25 deals with it. It is mentioned in both versions of the Sermon on the Mount (Matt. 5:3 and Luke 6:20). It is mentioned in the first and last chapters of James. It is found throughout Proverbs and Job and is defined in Revelation 13:13--18. The economic system of the European Common Market and America will be controlled by one man who will operate it by electronic computers and kill anyone who doesn't take his mark, and if he cannot capture the "uncooperative," anti one worlder, he will install a system to STARVE him to death. This is what is going on in Psalms 37:14; 14:6; 34:6; 41:1; 69:33; 72:13; 74:19, 21; 82:3, 4; 86:1; 107:41; 109:16, 22, 31, and 113:7.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 16 will take place ]{.class_4938}[literally, ]{.class_4937}[and the ]{.class_4938}["lamp" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 17) for David's house is the one that He said would always be burning, in 1 Kings 11:36. PSALM 133

133:1 "Behold, how good and how pleasant it is for brethren to dwell together in unity!

2 It is like the precious ointment upon the head, that ran down upon the beard, even Aaron's beard: that went down to the skirts of his garments;

3 As the dew of Hermon, and as the dew that descended upon the mountains of Zion: for there the LORD commanded the blessing, even life for evermore."

[Although there is nothing particularly dispensational in this Psalm---the next to the last in the "songs of degrees"---it still states a general truth (vs. 1) within a Jewish context. Although we can spiritualize verse 3 by including Mount Calvary as one of ]{.class_4938}["the mountains of Zion," ]{.class_4939}[to get ]{.class_4938}["life for evermore," ]{.class_4939}[Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown see what is involved and wisely say, "The dew is the symbol of ever fresh youth and resurrection---immortality descending from heaven at Christ's Second Coming." (Note that ]{.class_4938}["dew" ]{.class_4939}[back in Psalm 110:3, and the remarks we made in regard to it.) Spurgeon's ridiculous comment ("where love reigns, God reigns") is not to be countenanced by anyone with enough sense to quote John 3:16. "Love is LIFE, let us love forevermore," is just so much hogwash designed to take your mind off the Scriptures. John Calvin says, "God commands His blessing where peace is cultivated," which means about as much as saying, "Smile, God loves you; have a nice day."]{.class_4938}

[The ]{.class_4938}["brethren" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 1) are the brethren of Matthew 25:34--42, which see. They are Christ's brethren according to Matthew 28:10. As far as I know, there hasn't been a local church in America since 1933 that was able to perform these commands. The church that brags the most about unity is the Roman Catholic Church, but its basis of unity is not the Bible. Catholic unity is a political bond held together by a vast bureaucracy of hirelings, flunkies, and officials who are bound to a political organization by oaths, birth, and social ties. The real ]{.class_4938}[unity ]{.class_4937}[of Catholicism lies in its open ]{.class_4938}[rejection ]{.class_4937}[of what the Bible says about authority, heaven, salvation, hell, Mary, the church, and the Bible itself. Their strong body of "unity" is one of convenient agreement to get rid of what God said. For example, the Catholic conscience is offended by street preaching---which is the standard preaching style in both Testaments. It is ]{.class_4938}[not ]{.class_4937}[offended by gambling at Bingo on Friday and Saturday night. The Catholic conscience is upset at any Catholic being immersed in water by a Baptist, after he becomes an adult. It does ]{.class_4938}[not ]{.class_4937}[get upset about signing concordats with Fascist dictators (Mussolini, Franco, Hitler, for example). The Catholic conscience is not disturbed by all of its priests, leaders, bishops, cardinals, and popes making a liar out of the Holy Spirit in Psalm 69:8, but would be terribly upset over a Chick comic book that said Catholic priests call a wafer "my Lord and my God."]{.class_4938}

[There never has been any ]{.class_4938}[Christian ]{.class_4937}[unity in the Roman Catholic Church, because the proof text for that church states that it is founded on Satan (Matt. 16:18, 23). If you think the Council of Papal Infallibility (1870) presented a "united front" as a testimony to John 10:16 (every pope's favorite verse), you didn't read the account. Frank Norris going after Beauchamp Vick (1950--1952). ]{.class_4938}[Unity ]{.class_4937}[as a mark of the "one true, holy, apostolic church" is a horselaugh. It is true that there have been small congregations of real Christians, overseas, who have managed to live in unity, but this is nearly always in a situation where the persecution is so fierce from papists, or Communists, that they have to stick together. The result of this genuine, spiritual love, through a period of years, is an appalling ignorance of Biblical doctrines. When the Berlin Wall finally collapsed, Bible-believing missionaries, going into eastern Europe, found pastors who knew nothing about the restoration of Israel, the Rapture, the Judgment Seat of Christ, the Millennium, eternal security, the New Testament church, dispensational teaching, how to win souls, or the prooftexts that revealed Satan, false religions, and false doctrine. The Bible had disappeared, and only their love for Christ and their love for each other kept them together.

[I have been a Christian for forty four years. In that time I have had three church splits in my own pastorates and seven splits in churches where I was holding evangelistic services. Branson's ]{.class_4938}[Church Splits]{.class_4937}[ (1990) was about as naive a work as I ever read in my life. In forty three years in the ministry I have started three Baptist churches from scratch and in that time have never let one church go into debt. Once I bailed a previous pastor out of \$200,000 debt, without pushing attendance or offerings one time, not even for one Sunday. No dinners, no drives, no stewardship programs, no big building programs, and no emphasis on storehouse tithing. The church I pastor, and the school I teach in have been debt free for more than eight years and were never in debt more than ten years, ]{.class_4938}[with every bill paid when due.]{.class_4937}

Gilboa, Mt. Ephraim, Mt. Ebal, Mt. Gerizim, and Mt. Tabor, you can't get any dew that will travel less than sixty miles to descend on said mountains. [Spiritually ]{.class_4937}[speaking, we may note that in Palestine the "dew" probably affects northern Palestine before southern Palestine; that is, in the early morning hours, the dew shows up in Hermon, Galilee, Merom, Dan, and Lebanon before it shows up in the Plain of Sharon, Ephraim, and Judea. From outer space this might appear as a blanket of dew, beginning with Phoenicia and Damascus, coming down slowly across the twelve tribes of Israel from Mt. Hermon. The order would be down over Naphtali and Asher, through Zebulun, Issachar, Reuben, and the half tribe of Manasseh, down through Gad, Manasseh, and Ephraim and on to Dan, Benjamin, Judah, and Simeon.]{.class_4938}

Dan would be first if one took Dan's permanent position in the north to be the location of that tribe; originally, it was WEST of Judah (see Judges 18--19).

There is probably some sermonic material in this order if it were "dug out." It would be gleaned from the prophecies given on the tribes in Genesis 49 and Deuteronomy 33.

PSALM 134

134:1 "Behold, bless ye the LORD, all ye servants of the LORD, which by night stand in the house of the LORD.

Thy will be done in earth, as it is in heaven" ]{.class_4939}[(Matt. 6:10) has nothing to do with the Church Age or the Old Testament temple sacrifices. Well, it was Romans. 9:25--26 that made them convert Israel into the Church so ]{.class_4938}[they ]{.class_4937}[(in the Church) could ]{.class_4938}[steal ]{.class_4937}[the promises given to Israel. They were given to the physical descendants of Jacob (Rom. 3:1--2); and the same writer then warned the reader that Israel was still ]{.class_4938}["his people"]{.class_4939}[ (Rom. 15:10), in distinction from the Gentile converts (Rom. 11:20). Notwithstanding, Kroll took the Catholic bait and regressed one hundred years to the postmillennialists of the nineteenth century. Romans 11:2 says that Israel are ]{.class_4938}["his people," ]{.class_4939}[right after the spiritual applications in Romans 9:25--26, which were not ]{.class_4938}[doctrinal. ]{.class_4937}[Liberty University and Bob Jones University seem to have a terrible time with ]{.class_4938}["sound doctrine" ]{.class_4939}[(2 Tim. 4:3). As a matter of fact, they have such a horrible time of it that they (along with BBC, Tennessee Temple, and PCC) have decided to attach the label "heretic" to anyone who teaches sound doctrine. Their "fathers" (Matt. 23:34, 37), in Rome, preceded them in these matters by a good bit. Papists have always called Christian "heretics" if they taught anything contrary to their own "historic positions."]{.class_4938}

135:15 "The idols of the heathen are silver and gold, the work of men's hands.

16 They have mouths, but they speak not; eyes have they, but they see not;

17 They have ears, but they hear not; neither is there any breath in their mouths.

18 They that make them are like unto them: so is every one that trusteth in them."

[The reader should recognize the familiar words of Psalm 115:4--8, upon which we have already commented. People never rise above the level of their gods (Andrew Robert Fausset). A man molds himself after his "god." Your supreme affection will determine your character, and that will eventually determine your destiny. Men wind up like their gods. Young men wear long hair because they worship ]{.class_4938}[perversion ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[filth. ]{.class_4937}[It isn't whether or not you let it touch your collar; it is WHY do you want it to touch your collar? Who are you trying to be like? Jesus Christ? Then get rid of your car and walk, eat barley bread, and go to work in a carpenter's shop for eighteen years. Catholics are like their statues: they cannot see the truth, they cannot hear the truth, they cannot speak the truth, and they have no life in them (vs. 17). They are ]{.class_4938}["dead in trespasses and sins" ]{.class_4939}[(Eph. 2:1--4), for they are counting on water sprinkling to regenerate them (give them life, vs. 17), and water couldn't give spiritual life to a billygoat. Popes and cardinals are ]{.class_4938}["the work of men's hands" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 15). God had nothing to do with their creation, their ordination, their calling, or their ministries. Their "holy orders" are just as unholy as the monsters they created (see "Hellish Mother Whore," chapter 14, "The Bride of Satan, " chapter 17, ]{.class_4938}[History of the New Testament Church]{.class_4937}[), for both were ]{.class_4938}["the work of men's hands" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 15).]{.class_4938}

135:19 "Bless the LORD, O house of Israel: bless the LORD, O house of Aaron:

20 Bless the LORD, O house of Levi: ye that fear the LORD, bless the LORD.

21 Blessed be the LORD out of Zion, which dwelleth at Jerusalem. Praise ye the LORD."

[We are back in the Millennium again. Note the expression ]{.class_4938}["Blessed be the LORD out of Zion"]{.class_4939}[;]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4939}[run it back to 128:5; 20:2; 53:6; 99:2, and 110:2. There cannot be the slightest doubt about the doctrinal meaning of the passage. The Scriptures have already declared, Scripture with Scripture, that the references have nothing to do with anyone in the Church Age. Having seen this demonstrated before your eyes, what do you suppose a Fundamentalist (with degrees from three orthodox seminaries) is going to do with the passage? You get one guess:]{.class_4938}

"[With the call for the Israelites to bless Jehovah's name, the psalmist includes a call to the priests of Aaron\...the priests and laity alike are to bless the Lord. As God gives His people \[look out: he just said "God's people" were New Testament Christians in verse 14\] blessings 'out of Zion' \[put in quotations to indicate that it is a spiritual expression, not to be taken literally\] so too He is to be praised out of Zion for it is here that Jehovah dwelleth \[present tense\]."]{.class_4938}

He never dwelt there for fifteen seconds since Matthew 28.

It wasn't even His "hometown" before Matthew 28 (see Matt. 2).

[This is Baptist Fundamentalism in 1991. Kroll simply destroyed the ]{.class_4938}[doctrinal ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[prophetic ]{.class_4937}[content of the ]{.class_4938}[entire Psalm; ]{.class_4937}[he did it by taking the postmillennial stance of the Roman Catholic popes, which they have used for 1,500 years to prove that God is through with Israel. God dwelled at Jerusalem when the Psalmist wrote the Psalm, but He deserted it completely ]{.class_4938}[after ]{.class_4937}[the Psalmist wrote the Psalm (see 2 Kings 23:27; Jer. 7:15; 6:6--9; 7:17--34; Isa. 3:1--8). Verse 14, in this Psalm, located the exact time that God would dwell in Jerusalem. It certainly was not one time since 606 B.C.]{.class_4938}

This is Baptist Fundamentalism in 1991.

Just as blind as Stevie Wonder with a sack over his head.

"[Israel\...Aaron\...Levi\...ye that fear the LORD" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 19--20). See the comments already made under Psalm 115:9--11.]{.class_4938}

PSALM 136

136:1 "O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.

2 O give thanks unto the God of gods: for his mercy endureth for ever.

3 O give thanks to the Lord of lords: for his mercy endureth for ever.

4 To him who alone doeth great wonders: for his mercy endureth for ever.

5 To him that by wisdom made the heavens: for his mercy endureth for ever.

6 To him that stretched out the earth above the waters: for his mercy endureth for ever.

7 To him that made great lights: for his mercy endureth for ever:

8 The sun to rule by day: for his mercy endureth for ever:

9 The moon and stars to rule by night: for his mercy endureth for ever."

[All twenty six verses end with ]{.class_4938}["for his mercy endureth for ever." ]{.class_4939}[The King James' italics (]{.class_4938}["endureth"]{.class_4939}[) are recognized by the Hebrew Masoretic Society as belonging in the Bible, so the Jewish Publications Society includes them as regular print, translating the Masoretic Hebrew. Four times in the Psalm we read ]{.class_4938}["O give thanks" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 1--3, and 26), and this thanks is given to:]{.class_4938}

[1. ]{.class_4938}["the Lord."]{.class_4939}

[2. ]{.class_4938}["the God of gods."]{.class_4939}

[3. ]{.class_4938}["the Lord of lords."]{.class_4939}

[4. ]{.class_4938}["the God of heaven."]{.class_4939}

[These four Scriptural titles sum up the majesty and power of God Almighty. He is THE Lord, THE God, and more than that, He is the God of HEAVEN, in that He created it (see Ps. 148:5), and then, beyond that, the God of all the gods (see Ps. 82, 86:8, and 95:3), plus Lord of all the lords (see 1 Cor. 8:5--6). P.S.---He is ]{.class_4938}["King of kings" ]{.class_4939}[(Rev. 19:16) as well. "All Hail the power of Jesus' name, let angels prostrate fall. Bring forth the royal diadem and crown Him Lord of all."]{.class_4938}

The Trinity is apparent in the first three verses. Andrew Bonar has an excellent devotional outline for the Psalm which runs as follows:

1\. Praise Him for what He is (vss. 1--3).

2\. Praise Him for what He has done (vss. 4--9).

3\. Praise Him for redeeming Israel (vss. 10--15).

4\. Praise Him for His providence (vss. 16--22).

5\. Praise Him for grace in times of calamity (vss. 23--24).

6\. Praise Him for His grace to the world, at large (vs. 25).

7\. Praise Him for being the God of Heaven (vs. 26).

"[To him who alone doeth great wonders" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 4). No credit is given to man for doing anything. No credit is given to the 300 million gods of Hinduism for ]{.class_4938}[one, single, solitary wonder. ]{.class_4937}[No credit is given to one scientist, inventor, explorer, philanthropist, humanist, educator, social worker, doctor, nurse, or national leader for doing anything wonderful. Credit is given ]{.class_4938}["to him who alone doeth great wonders" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 4). Verse 5 is a reference to Genesis 1:8. Verse 6 is a reference to Genesis 1:6. Verses 7--8 are references to Genesis 1:14--16 showing, again, that no SUN was present until the earth had been sailing through space four days. Note further, that in this anti intellectual tirade against college-educated idiots, the stars now present (vs. 9) did not antedate the earth. The earth preceded ]{.class_4938}[them. ]{.class_4937}[Tell that to your nearest monkey-man who runs a fruitstand for Darwin!]{.class_4938}

"[For his mercy endureth for ever." ]{.class_4939}[If He had not been merciful to the stars and planets, there would have been "big bangs" going off at intervals, and the planets and stars would be colliding with each other. If He had not been merciful, the moon would have been placed half its distance to the earth, and high tides would cover Denver, Berlin, Moscow, and Kalispell every twelve hours. If He had not been merciful to the earth, the sun would be scorching it twelve hours a day. If He had not been merciful, you couldn't have lived here, because you would have been under water (vs. 6).]{.class_4938}

136:10 "To him that smote Egypt in their firstborn: for his mercy endureth for ever:

11 And brought out Israel from among them: for his mercy endureth for ever:

12 With a strong hand, and with a stretched out arm: for his mercy endureth for ever.

13 To him which divided the Red sea into parts: for his mercy endureth for ever:

14 And made Israel to pass through the midst of it: for his mercy endureth for ever:

15 But overthrew Pharaoh and his host in the Red sea: for his mercy endureth for ever."

The next six verses link the God of creation (see Ps. 102:18 and comments) with ONE nation that God created (Isa. 42:5; 43:1; 45:12, and 43:21). Israel is a separate creation, exactly as the Body of Christ is a separate creation (Eph. 1--3).

[Verse 10. It was a mercy to Israel that God finally forced Pharaoh to let them go; nothing short of death accomplished this. Note the two edged sword of mercy. What is ]{.class_4938}[mercy ]{.class_4937}[for one man is death and destruction for another man. "One man's pie is another man's poison."]{.class_4938}

"[I say, guide! The hunting today was simply awful! God had no mercy on me at all today. I fired at two tigers and missed both of them." "Yes, Sahib, today God was merciful to the tigers."]{.class_4938}

[If Christians could get a break and were allowed civil rights, as a minority, to carry their Bibles to school and pray in the hallways, it would be an ]{.class_4938}[agony ]{.class_4937}[and an ]{.class_4938}[offense ]{.class_4937}[to every homosexual, thief, dopehead, cusser, and liar in the school. Majority rights. What is a ]{.class_4938}[blessing ]{.class_4937}[to a Bible believer is a ]{.class_4938}[torture ]{.class_4937}[to a moral leper (see remarks on the Indiana State Department of Public Parks; Ps. 37:12). God's mercy to Adolph Hitler (the miscarried "bomb plot") cost the whole nation of Germany its national integrity and unity for forty years and the death of more than 500,000 soldiers who would have been spared under a negotiated peace with the allies. ]{.class_4938}[Mercy ]{.class_4937}[for one man is ]{.class_4938}[hell ]{.class_4937}[for another (see vss. 17--20). It is God's mercy to the sex perverts in San Francisco and Key West that is costing the lives of nearly 100,000 people, yearly, who are not guilty of sex perversion. The more "mercy" the more death, disease, divorce, debt, disability, drugs, desertion, and damnation.]{.class_4938}

[Get it right. If it is just "mercy" that God doles out, the whole human race is damned now and forever. If God let every unsaved sinner into heaven, they would be in hell. What could be worse than having to live forever in the holy presence of a sinless Saviour who loved you enough to die for you, knowing that you rejected Him and hated holiness? If Bloody Mary, Ted Kennedy, Ignatius Loyola, Pope John Paul II, Torquemada, Arafat, Moshe Dayan, Hussein, Golda Meir, Tom Paine, Karl Marx, Adolph Hitler, Pope Pius XIII, Madonna, Jim Jones, Dean Luther Weigle, Fidel Castro, Gorbachev, FDR, Hank Williams, Saddam Hussein, John Lennon, Timothy O'Leary, and Ho Chi Minh got to heaven because God had nothing to dispense but mercy, what would heaven be like in two weeks? Can't you see Mohammed and Martin Luther chitchatting? How about the Stern Gang and the PLO? How about those 200,000 Serbians, that the Roman Catholics murdered in 1942, having fellowship with Archbishop Stepinac and Ante Pavelich? Can't you just see the producer of ]{.class_4938}[The Last Temptation of Christ ]{.class_4937}[and the director of ]{.class_4938}[Jesus Christ, Superstar ]{.class_4937}[enjoying the company of Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John?]{.class_4938}

[In view of what the Book says about Heaven (Rev. 4--5, 20--21), it would be ]{.class_4938}[mercy ]{.class_4937}[that would keep a sinner out. Mercy for one man is hell for another (see Rev. 21:1--6 and 22:15).]{.class_4938}

[We have commented on vss. 10--15 under 135:8--12. It was God's ]{.class_4938}[mercy ]{.class_4937}[that prevented Pharaoh from getting through the sea (vss. 13, 15). If he had made it, he would have slaughtered every male in Israel and carried all the women and children back to Egypt as slaves (see Exod. 14:5).]{.class_4938}

136:16 "To him which led his people through the wilderness: for his mercy endureth for ever.

17 To him which smote great kings: for his mercy endureth for ever:

18 And slew famous kings: for his mercy endureth for ever:

19 Sihon king of the Amorites: for his mercy endureth for ever:

20 And Og the king of Bashan: for his mercy endureth for ever:

21 And gave their land for an heritage: for his mercy endureth for ever:

22 Even an heritage unto Israel his servant: for his mercy endureth for ever.

23 Who remembered us in our low estate: for his mercy endureth for ever:

24 And hath redeemed us from our enemies: for his mercy endureth for ever.

25 Who giveth food to all flesh: for his mercy endureth for ever.

26 O give thanks unto the God of heaven: for his mercy endureth for ever. "

[Verse 16 is clear. In verses 17--21, mercy for ]{.class_4938}[Israel ]{.class_4937}[meant a deathbed in hell (see Ezek. 26:20; 32:18--30) for ]{.class_4938}[someone else. ]{.class_4937}[It was God's mercy that created the holocaust in Palestine (Josh. 6--13), and this holocaust was ]{.class_4938}[genocide ]{.class_4937}[(Deut. 7:1--6; 12:2--3; Num. 21:2--3; Deut. 2:34; 3:6, etc.). Think about that for a while. If God had not wiped out those nations---and some of them were left, not because of God's mercy, but because of Israel's disobedience (see Judg. 1 and 2)---they would have introduced sex perversion and prostitution into the worship services of the nation of Israel (]{.class_4938}[they did]{.class_4937}[---see 2 Kings 23:7), and God would have had to kill His own people (]{.class_4938}[He did: ]{.class_4937}[Jer. 52). Mercy for the Canaanites meant the destruction of Israel. Conversely, God's mercy to Israel meant the destruction of the Canaanites.]{.class_4938}

[Verses 23 and 24 will not be fulfilled until the Millennium (see Hannah and Zacharias again: 1 Sam. 2 and Luke 1:71). The inclusion of ]{.class_4938}["food," ]{.class_4939}[in verse 25 is entirely out of place unless one takes into account what we have run into already on a score of occasions: ]{.class_4938}[Israel ]{.class_4937}[being fed again in the wilderness (see Ps. 74:14), and ]{.class_4938}[Israel ]{.class_4937}[starving under the Antichrist (see Ps. 132:15). In dealing with these great Biblical truths, Kroll doesn't know whether he has been called to climb trees or go snorkeling. Neither does Motyer, Yates, or Dummelow.]{.class_4938}

[If a man cannot find any reason on earth to thank God (Rom. 1:21), he can always rely on one reason: ]{.class_4938}["for his mercy endureth forever." ]{.class_4939}[While there is life, there is hope; so long as you can breathe, hell has not closed its]{.class_4938}[ "gates"]{.class_4939}[ (Matt. 16:18) on you, nor has death shut its doors (Job 38:17). You have a chance to be saved. God has told you ]{.class_4938}[where ]{.class_4937}[He will be merciful to you (Rom. 9:16), and He will have mercy on you at ]{.class_4938}[that place ]{.class_4937}[(Rom. 5:1--10) and no other place. Your will (Rom. 9:18) will not be the deciding factor on ]{.class_4938}[how, when, ]{.class_4937}[or ]{.class_4938}[where ]{.class_4937}[you will get ]{.class_4938}["mercy." ]{.class_4939}[God has already decided those three issues. The only thing your will can decide (John 1:12) is whether or not you will receive HIS terms. God's mercy does not extend to a man who rejects His terms for giving mercy. For that man, there is only His ]{.class_4938}["power" ]{.class_4939}[and His ]{.class_4938}["wrath"]{.class_4939}[ (Rom. 9:22).]{.class_4938}

PSALM 137

137:1 "By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down, yea, we wept, when we remembered Zion.

2 We hanged our harps upon the willows in the midst thereof.

3 For there they that carried us away captive required of us a song; and they that wasted us required of us mirth, saying, Sing us one of the songs of Zion.

4 How shall we sing the LORD's song in a strange land?

5 If I forget thee, O Jerusalem, let my right hand forget her cunning.

6 If I do not remember thee, let my tongue cleave to the roof of my mouth; if I prefer not Jerusalem above my chief joy.

7 Remember, O LORD, the children of Edom in the day of Jerusalem; who said, Rase it, rase it, even to the foundation thereof.

8 O daughter of Babylon, who art to be destroyed; happy shall he be, that rewardeth thee as thou hast served us.

9 Happy shall he be, that taketh and dasheth thy little ones against the stones."

[Finally, after breaking their ankles, backs, and necks stumbling over chairs, milk bottles, Lego sets, tricycles, dishpans, and Slinkies, the Scholar's Union arrives at a Psalm they can almost handle. This time, the setting is so obvious that not even Woodrow Kroll could miss it. It is obviously the Babylonian captivity of 586--516 B.C. Bullinger makes the captivity 477--407 B.C., which is nearly 109 years off the traditional date. (In his system, the dedication of Solomon's temple is about 910 B.C. instead of the usual 1000 B.C. Bullinger has a long and very complicated explanation for this in the appendices of his ]{.class_4938}[Companion Bible, ]{.class_4937}[which may or may not be right.)]{.class_4938}

Matters of chronology get to be a joke after a while. I have, on my shelf, ten books that claim to straighten out Ussher's chronology, and only three of them agree on every date, and two of them copied the first one. Four more of them agree on a date for Creation but can't date the Flood, or the Exodus, in the same year after they started.

[Tell me all about it; straighten me out. If our calendar had been right, and Christ had been born in 4 B.C.---like all the commentators say He was---the Rapture would have been in 1989---]{.class_4938}[unless ]{.class_4937}[part of Daniel's Seventieth Week took place in the book of Acts. If He had been born in 3 B.C., the Rapture would have taken place in 1990, ]{.class_4938}[unless\...]{.class_4937}[etc. When you get the Gregorian calendar lined up with Ptolemy and Copernicus, and the Julian year related to Roman and Greek chronology, and make the adjustments to the lunar year versus the solar year (with the solstices and eclipses all located), all you have is one huge, intangible ball of wadded up shoelaces and cotton yarn. Nobody knows what they are talking about.]{.class_4938}

I have heard Exodus 12:40, Galatians 3:17, and 1 Kings 6:1 explained so many times that I should be able to give you the day and the hour that your wood, hay, and stubble burns up at the Judgment Seat of Christ. But the only man that professed to be able to do that was a Charismatic crook in America, back in 1988, who wrote a book dating the beheading of Moses and Elijah and the start of World War III. He gave fifteen specific future events to the day, and even the hour, and not one of them came within two years of being the right date. Like Oral Roberts, Marilyn Hickey, Jimmy Swaggart, Kenneth Copeland, Kenneth Hagin, Gorman, and Shambach, he made a million dollars off the suckers. "There's a sucker born every minute" (Barnum).

At any rate, the Jews are in captivity by the Euphrates and the Tigris rivers, which come within forty miles of each other near Babylon. The Chaboras is another river, and Ezekiel mentions Chebar (Ezek. 1:1), which was Babylonian territory at the time. Nearly all the commentators forgot that all of the Euphrates was Babylonian (see Jer. 46:2, 6; 2 Chron. 35:20). Babylon was not just a city; it was a kingdom under Nebuchadnezzar, and included everything from Egypt to Ararat to west India. The commentators have all of the captivity stuck in one city, but they were scattered all over Syria, Assyria, Media, Persia, and Babylonia (see 2 Kings 6). Verse 1 is self-explanatory. They wept when they thought of the ruined temple, the ravished women (Lam. 5:11), their dead loved ones (Lam. 4:4--12), and former days, which may have included some happy feasts and times of celebration.

Devotionally, nothing is more pitiful than a Bible believing Christian who has had to leave an evangelistic, soul winning, Bible-believing church and, then, go out in search of something its equal. Sometimes this is forced upon him by a change of job or residence, but many times it is due to church splits where pride gets a hold of him, and he leaves to "prove his point." Once out in the desert of modern American Christianity---and today it is a literal desert (Amos 8:11--12)---he realizes what he has left. Not one of ten ever returns to it because pride will not allow him to "eat crow." America is filled with Christians---especially Baptists---who once were in "live" congregations where every preaching service was a revival meeting and every song service an uplifting, exhilarating experience. Out in the desert they encounter, "As this version puts it," "Sharing and coping," "Our attendance is off today," "Next month's goal is," "A better translation would be," "Our stewardship banquet will be on," "Today is Remember your Aunt Sally's Butter Churn Day," "We have a special prize for the one who," "Will a man rob God?" "Unfortunately, the King James' translators here said," "We want to honor our singles with a special," etc.

"[Ichabod\...the glory is departed" ]{.class_4939}[(1 Sam. 4:21).]{.class_4938}

Nine out of ten die in the desert. Family ties, opinions of relatives, or commitments to carnal Christians force them to stand by their vows, even after they realize their "vows" weren't worth the price they had to pay. They weep when they remember "Zion."

[I am eighty one years old this year. For fifty four years I have been with the "shout." For fifty four years, without a let up, I have seen adults genuinely converted and young men called into the ministry (over three hundred of them). True, it has gotten "thinner and thinner" every year, but it is still there. There are still ]{.class_4938}["streams in the desert" ]{.class_4939}[(Isa. 35:6).]{.class_4938}

[I can give you the names of three of the largest churches in America (or were) that shrunk from Mt. Everest to Mt. McKinley to Grandfather Mountain, down to Stone Mountain, and wound up as mounds of fill dirt near the city dump. No more "Amen!," no more "Glory to God!" no more altars filled with men getting saved, no more young people preaching on the streets and passing out tracts, no more packed out Bible studies, and no more weeping over lost people. Today, they are shaking hands or hugging each other, "giving" and "sharing," "giving" and "coping," and "giving," and correcting the Book. (See Hosea 4:18 and comments in ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on the Minor Prophets,]{.class_4937}[ Vol. 1).]{.class_4938}

Ichabod.

[Burgon says, "they sat in silence, they remembered in silence, and they wept in silence." Like many a prisoner in solitary at Brushy Mountain, Tennessee, no one knew what went on inside them. They laid aside the musical instrument that David used for praising God (vs. 2). (Note that this was the "heavenly instrument" that we commented on under Psalm 33:2 and 81:2.) The typology is striking, for it identifies a citizen of the real mount Zion (see Rev. 14:1--2; Ps. 48:2; and Heb. 12:22) away from home (Heb. 13:14), and unable to praise God in ]{.class_4938}["spiritual songs" ]{.class_4939}[(Col. 3:16). He has lost his joy (Ps. 51:12), so he checked his instrument back into the band room.]{.class_4938}

[It is ]{.class_4938}[Seth's seed ]{.class_4937}[(Gen. 5:29) that are unimpressed by evolution and progress (Gen. 4:20--22 and comments in that Commentary). They are aware of the curse (Gen. 3:17) and long for rest (see Gen. 5:29 and comments in that Commentary).]{.class_4938}

[Instead of mountains producing honey, milk, and plentiful rainfalls from heaven (Deut. 11:11), the exile found himself on a flat plain, under a burning sun. Instead of the numerous mountain streams, two broad flat rivers are there, with artificial canals going out from them. Instead of God's house (see Ps. 26:8, 27:4), there towers over the captives a five hundred foot temple to Bel, the Baal of Babel (Gen. 10:10). When they look at the palace of their conqueror, they see a building as large as the whole city of Jerusalem (Dan. 4:30). Zion was not like ]{.class_4938}[Babylon. ]{.class_4937}[Pensacola is not like New Jerusalem; nor is Miami, Chicago, Memphis, Tokyo, London, Cincinnati, St. Louis, Berlin, San Paulo, Los Angeles, San Francisco, Mobile, Honolulu, Atlanta, Paris, Rome, or Oshkosh. When Daniel ]{.class_4938}["remembered Zion," ]{.class_4939}[he prayed (Dan. 6:10).]{.class_4938}

[A very interesting historical footnote can be found in Spurgeon's ]{.class_4938}[Treasury. ]{.class_4937}[It deals with that great anti Catholic from the history of Great Britain: Oliver Cromwell. During the Commonwealth, he not only allowed Jews to settle in London (Gen. 12:3), but for their benefit, he let them observe the Feast of Tabernacles on the banks of the Thames River where numerous willow trees grew; the trees were brought to England via Alexander Pope, who received them from Turkey. This breed was called "a weeping willow," and it fathered every weeping willow in England, Scotland, and Ireland (]{.class_4938}[Treasury]{.class_4937}[, pp. 234, 235).]{.class_4938}

[Two things were required of the miserable captives. They were required to tell some jokes and to liven things up with a little "song and dance." The sadism in such a request is evident. Watch Samson's adversaries doing the same thing with him when he is helpless (Judg. 16:25). ]{.class_4938}[Human nature never changes. ]{.class_4937}[The Jews in Buchenwald (1940) were required to sing "The Jew's Song," sometimes an hour at a time; if they didn't sing it with enough "gusto," they had to repeat it. The song was about "wicked, crafty, long nosed Jews" finally being caught in their "treachery" and getting the "due reward" of their deeds. In Treblinka, Jews were sometimes required to sing the German national anthem (]{.class_4938}[Deutschland Über Alles]{.class_4937}[). There is nothing new under the sun. Other words that have degenerated through the years are "vagabond," which originally meant simply a wanderer, not an irresponsible person. "Simple," which originally meant someone without guile, someone who was not tricky. "Silly," which originally meant harmless and, therefore, has no defense against trickery. "Libertine," which referred to freed Roman slaves, but later meant an immoral person. Other words like this are "parasite," "piker,", "fool," "shambles," "entertain," and "passion."

PSALM 138

138:1 "I will praise thee with my whole heart: before the gods will I sing praise unto thee.

2 I will worship toward thy holy temple, and praise thy name for thy lovingkindness and for thy truth: for thou hast magnified thy word above all thy name.

3 In the day when I cried thou answeredst me, and strengthenedst me with strength in my soul.

4 All the kings of the earth shall praise thee, O LORD, when they hear the words of thy mouth.

5 Yea, they shall sing in the ways of the LORD: for great is the glory of the LORD.

6 Though the LORD be high, yet hath he respect unto the lowly: but the proud he knoweth afar off.

7 Though I walk in the midst of trouble, thou wilt revive me: thou shalt stretch forth thine hand against the wrath of mine enemies, and thy right hand shall save me.

8 The LORD will perfect that which concerneth me: thy mercy, O LORD, endureth for ever: forsake not the works of thine own hands."

Now we are going to see the Alexandrian Cult in action. Here is the place to find out who believes the Bible and who does not, for in verse 2 is the most Bibliolatrous statement that any Bibliolater ever made in the Old Testament. You have to read Paul in Romans 9:17 and Galatians 3:8 to find something as "anti intellectual."

5\. Watch old H. Hammond trying to cheat you out of your birthright: "His word, being here annexed to 'lovingkindness and truth,' must needs be that part of his His Word to which these two are applicable \[he is trying to "context" you out of the truth\] and then to 'magnify' this 'word' or promise\...seems to signify two things\...the making very great and excellent promises, and then the performing of them punctually."

"[The making very great and excellent promises"?]{.class_4938}

[Why, that isn't even a grammatical clause, Esau. Did He mean "The making of very great\...etc."? If He did, what is this ]{.class_4938}["above all thy name"? ]{.class_4939}["Above all that men have apprehended or spoken of thee."]{.class_4938}

Shut up, Esau. That isn't what He said.

"[The ]{.class_4938}[name ]{.class_4937}[of God includes all the perfections of God; everything that God is, and which God has revealed Himself as having\...." Right over the middle of the plate, waist high, completely contrary to every discussion of Scripture published in ]{.class_4938}[The Biblical Evangelist, The Fundamentalist, The Biblical Viewpoint, Christianity Today, The Baptist Tribune, ]{.class_4937}[and ]{.class_4938}[Baptist Biblical Heritage. ]{.class_4937}[Philpot gives you all fifty two, "face up," completely contrary to the written professions of faith of every major Christian college and university in America, plus the writings of John R. Rice, Curtis Hutson, Bob Jones III, Bob Jones IV, Robert Sumner, Ronald Walker, Zane Hodges, Wilbur Pickering, Donald Waite, Gary Hudson, A. T. Robertson, Machen, Warfield, Wuest, Schaff, Afman, James Combs, James Price, Martin, Custer, MacRae, Wisdom, Panosian, Newman, and everyone ]{.class_4938}[like them.]{.class_4937}

"[What a firm salvation then is ours, which rests upon His word, when God has magnified His word above all His name! What volumes of blessedness and truth are contained therein, so that if God has revealed His truth to your soul and given you faith to anchor in the word of promise, sooner than that should fail He would suffer the loss of all: ]{.class_4938}[for He has magnified His word above all His name."]{.class_4937}

[Exactly; all honor and blessing on the bottom of the pile, His name above all honor and blessing, and then His word (not "Word" or "Word of God" or "God's Word") ]{.class_4938}[above ]{.class_4937}[the name that is ]{.class_4938}["above every name." ]{.class_4939}[That ]{.class_4937}[is what the text said. That is what the text ]{.class_4938}[meant. ]{.class_4937}[But the shifting, proud, nervous, conceited, backslidden, apostate Fundamentalists named above could not admit that, because people would spot them for what they are: "raging egomaniacs." [If Sumner or Kutilek (or someone just as carnal and stupid) had tried to exposit the verse, you would have gotten something like this: "What the original Hebrew is telling us here is that the thoughts of God are as great as God Himself because His eternal, infallible, verbally inspired, original autographs are settled in heaven, and heaven is 'above' the reputation God has gained down here through reliable translations. The Hebrew, here, for 'word' means 'recent promises,' given in 1000 B.C., and the word for 'magnify' doesn't actually mean ]{.class_4938}[magnify. ]{.class_4937}[It is unfortunate the ]{.class_4938}[King James ]{.class_4937}[translators rendered the word this way. An Gottes segen ist alles gelegen.

Scripture with Scripture. Everything in line, everything matching. This changing of chronological order is a common thing in the Book. Observe that Ruth took place before Judges 1--4, although the book of Ruth comes AFTER Judges. Luke 1--3 took place before Mark 2. PSALM 139

139:1 "O LORD, thou hast searched me, and known me.

2 Thou knowest my downsitting and mine uprising, thou understandest my thought afar off.

3 Thou compassest my path and my lying down, and art acquainted with all my ways.

4 For there is not a word in my tongue, but, lo, O LORD, thou knowest it altogether.

5 Thou has beset me behind and before, and laid thine hand upon me.

6 Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high, I cannot attain unto it."

[Before continuing, let the "serious Bible student"---that is, a man who never takes the faculty, or staff of ]{.class_4938}[any ]{.class_4937}[major college, university, or seminary seriously---ponder what he has just come through. ]{.class_4938}[Five times ]{.class_4937}[we have seen the Alexandrian Cult (every destructive Bible critic who swears by "plenary, verbally inspired, infallible originals") faced with the power and authority of the written and ]{.class_4938}[available ]{.class_4937}[Holy Scriptures. These places were Psalm 12:6--7; 119:11; 119:89; 119:162, and 138:2. Five times, ]{.class_4938}[in one Book, ]{.class_4937}[is enough to teach the most naive and simple minded that when you get to the New Testament and hit passages like Matthew 4:4; Ephesians 6:17; 1 Thessalonians 2:13; Romans 9:17; John 5:39; Galatians 3:8; John 8:31--47; Acts 17:11; and John 12:48, that the same crew is going to do everything within their power to destroy your faith in ]{.class_4938}[the Bible ]{.class_4937}[as the final authority in your life. The best of these men (Torrey, Spurgeon, Caroll, Broadus, Scofield, English, et al.) will, at certain times, do all they can to get you to worship ]{.class_4938}[them ]{.class_4937}[and their Union as the supreme authority for your life. It is their Adamic natures that cause them to do this, and they will sin in this fashion instead of drinking, swearing, stealing, fornicating, and killing. They swap sins.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 1. God searches every man (1 Chron. 28:9) and knows him (Jer. 6:19; Dan. 2:29; Matt. 9:4). David asked to be searched in this same Psalm (vs. 23). When you search for something, you hunt for it till you find it (Job 28:3; Rom. 8:27). ]{.class_4938}["The Spirit searcheth all things" ]{.class_4939}[(1 Cor. 2:10), and the things that God knows about man from having searched him out (Job 11:11; Ps. 10:15) are these:]{.class_4938}

1\. He knows when a man sits down, lies down, and gets up (vss. 2--3).

2\. He knows every word that a man says (vs. 4).

3\. He knows all the thoughts that go through a man's head (vs. 2).

4\. He knows what is behind the man and what is in front of him (vs. 5).

5\. He knows the man's personal habits (vs. 3).

[If this is true, do you mean to tell me that the man who "fears God" doesn't ever TREMBLE and shake in his boots? ]{.class_4938}[I don't believe a word of it. ]{.class_4937}[If a man isn't scared slap out of his skin, ]{.class_4938}[at times, ]{.class_4937}[by the words of God in that Book, it is because he doesn't think God knows what he is doing or thinking, at ]{.class_4938}[certain ]{.class_4937}[times.]{.class_4938}

"[Such knowledge is too wonderful for me" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 6). How absolutely true. This explains why atheists and agnostics rule God out. Since their feeble, finite, puny brains cannot comprehend a Being who can perceive the minds, words, works, events, and calamities that are connected with three billion people at one time, they just decide in their own favor: "There isn't any such being. If I cannot understand Him, He doesn't exist! There! That takes care of that!" Well, it seals your damnation, if that is what you mean.]{.class_4938}

How often (O Lord, my God, how often!) I have flown over huge cities and looked down at the teeming interstates and cloverleafs and said to myself, "How on earth could God know what is going on in every car down there (including the conversations) and still know the thoughts of every passenger in this plane?"

Then it gets worse. He said that He knows the conversations going on in every bedroom, office, dining room, living room, back yard, restaurant, and shower room in that town, while keeping track of the same material in the last ten towns I just flew over.

But it gets worse. If Psalm 139 is true, God did not vacate the Philippines when I left there in 1947, or Japan when I left there in 1948, or Germany when I left there in 1980, or Canada, etc. He kept track of all the conversations (and thoughts!) of the entire populace in every town through which I ever walked (Manila, Tokyo, Nikko, Paranaque, Angeles City, Katsura, Dryden, Thunder Bay, Bad Ischl, Frankfort, Munich, Fussen, Berchtesgaden, Honolulu, Haliewa, Wainae, Los Angeles, Las Vegas, Reno, Key West, Oberammergau, Mittenwald, San Diego, New York, Chicago, Memphis, St. Louis, Topeka, Wichita, Philadelphia, Salzburg, Washington D.C., Fort Benning, Fort Ord, Fort Rucker, Fort Leavenworth, Seattle, Green Bay, Vienna, Cincinnati, Detroit, and Nuremberg) before I was born, all during my life, and will, later, if I die.

"[Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high, I cannot attain unto it." ]{.class_4939}[No, I sure can't. But I get one thing; I get one message: if God is like that (and Ps. 139 says He is), can you imagine such a God]{.class_4938}[ losing the words He wants me to read and memorize, ]{.class_4937}[so that I "might not sin against him"? Why, that is absolutely preposterous! Do you know what is wrong with the Alexandrians in the Scholar's Union? They have simply conformed God Almighty to their own image. They ]{.class_4938}["limited the Holy One of Israel" ]{.class_4939}[(Ps. 78:41). They thought you and I were dependent on ]{.class_4938}[them ]{.class_4937}[to find those words! ]{.class_4938}[Flap doodle. ]{.class_4937}[A God who can read the minds of three billion people simultaneously---without forgetting the material He got out of the skulls of eight billion people that preceded them (4000 B.C.--A.D.1990)---isn't going to have to go to a Hebrew or Greek lexicon to help me out---or you, either.]{.class_4938}

"[Every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment" ]{.class_4939}[(Matt. 12:36). ]{.class_4938}["God shall bring every work into judgment with every secret thing" ]{.class_4939}[(Ecc. 12:14). ]{.class_4938}["In the day when God shall judge the secrets of men by Jesus Christ" ]{.class_4939}[(Rom. 2:16). Are you ready to have your ]{.class_4938}[secrets ]{.class_4937}[judged? Einstein wasn't, at any time in his life; neither were Karl Marx, Martin Luther King Jr., Mikhail Gorbachev, Spiro Agnew, Josef Stalin, FDR, Hitler, Hirohito, Castro, Jack Kennedy, Henry Kissinger, Tip O'Neill, Lenin, or Freud. A man, who is prepared to have his ]{.class_4938}[secrets ]{.class_4937}[judged, only got prepared one way, and the men listed above never professed to have taken that way (John 14:1--6). Neither did any Roman Catholic pope since A.D. 300. The tragic fact is that no man's life would pass such a judgment no matter how good he was, and although God allowed this under the law (see Ezek. 14:14) and winked at it under conscience (Acts 17:30), there are no longer any loopholes (Acts 17:31). You will have to have God's Righteousness (100%, perfect, sinless holiness) to get by (Rom. 10:1--3). Your ]{.class_4938}[secrets]{.class_4937}[ will have to remain secret, under the blood, in order for you to enter a city where ]{.class_4938}["any thing that defileth" ]{.class_4939}[shall not enter (Rev. 21:27).]{.class_4938}

[Even in the Tribulation, faith in Christ is required (Rev. 7:14, 14:12), and the ]{.class_4938}["blood of the Lamb" ]{.class_4939}[is necessary for the secret things (Rev. 12:11). The requirement in this age, for salvation, is sinless perfection. You cannot get it apart from the finished blood atonement of a perfectly sinless man (Heb. 10:8--12, Rom. 3:21--25, 5:1--10).]{.class_4938}

139:7 "Whither shall I go from thy spirit? or whither shall I flee from thy presence?

8 If I ascend up into heaven, thou art there: if I make my bed in bell, behold, thou art there.

9 If I take the wings of the morning, and dwell in the uttermost parts of the sea;

10 Even there shall thy hand lead me, and thy right hand shall hold me.

11 If I say, Surely the darkness shall cover me; even the night shall be light about me.

[12 Yea, the darkness hideth not from thee; but the night shineth as the day: the darkness and the light are both alike to thee.]{.class_4939}["]{.class_4938}

[This section is on the omnipresence of God. "You can't go no where that God ain't," is the way one Hamite preached it, back in 1980. Jonah, trying to flee from the presence of the Lord, is like a little boy covering his eyes with his hands and saying to his father, "I'll bet you can't find me now!" Everyone is familiar with the dissection that converts "God is nowhere" to "God is now here." Running from God is the most futile race a man ever ran. He said that He filled heaven and earth (Jer. 23:24). At the same time, when He boasted about His omnipotence and His omniscience (Isa. 44:6--9, 24--26; 45:5--12, 19--20; 46:4--5, 9--10; 48:3--8, 12--13, 17--18; 49:5--7, 18), He took time out to ridicule modern man (see Isa. 40:15--17, 25--26; 41:21--24) and challenge him to prove that he was anything more than a total blank (Isa. 41:24), and that anyone who followed man was not an ]{.class_4938}["abomination" ]{.class_4939}[(Isa. 41:24). In the Book, "modern man" is a deceived, self righteous fool, and that is why modern man is against the Book. David had much more scientific knowledge about these matters than Planck, Heisenberg, Einstein, Oparin, Salk, Curie, and von Braun.]{.class_4938}

[In verses 7 and 8, David states that he could not get out of God's presence, even in Hell. If we ]{.class_4938}["live, and move" ]{.class_4939}[in Him and ]{.class_4938}["have our being" ]{.class_4939}[in Him (Acts 17:28), we would have to get outside the universe to get out of Him, and then you would find that if the universe were "limited," its Creator is not. This cosmological truth (vs. 8) has a terrible reality for lost men and women, because it means that since ]{.class_4938}["our God is a consuming fire" ]{.class_4939}[(Heb. 12:29), the ]{.class_4938}["lake of fire"]{.class_4939}[ (Rev.]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4939}[20:14) is a manifestation of one of God's attributes: His wrath. When David says these words, ]{.class_4938}["hell"]{.class_4939}[ can be anywhere in Sheol, Abraham's bosom, Paradise, Tartarus, or the fiery hell; but in eternity, death and hell have been cast into the Lake of Fire, so it doesn't do the Alexandrians any good to quibble about ]{.class_4938}["hell"]{.class_4939}[ in the passage before us, although all of them will. They like "hellless" Bibles; it makes them feel more comfortable and helps their "testimony." Know what I mean, jellybean?]{.class_4938}

"[Hell," for an unsaved man, is the]{.class_4938}[ presence of God. ]{.class_4937}[The trouble with this presence is the holy, pure, spotless, sinless, white hot heat of absolute, eternal energy. Einstein knew nothing about it, but he does NOW. God was present in ]{.class_4938}["the den of]{.class_4939}[ ]{.class_4938}[lions"]{.class_4939}[ (Dan. 6:16), but only for its first occupant. The next shift (Dan. 6:24) found out that being in God's presence didn't do anything for them. What is meat for one man is poison for another (see Ps. 136 and comments). God's eternal, sinless presence will be heaven for the saved, for they will be]{.class_4938}[ "like him" ]{.class_4939}[(1 John 3:1--3). The problem is ]{.class_4938}[conversion. ]{.class_4937}[Before the sinner can adjust to his eternal company, his ]{.class_4938}[body ]{.class_4937}[must be converted into a sinless, holy body of pure energy (see Heb. 1:7). Without the "new birth," in this age, it is impossible. ]{.class_4938}[Hell ]{.class_4937}[for an unsaved man, ]{.class_4938}[in this age, ]{.class_4937}[does not come as the vengeful act of an outraged torture master---like the college and university professors put it. It comes as the perfectly ]{.class_4938}[natural consequence ]{.class_4937}[of a sinner's deliberate and willful rejection of the ]{.class_4938}[alternative. ]{.class_4937}[It is the natural (and predictable) result of refusing to be ]{.class_4938}[converted.]{.class_4937}

"[If I take the wings of the morning" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 9). Here is one expression that is plainly "poetic" and figurative. Two ideas are involved. The first is that the ]{.class_4938}["morning" ]{.class_4939}[literally takes off faster than a jet. The wings of the eagle (Deut. 32:11) and the peacock (Job 39:13) can't keep up with it. No bird could outfly the dawn, which moves around the earth at better than a thousand miles an hour at the equator. Again, considering the speed of light, when the sun appears above the horizon its beams would be traveling at least one hundred thousand times faster than the fastest jet in the twentieth century. Thus, two things are involved: the flight of light and the flight of the sun itself, as it appears from the earth. For the saint, God is not only a "presence," but he is ]{.class_4938}[led ]{.class_4937}[by God and ]{.class_4938}[held ]{.class_4937}[by God (vs. 10), in the sense of Divine guidance and protection.]{.class_4938}

"[There is no darkness, nor shadow of death, where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves" ]{.class_4939}[(Job 34:22) is the theme of verses 11 and 12. The book of Job is full of this (see Job 12:22, 28:3, 38:19). When the lights go out in Egypt (Exod. 10:21--23), the Lord is not going to lose anything in the dark or need a flashlight to get around. You will need it, not Him. God can dwell in ]{.class_4938}["thick darkness" ]{.class_4939}[(Exod. 20:21) and you can't. Three months in "solitary," in a cell where there is no light of any kind, twenty four hours a day, can drive a man crazy. (Ask Harlan Popov or Richard Wurmbrand, or a dozen others when you see them in glory if this is so.) Unlike the ying and yang "dual nature" of reality, taught by the Orientals, God condemns any fool who ]{.class_4938}[pretends ]{.class_4937}[that light and darkness are the same, or that they are interchangeable (Isa. 5:20). God created both (Isa. 45:7), so to ]{.class_4938}[Him ]{.class_4937}[they are the same. The attempt of some to play God by thinking ]{.class_4938}[themselves ]{.class_4937}[into God's position (see John 1:13) doesn't work (see ]{.class_4938}[The Damnation of a Nation, ]{.class_4937}[chapter 6, 1992). ]{.class_4938}["He knoweth what is in the darkness" ]{.class_4939}[(Dan. 2:22).]{.class_4938}

[Spurgeon does his best to rid the "non elect" of their fear of hell. No one likes to talk about HELL.

[Fundamentalists ]{.class_4937}[are afraid to say the word.]{.class_4938}

There are no comments on hell in the Commentaries.

[Conservatives ]{.class_4937}[are afraid to say the word.]{.class_4938}

[Suppose that the reference was to the underworld, in general, instead of ]{.class_4938}[specifically ]{.class_4937}[a place of punishment; what then? ]{.class_4938}["Death and hell" ]{.class_4939}[are cast ]{.class_4938}["into the lake of fire" ]{.class_4939}[(Rev. 20:14).
