139:13 "For thou hast possessed my reins: thou hast covered me in my mother's womb.

14 I will praise thee; for I am fearfully and wonderfully made: marvellous are thy works; and that my soul knoweth right well.

15 My substance was not hid from thee, when I was made in secret, and curiously wrought in the lowest parts of the earth.

16 Thine eyes did see my substance, yet being unperfect; and in thy book all my members were written, which in continuance were fashioned, when as yet there was none of them.

17 How precious also are thy thoughts unto me, O God! how great is the sum of them!

18 If I should count them, they are more in number than the sand: when I awake, I am still with thee."

[Verse 13 is a proof text for anti abortionists to prove that abortion is ]{.class_4938}[murder. ]{.class_4937}[The idea is that a grown, living man, writing about his past, proves that an unborn embryo (that never lived to write anything) was in the same condition. Luke 1:41 is another text, and it is used in conjunction with Jeremiah 1:5. In all three cases ]{.class_4938}[no one has been aborted. ]{.class_4937}[All three men grow up to adult life. The idea with the "pro lifers" is that every unborn embryo was predestinated to live (vs. 16), so it was a "personality" in the womb (vs. 13). I have never been convinced of these matters for three reasons:]{.class_4938}

1\. John was born like no human being on this earth was ever born (Luke 1:15), and Jesus Christ says so (Luke 7:28).

2\. It is one thing for God to tell an adult man about his pre birth existence (Jer. 1:4--5), and another for God to say NOTHING about someone who never saw the light of day (Job 3:10--13). See Hosea 9:9--16.

[3. I find no ]{.class_4938}["life"]{.class_4939}[ in any man in that Book until he ]{.class_4938}[breathes ]{.class_4937}[(Gen. 2:7); not even when his body is fully formed as an adult (Ezek. 37:8). If abortion is murder, and ]{.class_4938}[maybe ]{.class_4937}[it is, then all doctors who perform it should be executed, according to both Testaments (Acts 25:11; Rom. 13:1--6; Num. 35:16). I don't remember seeing any signs by pro lifers picketing "clinics" which recommend that Biblical procedure.]{.class_4938}

[Nonetheless, ]{.class_4938}["I am fearfully and wonderfully made" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 14). All of the dissecting of the human organism in the last five hundred years hasn't really accomplished much of anything. Man's knowledge of the respiratory system, the nervous system, the reproductive system, the circulatory system, the skeletal system, and the lymph glands, etc., hasn't really done anything, if you actually sit down and think for about five minutes instead of just passing out under an anesthesia of propaganda that the AMA puts out. Be sensible. What has the modern practise of medicine done for any man on the face of this earth except two things:]{.class_4938}

1\. Enable him to live a little bit longer, if he has the MONEY to take the treatments and the care.

[2. Ease his pain or discomforture, ]{.class_4938}[if he had the money to pay for the drugs or the operation.]{.class_4937}

[Tell the truth about it. If anything went wrong with you and you didn't have the MONEY to pay for a doctor or a hospital, what could "modern science" do for you? There is not one modern, "scientific" doctor on the face of this earth (nor any hospital) who knows the cure for a bad back or a common cold. Any ]{.class_4938}[honest, ]{.class_4937}[twentieth-century doctor will tell you that once that back goes, no knowledge of the nervous system (or skeletal system) can enable you to live without pain unless you lose the use of your legs. Radar, lasers, miracle drugs, "major breakthroughs," etc., accomplish absolute ]{.class_4938}[nothing. ]{.class_4937}[Drug the pain out, ]{.class_4938}[if you have the money ]{.class_4937}[to pay for the drugs. Cutting open the body and morbidly analyzing its content has done very little for the average MD but make an atheist out of him. You begin in high school by dissecting frogs.]{.class_4938}

[The human body is a miracle that no scientist has been able to duplicate, nor ever will. The eyes, ears, and brain make decisions about directions, distances, and measurements that take less than one-thousandth of a second. Our blood carries oxygen to all parts of the body and then returns the spent oxygen to our lungs in the form of CO]{.class_4938}[2]{.class_4945}[. How does the heart pump millions of gallons of blood through arteries and capillaries not as thick as a straw, that sometimes run a mile in length, and then back again? Sure you can "explain" it, but not ]{.class_4938}[rationally. ]{.class_4937}[All you can do is observe it, note it, document it, ]{.class_4938}[give tradesman's names to what you are looking at, ]{.class_4937}[and then pretend that you ]{.class_4938}[understand ]{.class_4937}[it because you can ]{.class_4938}[manipulate ]{.class_4937}[it. But it hasn't been ex]{.class_4938}[plained ]{.class_4937}[one time since Eve messed up her circulatory system (see Gen. 3:4--5 in that commentary). If you could really explain it, you could ]{.class_4938}[duplicate ]{.class_4937}[it. When man says there is a natural explanation for everything, what he means is: "We have assigned NAMES to everything we ]{.class_4938}[don't ]{.class_4937}[understand so that we can cite these NAMES as an explanation." Quatsch!]{.class_4938}

[There are something like two thousand nerve endings in each square inch of the skin in our bodies, each one connected to some area in the brain. Now that you know that and can "explain it," let's see you manufacture a LIVE nerve to replace a DEAD one. You ]{.class_4938}[can't do it. ]{.class_4937}[Modern science is just as helpless before such a simple problem as a witch doctor in Nigeria in 3000 B.C. Our bodies are coated with skin that lets out salts and water as perspiration, but keeps water OUT through the same pores. No robot man ever created, since 1980, could approximate a human body in a thousand details. Hit the robot on his left big toe, and see how long it takes the toe to signal the "pain" to the "brain," and then for the "brain" to tell the eyes to look down at where the toe was "hit." Your stomach weighs about twelve ounces, but it can digest a pound of steak in less than two hours. To do this, it has to be protected from the acids and enzymes that digest the steak. Your tummy was built by ]{.class_4938}[whom? ]{.class_4937}["Mother nature"? Wouldn't she have made a three pound stomach to take care of a two pound steak? Why don't your fingerprints match mine? Did "Mother Nature," in a sudden whim of caprice, decide ]{.class_4938}[accidently ]{.class_4937}[to put a different set of prints on six billion pairs of hands? ]{.class_4938}[What would be the purpose in this? ]{.class_4937}[Talk about superfluous extravagance! Don't ever ask "Mother Nature" to budget your income; you'd be bankrupt in a week.]{.class_4938}

[Certainly something is ]{.class_4938}["fearfully and wonderfully made"]{.class_4939}[ that contains 213 bones, 600 muscles, 970 miles of blood vessels, and can distinguish 600 different colors. The "engine" beats 36,792,000 times a year and pumps 4,000 tons of liquid. The lungs take in 2,400 gallons of air a day. The jaw of this little animal has forty pounds of pressure, and he totes around 10,000,000 nerves, which can be harp strings for the angels to play on or tom toms for the devil to beat on.]{.class_4938}

[And now it is time to put the kiddies to bed (Kroll, Motyer, Spurgeon, Yates, Lange, Henry, et al.). Verse 15 cannot be a reference to any unaborted child or embryo. No "embryo" is formed ]{.class_4938}[underground.]{.class_4937}

To reinforce this outrageous nonsense, Kroll runs to Spurgeon and picks up this: "A great artist will often labour alone in his studio and not suffer his work to be seen until it is finished; even so did the Lord fashion US when no eye beheld us, and the veil was not lifted till every member was complete."]{.class_4938}

"The Psalmist is saying \[now go on and ]{.class_4938}[ignore ]{.class_4937}[what he said, and replace it with hot air\] that the omnipotence of God guided the very formation of life in the womb\..." (Kroll, p. 1176).]{.class_4938}

"[Are ye also yet without understanding? ]{.class_4939}[(Matt. 15:16). Well, drown the Alexandrian Cult. Make the Scholar's Union walk the plank, and throw their libraries out the porthole after them. When in doubt, throw Christian scholarship out! O U T!]{.class_4938}

[There has only been one man on this earth who was ]{.class_4938}["curiously wrought in the lowest parts of the earth," ]{.class_4939}[and since that man was ]{.class_4938}["of the earth" ]{.class_4939}[(1 Cor. 15:47), he was ]{.class_4938}["earthy" ]{.class_4939}[(1 Cor. 15:49). What happened to the Scholar's Union? Nothing; the supernatural simply showed up again (see Ps. 48:2, 52:3--6), and they couldn't handle it. They never could. They become ]{.class_4938}["of the earth, earthy" ]{.class_4939}[whenever anything in the Bible shows up that, ]{.class_4938}[if they believe it, ]{.class_4937}[could make them look stupid in the eyes of other Alexandrians.]{.class_4938}

[David is referring to himself as a man, so he makes reference to his original source: ]{.class_4938}[Adam. ]{.class_4937}[It is Adam who was made of the ]{.class_4938}["dust of the ground," ]{.class_4939}[but this ]{.class_4938}["dust" ]{.class_4939}[is not located in Genesis 2:7. We are told that dust was the foundation for a body made of clay (Job 4:19). Dust can be wet because it can come in ]{.class_4938}["clods" ]{.class_4939}[(see Job 7:5. Note "clodhopper"). When Job says he will "sleep in the dust" (Job 7:21), he is not talking about loose particles of disintegrated dirt. Dust can be clay (see Job 10:9). In one case the ]{.class_4938}["dust"]{.class_4939}[ is associated directly with ]{.class_4938}["the lowest parts of the earth," ]{.class_4939}[for it is likened to ]{.class_4938}["the bars of the pit" ]{.class_4939}[(Job 17:16). All of the Alexandrians ignored the oldest book ]{.class_4938}[in the Bible: the one written before Moses wrote the Genesis account of Adam. ]{.class_4937}[Again, in Psalm 30:9, the ]{.class_4938}["pit"]{.class_4939}[ is mentioned with the ]{.class_4938}["dust."]{.class_4939}[ There is dust on the mountains and in the atmosphere (Prov. 8:26). The ]{.class_4938}["dust"]{.class_4939}[ is measured (Isa. 40:12).]{.class_4938}

[If you believe the Book---and God knows the faculties and staffs of BBC, PCC, BJU, and Liberty never believed it after they went to college---David is speaking for the structure of man, not just ]{.class_4938}[himself. ]{.class_4937}[It is ]{.class_4938}[man ]{.class_4937}[who is ]{.class_4938}["fearfully and wonderfully made,"]{.class_4939}[ and it is man who is now covered in the womb (vs. 13), and it is man whose substance was wrought in the ]{.class_4938}["lowest parts of the earth." ]{.class_4939}[Clay mud pies; that is the ticket. The man, as the fowls (see Gen. 1:20 and 2:19), was of the ]{.class_4938}["dust of the ground," ]{.class_4939}[but ]{.class_4938}[water ]{.class_4937}[brought him forth. It ]{.class_4938}[was ]{.class_4937}[a subterranean workshop, just like David McLaren said it ]{.class_4938}[wasn't ]{.class_4937}[(1853), and just like Woodrow Kroll thought it ]{.class_4938}[wasn't. ]{.class_4937}[All men were "in Adam" according to verse 16: ]{.class_4938}["and in thy book all my members were written, which in continuance were fashioned, when as yet there was none of them." ]{.class_4939}[Ditto Levi in Hebrews 7:10. Abraham's ]{.class_4938}["loins" ]{.class_4939}[were NOT Leah's womb (see Gen. 29:34). The Christian scholars should have gone to bed an hour ago. Tuck the little ones in; children should be seen, not heard.]{.class_4938}

[Verses 17 and 18 are self explanatory. David could have no way of knowing God's ]{.class_4938}["thoughts" ]{.class_4939}[(Isa. 55:8--9) apart from direct revelation in written or spoken communications (2 Sam. 7:4--10). It is a little too hard to explain the numerous thoughts of God being more than "the sand of the sea," so the commentators content themselves with the quality of God's thoughts toward the New Testament Christian (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, pp. 395--396, which is quoted by Spurgeon). David could be recalling the past and trying to number the many times that God showed His mind to him by dealing with him in certain ways. But even these could be numbered. Literally, it means that no man could count the thoughts that God thinks (Isa. 55:8--9; Rom. 11:34) even if they are ]{.class_4938}["precious" ]{.class_4939}[to him.]{.class_4938}

"[When I awake, I am still with thee" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 18). Of course David is; he couldn't be anywhere else, and neither could anyone else (vs. 7). The meaning must be that he is ]{.class_4938}["with" ]{.class_4939}[God in the sense that he is still thinking about Him, and his mind is still on God's ]{.class_4938}["thoughts."]{.class_4939}

139:19 "Surely thou wilt slay the wicked, O God: depart from me therefore, ye bloody men.

20 For they speak against thee wickedly, and thine enemies take thy name in vain.

21 Do not I hate them, O LORD, that hate thee? and am not I grieved with those that rise up against thee?

22 I hate them with perfect hatred: I count them mine enemies.

23 Search me, O God, and know my heart: try me, and know my thoughts:

24 And see if there be any wicked way in me, and lead me in the way everlasting."

Verses 19 and 20 are clear. There are direct Tribulation references in both of them, as we have seen from Psalm 109 and 101:7. Cursing is taking God's name "in vain" (vs. 20). Note again, that a spiritual man, who thinks like a Christian should, is a good HATER (vs. 21). Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown say it right: "One cannot at once love the Lord and love His enemies." You can love the unsaved enough to die for them (Rom. 9:3), but this love is Divine love (Rom. 5:5) that is aimed at their SOULS. While loving them this much, you are to avoid their company, their friends, their fellowship, their activities, their goals, their aims, their standards, their beliefs, and their religions, like you would a two headed cobra (2 Cor. 6:14; 1 Cor. 5:9--11).

[This is what is called "hate literature" out in the world. Since the world never gets ]{.class_4938}[anything ]{.class_4937}[right (especially spiritual truths that deal with moral issues), it turns out that those who talk the most about love, ]{.class_4938}[hate the deepest, ]{.class_4937}[and those who complain about "hate literature" and "hate preaching" are those that spend their lifetimes trying to stop free speech and censor Biblical truths.]{.class_4938}

[Two examples will suffice. The two religious systems, on this earth, that have ]{.class_4938}[murdered ]{.class_4937}[more people in cold blood than any two religious, philosophical, economic, or political systems on the face of this earth are Catholicism (A.D. 325 to A.D. 1900) and Communism (A.D. 1900 to A.D. 2000). Neither religion believes that shed blood is enough to prepare a sinner to meet God, so ]{.class_4938}[both of them shed blood by the tub full. ]{.class_4937}[Both religions (Communism and Catholicism) demand their rights, while confessing that they are both ]{.class_4938}[totalitarian systems, ]{.class_4937}[systems that deny anyone any "rights" but ]{.class_4938}[themselves. ]{.class_4937}[Both systems profess to be helping out their fellowman and making the world "a better place to live in," while slaughtering more than 40,000,000 of its inhabitants. ]{.class_4938}[They ]{.class_4937}[are the people in America who accuse Bible believing preachers of preaching "hate" and spreading "hate literature." The hypocrites didn't all die out with Judas.]{.class_4938}

"[Perfect hatred" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 22) is hatred that is:]{.class_4938}

1\. Based on what God says a man should hate.

2\. Based on the things that God hates (Prov. 6:16--19, for example).

3\. Without personal resentment or personal insults or injury.

4\. Objective and complete, without any intention of coming to terms with the thing hated.

"[Search me, O God" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 23). He will. He searches all hearts (Jer. 17:10). ]{.class_4938}["Try me, and know my thoughts\...."]{.class_4939}[ He will and He ]{.class_4938}[does ]{.class_4937}[(Ps. 11:4; 1 Cor. 3:13). David is merely submitting to it voluntarily, as in Psalm 26:2. He honestly wants to know himself, and the best way to self knowledge is not to follow some blockheaded nincompoop who says "To thine own self be true, and thou canst not be false to any man." Lee Oswald was true to himself, and so were Jim Jones and Adolph Hitler. If you are true to ]{.class_4938}[yourself,]{.class_4937}[ you will eventually give in to every fleshly craving that pops up from your fleshly nature. The way to ]{.class_4938}[self-knowledge]{.class_4937}[ is for God to put you through the ringer, so you can see what you actually would do in a given set of circumstances (Heb. 5:14). At this point, David is pretty sure that his thoughts (vs. 23) are right, so he doesn't mind the "house to house search." He certainly would not have prayed this prayer from the time that Bathsheba took her bath to the time that Nathan socked it to him."]{.class_4938}

[A ]{.class_4938}["wicked way" ]{.class_4939}[WAS found in David, and more than one, at that; but God led him in the way of everlasting life just the same, so the prayer was answered. If every unsaved sinner in the world would sincerely pray verses 23 and 24, there would be ]{.class_4938}[complete, international, world wide conversion of every sinner on the face of this earth. ]{.class_4937}[If a man calls on God (Rom. 10: 13) for searching and testing, and asks God to get rid of his wicked ways and show him everlasting life (Cornelius, Acts 10: 1 6), the Lord does it (Rom. 2:7).]{.class_4938}

[Would to God I could get every adult man in Pensacola (say from twenty to eighty years of age) to pray ]{.class_4938}[that ]{.class_4937}[prayer!]{.class_4938}

PSALM 140

140:1 "Deliver me, O LORD, from the evil man: preserve me from the violent man;

2 Which imagine mischiefs in their heart; continually are they gathered together for war.

3 They have sharpened their tongues like a serpent; adders' poison is under their lips. Selah.

4 Keep me, O LORD, from the hands of the wicked; preserve me from the violent man; who have purposed to overthrow my goings.

5 The proud have hid a snare for me, and cords; they have spread a net by the wayside; they have set gins for me. Selah."

6 I said unto the LORD, Thou art my God: hear the voice of my supplications, O LORD.

[Here, we encounter a familiar face from Psalm 89:48, ]{.class_4938}["Selah." ]{.class_4939}[We know what this means, for wherever it occurred before, there was a prophetic reference to the Second Advent within two verses of it. Once again, that old singular plural shows up (see Ps. 14:1), indicating the poly monster of Job 40:15: ]{.class_4938}["Behemoth." ]{.class_4939}[The ]{.class_4938}["evil man" ]{.class_4939}[and the ]{.class_4938}["violent man" ]{.class_4939}[of the first verse are plainly the Antichrist. He is ]{.class_4938}["they"]{.class_4939}[ in verses 2 and 3. God returns to the singular ]{.class_4938}["the wicked\...the violent man" ]{.class_4939}[in verse 4, and then resorts to ]{.class_4938}["they"]{.class_4939}[ again in the next verse.]{.class_4938}

Now, the best way to learn the Book is "sola scriptura," the Scriptures alone. Compare the following verses and see exactly what David is getting into doctrinally.

[1. ]{.class_4938}["The violent man" ]{.class_4939}[of the text (vss. 1, 4, 11) imagines mischief (see Ps. 7:14, 16; 10:7; and 36:4). There is no doubt about his identity at all.]{.class_4938}

[2. ]{.class_4938}["The violent man" ]{.class_4939}[is out to get poor folks that are needy (vs. 12). Compare this with Psalms 9:18; 10:2, 8--10, 14; 12:5, and 37:14. There can be no doubt, whatsoever, about who the violent man is.]{.class_4938}

[3. ]{.class_4938}["The violent man"]{.class_4939}[ is connected with ]{.class_4938}["serpents"]{.class_4939}[ and ]{.class_4938}["adders"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 3). 140:7 "O God the Lord, the strength of my salvation, thou hast covered my head in the day of battle.

PSALM 141

141:1 "LORD, I cry unto thee: make haste unto me; give ear unto my voice, when I cry unto thee.

2 Let my prayer be set forth before thee as incense; and the lifting up of my hands as the evening sacrifice.

3 Set a watch, O LORD, before my mouth; keep the door of my lips.

4 Incline not my heart to any evil thing, to practise wicked works with men that work iniquity: and let me not eat of their dainties.

5 Let the righteous smite me; it shall be a kindness: and let him reprove me; it shall be an excellent oil, which shall not break my head: for yet my prayer also shall be in their calamities.

6 When their judges are overthrown in stony places, they shall hear my words; for they are sweet."

Most of the verses of this Psalm are practical, but on occasion, prophecy peeks through them, always in such a way as to be completely indiscernible to the Alexandrian Cult of Bible correcting Conservatives.

[Note the connection between the apparently innocent ]{.class_4938}["let me not eat of their dainties" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 4) and Proverbs 23:1--3; Daniel 11:27; and Isaiah 28:15. The Alexandrian Cult is absolutely incapable of this kind of Scripture with Scripture study, the main reason being that none of them think they are reading the ]{.class_4938}[Scriptures ]{.class_4937}[when they sit down to correct the ]{.class_4938}[Authorized Version.]{.class_4937}[ ]{.class_4938}["Their judges" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 6) goes with Psalm 82:2; Revelation 17:12; Daniel 2:41--43; and the last two verses in this Psalm match the previous one (Ps. 140:5, 8, and 11). Those verses were marked TWICE with ]{.class_4938}["Selah" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 5 and 8); but all of this is obscure to people like Hutson, Hudson, Pickering, Waite, Afman, Price, Walker, Panosian, Bob Jones III, Wisdom, Neal, Farstad, Hodges, Bob Jones IV, MacArthur, Swindoll, Horton, Hindson, Hymers, and Kutilek as a German infantry field manual would be to Mike Tyson or Joe Montana.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 1 is self explanatory. He needs help and needs it in a hurry (see Ps. 31:2 and 69:17). Incense is a type of prayer (vs. 2), according to Luke 1:9--11 and Revelation 8:3, which see. Prayer is done with the hands lifted up toward heaven (1 Kings 8:22), even in the New Testament (see 1 Tim. 2:8). Doctrinally, the Psalmist must be very careful what he says. No scholar could find out why. They couldn't find Proverbs 23:1--6, and there, right in the context of eating the ]{.class_4938}["dainties," ]{.class_4939}[is the warning (Prov. 23:8--9). To make sure the blind, bungling, stupid ]{.class_4938}[King James Bible ]{.class_4937}[correctors---who believe in "Dead Duck Otherism"---can't locate the passage, the Holy Spirit led the ]{.class_4938}[AV ]{.class_4937}[translators to write ]{.class_4938}[the same word in both places. ]{.class_4937}[(This is what blows an Alexandrian's mind: matching English words that show how to interpret a context without resorting to any Greek or Hebrew words, any Greek or Hebrew manuscripts, or any Hebrew or Greek scholars. That is what puts the Alexandrian Cult into orbit.)]{.class_4938}

[Verse 3. A "doorkeeper" is needed so that nothing wrong goes into, or out of, the mouth. He requires a password that must be acceptable to the Owner of the house (1 Cor. 6:19--20). [Verse 4 is self explanatory. The heart can be inclined toward something before it goes down the "incline." The prayer is similar to the doctrinal prayer of Jews in the Tribulation who need their bread daily (Rev. 12:14): ]{.class_4938}["Lead us not into temptation"]{.class_4939}[ (Matt. 6:13).]{.class_4938}

[Judges 9:3 shows the inclination. The prayer, then, is for God to begin to move the heart in the right direction ]{.class_4938}[before ]{.class_4937}[any decisions have to be made, and not give the Devil permission to tilt the inclinations in his favor.]{.class_4938}

"[Let the righteous smite me" ]{.class_4939}[Joseph Alexander says that "the verse is so obscure as to be unintelligible." He means for people like ]{.class_4938}[himself. ]{.class_4937}[John Owens corrects the Holy Bible by saying it should read "Let the righteous smite me friendly, or kindly." 141:7 "Our bones are scattered at the grave's mouth, as when one cutteth and cleaveth wood upon the earth.

8 But mine eyes are unto thee, O GOD the Lord: in thee is my trust; leave not my soul destitute.

9 Keep me from the snares which they have laid for me, and the gins of the workers of iniquity.

"Breaketh up"]{.class_4938}[ is not ]{.class_4937}[a translation of either word. By going to Arabic, instead of Hebrew, you can justify translating "baqa" as "ploughing," but that still doesn't solve your problem. ]{.class_4938}[Palach ]{.class_4937}[is NOT a reference to ]{.class_4938}[plowing ]{.class_4937}[in Hebrew or Arabic. [All of the Alexandrians in the Scholar's Union missed all the verses---as usual. Keep the ]{.class_4938}[AV ]{.class_4937}[text and throw the ]{.class_4938}[NKJV ]{.class_4937}[out the window; followed by "Dead Duck Otherism" (Bob Jones University's hang up): the ]{.class_4938}[RV, RSV, NRSV, ASV, NASV, NIV, TEV, TLB, ]{.class_4937}[Nestle, Aland, Metzger, Kittel, Delitzsch, Gesenius, and the ]{.class_4938}[International Standard Bible Encyclopedia. ]{.class_4937}[One dead duck is just as good as another.]{.class_4938}

[The close of the Psalm is milky enough not to give the Alexandrians gas on the stomach. Verse 8 is self explanatory, and verses 9 and 10 are a prayer for Psalm 140:9, 11 to take place. See Psalm 9:16 and comments. The ]{.class_4938}["gins" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 9 (see Ps. 140:5) are not gin rickeys or Tom Collins cocktails made with gin. Gin, technically, is any mechanical device, such as "Whitney's cotton gin." PSALM 142

142:1 "I cried unto the LORD with my voice; with my voice unto the LORD did I make my supplication.

2 I poured out my complaint before him; I shewed before him my trouble.

3 When my spirit was overwhelmed within me, then thou knewest my path. In the way wherein I walked have they privily laid a snare for me.

4 I looked on my right hand, and beheld, but there was no man that would know me: refuge failed me; no man cared for my soul.

5 I cried unto thee, O LORD: I said, Thou art my refuge and my portion in the land of the living.

6 Attend unto my cry; for I am brought very low: deliver me from my persecutors; for they are stronger than I.

7 Bring my soul out of prison, that I may praise thy name: the righteous shall compass me about; for thou shalt deal bountifully with me."

The Psalm has three applications:

1\. A sinner in desperate trouble who is pleading for help and deliverance.

2\. An Old Testament saint in Abraham's bosom, waiting for Christ, or a Tribulation saint in Revelation 6:9--11 waiting for the resurrection at the end of the Tribulation (see Job 42:13 and comments).

3\. Christ Himself.

For the skim milk, read Yates, Kroll, Lange, Clarke, Henry, Dummelow, Spurgeon, Christopher Wordworth, A. S. Aglen, John Newton, E. D. Pressense, Joseph Alexander, Franz Delitzsch, Joseph Caryle, and Thomas Boston.

[Verse 1 is David in personal distress, or any saint in personal distress, or Christ as in Psalm 6:6--10. Verses 2 and 3 are more of the same. The ]{.class_4938}["snare" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 3 is the Tribulation reference, as given in Psalm 38:12, 64:5, and 141:9. Note the ]{.class_4938}["snares of death" ]{.class_4939}[in Christ's case (Ps. 18:5). Verses 5 and 6 are directly applicable to someone in the Tribulation, and verse 7 is directly applicable to the Tribulation saints who are in prison in Revelation 6:9--10.]{.class_4938}

Spiritual application can be made throughout the Psalm. There are twelve possessives in the Psalm.

1\. My voice.

2\. My supplication.

3\. My complaint.

4\. My trouble.

5\. My spirit.

6\. My path.

7\. My right hand.

8\. My soul.

9\. My refuge.

10\. My portion.

11\. My cry.

12\. My persecutors.

Any point could be developed into a three-point message, making thirty six messages in all.

[He is crying to the Lord, so his prayer life has not died out. He is sending up "smoke signals" (see Luke 1:9--10). A fruit-bearing Christian ]{.class_4938}[prays]{.class_4937}[.]{.class_4938}

This means that "where there is smoke, there is fire." In Florida, we burn smudge pots to keep the frost off the orange crop. I said, "to keep the frost off of the fruit."

[Notice that in the right]{.class_4938}[ "way"]{.class_4939}[ or the right ]{.class_4938}["path" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 3), you can run into a ]{.class_4938}["snare."]{.class_4939}

[Verse 4 is a great sermon coming from the lips of an unsaved man. There are millions in Africa, Europe, China, and the United States who could make this complaint in the spiritual sense. Christians do NOT care for them enough to tell them of their true condition and the ONLY remedy for that condition. The Christians are playing it safe by "coping," "sharing," "adjusting," and "relating," being just as careful as they can be that no one finds out what they really believe about the ]{.class_4938}[destiny of ]{.class_4937}[unregenerate mankind. Some of the braver ones (about as brave as a draft dodger) will go so far as to say that if "we don't accept Christ as our Saviour, the wages of sin is eternal separation from God." Which doesn't mean anything except that the speaker is ]{.class_4938}[lost ]{.class_4937}[by his use of the ]{.class_4938}[first person plural; ]{.class_4937}[and in the "separation" there is no fire, pit, furnace, darkness, everlasting shame, reproach, eternal torment, weeping, wailing, or gnashing of teeth. ]{.class_4938}[Modern Fundamentalism is a joke. ]{.class_4937}[It is a kiddy's game for middle-class sissies who want to get along with their wives and her friends. Some day the bums from the lower ends of New York, Los Angeles, Miami, and St. Louis will rise up and confront the Body of Christ with ]{.class_4938}["no man cared for my soul," ]{.class_4939}[and along with them, the billions of Africans and Asiatics who were left to die in darkness.]{.class_4938}

[Verses 5 and 6 are self explanatory. ]{.class_4938}["The righteous shall compass me about" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 7) is a direct reference to Jesus Christ, as already stated in Psalm 7:7; 22:22; 35:18; 107:32, etc. Kroll can't find any of]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4937}[the references, any of the doctrinal meanings, any of the revelations, or any of the subject matter. But then neither could Dummelow, Yates, Motyer, or the men they sat under.]{.class_4938}

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ4486.xhtml}

PSALM 143

143:1 "Hear my prayer, O LORD, give ear to my supplications: in thy faithfulness answer me, and in thy righteousness.

2 And enter not into judgment with thy servant: for in thy sight shall no man living be justified.

3 For the enemy hath persecuted my soul; he hath smitten my life down to the ground; he hath made me to dwell in darkness, as those that have been long dead.

4 Therefore is my spirit overwhelmed within me; my heart within me is desolate.

5 I remember the days of old; I meditate on all thy works; I muse on the work of thy hands.

6 I stretch forth my hands unto thee: my soul thirsteth after thee, as a thirsty land. Selah."

The Psalm is in the same refrain as the preceding one. The righteousness and faithfulness of verse 1 are not in the sense of Romans 10:1--10, but rather in the sense of Job 8:6 and Genesis 18:25. He wants God to fulfill His promise because God is faithful, and he is claiming that, at this time, God would be doing right to deliver him. No man could be justified in God's sight if God entered into judgment with that man (vs. 2). Observe, however, that Abraham was justified in God's sight when he offered up his only begotten son (James 2:21), and observe further that God justified Job on the grounds of Job's righteousness (Job 42:7; Ezek. 14:14), after telling him that his own righteousness could not justify him.

[These are the kinds of things that put the Scholar's Union into orbit. They cannot see the difference between Old Testament and New Testament salvation, so verses like Romans 4:3 just send them further into the exosphere than they would have gone under their own power. Every man on this earth who believed in Jesus Christ was justified in no uncertain terms (Rom. 3:24, 28; 5:1, 9; 8:30). The problem is verses like Romans 2:13 and Job 13:18. No man in either Testament is going to be justified if he is counting on his righteousness ]{.class_4938}[alone ]{.class_4937}[to justify him; that was Job's problem (see comments in that commentary), but God declared Israel ]{.class_4938}[just ]{.class_4937}[and did not impute sin to her (Num. 23:21; Rom. 4:8) when she was ]{.class_4938}[unjust ]{.class_4937}[(see Exod. 32:9--10).]{.class_4938}

[Observe that in the New Testament, none of Abraham's sins are mentioned; nor are the sins of Isaac, Jacob, Joseph, Noah, David, Samson, Rebekah, Rachel, Sarah, or Moses. But they are mentioned in the Old Testament. Sin IS imputed to Achan, Korah, Dathan, Abiram, Saul, Ahab, Manasseh, and a score of others; and it costs Joab, Ahab, Nadab, Abihu, Uzzah, Onan, and Pharaoh their lives. You never know in the Old Testament to whom God is going to impute sin, but you DO know in the New Testament. New Testament salvation and Old Testament salvation are NOT the same. Abraham (before the Law) is given as an example of imputation (Rom. 4:3), but only David, ]{.class_4938}[under the law ]{.class_4937}[(Rom. 4:6), is given as an example. The rest of them "get it in the neck."]{.class_4938}

[Verse 3 is ]{.class_4938}[literally ]{.class_4937}[Messianic, exactly as in Psalm 88:6--12; see especially verse 6. Verses 4 and 5 could apply to any saint in deep distress and depression.]{.class_4938}

[Again, our old friend ]{.class_4938}["Selah" ]{.class_4939}[shows up at verse 6, reminding us that verses 1--6 have a direct ]{.class_4938}[doctrinal ]{.class_4937}[application to the Jews in the Tribulation, who are being annihilated by the Son of Perdition. ]{.class_4938}["The days of old" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 5) are Israel's days, as reviewed in Psalm 78 and 136. The persecuted saint is thirsty ]{.class_4938}[literally ]{.class_4937}[as well as ]{.class_4938}[spiritually ]{.class_4937}[(vs. 6), because he is back in the wilderness (Hos. 2:3) as at the beginning, and neither water nor bread is available (see Lam. 5:9).]{.class_4938}

"[He allowed his soul to thirst after God" is all that Kroll can get out of the verse. Kroll, in 1980, was still hung up back in 1780 with John Trapp, Sir Richard Baxter, Thomas Pierson (1580!!), Archibald Symson, Henry Drummon, and Spurgeon.]{.class_4938}

[The Jews are in a ]{.class_4938}["thirsty land" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 6) when this prayer is made (Ps. 78:15). They will have to get water supernaturally, as they got it in Exodus and Numbers. Look at Isaiah 41:17 and 49:10. You had better look, because the faculties and staffs of Louisville Theological Seminary, Dallas Theological Seminary, Denver Theological Seminary, Grace Theological Seminary, and New Orleans Theological Seminary cannot look. God put their eyes out many years ago.]{.class_4938}

143:7 "Hear me speedily, O LORD: my spirit faileth: hide not thy face from me, lest I be like unto them that go down into the pit.

8 Cause me to hear thy lovingkindness in the morning; for in thee do I trust: cause me to know the way wherein I should walk; for I lift up my soul unto thee.

9 Deliver me, O LORD, from mine enemies: I flee unto thee to hide me.

10 Teach me to do thy will; for thou art my God: thy spirit is good; lead me into the land of uprightness.

11 Quicken me, O LORD, for thy name's sake: for thy righteousness' sake bring my soul out of trouble.

12 And of thy mercy cut off mine enemies, and destroy all them that afflict my soul: for I am thy servant."

[Neatly disguised as the spiritual exercises of a saint, who wants relief in the spiritual sense, is the ]{.class_4938}[doctrinal ]{.class_4937}[prayer of a Tribulation saint. All the commentators, expositors, exegetes, revisors, commentators, teachers, and scholars forgot that the ]{.class_4938}["soul"]{.class_4939}[ of verses 6, 8, and 12---as the ]{.class_4938}["soul"]{.class_4939}[ of Psalm 142:4---was a man's physical life, in the Old Testament, as well as his soul. (See the comments under Gen. 2:7 in ]{.class_4938}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis, ]{.class_4937}[written more than twenty years ago.) In the Old Testament, the soul was stuck to the body, making them nearly ]{.class_4938}[synonymous ]{.class_4937}[on occasion (see Gen. 19:20 and Ezek. 18:4--15, specifically). This relationship was so close that it caused Paul to liken the soul to a wife who was one flesh with her husband, because she was joined to him (Rom. 7:2), and the husband was the fleshy body (Rom. 7:4--5). The relationship was so close it caused Pastor Russell and Judge Rutherford to set up The Watchtower Society for the propagation of an outrageous lie: that the body was the soul, and therefore that when a man's body went into the dirt, his soul went into the dirt with him.]{.class_4938}

[Observe the Tribulation theme in the Psalm after ]{.class_4938}["Selah" ]{.class_4939}[showed up:]{.class_4938}

[1. ]{.class_4938}["Go down into the pit" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 7). This is a pit, as in P I T. See Revelation 9:3, 20:1--2, and especially Isaiah 14:15; 24:17, 18, and 22. (Also Ps. 7:15 and 9:15).]{.class_4938}

[2. ]{.class_4938}["Thy lovingkindness in the morning" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 8). This is the "morning" of 2 Samuel 23:4; Matthew 13:43; Psalm 30:5; and 49:14. All of the commentators miss all the references. (What else could you expect from "plenary, verbally inspired" originals?)]{.class_4938}

[3. ]{.class_4938}["Into the land of uprightness" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 10). What land would this be? Palestine? Iraq? Greece? Italy? England? New Jerusalem? Alaska? Germany? Australia? North Dakota? Andrew Bonar makes it ]{.class_4938}[New Jerusalem, ]{.class_4937}[so Kroll, speaking for "Bible believing Baptists" (!!), one hundred years later, agrees that is the reference. It is NOT the reference. They are both wrong. God, in ]{.class_4938}["mercy"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 12), does NOT cut off all of your enemies; God does NOT destroy all them that afflict your soul, and you have already been quickened (vs. 11). The ]{.class_4938}["land of uprightness" ]{.class_4939}[has to be Palestine, during the Millennium (Isa. 26:1), or the new earth after Revelation 20. It is Israel that is ]{.class_4938}["the]{.class_4939}[ ]{.class_4938}[land of promise" ]{.class_4939}[in the New Testament (Heb. 11:9). It is a righteous land when the Messiah sits on Mt. Zion on the throne of David. ]{.class_4938}[There is no reference to New Jerusalem within five verses either ]{.class_4937}[way.]{.class_4938}

[The ]{.class_4938}["servant" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 12,]{.class_4938}[ doctrinally, ]{.class_4937}[is one of the servants mentioned in Revelation 6:9--11. The ]{.class_4938}["trouble" ]{.class_4939}[he is in (vs. 11) is ]{.class_4938}["the time of Jacob's trouble" ]{.class_4939}[(Jer. 30:7). It is in ]{.class_4938}["the days of Lot"]{.class_4939}[ (Luke 17:28) that Lot says his]{.class_4938}[ "soul"]{.class_4939}[ (Gen. 19:20) is his physical life.]{.class_4938}

[5. The Psalmist is running (vs. 9), and he is running to hide from his enemies. Now go back and read Psalms 11:1, 27:5, and 84:2--3 and then tell me that this is merely the spiritual exercises of a ]{.class_4938}["soul"]{.class_4939}[ trying to get into fellowship with God!]{.class_4938}

[Of course, we can make ]{.class_4938}[spiritual ]{.class_4937}[applications. Who couldn't? No Christian had to go to high school to make spiritual application, but these Alexandrian Nit Wits go to school ten to fifteen years beyond a Bachelor's degree, and then, being unable to understand what is going on in the Scripture, they bull and bluff their way through with pious spiritual applications as a replacement for sound doctrine. "Can the education," if that is all you are going to do; save the tuition.]{.class_4938}

There are twelve petitions in the section. Notice this unusual series of twelves, as they occur here and in Psalm 142, 146, 147, and 150.

1\. Hear me.

[2. ]{.class_4938}["Hear me speedily."]{.class_4939}

3\. Don't hide your face from me.

[4. ]{.class_4938}["Cause me to hear thy lovingkindness."]{.class_4939}

5\. Cause me to know how I should walk.

[6. ]{.class_4938}["Deliver me."]{.class_4939}

[7. ]{.class_4938}["Teach me to do thy will."]{.class_4939}

[8. ]{.class_4938}["Lead me into the land of uprightness."]{.class_4939}

[9. ]{.class_4938}["Quicken me."]{.class_4939}

[10. ]{.class_4938}["Bring my soul out of trouble."]{.class_4939}

[11. ]{.class_4938}["Cut off mine enemies."]{.class_4939}

12\. Destroy them that afflict me.

Each petition could make a three to five point sermon. The Old Testament expressions in verses 3, 4, 9, 11, and 12 can be spiritualized as follows:

1\. Stay in fellowship with me, so that I can maintain a sense of your presence.

2\. Cause me to believe that you have been kind to me and do love me, by answering a request as evidence.

[3. ]{.class_4938}["Quicken me" ]{.class_4939}[in the sense of letting me live according to ]{.class_4938}["the new man" ]{.class_4939}[(see Eph. 4:24), and not according to the dead, Adamic ]{.class_4938}["old man" ]{.class_4939}[(see Eph. 4:22).]{.class_4938}

4\. Cut off the influence of the devil, the world (TV, radio, magazines, newspapers), and the flesh (bad companions, backslidden relatives, my own fleshy nature).

[5. Destroy ]{.class_4938}["fleshly lusts, which war against the soul" ]{.class_4939}[(1 Pet. 2:11; 2 Cor. 10:3).]{.class_4938}

[See how easy it is? Nothing to it. Such exposition requires no study, no searching the Scripture, no meditating, or ]{.class_4938}["labour in the word" ]{.class_4939}[(1 Tim. 5:17). This is not to say that such expositions are useless or unimportant, but in view of the fact that both Testaments stated them a ]{.class_4938}[dozen times, ]{.class_4937}[what is the point in obliterating ]{.class_4938}[other truths ]{.class_4937}[by repeating these ]{.class_4938}[known truths ]{.class_4937}[over again? Isn't that spiritual FRAUD? If you collated all of Spurgeon's sermons and his ]{.class_4938}[Treasury,]{.class_4937}[ you would find that all he did was repeat about ]{.class_4938}[thirty basic New Testament truths over and over again, because he couldn't find any further truth. ]{.class_4937}[It was not simply that he ]{.class_4938}[thought ]{.class_4937}[the thirty were so fundamental they needed to be repeated constantly (at the expense of other truths), nor that nothing else in the entire Book was worthy of being preached. It was simply that, having taken a postmillennial, allegorizing view of the Old Testament (as Origen, Augustine, Jerome, Hort, and the popes), ]{.class_4938}[more than three-fourths of the Bible was a closed Book to him,]{.class_4937}[ so he filled in the blanks from his vast store of oft repeated "fundamentals," which had ]{.class_4938}[absolutely nothing to do with the Scripture he was expounding. ]{.class_4937}[This is a well known Alexandrian gimmick.]{.class_4938}

[]{#Uxd5JyNYSWCtgFM2T9UhJQ4539.xhtml}

PSALM 144

144:1 "Blessed be the LORD my strength, which teacheth my hands to war, and my fingers to fight:

2 My goodness, and my fortress; my high tower, and my deliverer; my shield, and he in whom I trust; who subdueth my people under me.

3 LORD, what is man, that thou takest knowledge of him! or the son of man, that thou makest account of him!

4 Man is like to vanity: his days are as a shadow that passeth away.

5 Bow thy heavens, O LORD, and come down: touch the mountains, and they shall smoke.

6 Cast forth lightning, and scatter them: shoot out thine arrows, and destroy them.

7 Send thine hand from above; rid me, and deliver me out of great waters, from the hand of strange children;

8 Whose mouth speaketh vanity, and their right hand is a right hand of falsehood."

[The whole Psalm describes the Tribulation (vss. 1--8) and the Millennium (vss. 9--15). Liberty University cannot make one correct application throughout the entire Psalm, and when verse 5 arrives, you get a pitiful lame exegesis resurrected from Spurgeon's ]{.class_4938}[Treasury]{.class_4937}[. It is about as "Christian" as Mohammed and about as "Biblical" as Bob Jones IV. We are told that the prayer for God to ]{.class_4938}["Bow thy heavens, O LORD, and come down" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 5) was a reference to the Incarnation (Luke 1 and 2). This ridiculous nonsense came from Jonathan Edwards, but because he was a "godly" man and "greatly used of God," you are to accept this ludicrous bit of rhetoric as the truth. Verses 5--7 have no more been fulfilled at any time before 2000 than Isaiah 2 or 11. Poor, old, blind, stumbling, bungling Kroll (Liberty University) says that the ]{.class_4938}["great waters" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 7 are a "common metaphor in the Psalms for danger or peril." Well, if you studied under Philo, Clement, or Origen at Alexandria, yes. Otherwise, nuts.]{.class_4938}

Observe that verses 1 and 2 are Psalm 18:2, 34; 2 Samuel 22:3 and 35 almost verbatim. Look at those passages, and tell me that the subject is not the Second Advent of Jesus Christ. Look at verses 5--7 in the passage, and then look at 2 Samuel 22:15--17 and Psalm 18:9, 11, 13--14. Now then, tell me how ANY man, saved or lost, Catholic or atheist, Liberal or Conservative, could get through that without seeing God Himself coming to earth in rage, wrath, and power and delivering Israel by wiping out their enemies.

[Who are the people that will be subdued under the Psalmist (vs. 2)? Who would you guess after reading Isaiah 63:1--6; Psalm 110; and Joel 2--3? When Kroll sees ]{.class_4938}["who subdueth my people under me" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 2), he goes completely blank and has a clinical attack of forgetfulness. He ignores all six verses and puts the whole passage back to 1100 B.C. The ]{.class_4938}["strange children" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 7 are defined in Isaiah 57:4; Jude 12; 2 Peter 2; and Deuteronomy 32:5. Their ]{.class_4938}["right hand" ]{.class_4939}[is Satan (vs. 8) incarnate (look at Ps. 109:6). God's ]{.class_4938}["right hand" ]{.class_4939}[is Jesus Christ (Psa. 17:7, 18:35, 20:6, 21:8).]{.class_4938}

[What are they doing at Lynchburg? Nothing. You couldn't find an elder who labored ]{.class_4938}["in the word and doctrine" ]{.class_4939}[(1 Tim. 5:17) at Liberty University with a laser beam, a microscope, a telescope, a magnifying glass, and a 2,000 watt floodlight.]{.class_4938}

Verse 3 has been commented on under Psalm 8:4. Even though the reference is to mankind in general from Adam, one cannot help but notice the direct reference to the Second Advent, as interpreted by the Holy Spirit in the New Testament (Heb. 2:6--7). That is, one cannot help notice it unless one has been raised on Dummelow, Baethgen, Kroll, Perowne, Calvin, Bonar, Hitzig, Delitzsch, Briggs, Davidson, and company.

"[His days are as a shadow" ]{.class_4939}[(see Ps. 102:11 and comments). The great discourse on humanity is Ecclesiastes 1--10. Strangely, the word ]{.class_4938}["Abel"]{.class_4939}[ (Gen. 4:2) means "transitory" or "passing," a word very much like the Old Testament ]{.class_4938}["vanity,"]{.class_4939}[ (Ecc. 1:2), which means "like the wind." Here today, gone tomorrow; empty, worthless, temporary. Most of the Hebrew texts say "Adam is like Abel." Adam lived more than nine hundred years, but there was a sense in which his life was as transitory as Abel's, who was cut off before his time. The prayer is for the Second Advent to stop man because man is worthless; ]{.class_4938}[he is a vapor.]{.class_4937}

144:9 "I will sing a new song unto thee, O God: upon a psaltery and an instrument of ten strings will I sing praises unto thee.

10 It is he that giveth salvation unto kings: who delivereth David his servant from the hurtful sword.

11 Rid me, and deliver me from the hand of strange children, whose mouth speaketh vanity, and their right hand is a right hand of falsehood:

12 That our sons may be as plants grown up in their youth; that our daughters may be as corner stones, polished after the similitude of a palace:

13 That our garners may be full, affording all manner of store: that our sheep may bring forth thousands and ten thousands in our streets.

14 That our oxen may be strong to labour; that there be no breaking in, nor going out; that there be no complaining in our streets.

15 Happy is that people, that is in such a case: yea, happy is that people, whose God is the LORD."

[To mess up the scholars, the Holy Spirit sticks in verse 11, which is someone still in the Tribulation (as vs. 8), but all around it (vss. 9--10, 12--15) are verses that deal with the Millennium. The ]{.class_4938}["new song" ]{.class_4939}[has been commented on under Psalm 33:3 and 40:3. In verse 10, David has been delivered from the sword of Saul (1 Sam. 19:11), the sword of Goliath (1 Sam. 17:51), the sword of the Philistines (2 Sam. 21:15), and the sword of the Syrians (2 Sam. 8:5). ]{.class_4938}["The hurtful sword" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 10), historically, could have been the last one aimed at him that nearly killed him (2 Sam. 21:16). The ]{.class_4938}["salvation" ]{.class_4939}[in the verse is not spiritual salvation but the preservation of a king's life in battle (see 2 Chron. 35:20--23). But the verse goes beyond this, into God's ]{.class_4938}["servant"]{.class_4939}[ (see Isa. 52:13) being delivered from Satan (see Job 5:15, 20; 19:29; and especially Ps. 17:13).]{.class_4938}

"[That our sons\...our daughters\...our garners\...our oxen\...our streets\...yea, happy is that people whose God is the LORD" ]{.class_4939}[(vss. 12--15). One can make all kinds of beautiful sermons from the passage, but the context is Israel, and every ]{.class_4938}["our" ]{.class_4939}[in the passage is a reference to Jews in the Millennium. The Antichrist and his people are in verse 11. Those who follow (vss. 12--14, etc.) have been delivered from him, as ]{.class_4938}["David" ]{.class_4939}[was delivered in verse 10. Observe that Job 42 defines the context, for Job is the greatest type of the Great Tribulation of any book in the entire Bible, followed by Daniel and Jeremiah. In Job 42, the ]{.class_4938}["captivity" ]{.class_4939}[of Job is turned (vs. 10), and then the subject matter of Psalm 144 follows:]{.class_4938}

[1. Verse 12, ]{.class_4938}["sons" ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["daughters" ]{.class_4939}[(see Job 42:13).]{.class_4938}

[2. Verse 13,]{.class_4938}[ "sheep"]{.class_4939}[ and ]{.class_4938}["oxen"]{.class_4939}[ (see Job 42:12).]{.class_4938}

3\. Verses 14 and 15, happiness and contentment (see Job 42:10, 16).

[All of the commentators missed all the references, ]{.class_4938}[as usual. ]{.class_4937}[No one at Liberty University in Lynchburg had one iota, or isotope, of light on the passage that wasn't already in clear view in 1700. Ignorance of the Scriptures seems to be the outstanding achievement of higher Christian education in the latter part of the twentieth century.]{.class_4938}

[To give you some idea of the depraved and godless mess you can get into by listening to these Bible rejecting milksops, try this: Paul had no sons and daughters, although his God was certainly ]{.class_4938}["the LORD" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 15). He had no ]{.class_4938}["garners" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 13) to put anything into. He didn't own a leg of mutton, let alone ten thousand sheep (vs. 13), and he had no streets to call his own (]{.class_4938}["our streets," ]{.class_4939}[vs. 14) because he had ]{.class_4938}["no continuing city" ]{.class_4939}[(Heb. 13:14). He never was in ]{.class_4938}["such a case" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 15) a day in his life, yet he told you to follow HIM (2 Thess. 3:9; 1 Cor. 4:16, 11:1). Paul fulfilled ]{.class_4938}[every requirement ]{.class_4937}[for verse 15, but he did not get ]{.class_4938}[anything promised ]{.class_4937}[to a man who fulfilled it (vss. 12--14).]{.class_4938}

PSALM 145

145:1 "I will extol thee, my God, O king; and I will bless thy name for ever and ever.

2 Every day will I bless thee; and I will praise thy name for ever and ever.

3 Great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised; and his greatness is unsearchable.

4 One generation shall praise thy works to another, and shall declare thy mighty acts.

5 I will speak of the glorious honour of thy majesty, and of thy wondrous works.

6 And men shall speak of the might of thy terrible acts: and I will declare thy greatness.

7 They shall abundantly utter the memory of thy great goodness, and shall sing of thy righteousness.

There are nine attributes of God in the first section (see "9" and "fruitfulness" in Ps. 150).

[1. ]{.class_4938}["Thy name" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 1).]{.class_4938}

[2. ]{.class_4938}["Thy works" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 4).]{.class_4938}

[3. ]{.class_4938}["Thy mighty acts" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 4).]{.class_4938}

[4. ]{.class_4938}["Thy majesty" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 5).]{.class_4938}

[5. ]{.class_4938}["Thy wondrous works" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 5).]{.class_4938}

[6. ]{.class_4938}["Thy terrible acts" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 6).]{.class_4938}

[7. ]{.class_4938}["Thy greatness ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 6).]{.class_4938}

[8. ]{.class_4938}["Thy great goodness" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 7).]{.class_4938}

[9. ]{.class_4938}["Thy righteousness" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 7).]{.class_4938}

[There are at least ninety sermons in these nine attributes. Practically, David is praising God and prophesying; note the ]{.class_4938}["shall" ]{.class_4939}[in verses 4, 6--7. When David says he will bless and praise God's name ]{.class_4938}["for ever and ever" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 2), he is speaking of his life on earth, but as it turns out, both he and the New Testament Christian do this literally, for they wind up with eternal life (Rev. 21--22). Their praises join the seraphic, cherubic, and angelic hosts in eternity. The context, however, is ]{.class_4938}["O king" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 1), and this is the King of the Jews---the Jewish Messiah of Israel---as we have seen on many occasions before now (Ps. 2:6, 5:2, 18:50, 20:9, 21:7, 44:4, and all of Ps. 45).]{.class_4938}

[Verse 3 is like Psalm 48:1, and the first nine words have been made into a hymn. Verse 4 takes place literally. Noah's parents get their information from individuals who knew Seth personally (Gen. 5:7), and Abraham met someone who was on the ark (Shem, Gen. 11:11). Moses's mother (Exod. 2:7--8) undoubtedly told him all about Isaac and Jacob, for she was ]{.class_4938}["a daughter of Levi" ]{.class_4939}[(Exod. 2:1). This explains a phenomenon that no atheist, agnostic, or humanist has been able to explain. The Jews are the only race who can trace their ancestry back to the "first" of their race. No German can tell you who the first German was. Not one Indian or Chinaman has the "foggiest" as to who his FIRST ancestor was. With all of their Sutras, Vedas, Shastas, Puranas, and "ancient wisdom of the East," not one Brahman or Hindu can tell you from where he came. There is not in America one individual of French, Italian, Polish, or Scandinavian ancestry who can tell you who the FIRST member of his race was. A Jew can, and an Arabian almost can. But both of these groups have their genealogies recorded in ONE BOOK. It is not the Koran, and it is not any other so called "scripture" from another religion. It is in the Bible; THE BOOK.]{.class_4938}

[The ]{.class_4938}["works," "mighty acts," ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["terrible acts"]{.class_4939}[ of God (vss. 4--6) were repeated by word of mouth from generation to generation, so that archaeologists have found evidence of the Deluge among every major race on the earth (Melanesians, Canadian Indians, Europeans, Africans, Polynesians, Maori, Babylonians, Japanese, and Chinese). The true account of such acts are recorded in a Book that survives to this day, although it took two thousand years to write.]{.class_4938}

The Jews passed on what they heard from Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. The prophets passed on what they heard from Moses, Joshua, the judges, and David. The Lord Jesus Christ reviewed it, added to it, and the apostles passed it on. Paul passed it on to the Gentile converts, and they carried it to the ends of the earth, passing these things on by way of evangelists and witnesses and, eventually, pastors and Bible teachers. The world little notes nor long remembers the deeds of Roland, DuGeslein, or Richard the Lionhearted, but there are more than four million children in America who all know the name of a man who built a boat and the name of a man who got swallowed by a whale. One (Jonah) lived two thousand years before Christopher Columbus was born, and the other one (Noah) lived 2,500 years before Julius Caesar was assassinated.

[Note how David's prophesying comes to pass literally in that Christians sing about God's ]{.class_4938}["righteousness" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 7). Witness: "My hope is built on nothing less than Jesus' blood and righteousness." "Dressed in His righteousness alone, faultless to stand before the throne," "Could my tears forever flow, could my zeal no languor know, these for sin could not atone; Thou must save and Thou alone." "Universal peace be flowing like a river, and His righteousness will go from shore to shore," "My hope, blessed Jesus, is anchored in Thee, Thy righteousness only now covereth me." "Let not conscience make you linger, nor of fitness fondly dream; all the fitness He requireth is to feel your need of Him."]{.class_4938}

145:8 "The LORD is gracious, and full of compassion; slow to anger, and of great mercy.

9 The LORD is good to all: and his tender mercies are over all his works.

10 All thy works shall praise thee, O LORD; and thy saints shall bless thee.

11 They shall speak of the glory of thy kingdom, and talk of thy power;

12 To make known to the sons of men his mighty acts, and the glorious majesty of his kingdom.

13 Thy kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and thy dominion endureth throughout all generations."

[Verses 8 and 9 are self explanatory. Illustrations of God putting up with man are too numerous to list. He waits four hundred years for the Amorites to get right (Gen. 15:13--16). He gives ]{.class_4938}["Jezebel\...space to repent"]{.class_4939}[ (Rev. 2:20--21). He puts up with Ahab till he repents, although he was the worst king Israel ever had (1 Kings 21:29). He put up with Noah's generation for 120 years, before drowning them out (Gen. 6:3). He delayed the destruction of Jerusalem till forty years, after it rejected His Son. He has put up with slavery, perversion, drunkenness, fraud, embezzlement, torture, war, stealing, lying, cheating, murder, adultery, pornography, fornication, cannibalism, and sex perversion for more than 1,960 years without yet unleashing "the fateful lightning of His terrible swift sword" (Rev. 19:15). ]{.class_4938}["Slow to anger."]{.class_4939}

"[The LORD is good to all" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 9). He causes His rain to fall on the just and the unjust, and He allows many sinners to live eighty years, with all of their necessities met, when they do nothing but ignore Him, insult Him, and often curse Him. Verses 10--13 are Millennial. Note the word ]{.class_4938}["kingdom" ]{.class_4939}[showing up four times.]{.class_4938}

[The scholars will get sidetracked at verse 13 and confound the literal Millennial reign of Jesus Christ with God's sovereignty over the nations (see Ps. 47:8, 93:1, 96:10, 97:1, etc.). The ]{.class_4938}["generations" ]{.class_4939}[of verse 13 spread the knowledge of the Lord to the ends of the earth---not prophesying (Zech. 13:1--3), but by bragging about a literal King (Mal. 1:11; Isa. 2:1--6), sitting on a literal throne, and having dominion over the earth (see Ps. 72:8 and compare with vs. 13) from a literal mountain (Isa. 32:1, 33:22, 44:6). The constant effort of the Alexandrian Cult to bury the Bible in the ]{.class_4938}[past, ]{.class_4937}[only shows their lack of spiritual discernment and Scriptural understanding. No one in verses 11--13 is ]{.class_4938}[preaching the gospel. ]{.class_4937}[They are praising a majesty like Solomon's (1 Chron. 29:25). This is ]{.class_4938}["the kingdom, and the power, and the glory" ]{.class_4939}[(Matt. 6:13) ]{.class_4938}[literally ]{.class_4937}[coming to earth. It is in the future. The Alexandrian Cult wants it buried in the ]{.class_4938}[past, ]{.class_4937}[or else converted into a spiritual, metaphorical, allegorical generalized condition that exists today, that has nothing concrete or operative about it.]{.class_4938}

[Kroll's bumfoozled exegesis is typical. He says that verses 11--13 refer to the kingdom of God. He was told by the Holy Spirit, in Romans 14:17, that the kingdom of God was a spiritual kingdom and ]{.class_4938}[never once referred to a literal kingdom in this age. ]{.class_4937}[Not knowing the difference between the kingdom of God and the kingdom of Heaven---and I never met a Bible-correcting Alexandrian who knew the difference---Liberty University pretends that ]{.class_4938}["the glorious majesty of his kingdom" ]{.class_4939}[is\...what? Nothing. He simply strikes the whole of verse 12 out of the passage because it will not match his private interpretation. And who could forget poor, old "sissy britches," Gary Hudson, way back in 1990, with his "ten errors" in the ]{.class_4938}[King James Bible, ]{.class_4937}[producing, for one of them, the word "majesty" from the ]{.class_4938}[New King James Version ]{.class_4937}[as a substitute for ]{.class_4938}["mighty power"]{.class_4939}[ (Luke 9:43)? Who could forget his combobulating, confounding of the Church Age with Christ's coming in power and glory (Ps. 45:3, 4; 93:1; 96:6; Isa. 2:10, 19; 26:10; 2 Pet. 1:16)? The word]{.class_4938}[ "majesty"]{.class_4939}[ describes a king, and not one time in either Testament is it ever found as a reference to Christ's ]{.class_4938}[first coming. ]{.class_4937}[Peter applies it to His transfiguration, not His "body of humility." Of twenty two occurrences in the Old Testament, not once is it a reference to anything on earth in the Church Age. ]{.class_4938}[Megaleioteti ]{.class_4937}[(μεγαλειοτητι),]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4937}[in Luke 9:43, could not have been translated as "majesty" if every Greek lexicon written by man said that was the way it should be translated. The Holy Spirit would have already overruled that private interpretation ]{.class_4938}[before ]{.class_4937}[the New Testament was written. These irreverent fools (and that is a compliment in their case) thought because the Greek word had been translated that way ]{.class_4938}[one time ]{.class_4937}[(2 Pet. 1:16), that it had to be that way every other time it showed up. By following their depraved and degenerate "lexicons," the translators of the ]{.class_4938}[NKJV ]{.class_4937}[(Curtis Hutson, James Price, Arthur Farstad, Elmer Towns, Harold Ockenga, et al.) produced a whole mob of people (Luke 9:37) who saw Christ's transfigured body, which, actually, only Peter, James, and John saw (Luke 9:28).]{.class_4938}

[That is the end result of mistaking the Second Coming and the Millennial Kingdom with God's "general sovereignty" as Lord of the Universe. ]{.class_4938}["And sin, when it is finished, bringeth forth death" ]{.class_4939}[(James 1:15).]{.class_4938}

In the Millennium, most of history is past, and this explains why the saints in the passage are talking about what the Lord has done, not what He is going to do. They speak of a present Jewish theocracy (vs. 11) and talk about what God did to bring it about (vs. 12), prophesying (vs. 13) that it will have no end, and so it comes to pass (Rev. 20--21), for the end of the Millennial Kingdom (Rev. 20:10) goes right into the new heavens and earth without a break (see comments on the "seventh day" in Genesis 2:1--2 in that commentary).

145:14 "The LORD upholdeth all that fall, and raiseth up all those that be bowed down.

15 The eyes of all wait upon thee; and thou givest them their meat in due season.

16 Thou openest thine hand, and satisfiest the desire of every living thing.

17 The LORD is righteous in all his ways, and holy in all his works.

18 The LORD is nigh unto all them that call upon him, to all that call upon him in truth.

19 He will fulfill the desire of them that fear him; he also will hear their cry, and will save them.

20 The LORD preserveth all them that love him: but all the wicked will he destroy.

21 My mouth shall speak the praise of the LORD: and let all flesh bless his holy name for ever and ever."

[Although the passage is preeminently practical, we must still tread carefully. For example: how would you apply verses 15 and 16 to a million starving African children or eight hundred thousand prisoners in Russian jails who haven't had a staple diet for years? Many a Jew who fell (vs. 14) and was ]{.class_4938}["bowed down" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 14) wasn't raised up at all. He fell on his face after trying to work a fourteen-hour day on eight hundred calories (1944), and then he went to hell, and his body was cremated. You don't just go hop, skip, jump through the Psalms like Spurgeon, Kroll, Yates, and Motyer do. Not if you have "breathin' sense." The way Adam Clarke, Martin Geier, John Trapp, John Lorinus, Albert Barnes, Henry Melvill, Alexander Pitcairne, William Secker, and Hannah Moore get around the truth in these matters is by ]{.class_4938}[pretending ]{.class_4937}[that the people mentioned in verses 15 and 16 are all people, in this age, who ]{.class_4938}["call upon" ]{.class_4939}[God (vs. 18) and ]{.class_4938}["fear him" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 19). But that is not what the Holy Bible said---in any version of any translation coming from any set of manuscripts.]{.class_4938}

"[If he relies on God\...God upholds him" (]{.class_4938}[Clarke]{.class_4937}[).]{.class_4938}

"[None are raised but by supernatural interposition" (]{.class_4938}[Martin Geir]{.class_4937}[).]{.class_4938}

"[The eyes of all ought to wait upon the Lord" (]{.class_4938}[George Stanhope]{.class_4937}[).]{.class_4938}

[The meat (vs. 15) is the flesh of Christ, and it should be "Givest them their meat in His time" (]{.class_4938}[John Gilo]{.class_4937}[).]{.class_4938}

"[If there are any desires in us which are not satisfied (vs. 16) it is through their being self created ones, which is our own fault" (]{.class_4938}[Andrew Fuller]{.class_4937}[).]{.class_4938}

Pious slop isn't it?

Is it a "self created" desire to want a piece of bread after you have been in a "dog cell" at Buchenwald (1942) for a week with nothing to eat?

Paul Getty, Bill Cosby, Eddie Murphy, Bing Crosby, Bob Hope, Ted Turner, M. L. King Jr, and Jesse Jackson) than those who do (John Paton, Gen. William Booth, John Wesley, Pappy Reveal, Lester Roloff, Fannie Crosby, et al.).]{.class_4938}

[The passage said God used supernatural interposition (see Geier, above) for ]{.class_4938}["all that fall" ]{.class_4939}[and ]{.class_4938}["all those that be bowed down." ]{.class_4939}[Why, then, did Geier imply that some are not raised up because they have no "super interposition"? He meant that God didn't mean what He ]{.class_4938}[said.]{.class_4937}

"[If he relies on God\..." (see Clarke, above); there was no "if' anywhere near verse 14.]{.class_4938}

[There is nothing like doubly separated, militant Fundamentalism, drooling pious clichés, to make a ]{.class_4938}[liar ]{.class_4937}[out of God.]{.class_4938}

[1. The ]{.class_4938}["bowed down" ]{.class_4939}[ones (vs. 14) fell in Daniel's Seventieth Week, and every one of them was upheld by God so he did not take the mark of the beast. Every one of them was literally raised up at the Advent (see Job 42:13 and remarks in that commentary).]{.class_4938}

[2. Verse 15 is a statement that animals, as well as men, have to look to God for their sustenance (Ps. 104:21). If they get their meat, they get it in ]{.class_4938}["due season." ]{.class_4939}[If it is not ]{.class_4938}["due,"]{.class_4939}[ they don't get it. Couldn't any fool have seen that when he read Psalm 34:10? In the Millennium, the verse can be taken ]{.class_4938}[literally ]{.class_4937}[and inclusively. It cannot be taken so now, and even in the Millennium the disobedient who refuse to come up to the Feast of Tabernacles (Zech. 14:16--17) will suffer droughts that may prevent them from having ]{.class_4938}["their meat in due season." ]{.class_4939}[I repeat: you do not go slap, crash, slam bang, rip roar through the Psalms.]{.class_4938}

[3. The ]{.class_4938}["desire of every living thing" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 16) is]{.class_4938}[ not ]{.class_4937}[satisfied until the Millennium. The general truth is that what men and animals really want can only come from the hand of God. Mother Nature alone, or man himself, cannot supply anything that is really desirable. In the case of food, clothing, transportation, and communications, man simply takes credit for what God graciously allows him to find out or what God graciously provides, at certain times.]{.class_4938}

[I have known men who desired to be saved, but rejected Christ and wound up in the Lake of Fire; God did NOT satisfy their ]{.class_4938}["desire" ]{.class_4939}[because they wouldn't meet His terms for satisfying it. One desired for God to spare his baby, but God took it. One wanted his boy to grow up, and God killed him in an auto accident. Do the scholars mean to tell me these are "self-created desires" and not just as fundamentally human (and natural) as Mary's desire to keep her Son from being killed (Matt. 2:12)? These super pious asses are saying that the only desires that God fulfills are ]{.class_4938}[supernatural ones that He instills in them. ]{.class_4937}[Rubbish: Quatsch! The commentators know nothing about life. No man who knew anything about life would be stupid enough to think that verses 15--16 could be fulfilled ]{.class_4938}[literally]{.class_4937}[ in the Church Age.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 17 is self explanatory. God makes no errors or mistakes, not even in a violent, contradictory case like Job (see Job 2:3 and comments in that commentary). He is right, and we are wrong, no matter how it looks. He is righteous in drowning millions of babies in Noah's day and burning several hundred in Lot's day. See what I'm driving at? If a man winds up in a lake of fire burning forever, it cannot be because ]{.class_4938}[God ]{.class_4937}[was unrighteous or because God made a mistake. You will have to look elsewhere for the problem. Was God ]{.class_4938}["righteous" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 17) in allowing Stephen to be stoned? How about letting His own Son be whipped and crucified? How about those children in Bethlehem under two-years-old? Now THERE is a case. God could have prevented all of these, and He ]{.class_4938}[didn't, ]{.class_4937}[yet He is ]{.class_4938}["righteous in all his ways." ]{.class_4939}[This was Job's problem (Job 9:17, 19:7). It wasn't that Job ]{.class_4938}[wasn't ]{.class_4937}[righteous (Job 1:1; he was, Job 31). The problem was what would he say or do when God did something that ]{.class_4938}[Job ]{.class_4937}[didn't think was right. That's where "the rubber meets the pavement." ]{.class_4938}[That ]{.class_4937}[is what produces atheists.]{.class_4938}

[Verse 18 is self explanatory. God will save a man who calls ]{.class_4938}['upon Him"]{.class_4939}[ (Rom. 10:13), but he must believe that God "IS." In the case of salvation, he is required to believe ]{.class_4938}[three ]{.class_4937}[more things, beside the ]{.class_4938}[base ]{.class_4937}[existence of God (in which devils believe; see James 2:19). He is to believe that the Lord Jesus died for his sins, was buried, and rose from the dead (Rom. 10:9--10; 1 Cor. 15:1--5). You cannot call upon God ]{.class_4938}["in truth," ]{.class_4939}[in this age, without approaching Him through Jesus Christ (see Matt. 11:27 and John 14:6). It is true that God will often answer a call of distress from a sinner without the name of Jesus being mentioned (see Acts 10:1--5), but this is ]{.class_4938}[temporal, ]{.class_4937}[not eternal salvation. Salvation is through the Son (1 John 5:8--13), and if you call upon God ]{.class_4938}["in truth" ]{.class_4939}[in this age, you must come in the name (John 14:6; 1 John 5:13) of Jesus Christ. To call upon God ]{.class_4938}["in truth," ]{.class_4939}[for Christians, means to pray with a stable mind, without unconfessed sin in the life, with belief fining the heart, and with the glory of God in mind, according to the revealed will of God in the Scriptures.]{.class_4938}

[The Tribulation conditions---works---are stated next. The bowed down man who is going to be upheld and raised up and fed is the man who ]{.class_4938}[fears God ]{.class_4937}[(see Ps. 25:12, 112:1, and comments) and ]{.class_4938}[loves God ]{.class_4937}[(see James 1:12). When he does this, the wicked will be destroyed (vs. 20), ]{.class_4938}["all the wicked will he destroy." ]{.class_4939}[You couldn't jam these verses into the Church Age with a jamming machine. In the next verse (vs. 21), you are told that ]{.class_4938}["all flesh"]{.class_4939}[ will bless God's ]{.class_4938}["holy name for ever and ever." ]{.class_4939}[This is the ]{.class_4938}["all flesh"]{.class_4939}[ that comes to worship the Lord in ]{.class_4938}["the new heavens and the new earth," ]{.class_4939}[according to Isaiah 66:22--23. Sayonara, Auf wiedersehn, Aloha, Adios, Ade, Au revoir, and Goodbye!

Spurgeon says: "All men of every race, condition, or generation should unite to glorify God\...our hearts revel in the delight of praising Him. Our mouth, our mind, our lip, our life shall be our Lord's throughout this mortal existence and when time shall be no more."

PSALM 146

146:1 "Praise ye the LORD. Praise the LORD, O my soul.

2 While I live will I praise the LORD: I will sing praises unto my God while I have any being.

3 Put not your trust in princes, nor in the son of man, in whom there is no help.

4 His breath goeth forth, he returneth to his earth; in that very day his thoughts perish.

5 Happy is he that hath the God of Jacob for his help, whose hope is in the LORD his God:

6 Which made heaven, and earth, the sea, and all that therein is: which keepeth truth for ever:

7 Which executeth judgment for the oppressed: which giveth food to the hungry. The LORD looseth the prisoners:

8 The LORD openeth the eyes of the blind: the LORD raiseth them that are bowed down: the LORD loveth the righteous:

9 The LORD preserveth the strangers; he relieveth the fatherless and widow: but the way of the wicked he turneth upside down.

10 The LORD shall reign for ever, even thy God, O Zion, unto all generations. Praise ye the LORD."

The whole Psalm is in the context of the Tribulation. Verses 7--10 are prophecies, and they are given as assurances to Tribulation saints. The "fruit loops" in the Scholar's Union see only the bare thread of a devotional discourse.

Note the following things in the Psalm which we have already commented on a dozen times in the Psaltery:

[1. The ]{.class_4938}["poor and needy" ]{.class_4939}[that need ]{.class_4938}["visitation" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 9 with Ps. 109:16; James 5:1--8; and Matt. 25:31--36). There can be no doubt whatsoever about the doctrinal application. The Scholar's Union was simply too busy correcting what they couldn't understand and trying to bring the ]{.class_4938}[AV ]{.class_4937}[into line with their own ignorance.]{.class_4938}

2\. The LORD reigning at Zion (vs. 10) which, again, is misread for his general sovereignty over the universe (see Ps. 45:6 and 103:19). The reign, here, is in the future (as in Rev. 20:1--8), and it begins not with Genesis 1, but with Revelation 11:15.

3\. The hungry who are fed (vs. 7) are those of Lamentations 5:9 and Revelation 12:10--14, who do not take the mark of the beast.

[4. It is ]{.class_4938}["the God of Jacob" ]{.class_4939}[who delivers in that dispensation, for it is ]{.class_4938}["the time of Jacob's trouble" ]{.class_4939}[(Jer. 30:7). Daniel calls this ]{.class_4938}["a time of trouble" ]{.class_4939}[(Dan. 12:1) connected with ]{.class_4938}["the great prince which standeth for the children of thy people." ]{.class_4939}[The church is found nowhere in either context.]{.class_4938}

[5. The ]{.class_4938}["prisoners" ]{.class_4939}[of Psalm 142:7 show up again in verse 7; see also Psalm 69:33.]{.class_4938}

6\. Finally, notice the key reference to the God of creation (vs. 6), which characterizes the "other gospel" which will be preached in the Tribulation (Rev. 14:6) as the last message the Gentiles will hear before the Advent (Joel 2). See comments under Psalms 69:34, 77:18, 102:19, 113:6, 115:15, 16; 121:2, and 124:8.

Nothing is obscure. Acts 14:15--17 and 17:26--29 show why Psalm 146 contains verse 6---which, to the Scholar's Union, appears only as proof that God can help you and feed you (vs. 7), since He had enough power to create the heaven and the earth. Look where the angel quotes Psalm 146 almost verbatim (Rev. 10:5--6). No Bible correcting fool---and that is the mildest term possible for the Scholar's Union---could find one verse that gave any light on anything that anyone hadn't figured out in Chrysostom's day (A.D. 400).

There is a twelve point sermon in the Psalm for there are twelve things that it says God is able to do:

[1. He is able to ]{.class_4938}[create, ]{.class_4937}[for He created the heaven, the earth, and the sea (vs. 6).]{.class_4938}

[2. He is able to keep ]{.class_4938}[truth ]{.class_4937}[forever, despite the feebleminded handling of Psalm 12:6--7 and John 8:47 by the Alexandrians.]{.class_4938}

[3. He is able to execute ]{.class_4938}["judgment for the oppressed"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 7), even when he doesn't (see Ecc. 8:14). This judgment is often delayed for years, and sometimes it is delayed unto ]{.class_4938}["the day of judgment and perdition of ungodly men" ]{.class_4939}[(2 Pet. 3:7). [4. He is able to give ]{.class_4938}["food to the hungry"]{.class_4939}[ (vs. 7), although there are idolators in India whom He will allow to starve to death in the streets of Calcutta; babies of sex crazed, demon-possessed Africans that he will let starve to death; and Jews that he let starve to death in Treblinka, Buchenwald, Belsen Belsen, and Auschwitz. God does NOT always do what He is able to do.]{.class_4938}

5\. He is able to release prisoners. But he did not allow a jail break from Dachau or Buchenwald, and He never allowed more than a few men to get off "The Rock" (Alcatraz). America's prisons, since 1964 (the Criminal's Rights Act, erroneously called the "Civil Rights Act"), have been stuffed with so many prisoners that their release is not determined by God at all.
