6\. The Lord can open the eyes of the blind: both physically blind (Matt. 9:28, 11:5) and spiritually blind (John 9:39, 41; Luke 24:31, 45). Note the doctrinal application of the verse in Daniel's Seventieth Week (Isa. 29:18, 35:5, and 42:7).

7\. The Lord can raise up those that are bowed down with age or crippling diseases (see Luke 13:11, John 5:8), and He can raise up the dead (John 11:43).

[8. ]{.class_4938}["The Lord loveth the righteous" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 8), which obviously has nothing to do with YOU. 10\. He is able to relieve the fatherless children and the woman with a family, but no husband; but often (at least in New York, Chicago, St. Louis, Philadelphia, Baltimore, Detroit, and Washington, D.C.) he just lets them kill each other off so the news media can blame it on the "prejudice" of white Southerners. King Jr. (or an anti Constitutional lawbreaker like Abraham Lincoln or a Catholic dictator like John Kennedy), their "ways" are often cherished, reverenced, kept, promoted, and propagated long after they are dead. 12\. The Lord is able to reign on this earth over the United Nations, the European Federation, and the Orient. This He will most certainly do (Isa. 4:5, 8:18, 12:6, 24:23, and 31:4).

PSALM 147

147:1 "Praise ye the LORD: for it is good to sing praises unto our God; for it is pleasant; and praise is comely.

[Not one professor at one Fundamental or Conservative college, seminary, university, or Bible institute in North America could find the revelations as given in English ]{.class_4938}[in 1611. ]{.class_4937}[These ]{.class_4938}[advanced revelations ]{.class_4937}[come from the English text of 1611, and not one scholar (living or dead), who ever recommended ]{.class_4938}[one ]{.class_4937}[other English translation, was able to find the first reference in the chain that led to the revelation. [Snow was called "wooly water" by the ancients (]{.class_4938}[Treasury]{.class_4937}[, p. 432). Aristophanes called it "flying fleeces of wool." As wool is a protective blanket against the cold, so snow not only enriches the ground with minerals for the spring crops, but protects them from temperatures far below zero. As wool preserves a man in a blizzard, so snow helps preserve man by furnishing torrents of fresh water where rain is scarce. Melted snow and ice fills the rivers of south Germany, western Austria, southern France, northern Italy, and the western part of the United States.]{.class_4938}

PSALM 148

148:1 "Praise ye the LORD. Praise ye the LORD from the heavens: praise him in the heights.

2 Praise ye him, all his angels: praise ye him, all his hosts.

3 Praise ye him, sun and moon: praise him, all ye stars of light.

4 Praise him, ye heavens of heavens, and ye waters that be above the heavens.

5 Let them praise the name of the LORD: for he commanded, and they were created.

6 He hath also stablished them for ever and ever: he hath made a decree which shall not pass."

God is to be praised twelve times in the Psalm (three times in vs. 1, twice each in verses 2--3, once in verse 4, again once in verse 5, once in verse 7, once in verse 13, and once in verse 14). Twelve is Israel's number, the number by which all groups must be regulated. Hence, twelve gates on Jerusalem, twelve birthstones on the high priest's breastplate, twelve apostles, twelve patriarchs, twelve months, twelve fruits, twelve nations to match the twelve tribes, twelve half notes on the scale (Ab, A, Bb, B, C, Db, D, Eb, E, F, F#, G), twelve colors (red, yellow, blue, green, purple, orange, yellow orange, blue green, yellow green, bluish-purple, reddish purple, and black), and twelve constellations to be inhabited after the Millennium (see Rev. 21--22 in that commentary). I have only listed eleven sets of twelve. There has to be ONE more somewhere. I can guess that it may be ANIMALS, but this in only a guess, about which I am not sure.

You may have noticed that Psalm 144 dealt with the restoration of Israel to the land of Palestine under the Jewish Messiah. One hundred forty-four is 12 times 12.

Perhaps the fruits are figs, grapes, dates, nuts, apples, oranges, peaches, pears, bananas, cherries, mangoes, and avocados; but these are only guesses.

Counting the Biblical animals, they might be the lion, leopard, bear, horse, camel, ox, sheep, wolf, snake, fish, whale, and dog (although the dog and fox are like the "wolf"). Or it could be twelve domesticated animals: horse, mule, sheep, chicken, duck, goose, pig, camel, dog, cat, mouse, turkey, and squirrel; plus twelve wild animals: lion, leopard, bear, elephant, rhinoceros, wolf, water buffalo, ape, tiger, snake, deer, and whale. But all of this is guesswork.

[The praise begins ]{.class_4938}["from the heavens," ]{.class_4939}[which must include ]{.class_4938}["ye heaven of heavens,"]{.class_4939}[ and ]{.class_4938}["ye]{.class_4939}[ ]{.class_4938}[waters that be above the heavens" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 4). And there went both Scofield Board of Editors down the tube, with all the faculties and staffs of every seminary in America and Europe. ]{.class_4938}[The supernatural struck again. ]{.class_4937}[(See Ps. 48:2.) I trow not. The whale is a type of Satan, and this is why the term ]{.class_4938}["leviathan" ]{.class_4939}[is applied to him ]{.class_4938}[one ]{.class_4937}[time in the Scriptures. He is distinct from beasts and cattle. PSALM 149

149:1 "Praise ye the LORD. Sing unto the LORD a new song, and his praise in the congregation of saints.

2 Let Israel rejoice in him that made him: let the children of Zion be joyful in their King.

3 Let them praise his name in the dance: let them sing praises unto him with the timbrel and harp.

4 For the LORD taketh pleasure in his people: he will beautify the meek with salvation.

5 Let the saints be joyful in glory: let them sing aloud upon their beds.

6 Let the high praises of God be in their mouth, and a two-edged sword in their hand;

7 To execute vengeance upon the heathen, and punishments upon the people;

8 To bind their kings with chains, and their nobles with fetters of iron;

9 To execute upon them the judgment written: this honour have all his saints. Praise ye the LORD."

The entire Psalm is Millennial in doctrinal application. There isn't one difficult verse, so you know what to expect from Liberty University. You get it. "Like the other Psalms\...Psalm 149 appears to fit best at a later date in Israel's history\...many good authors believe it not beyond the realm of possibility for David to have written this psalm. The question remains open. Over the centuries this Psalm has been misunderstood and misused."

[No question is open or closed. ]{.class_4937}[It wouldn't make any difference WHEN the Psalm was written or WHO wrote it. If it was ever "misused" or "misunderstood," it was misunderstood and misused by someone exactly like Kroll or the faculty at Liberty University. PSALM 150

150:1 "Praise ye the LORD. Praise God in his sanctuary: praise him in the firmament of his power.

2 Praise him for his mighty acts: praise him according to his excellent greatness.

3 Praise him with the sound of the trumpet: praise him with the psaltery and harp.

4 Praise him with the timbrel and dance: praise him with stringed instruments and organs.

5 Praise him upon the loud cymbals: praise him upon the high sounding cymbals.

6 Let every thing that hath breath praise the LORD. Praise ye the LORD."

[We have come a long way since ]{.class_4938}["Blessed is the man" ]{.class_4939}[(Ps. 1:1). Psalm 150 is divisible by 10, 6, 5, 3, and 2. (Psalm 149 was not divisible by anything.) The term ]{.class_4938}["Praise him" ]{.class_4939}[occurs nine times in the Psalm, which is the number of fruitfulness, contrary to every book written on the subject before 1970. The word ]{.class_4938}["praise" ]{.class_4939}[occurs thirteen times. ]{.class_4938}[Thirteen is ]{.class_4937}[a "bad cat" just about every time it shows up in the Scripture (see ]{.class_4938}[The Mark of the Beast, ]{.class_4937}[1959). All you could say, here, would be that if anything refuses to "Praise the Lord" they are going to "get it in the neck."]{.class_4938}

There are two sources of this praise:

[1. Praise Him ]{.class_4938}["in his sanctuary." ]{.class_4939}[That will be in the Temple at Jerusalem, in the Millennium, if you are a human being; it will be in the third heaven (Rev. 4:2) if you are an angel or a cherubim.]{.class_4938}

[2. Praise Him ]{.class_4938}["in the firmament of his power." ]{.class_4939}[That will be the one in Psalm 19, which refers to the sun, moon, stars, nebulas, star clusters, and galaxies.]{.class_4938}

You are to praise God for two things:

[1. ]{.class_4938}["His mighty acts."]{.class_4939}

[2. ]{.class_4938}["His excellent greatness" ]{.class_4939}[(see Ps. 76:4, 148:13, 7:17, 89:5, 145:4).]{.class_4938}

[There are nine things you are to praise Him with: the trumpet, psaltery, harp, timbrel, dance, stringed instruments, organs, loud cymbals, and high sounding cymbals. ]{.class_4938}[Nine is the number of fruitfulness. ]{.class_4937}[The Calvinists in Spurgeon's ]{.class_4938}[Treasury ]{.class_4937}[check out at this point and eliminate all nine instruments of praise from their church services. They are all good Campbellites. Alibiing their rejection of Psalm 150 on the grounds of Colossians 3:16 in the New Testament, and the New Testament's lack of instructions on musical instruments; Spurgeon, Calvin, Dabney, Hodge, Berkhof, Kuiyper, Gill, Caryl, Anderson, Stainer, LeBlanc, Dickson, Wordsworth, Bonar, Kitto, et al., get rid of the orchestra but retain the church building, ]{.class_4938}[which is not in the Old or the New Testament; ]{.class_4937}[the pulpit, ]{.class_4938}[which (as it exists today) is not in the Old or the New Testament, ]{.class_4937}[the pews, ]{.class_4938}[which are not in the Old or the New Testament; ]{.class_4937}[the parking lot, ]{.class_4938}[which is not in the Old or the New Testament; ]{.class_4937}[and the Sunday School, ]{.class_4938}[which is not in the Old or the New Testament. ]{.class_4937}[You keep what you like and throw out what you don't like. (It is kinda like Roman Catholicism and Secular Humanism in that respect.)]{.class_4938}

[Bible Baptist Church of Pensacola, Florida, which I pastor, has a thirty two piece orchestra of men and women, boys and girls, including former professional musicians and three converted rock drummers. "Yours truly" plays the tuba. "God hath chosen the bass things of this world\...." (Just kiddin, folks. I just like to hear the educated Christian celebrities in the Alexandrian Cult gag, cough, spit, and cuss under their breath, ]{.class_4938}[where no one else can hear them]{.class_4937}[.)]{.class_4938}

[The ]{.class_4938}["time to dance" ]{.class_4939}[is when a soul gets saved (Ecc. 3:4; see Luke 15:25). This dance is only for the purpose of praising God (see 2 Sam. 6:14) and is uni sex: the males and females do NOT dance together; see Exodus 15:20.]{.class_4938}

I have seen this dance, and it is not the soft shoe shuffle (or buckwing) done by the Charismatics. It is a leaping and stomping, more like a polka, and trotting, like a Jewish "hora," that takes place when the Holy Spirit seizes a congregation of Bible believing people in the midst of a service. My peers---the other Ph.D.s who profess to believe the "Bible" and teach Greek, Hebrew, and Theology---have never seen this in a Biblical setting. They are too dead to be caught in a "live" congregation.

[It is one of the greatest blessings in my life to look back on forty three years of service as an evangelist, pastor, and Bible teacher and realize that not once did I ever "lose the shout." The children in my congregation, who are under twelve years old, have seen and heard what no adult has seen or heard on the campus of any major, recognized Christian school in the world since 1800. My "kids" have seen "the old time religion" of the mountain people (between 1800 and 1930) manifested right in the ]{.class_4938}[middle ]{.class_4937}[of the desultory and deadly Charismatic ministries, the milk soppy, soupy ministries of John Rice and Curtis Hutson; the shallow, pseudo psychiatric counseling of Swindoll, MacArthur, and Gothard; the ecumenical gestures of Stanley, Graham, Van Impe, and Robinson; the Communist efforts of the NCC and the Roman Catholic Church, the neo-Orthodoxy of Bob Jones University and Tennessee Temple, and the apostasy and degeneracy of BBC and PCC. They have heard roars of praise that lifted the rafters from the roof and have seen the altar packed, time after time, with weeping saints shedding literal tears. They have seen grown men, of the roughest caliber, going down into the waters of baptism. They have heard a full piece orchestra putting out "The Old Account," "On Zion's Hill," "The Home Over There," "Ivory Palaces," "Hold the Fort," and "Sweeter As The Days Go By" like no one on this planet will ever hear them again.]{.class_4938}

[My "kids" (1972--2002) have heard shouts of praise as loud as the shouts at a Superbowl game (literally). My 8, 9, 10, and 11 year old children have heard God praised so loudly, by a congregation of five hundred people, that you could not hear the sound system, for a slide presentation, through two 4 foot speakers mounted on the platform. I have had two generations sit under my preaching that have witnessed grown men "running the bases," and even running clear around the building outside, praising God. No child, in my church, was ever left with the impression that ]{.class_4938}[anything ]{.class_4937}[the world had to offer---including degrees, sports, music, awards, news, entertainment, income, religion, or art---was as thrilling, as satisfying, and as exciting as Bible preaching and Biblical salvation. No child, under my ministry, ever got the idea that the world and Satan were the only things that could affect a Christian or a sinner, emotionally. They saw the "old time religion" in practise, despite constant pressure, from every angle---]{.class_4938}[including all of the major Christian educators and all the major Christian schools---]{.class_4937}[to]{.class_4938}[ ]{.class_4937}[abandon it. All the major Christian schools wanted my kids to drop the old Book, the old way of preaching, the old way of taking up offerings, the old way of doing personal work, the old way of sending out missionaries, the old way of training ministerial students, and the old way of praising God. We didn't follow their example, so the ]{.class_4938}["shout" ]{.class_4939}[is still in the camp (Num. 23:21). We praise him with the trumpet, the organ, the piano, the guitar, the drum, the bassoon, the oboe, the flute, the clarinet, the trombone, the cello, the violin, the accordion, the tuba, and even the harmonica and the washtub. I have played a washtub "bass fiddle" on my pulpit, with Christian "hillbillies" singing "Life Is Like A Mountain Railway" and "I'll Fly Away."]{.class_4938}

[Don't like it, do some of you? I didn't think you would. You lost the shout so long ago---]{.class_4938}[if you ever had it---]{.class_4937}[you would break out in a sweat if you got into my congregation for one service on Wednesday night.]{.class_4938}

[1. Not ]{.class_4938}[once, ]{.class_4937}[since 1949, have I ever lost the shout of victory; it has been in the congregations where I preached the entire time and still is.]{.class_4938}

[2. Not ]{.class_4938}[once, ]{.class_4937}[since 1949, have I ever had to promote, or even push for, attendance at any service in my own pastorate or anywhere else.]{.class_4938}

[3. Not ]{.class_4938}[once, ]{.class_4937}[since 1949, have I ever had to have a "stewardship drive" for funds. I have preached on tithing only six times in forty two years. For a ]{.class_4938}[pastor, ]{.class_4937}[that probably is a world record. My radio and TV bills have always been paid when due. I have never had to solicit funds from anyone, for any reason, by any method. "[Praise ye the LORD" ]{.class_4939}[(vs. 6).]{.class_4938}

[Here the Psalter closes, as it should, on the note of PRAISE. The note it began on was Bible reading and meditating on the written words of God (Ps. 1:2).
